title: core-M2: createCoreApp factory + boot plumbing (bodyLimit, helmet, CSP, CORS, request-id, redaction)
description:
## Goal
The `createCoreApp(config, options?)` Fastify factory with all baseline middleware applied in order: secret redaction, request-id, body limit (16MB), helmet, CSP, CORS, error handler hook stub, **trustProxy for PaaS deployment**.

## Why it matters
Every M2 route bead depends on this factory existing with the correct middleware ordering. Wrong order (e.g. error handler before request-id) would propagate bugs to every endpoint. **`trustProxy` is critical for any PaaS deployment** — without it, rate limits see the proxy IP (not the client), logs show wrong IPs, and secure-cookie detection breaks for HTTPS-via-proxy.

## Acceptance criteria
- `createCoreApp(config: CoreConfig, options?: CreateCoreAppOptions): Promise<FastifyInstance>` exported from `@boring/core/server`.
- `CreateCoreAppOptions` type includes `authProvider?`, `userStore?`, `workspaceStore?`, `manageShutdown?`.
- Boot order (Fastify plugin registration):
  1. Fastify instantiated with `trustProxy: true` (or `trustProxy: ['fly-edge', '...']` if we know exact CIDRs). Reads `X-Forwarded-For` correctly through Fly/Render/Railway proxies. Configurable via `CoreConfig.trustProxy` (default `true`); only disable for dev with no proxy.
  2. pino logger with `redact` paths from CORE.md §Config §Secret redaction.
  3. `app.decorate('config', config)` + `db`, `userStore`, `workspaceStore`, `auth` decorators.
  4. Request-ID hook (read `x-request-id` else generate UUID, echo in response).
  5. CORS plugin with `origin: config.cors.origins`, `credentials: true`.
  6. Helmet plugin with strict defaults; CSP exception for `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` (CM6).
  7. Body-limit `config.bodyLimit` (default 16MB).
  8. `app.addRedactionPaths` extension method declared (FastifyInstance augmentation).
- App listens with `closeGraceDelay: 30000` for graceful shutdown.

## Considerations
- `trustProxy: true` accepts ALL forwarded headers — safe behind a known proxy (Fly/Render/Railway) but NOT safe if exposed to the public internet without a proxy. CORE.md §Deployment §Security must note this.
- The `options.authProvider` / `userStore` / `workspaceStore` overrides allow tests + Neon/Clerk swaps; defaults are `BetterAuthProvider` + `PostgresUserStore` + `PostgresWorkspaceStore`.
- `options.manageShutdown === false` skips SIGTERM handler installation (used by tests).
- The auth/route plugins land in subsequent M2 beads — this bead is just the spine.

## Tests
- Boot smoke: `createCoreApp(config)` returns a started Fastify instance.
- `app.config` is the config we passed.
- **trustProxy test**: send request with `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4, 10.0.0.1` → assert `request.ip === '1.2.3.4'` (the leftmost = original client). Without trustProxy, Fastify ignores XFF and reports the immediate connection IP.
- Sending `x-request-id: abc` → response echoes it; absent header → response carries a UUID.
- CORS preflight from an allowed origin returns 200; from a disallowed origin returns 403.
- Helmet's `Strict-Transport-Security` and `X-Content-Type-Options` headers present.

## Refs
CORE.md §API reference §`@boring/core/server`; §Config; §Auth §Security additions; §Deployment §What core requires from the environment.
close_reason: shipped — commit 1cdf4df. Reviewed by codex: revisions applied (addRedactionPaths dynamic keyword set, body-limit test fix).

title: Phase 1: Layout Presets (IdeLayout + ChatLayout)
description:
## Goal Two thin preset layouts (~30 lines each) that build a LayoutConfig and pass it to DockviewShell. ## What to implement ### IdeLayout (src/layouts/IdeLayout.tsx) - Props: { sidebar?: string, center?: string, right?: string } — all panel IDs, overridable - Defaults: { sidebar: 'filetree', center: 'empty', right: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - sidebar group: left, locked, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-400px - center group: center, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - right group: optional, hideHeader, constraints minWidth 300px - Returns Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/IdeLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function IdeLayout({ sidebar = 'filetree', center = 'empty', right }: IdeLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### ChatLayout (src/layouts/ChatLayout.tsx) - Props: { nav?: string, center?: string, surface?: string, sidebar?: string } - Defaults: { nav: 'session-list', center: 'chat', surface: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - nav group: left, locked, hideHeader, fixed 60px width (minWidth=60, maxWidth=60) - center group: center - sidebar group: optional, left, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-350px - surface group: optional, right, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - Returns - Note: surface group is where ArtifactSurfacePane renders a nested DockviewShell - ChatLayout supports an optional file tree sidebar (sidebar='filetree') Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/ChatLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function ChatLayout({ nav = 'session-list', center = 'chat', surface, sidebar }: ChatLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### Layout types (src/layouts/types.ts) ```typescript interface IdeLayoutProps { sidebar?: string // panel ID for left sidebar (default: 'filetree') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'empty') right?: string // panel ID for right rail (optional) } interface ChatLayoutProps { nav?: string // panel ID for nav rail (default: 'session-list') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'chat') surface?: string // panel ID for artifact surface (optional) sidebar?: string // panel ID for optional sidebar (e.g., 'filetree') } ``` ### NavRail props contract (for ChatLayout) - { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen } - App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern) ### Shared hooks extraction (Risk 10 mitigation) Both presets use DockviewShell which handles all heavy logic internally: | Shared concern | Location | Used by | |----------------|----------|---------| | useSidebarLayout() | DockviewShell (wireCollapsible) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelSizing() | DockviewShell (constraint enforcement) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelConstraints() | DockviewShell (group constraints) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | v2 architecture: DockviewShell handles groups, constraints, placeholders, collapse internally. Presets are ~30 lines each building LayoutConfig. No duplicated sidebar/sizing code. ## v1 references - IdeLayout.jsx (1718 lines) — study group arrangement, simplify to ~30 lines - ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) — nav rail + chat + surface arrangement - SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) — artifact surface dockview (nested) - useDockLayout.js (337 lines) — sidebar discovery, collapse toggle ## Tier 1/2/3 composability examples ```tsx // TIER 1: Preset layout with slot overrides // TIER 2: Custom layout via DockviewShell // TIER 3: Standalone components (no dockview, no WorkspaceProvider, no context) import { FileTree, CodeEditor } from '@boring/workspace'
``` ## Considerations - Both are THIN — they only build a LayoutConfig. All heavy logic is in DockviewShell. - Apps that need custom layouts skip presets and use DockviewShell directly - Presets are not special — just convenience functions - Layout config includes version: '2.0' for persistence version tracking ## Unit tests (layouts/presets.test.ts) ### IdeLayout - Test: IdeLayout renders with default props (sidebar=filetree, center=empty) - Test: IdeLayout accepts sidebar override (custom panel ID) - Test: IdeLayout with right prop adds right rail group - Test: IdeLayout without right prop has no right rail group - Test: sidebar group is locked and collapsible - Test: center group is dynamic with placeholder 'empty' ### ChatLayout - Test: ChatLayout renders with default props (nav=session-list, center=chat) - Test: ChatLayout with surface prop adds surface group (dynamic, placeholder) - Test: ChatLayout with sidebar prop adds collapsible sidebar - Test: ChatLayout without surface or sidebar → just nav + center - Test: nav group has fixed 60px width (minWidth=maxWidth=60) - Test: nav group is locked with hidden header ### Both presets - Test: preset output is a valid LayoutConfig object - Test: all panel IDs in LayoutConfig exist in registry (integration with Risk 11 validation) - Test: presets are <50 lines each (lint check) ## Success criteria - IdeLayout renders sidebar + center + optional right rail - ChatLayout renders nav + center + optional sidebar + optional surface - All slot overrides work (custom panel IDs) - Presets are <50 lines each (thin wrappers) - Can be skipped entirely in favor of direct DockviewShell usage
close_reason: done

title: core-M2: routes — workspaces CRUD (create/list/get/update/delete with 409-on-provisioning)
description:
## Goal
Workspace CRUD endpoints. Critical: every endpoint wears `requireWorkspaceMember` (the M2 audit blocker enforces this). DELETE returns 409 if runtime is provisioning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces`: auth-only (NOT requireWorkspaceMember since no `:id` yet).
  - Body: `{ name: string }` (1-100 chars, Zod-validated).
  - Side effect: `WorkspaceStore.create` MUST atomically create the workspace AND insert the calling user as `role='owner'` in `workspace_members` (single transaction). Caller becomes the workspace's first member.
  - If user has no other workspaces, the new one is created with `isDefault: true`; else `isDefault: false`.
  - Response 201: `{ workspace: Workspace, role: 'owner' }`.
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces`: lists workspaces for caller, filtered by `config.appId`. Response 200: `{ workspaces: Workspace[] }`. Order: `is_default=true` first, then `createdAt DESC`.
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id`: requireWorkspaceMember(); returns 200 `{ workspace, role: MemberRole }`.
- `PUT /api/v1/workspaces/:id`: requireWorkspaceMember('editor'); body `{ name?: string }` (Zod). Response 200 `{ workspace }`.
- `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); calls `WorkspaceStore.delete(id)`.
  - On `{removed: true}` → 200 `{ deleted: true }`.
  - On `{removed: false, code: 'workspace_provisioning'}` → 409 with code.
  - On `{removed: false, code: 'not_found'}` → 404.

## Considerations
- Zod schemas live in `src/server/routes/__schemas__/workspaces.ts` for reuse.
- Logging: pino `info` events: `workspace.create`, `workspace.update`, `workspace.delete.start`, `workspace.delete.blocked` (with reason), `workspace.delete.complete`.

## Tests
- Non-member GET → 403 not_member.
- Editor DELETE → 403 forbidden (need owner).
- DELETE during provisioning → 409 workspace_provisioning.
- POST with name > 100 chars → 400 validation_failed.
- POST → assert response.workspace.createdBy === caller.id AND a workspace_members row exists with role='owner' for caller.
- POST when user has no workspaces → response.workspace.isDefault === true.
- POST when user already has a workspace → response.workspace.isDefault === false.

## Refs
CORE.md §API reference §HTTP surface; §Workspace semantics & defaults; §Traps from v1 → Workspace-route authorization.
close_reason: Workspace CRUD routes shipped: POST/GET/GET:id/PUT/DELETE with requireWorkspaceMember + Zod + isDefault + 409 provisioning guard. 19 tests.

title: Wire provisioner into createCoreApp + workspace POST/DELETE handlers (with error-state semantics)
description:
## Context

This bead glues the provisioner SPI into the workspace HTTP routes. Without this, the SPI is dead code. With this, workspace create/delete actually invokes the provisioner.

## Scope

1. Update `CreateCoreAppOptions` in `src/server/app/types.ts`:
   ```ts
   export interface CreateCoreAppOptions {
     authProvider?: AuthProvider
     userStore?: UserStore
     workspaceStore?: WorkspaceStore
     provisioner?: WorkspaceProvisioner   // NEW
     manageShutdown?: boolean
   }
   ```
2. In `createCoreApp.ts`: `app.decorate('provisioner', options?.provisioner ?? null)`. Augment Fastify type module.
3. In `registerWorkspaceRoutes`:
   - **POST /workspaces**: after `store.create`, if provisioner: putRuntime(state=pending) → call provision → on success putRuntime(state=ready, volumePath); on throw putRuntime(state=error, lastError, lastErrorOp='provision') + log + throw 500 PROVISION_FAILED.
   - **DELETE /workspaces/:id**: BEFORE store.delete, if provisioner: try destroy → on throw putRuntime(state=error, lastError, lastErrorOp='destroy') + log + throw 500 DESTROY_FAILED. Then store.delete.
4. Update `WorkspaceStore.putWorkspaceRuntime` signature to accept `lastErrorOp` field.
5. Update `WorkspaceRuntime` shared type: `volumePath: string | null`, `lastErrorOp: 'provision' | 'destroy' | null`.
6. Tests at `routes/__tests__/workspaces.test.ts`:
   - **No-provisioner mode**: POST creates workspace with no runtime row; DELETE removes workspace, no errors.
   - **With FS provisioner**: POST creates workspace + runtime ready + volumePath set; dir exists.
   - **Provision failure**: mock throws → workspace stored, runtime error/last_error_op=provision, HTTP 500 PROVISION_FAILED.
   - **Destroy success**: DELETE → dir gone, both rows gone, 200.
   - **Destroy failure**: mock throws → both rows REMAIN, runtime error/last_error_op=destroy, HTTP 500 DESTROY_FAILED.
   - **Re-issue DELETE after destroy failure**: fix mock; second DELETE succeeds; rows gone.

## Why this exists

This is where the platform actually does work for the user. Without it, the SPI is just types; with it, every workspace create gets a real volume.

## Acceptance criteria

- All six test cases pass.
- Logs include workspace.provision.{start,success,failed} and workspace.destroy.{start,success,failed}.
- HTTP error codes match: PROVISION_FAILED, DESTROY_FAILED.
- No-provisioner mode is a clean code path — no runtime row inserted.

## Considerations

- `store.putWorkspaceRuntime` should be upsert.
- Fastify decorator typing: `app.provisioner` is `WorkspaceProvisioner | null`; handlers must handle null.
- Both Local + Postgres stores need `lastErrorOp` and `volumePath` fields.
- Destroy-failure test must verify workspace row NOT removed (`store.get(id)` returns workspace, not null).

## Tests

Listed above. Plus existing workspace route tests must still pass.

## Out of scope

- Implementing any async provisioner (sync only in v1).
- Background retry / saga / 202 / status monitor — explicitly cut.

## Spec reference

"Workflow — workspace create" + "Workflow — workspace delete" + "Provisioning lock semantics" in v7. Phase 2.
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met: provisioner wired into POST (pending→ready on success, error+lastErrorOp on failure), DELETE (destroy before soft-delete, error state on failure), no-provisioner mode unchanged. 26 route tests pass (7 new provisioner tests). 676/676 core tests green. Commit c7c24d9.

title: core-M2: requireWorkspaceMember preHandler factory (role hierarchy)
description:
## Goal
`requireWorkspaceMember(role?: MemberRole): preHandlerHookHandler` — checks workspace membership for the route's `:id` param, with role-hierarchy gating.

## Why it matters
Every workspace-scoped route MUST wear this hook (M2 audit blocker). v1 didn't, leading to a known vulnerability where any signed-in user could read/write any workspace by guessing UUIDs.

## Acceptance criteria
- `requireWorkspaceMember(role?: MemberRole): preHandlerHookHandler` exported.
- Reads `workspaceId` from `request.params.id` (NOT `workspaceId` — route paths use `:id`).
- Uses `app.workspaceStore.getMemberRole(workspaceId, request.user.id)`:
  - null → 403 `{code: 'not_member'}`.
  - role insufficient → 403 `{code: 'forbidden'}`.
- Role hierarchy: `owner > editor > viewer`. Passing `'editor'` accepts editor + owner. Passing `'owner'` accepts only owner.
- Throws (not 401) if `request.user` is null — authHook should have already 401'd for unauth.

## Considerations
- Hook is pure: state lives on app.workspaceStore. No closures over per-request state.
- Order: auth hook runs FIRST (sets request.user), then this hook on workspace routes.

## Tests
- Owner accessing member route → 200.
- Member accessing owner-only route → 403 `forbidden`.
- Non-member → 403 `not_member`.
- Unauth → throws (caller of plugin should 401 via authHook first).

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §requireWorkspaceMember.
close_reason: requireWorkspaceMember preHandler factory with role hierarchy, 17 tests, codex SHIP after REVISE fix.

title: Migration: apps/minimal → v2
description:
**Goal:** Port apps/minimal to v2 workspace package. Minimal is the simplest app — just FileTree + Editor with no agent, no chat, no auth.

## Current v1 structure (apps/minimal/src/front/App.jsx)
- QueryClientProvider + DataContext.Provider wrapping
- createHttpProvider() for file operations
- Manual flexbox layout: sidebar (240px FileTree) + main (Editor)
- useState for selectedFile
- Components: FileTree (onSelectFile), Editor (path)
- No agent, no chat, no auth, no capabilities, no dockview

## Step-by-step migration checklist

1. **Dependency swap** — update apps/minimal/package.json:
   - Remove: direct ../../../../packages/ imports
   - Add: @boring/workspace as workspace dep

2. **App.jsx rewrite** — replace entire file:
   ```tsx
   // v2 — apps/minimal/src/front/App.tsx
   import { WorkspaceProvider, IdeLayout } from "@boring/workspace"

   export default function App() {
     return (
       <WorkspaceProvider
         layout="ide"
         apiBaseUrl="/api/v1"
         capabilities={["workspace.files"]}
       >
         <IdeLayout />
       </WorkspaceProvider>
     )
   }
   ```

3. **Layout upgrade** — v1 used manual CSS flexbox. v2 uses IdeLayout with dockview (resizable panels, tab management, layout persistence). This is a UX upgrade, not just a migration.

4. **Remove manual state** — v1 managed selectedFile via useState. v2 handles this via Zustand store + panel lifecycle. No app-level state needed.

5. **File extension** — rename .jsx → .tsx

6. **No agent panel** — IdeLayout without agent capabilities shows FileTree + Editor only (agent panel hidden via capability gating)

7. **Test:** `pnpm --filter apps/minimal dev` → file tree loads, clicking file opens editor tab, multiple tabs work, layout persists on reload

## Acceptance criteria
- apps/minimal boots with v2 packages
- FileTree renders and navigates files
- Clicking file opens in CodeMirror editor (tab)
- Multiple files can be open simultaneously (tab bar)
- Layout persists across reloads (Zustand persist)
- App.tsx is <15 lines
- No direct ../../../../packages/ imports remain
- No agent panel visible (capability gated)

## Estimated effort: ~0.25 day
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.
close_reason: shipped in bd50f06: new apps/minimal v2 app with BoringApp wrapper + core server boot + workspace file-tree/editor shell

title: core-M3: AppErrorBoundary + ConfigFetchError boundary UI
description:
## Goal

## Acceptance criteria
- `<AppErrorBoundary>` catches errors thrown during render or in providers.
- Default UI: "Something went wrong" + error.message + a "Reload page" button.
- ConfigFetchError gets a more specific UI: "Cannot reach server" + requestId + "Retry" button (re-mounts ConfigProvider).
- Errors logged to console + (optionally) to a configured error reporter via `onError` prop.

## Considerations
- React 19's error boundaries: use class component (functional error boundaries are still not first-class).

## Tests
- Render component that throws → boundary catches and renders fallback.
- Throw ConfigFetchError → specific UI with retry.

## Refs
CORE.md §Layer 2 §AppErrorBoundary; §Config §ConfigProvider error UX.

title: M3a: SSE file_changed events for tree/file invalidation
description:
**Goal:** When the agent writes files (via writeTool/editTool/bash), the frontend refreshes FileTree + Editor. Files: chat route + `src/front/hooks/useFileChangeStream.ts` (~60 LOC total).

**Architecture:** embedded in the chat UIMessage stream as `data-file-changed` parts. No separate SSE channel — same ordering guarantees, one transport.

**Event shape (AI SDK custom data part):**
```ts
{
  type: 'data-file-changed',
  data: {
    op: 'write' | 'edit' | 'unlink' | 'rename' | 'mkdir',
    path: string,                  // workspace-relative
    oldPath?: string,              // for rename only
    toolCallId: string,            // which tool triggered the change (for UX attribution)
    timestamp: string,             // ISO 8601
    size?: number                  // for write/edit (bytes written)
  }
}
```

**Emission points (backend):**
- `writeTool.execute()` → emit after successful `workspace.writeFile()`.
- `editTool.execute()` → emit after successful `workspace.writeFile()`.
- `bashTool.execute()` → NOT emitted directly (bash writes are invisible to us). Alternative: if sandbox supports fs-watch (future), emit from that. For v1, bash-driven writes won't invalidate FileTree automatically — users may need to refresh. Documented limitation.
- UI tools don't touch fs → no emission.

**Client hook:**
```ts
// src/front/hooks/useFileChangeStream.ts
export function useFileChangeStream() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  return {
    onPart(part: UIMessagePart) {
      if (part.type !== 'data-file-changed') return
      const { op, path, oldPath } = part.data
      // Invalidate file-tree caches
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['tree'] })
      // Invalidate specific file's cached content
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['file', path] })
      if (oldPath) queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['file', oldPath] })
    },
  }
}
```

**ChatPanel wiring:** subscribe to `useChat`'s `onData` or iterate `message.parts` after each streamed chunk.

**Batching:** if 10 files change in 100ms (e.g., npm install), emit all as individual events (don't batch). Client's React Query dedupes invalidations naturally.

**Tests:**
- writeTool → event emitted with op:'write' + correct path + size.
- editTool → op:'edit'.
- unlink → op:'unlink'.
- rename → op:'rename' + both paths.
- mkdir → op:'mkdir'.
- Client hook: event received → `invalidateQueries` called with correct keys.
- E2E: bash tool that writes a file → verify limitation (no auto-refresh); manual refresh picks up the file.

**Logging:** `[file-watch]` prefix. Log each emission with op + path + toolCallId (for correlating with chat transcript).

## Acceptance Criteria

All non-bash write paths emit `data-file-changed` events with the canonical shape. Frontend hook invalidates correct React Query keys. FileTree visibly updates after agent writes a file. Bash-driven writes don't auto-refresh (documented limitation; future fs-watch integration seam noted). Events correlate with tool calls via toolCallId.
acceptance_criteria: Bash tool that writes → chat stream contains `data-file-changed` part → FileTree refetches → new file visible.
close_reason: Implemented in-band file change events for chat stream: write/edit tools now attach fileChanges metadata; stream adapter emits data-file-changed parts (with toolCallId) before tool-output-available; frontend useAgentChat now wires useFileChangeStream invalidation for ['tree'] and ['file', path]/oldPath. Added targeted tests for tool metadata, stream emission ordering, and frontend invalidation hook behavior. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped tests: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/server/catalog/tools/__tests__/writeTool.test.ts src/server/catalog/tools/__tests__/editTool.test.ts src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/stream-adapter.test.ts src/front/hooks/__tests__/useAgentChat.test.ts src/front/hooks/__tests__/useFileChangeStream.test.tsx (46 passed, type errors: none). Bash-driven writes remain a documented limitation (no fs-watch emission path yet).

title: Gov: Review Decisions Registry (12 codex + 12 product/UX findings with adopt/defer rationale)
description:
**Goal:** Embedded registry of what was adopted vs deferred from two external reviews (spec §Review decisions). File: `docs/REVIEW_DECISIONS.md`.

**Why:** future contributors ask "why don't we have optimistic concurrency?" or "why no ChangeReviewDrawer?". Answer: we reviewed it and deferred — here's exactly what was considered and why. Beats re-litigating.

**External architecture review (Codex, 2026-04-22) — 12 findings:**

**Adopted (4):**
1. Workspace CRUD inconsistency → Removed `/api/v1/agent/workspaces` from HTTP surface; session routes trimmed.
2. Exec safety caps → `ExecOptions { timeoutMs, maxOutputBytes }`; `ExecResult` adds `durationMs`, `truncated`, Uint8Array-typed stdout/stderr.
6 (partial). Vercel API resilience → Circuit breaker wrapping the Vercel SDK client. Skipped lease/heartbeat/reconciler machinery (cloud-package territory).
11. Export surface consistency → Decision #15 rewritten. `SessionToolbar` instead of `SessionList`; no rename/delete dialogs; no `useRegisterTool` hook.

**Deferred (8)** — per "ship fast, accept known risk":
3. Optimistic write concurrency — accepted risk; silent last-write-wins on multi-tab (→ `risk.multi-tab-concurrency`).
4. `CanonicalSessionStore` (SQLite) — stay on pi JSONL. Drift risk accepted.
5. Performance budgets + benchmarks — no numbers until users complain.
6 (majority). Lease/heartbeat/reconciler — cloud-package territory.
7. Plugin name reservation + namespacing — last-registered wins (→ `research.plugin-name-collision`).
8. Control plane (policy/audit/telemetry) — retrofit when operationally needed.
9. `ChangeReviewDrawer` (review-before-apply) — workspace-package feature.
10. M0.5 invariant-tests milestone — invariants emerge via normal review.

**Product/UX review (internal, 2026-04-22) — 12 findings:**

**Adopted (11):**
1. Stream resumption in v1 (not deferred) → Session management + M3 milestone (→ `m3a.stream-resume`).
2. Uint8Array-typed exec output (binary-safe) → `ExecResult` interface.
3. Slash commands (`/clear`, `/reset`, `/model`, `/help`, `/cost`) → `m3b.slash-commands`.
4. CLI SSH/headless detection for browser-open → `m1.cli`.
5. Auto-gitignore for workspace artifacts → `m1.cli`.
6. Heartbeat events during long tool calls → `m3b.heartbeat-events`.
7. Vercel snapshot retention (keep-last-2) → `m2.snapshot-retention`.
8. CSS-var ↔ Tailwind contract worked example → `m3b.theme-css`.
9. `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` endpoint → `m3b.changes-route`. (Originally flagged as rejected but upgraded to adopt for Claude Code parity.)
10. CLI `--logout` / `--reset-key` flags → `m1.cli`.
11. Windows-native support statement (WSL2 only) → `m1.direct-sandbox`.
12. Test strategy statement → `infra.*` beads.

**Considered and rejected (0):** all 12 findings landed.

**Total impact:** ~300 LOC added over pre-review baseline (net v1 ≈ 3,070 LOC).

**Cross-reference index:** this doc links each finding → its implementing bead(s), so readers can trace "review said X" → "bead Y implements it."

**Maintenance:** if an accepted-risk item bites (user complains), upgrade it from deferred → new bead + add entry to this doc's changelog.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/REVIEW_DECISIONS.md` exists with all 24 findings (12 codex + 12 product/UX) + adopt/defer reasons + cross-links to implementing beads. Linked from README + DECISIONS.md. Changelog section for future upgrades of deferred items.

title: Migration: apps/agent-backend — ai-sdk → pi-coding-agent rewrite
description:

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Audit workspace_settings encryption + crypto tests + rotation docs
description:
## Context

`workspace_settings.value` is a `bytea` column. `coreConfigSchema.encryption.workspaceSettingsKey` is declared. The contract is: workspace settings (which will store sensitive third-party credentials — Slack tokens, Resend API keys, OAuth client secrets, etc.) are encrypted at rest. Until this audit, we don't know which algorithm is in use, whether per-row nonces are present, or whether the path actually round-trips correctly. v7 spec deliberately picked single-key encryption (no keyring) for v1 to reduce scope.

## Scope

1. Trace `PostgresWorkspaceStore.{getWorkspaceSettings, putWorkspaceSettings}` line-by-line. Identify how `config.encryption.workspaceSettingsKey` is consumed: cipher (AES-GCM? pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt? something else?), nonce shape (per-row? embedded in ciphertext? AAD-bound to workspaceId+key?), encoding format.
2. Add unit tests at `packages/core/src/server/db/stores/__tests__/workspaceSettingsCrypto.test.ts`:
   - **Round-trip**: PUT `{ foo: 'bar', api_key: 'sk_live_abcdef' }` → GET → assert plaintext returned.
   - **At-rest**: SELECT raw `value` from `workspace_settings`; assert bytes ≠ UTF-8 'sk_live_abcdef' (encrypted).
   - **Key-rotation graceful error**: write with key A, swap config to key B, GET should ERROR (not return garbage). v7's "single-key" model means rotation is a planned outage; the code should at least fail loudly when keys mismatch.
   - **Per-row nonce uniqueness** (if AES-GCM): write the same value twice; assert ciphertexts differ.
3. Document algorithm + rotation procedure in `packages/core/docs/CORE.md` under "Workspace settings encryption":
   - Algorithm identified.
   - Rotation procedure: lock writes (feature flag) → re-encrypt all rows with new key → swap config → unlock writes.
4. If the audit reveals settings stored plaintext: file a P0 follow-up bead and surface as a blocker.

## Why this exists

Settings will hold sensitive credentials. Storing plaintext is an audit failure. Confirming the encryption actually runs (and fails loudly on misconfiguration) is foundational. v7 explicitly defers keyring rotation to a future release; this audit confirms the simpler "lock → re-encrypt → unlock" procedure is viable.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four crypto tests pass against a real Postgres `bytea` column.
- `CORE.md` has a "Workspace settings encryption" section with algorithm + rotation steps.
- If plaintext is found: follow-up bead filed as P0.

## Considerations

- Tests must hit real Postgres (not Local store) — otherwise the bytea path isn't exercised.
- Use `sql.unsafe` or raw query to verify at-rest bytes.
- Constant-time comparison: subtle bug if the decrypt path uses `===`.

## Out of scope

- Migrating to keyring (v7 explicitly deferred).
- Force-migrating to AES-256-GCM unless audit reveals an algorithm that's incompatible with safe rotation.

## Spec reference

Decision #11 in v7 spec. Phase 1.
notes: Implementation committed in 4afefb8. Focused real-Postgres crypto test passes. Closure blocked by existing core build/typecheck/full-test failures tracked in boring-ui-v2-bkqe.1.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met: workspaceSettingsCrypto tests landed (4/4 passing), CORE.md documents pgcrypto OpenPGP algorithm + planned-outage rotation procedure (commit 4afefb8). Blocker (bkqe.1 v7 regressions) was closed at commit 20d3abb.

title: PATCH /role + DELETE /member with shared FOR UPDATE last-owner invariant
description:
## Context

Today's `removeMember` route protects against removing the last owner (existing logic). Adding role-change without the same protection introduces a concurrent-mutation race: two simultaneous `PATCH role=editor` on the only two owners could both succeed and end with zero owners. v7 closes this by sharing a transactional invariant across PATCH role AND DELETE member, using `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on all `workspace_members` rows for the workspace within a single transaction.

## Scope

1. Add `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members/:userId/role` route in `src/server/routes/members.ts`:
   - `requireWorkspaceMember('owner')` guard.
   - Body: `{ role: 'owner' | 'editor' | 'viewer' }`.
   - Validate body via zod.
   - Implementation in store: `BEGIN; SELECT * FROM workspace_members WHERE workspace_id = $id FOR UPDATE; -- count owners after proposed mutation; if zero → ROLLBACK 409 LAST_OWNER; UPDATE workspace_members SET role = $newRole WHERE workspace_id = $id AND user_id = $userId; COMMIT;`
   - Returns updated member.

2. Refactor existing `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members/:userId` route to use the same transactional invariant:
   - Allow self-removal (no requireWorkspaceMember('owner') for DELETE — a member can leave their own workspace; only kicking others requires owner).
   - Same SELECT ... FOR UPDATE pattern; same last-owner check before DELETE.

3. Add to `WorkspaceStore` interface: `updateMemberRole(workspaceId, userId, role): Promise<{ member?: WorkspaceMember; code?: 'LAST_OWNER' | 'NOT_MEMBER' | 'WORKSPACE_PROVISIONING' }>`.

4. Implement in `PostgresWorkspaceStore` (with the BEGIN/SELECT FOR UPDATE/COMMIT pattern) and `LocalWorkspaceStore` (with a process-local mutex around the read-check-write window).

5. Tests at `routes/__tests__/members.test.ts`:
   - **Promote viewer → owner** as owner: success, returns updated member.
   - **Demote last owner**: 409 LAST_OWNER, no change.
   - **Demote one of two owners**: success.
   - **Concurrent demote race**: two simultaneous PATCH role=editor on two distinct owners → exactly one succeeds (the second 409 LAST_OWNER). Use `Promise.all` against a real Postgres in test.
   - **Cross-mutation race**: PATCH demote-A + DELETE remove-self-B (both owners) → exactly one succeeds.
   - **Non-owner attempts PATCH**: 403.
   - **Self-removal as non-owner**: 200 (DELETE allows self-leave).

## Why this exists

Last-owner protection is invariant-of-the-system: a workspace must always have at least one owner. v6/v7 elevated this from a single-route check to a shared transactional invariant across all mutations that touch role/membership, closing concurrent-mutation races.

## Acceptance criteria

- All seven test cases pass against real Postgres (race tests need real DB serialization).
- Both PATCH and DELETE share the same transactional pattern (refactored, not duplicated).
- 409 returned with `code: 'LAST_OWNER'` per existing error-code conventions.
- Existing `removeMember` tests still pass.

## Considerations

- `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on Postgres serializes the read-check-write window; under default isolation this is sufficient. SERIALIZABLE isolation isn't needed.
- `LocalWorkspaceStore` doesn't have row-locking — use a per-workspace async mutex (Map<workspaceId, Promise>) to serialize.
- The `requireWorkspaceMember('owner')` guard on PATCH handles the "non-owner trying to promote/demote" case; the transactional check handles the "owner trying to demote themselves while last" case.
- Error envelope: existing `removeMember` returns `{ removed, code }`; mirror that shape for `updateMemberRole`.

## Tests

Listed above.

## Out of scope

- Bulk role updates.
- Audit log of role changes (deferred to future).
- Email notification on role change.

## Spec reference

Decision #6 in v7 spec ("Ownership transfer"). Phase 3.
close_reason: Implemented, all 17 tests pass, committed as c799698

title: [Plugin] Phase 1: Plugin model end-to-end (boring-macro acceptance gate)
description:
Implement the workspace plugin model per packages/workspace/docs/plans/PLUGIN_MODEL.md (v7.0) end-to-end. Phase 1's premise is a PURE-DATA Plugin contract that fans contributions into per-type registries; Phase 2 (npm distribution) and Phase 3 (agent-authored, hot-reload) are out of scope.

## v7.0 framing — separation of concerns

Two distinct concerns, two distinct switches, two distinct layers:

| Concern | Switch | Layer |
|---|---|---|
| Should the agent have file tools? | disableDefaultFileTools: true on createAgentApp | Harness config |
| Should the UI have a file tree / Files tab? | excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] on <WorkspaceProvider> | Workspace config |

These should NOT be conflated. v6.x's 'shared bundle, dual registration path' that tried to unify them was over-engineering; v7.0 untangles.

## Why this exists

Today boring-macro-v2 wires six contribution types — panels, catalogs, agent tools, server routes, chat suggestions, commands — through FIVE different APIs:
  - Panels: <WorkspaceProvider panels={...}>
  - Catalogs: <ChatCenteredShell data={DataPaneConfig}>
  - Agent tools: createAgentApp({ extraTools })
  - Server routes: app.register(registerMacroRoutes, opts)
  - Chat suggestions: <ChatCenteredShell chatSuggestions={...}>
  - Commands: useCommandRegistry().registerCommand (would be)

Plus ~150 LOC of @boring/workspace's UI bridge code inlined into apps/boring-macro-v2/src/server/uiBridge.ts because — historically — the workspace package's server export wasn't built. It now builds; the inlined copy is dead weight this work deletes.

## Acceptance gate

The boring-macro-v2 migration completes (Step 6, see j9p7.18-20 beads). Quantitatively: ~260 LOC → ~46 LOC. Qualitatively: all 10 existing macro e2e specs pass without edits — composer-border, deck, catalog-to-chart, catalog, split-no-clip, layout-persistence, chat-suggestions, chart-tabs, topbar, agent.

Plus the v7.0 'real opt-out' contracts hold:
- excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] truly removes the Files left-tab + code/markdown auto-routing (tools STAY — they're harness substrate)
- disableDefaultFileTools: true on createAgentApp removes file tools entirely (sandboxed agents)

## v7.0 final contract — 6 fields

interface Plugin {
  id: string                          // stable identity
  label?: string
  panels?: PanelConfig[]              // → PanelRegistry
  commands?: CommandConfig[]          // → CommandRegistry
  catalogs?: CatalogConfig[]          // → CatalogRegistry
  agentTools?: AgentTool[]            // → AgentToolRegistry (DOMAIN tools only)
}

filesystemPlugin: UI-only (panels + catalog, no agentTools — file tools live with the harness in @boring/agent via pi-tools-migration's bundle factories).

## Plan review history

- v5: rich contract with 13+ fields
- v5.1: codebase-reality-check sweep
- v5.2: codex round-2 patches (1 P0 + 3 P1 + 3 P2)
- v6: simplification — cut order/dependsOn/optionalDeps/version/routes/chatSuggestions/onMount/PluginInspector + 5 other fields
- v6.1: ultrathink self-audit (validation enumeration, plugin id collision policy, build invariants, testability section, concrete filesystemPlugin source, known-gaps register)
- v6.2: codex round-3 patches (filesystemAgentTools factory P0; drop dataCatalogPlugin from defaults P1; client/server split P2; ChatCenteredShell static-vs-dynamic P2)
- v6.3: gemini fresh-eyes patches (drop 'internal chat-shell plugin' P0; polymorphic Recent covers commands too P1; ExplorerRow JSON-serializable invariant P1; non-React adapter lifecycle limitation P1; makeStaticDataPlugin convenience factory P2)
- v7.0: separation of concerns — file tools are HARNESS substrate, filesystemPlugin is UI-only. Drops dual-registration. Substrate bundle accepts custom non-pi additions (watch_files, stat, git_status, multi_edit).

Both reviewers (codex + gemini) verdicted simplification cuts defensible. onMount has documented re-introduction trigger if a stateful non-React adapter appears.

## Sibling plan dependency

packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md ships FIRST and produces buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle) + buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle). Tracked under boring-ui-v2-uhwx.* bead chain. PLUGIN_MODEL v7.0 lands on top of those bundles — j9p7.2 wires them into createAgentApp; j9p7.9 contributes UI alongside.

## Scope — 19 child beads, 7 numbered steps

  Step 1 (independent reorgs):       3 beads (UI bridge / file ops bundle wiring / validateTool extraction)
  Step 2 (plugin primitives):        5 beads (Plugin types / CatalogRegistry / subscribe retrofit / bootstrap / path-aware resolver)
  Step 3 (default plugin):           1 bead  (filesystemPlugin UI-only)
  Step 4 (entry points):             2 beads (WorkspaceProvider / createWorkspaceAgentApp plain wrap)
  Step 5 (consumer refactors):       6 beads (SurfaceShell fallback / palette catalog-driven / polymorphic Recent / Recent migration / ChatCenteredShell prop drops / WorkbenchLeftPane registry)
  Step 6 (acceptance migration):     3 beads (macro plugin / macro app refactor / macro e2e gate)
  Step 7 (release):                  1 bead  (release notes for breaking changes)

ETA: 6-8 days of focused work; many beads parallelize.

## Three breaking changes (release notes content)

1. CommandPaletteProps — fileSearchFn / onOpenFile props removed (catalog-driven now)
2. ChatCenteredShellProps — data / extraPanels / withCommandPalette removed (chatSuggestions kept)
3. WorkbenchLeftPane — internal hardcoded tab API replaced with PanelRegistry-driven 'left-tab' placement
4. (v7.0 narrowing) excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] semantics changed — now removes UI only, NOT LLM tools. Document for hosts that may have relied on the v6.x conflated behavior.

## Out of scope (Phase 2/3)

- npm sub-path distribution pattern (./client / ./server exports)
- /api/v1/plugins discovery endpoint
- Generic search_catalog(id, query) agent tool
- Agent-authored plugins (create_plugin / update_plugin tools)
- Hot-reload / unregister cleanup
- Plugin sandboxing / capability flags
- Re-introducing onMount, dependsOn, etc. (Phase 2 triggers documented in v6.3 known-gaps register; v7.0 doesn't change those triggers)

This epic is the parent of all PLUGIN_MODEL Phase 1 beads. Do NOT close until macro e2e suite passes (j9p7.20) AND release notes merged (j9p7.21).
notes:
═══ POLISH ROUND 1 (2026-04-29) — v7.6 errata + AGENTS.md alignment ═══

Read AGENTS.md §"Critical architectural invariants" + §"Per-bead development loop" before starting any child bead. The notes below are inherited by ALL child beads.

## v7.6 path mapping (post-Step-0 / j9p7.30)

When a child bead's description says X, read it as Y per v7.6 layout:

| Bead-description path (PRE-v7.6)                          | v7.6 actual destination                                              |
|-----------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| packages/workspace/src/registry/                          | packages/workspace/src/front/registry/                               |
| packages/workspace/src/components/{ui, DataExplorer}/     | packages/workspace/src/front/components/{ui, DataExplorer}/         |
| packages/workspace/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx      | packages/workspace/src/front/components/CommandPalette.tsx          |
| packages/workspace/src/components/PluginErrorBoundary.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/PluginErrorBoundary.tsx     |
| packages/workspace/src/components/chat/                   | DOOMED — see Phases A/C/G                                           |
| packages/workspace/src/dock/                              | packages/workspace/src/front/dock/                                  |
| packages/workspace/src/events/                            | packages/workspace/src/front/events/                                |
| packages/workspace/src/hooks/                             | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/                                 |
| packages/workspace/src/layouts/                           | packages/workspace/src/front/layout/                                |
| packages/workspace/src/panes/                             | DOOMED — workbench panes go INTO their plugin (filesystemPlugin)    |
| packages/workspace/src/panes/EmptyFilePanel               | packages/workspace/src/front/chrome/empty-file-panel/               |
| packages/workspace/src/plugin/                            | SPLIT: shared/plugin/ (types/definePlugin/bootstrap) + front/plugin/ (CatalogRegistry/hooks) + server/plugin/ (server-side) |
| packages/workspace/src/plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin.ts| packages/workspace/src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/index.ts            |
| packages/workspace/src/WorkspaceProvider.tsx              | packages/workspace/src/front/WorkspaceProvider.tsx                  |
| packages/workspace/src/server/                            | (unchanged — already at correct level)                              |
| apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/{index,server}.ts         | apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/{index.ts, server.ts}, with internal {front,server}/ subdirs for components/tools |
| apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/{panels.ts,catalogs.ts}   | apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/front/{panes,sidebar,catalogs}/    |

Hierarchy at a glance (post-v7.6):
```
packages/workspace/src/
├── front/{chrome,components,registry,dock,events,bridge,hooks,layout,plugin,WorkspaceProvider.tsx}
├── server/{createWorkspaceAgentApp.ts, http/, ui-bridge/, plugin/}
├── shared/{ui-bridge.ts, events/, plugin/}
└── plugins/<plugin-id>/{index.ts, [server.ts], front/{panes,sidebar,catalogs}/, [server/{tools,routes}/]}
```

Each plugin INSTANCE owns BOTH halves under one folder (index.ts client + server.ts server). UI-only plugins (filesystemPlugin) only ship index.ts.

## AGENTS.md §3 — cross-review (mandatory before close)

Before closing ANY child bead:
1. Stage your diff (`git add` only the bead's files; isolate from sibling work)
2. Send the diff to the OPPOSITE-kind agent:
   - Claude Code → ask Codex: `cod exec "review staged diff for bead <id>"`
   - Codex → ask Claude Code: `cc -p "review staged diff for bead <id>"`
3. Verdicts: ship → commit + close. revise → fix + re-request (cap 3 rounds). reject → mark blocked + escalate via Agent Mail.
4. Never self-review for closure.

## AGENTS.md §"Critical architectural invariants" — relevant to this epic

Each child bead must NOT violate:
- Inv #1: No node:* imports in src/shared/** — ensure shared/plugin/ keeps clean
- Inv #2: No Buffer in src/shared/** — Uint8Array only
- Inv #6: UiBridge.postCommand is the SINGLE dispatch source — chat-stream data-ui-command parts are display-only derivatives
- Inv #7: Workspace package has ZERO imports from @boring/agent — apps wire ChatPanel via WorkspaceProvider's panel registry. Workspace can import @boring/agent/SHARED (types only) per build invariant.
- Inv #8: Every error has a stable code from canonical error-codes enum

If a bead's implementation requires violating an invariant, that's a STOP signal — escalate via Agent Mail.

## Test expectations (per beads-workflow polish prompt)

Every feature bead must include:
- **Unit tests** for the smallest functional unit (e.g., the helper, the validator, the hook). Test names describe behavior. Logging assertions where an event/command is emitted.
- **Integration tests** for cross-component flows (e.g., bootstrap + provider + registry consumer hook).
- **e2e tests** if the bead changes user-visible behavior. Macro's existing 10-spec suite (j9p7.20) is the canonical Phase 1 e2e gate; per-bead e2e is reserved for visible features (palette, panel routing, etc.).
- **Detailed logging** in tests: `console.info(`[bead j9p7.X] step Y: ...`)` so logs are searchable and ordered. NOT silent tests.
- **Logging in the implementation** for dev-mode events (late-wins-on-id, plugin error boundary catches, plugin inspector state). Pi's logging conventions apply.
- **Cross-review** verifies coverage; revise if test sections are vague or missing.

## Dependency on j9p7.30 (Step 0)

Most child beads now depend on j9p7.30 (Step 0 workspace reorg) because they want to land into the FINAL v7.6 layout. j9p7.30 is the SINGLE structural reorg pass; subsequent phases use the new layout directly. Do NOT move files into pre-v7.6 paths during a child bead — that's wasted work. Wait for .30 to close first.

## When to start implementation

- bv --robot-next OR br ready returns the highest-leverage available bead
- Verify the bead is unblocked (`br dep tree <id>`)
- Per AGENTS.md §1: register with Agent Mail, broadcast claim, file_reservation_paths, then start
- Land Step 0 (j9p7.30) FIRST — it unblocks the whole chain

═══ END POLISH ROUND 1 NOTES ═══

title: [Plugin] Step 1a: UI bridge ownership refactor — move from @boring/agent → @boring/workspace
description:
Move UI bridge implementation from @boring/agent into @boring/workspace, where it conceptually belongs (workspace-flavored substrate). Independently valuable refactor; lands as ONE PR; does not require any plugin model changes to be in flight first.

## Files to move

| From @boring/agent | To @boring/workspace | Lands as |
|---|---|---|
| src/shared/ui-bridge.ts (UiState, UiCommand types) | src/shared/ui-bridge.ts | Core types |
| src/server/ui-bridge/createInMemoryBridge.ts | src/bridge/createInMemoryBridge.ts | Core |
| src/server/catalog/standardCatalog.ts — get_ui_state, exec_ui tool factories | src/server/uiTools.ts | Substrate (registered directly by createWorkspaceAgentApp) |
| src/server/http/routes/ui.ts | src/server/routes/ui.ts | Substrate (registered directly) |

## Macro cleanup

Apps/boring-macro-v2/src/server/uiBridge.ts (currently 9.3 KB / ~150 LOC) is an inlined copy of the workspace's UI bridge core, made when the workspace's server export wasn't built. Now that workspace exports ./server (verified packages/workspace/package.json:27 + tsup.config.ts:7), the inlined copy is dead weight. Delete it; macro's server entry imports from @boring/workspace/server instead.

## Out of scope

- The plugin model (Plugin type, definePlugin, registries) — those land in Step 2.
- File ops bundle extraction — that's Step 1b (parallel-able).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] @boring/workspace exports `createInMemoryBridge` from its core/server entry
- [ ] @boring/workspace's server entry registers /api/v1/ui/* routes from the moved ui.ts
- [ ] @boring/workspace's server entry exposes get_ui_state / exec_ui as built-in agent tools (used by createWorkspaceAgentApp; not part of any plugin)
- [ ] @boring/agent no longer ships ui-bridge code (no UI-bridge imports from agent shared/server)
- [ ] apps/boring-macro-v2/src/server/uiBridge.ts DELETED; src/server/index.ts imports UI bridge from @boring/workspace/server (existing call sites adjusted)
- [ ] Macro starts and the workbench UI bridge round-trip works (open the app, /api/v1/ui/state returns; /api/v1/ui/exec accepts commands)

## Tests

- Existing UI bridge tests in @boring/workspace pass
- Add a smoke test that boots @boring/workspace's server and hits /api/v1/ui/state — confirms substrate routes are registered without any plugins
- Existing macro e2e specs that exercise UI bridge behavior (chart-tabs, topbar) keep passing

## Considerations

- Same code, different home — no behavior change; this is a pure refactor.
- The plugin model treats /api/v1/ui/* as SUBSTRATE (always registered) not part of any plugin — this is decided in v6.3 (PLUGIN_MODEL.md). Don't accidentally fold it into filesystemPlugin.
- get_ui_state / exec_ui registration moves to createWorkspaceAgentApp directly (NOT via a plugin) — they're substrate-level tools.

## Why independent

This work is purely structural. It removes 150 LOC of duplicated code from macro, makes the workspace package's server bundle the single source of truth for UI bridge, and unblocks B11 (createWorkspaceAgentApp expansion) which calls into the moved bridge code. Nothing in Step 2-7 depends on this PR's specifics; only B11 has a hard dep edge.
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: workspace-side moves complete. Verified: createInMemoryBridge.ts at packages/workspace/src/server/ui-bridge/; uiRoutes.ts at packages/workspace/src/server/http/uiRoutes.ts; @boring/agent's ui-bridge directory now empty (just __tests__). The macro-side deletion of the inlined uiBridge.ts is part of j9p7.19 (Step 6 macro app refactor) — separately tracked.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met: workspace and agent server exports resolve for macro startup and macro UI bridge smoke passes

title: [Plugin] Step 4a: <WorkspaceProvider plugins> + excludeDefaults + ./events export + makeStaticDataPlugin
description:
Promote WorkspaceProvider into the client-side bootstrap entry point: it accepts plugins, runs bootstrap(), wires registries to consumers via context, exports the events subpath plugin authors need, AND ships the makeStaticDataPlugin convenience factory for hosts with simple needs.

## What changes in <WorkspaceProvider>

Add new props:
```tsx
interface WorkspaceProviderProps {
  plugins?: Plugin[]
  excludeDefaults?: string[]   // currently only 'filesystem' is a valid id (Phase 1)
  // ...existing props (children, capabilities, etc.)
}
```

On mount:
1. Construct registries (PanelRegistry, CommandRegistry, CatalogRegistry).
2. Build defaults list: [makeFilesystemClientPlugin()] (just panels + catalogs, no agentTools — server has its own copy).
3. Run bootstrap({ plugins: opts.plugins ?? [], defaults, excludeDefaults: opts.excludeDefaults, registries }).
4. Mount existing CommandPalette (now catalog-driven via B13a).
5. Provide registries to children via existing context shape.

## events package export (codex round-2 P1 fix)

packages/workspace/package.json today exposes ./, ./testing, ./ui-shadcn, ./shared, ./server, ./globals.css. Add ./events:

```json
"./events": {
  "types": "./dist/events.d.ts",
  "import": "./dist/events.js"
}
```

Plus the corresponding tsup entry in packages/workspace/tsup.config.ts. Plugin authors then use:
```ts
import { events, useEvent } from "@boring/workspace/events"
import type { WorkspaceEventMap } from "@boring/workspace/events"
```

The barrel ALSO re-exports events for convenience (so `import { events } from "@boring/workspace"` works) but the subpath import is recommended for tree-shaking.

## makeStaticDataPlugin convenience factory (gemini P2)

Hosts that just want 'a data tab with my adapter' shouldn't have to author a full Plugin. Ship a factory:

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/plugin/factories/makeStaticDataPlugin.ts
import { definePlugin, type Plugin } from "../definePlugin"
import { DataExplorer } from "../../components/DataExplorer"
import type { ExplorerAdapter, ExplorerRow } from "../../components/DataExplorer/types"

export interface StaticDataPluginOpts {
  id?: string                                    // defaults 'static-data'
  label?: string                                 // defaults 'Data'
  adapter: ExplorerAdapter
  onActivate?: (row: ExplorerRow) => void
}

export function makeStaticDataPlugin(opts: StaticDataPluginOpts): Plugin {
  const id = opts.id ?? "static-data"
  return definePlugin({
    id,
    label: opts.label ?? "Data",
    panels: [{
      id: `${id}-tab`,
      title: opts.label ?? "Data",
      placement: "left-tab",
      component: () => <DataExplorer adapter={opts.adapter} onActivate={opts.onActivate} />,
      source: "app",
    }],
    catalogs: [{
      id,
      label: opts.label ?? "Data",
      adapter: opts.adapter,
      onSelect: opts.onActivate ?? (() => {}),
    }],
  })
}
```

Restores the one-liner ergonomics for the simple case:
```tsx
<WorkspaceProvider plugins={[makeStaticDataPlugin({ adapter: myAdapter })]}>
  <ChatCenteredShell />
</WorkspaceProvider>
```

Macro doesn't use this — it has custom row-activation behavior (open chart-canvas) that needs surfaceRef access; macro hand-authors macroSeriesPanel.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] WorkspaceProvider accepts plugins + excludeDefaults props
- [ ] On mount, bootstrap() runs once, registers default(s) + user plugins
- [ ] excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] excludes the default
- [ ] Existing children still work (capabilities, theme, etc.)
- [ ] packages/workspace/package.json exports ./events
- [ ] tsup builds dist/events.js + dist/events.d.ts
- [ ] @boring/workspace/events resolves cleanly from a Vite test (no node:* leak)
- [ ] makeStaticDataPlugin exported from @boring/workspace barrel
- [ ] makeStaticDataPlugin({adapter}) returns a Plugin with one panel + one catalog wired correctly

## Tests

- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[testPlugin]}> → useCommands/usePanels/useCatalogs hooks return testPlugin's contributions
- Integration: excludeDefaults excludes — Files left-tab not in PanelRegistry; LLM file ops not in agentTool list (server-side variant)
- Unit: makeStaticDataPlugin returns expected Plugin shape; panel id is `${id}-tab`; catalog id matches
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[makeStaticDataPlugin({adapter: stubAdapter})]}> → workbench shows the Data tab
- Bundle smoke: vite build of a thin app importing @boring/workspace/events does NOT include node:fs / node:path

## Dependencies

- B7 (bootstrap)
- B9 (filesystemPlugin — auto-mounted default)

## Considerations

- WorkspaceProvider is the CLIENT entry. The server entry (createWorkspaceAgentApp) is B11 — separate concerns.
- Don't change existing prop contract beyond adding plugins + excludeDefaults; many tests rely on the current shape.
- makeStaticDataPlugin is a CONVENIENCE — not load-bearing for macro. Macro's bespoke macroSeriesPanel sticks because it needs surface access. The factory exists for the ~80% case (host has an adapter, no custom activation).
- The events package export is small (one re-export of @boring/workspace/src/events/index.ts) but unblocks plugin authors writing 'import { events } from "@boring/workspace/events"' as documented in v6.3.
notes:
═══ Polish round 7 (v7.7) — P1: excludeDefaults semantics correction ═══

ROUNDS 1-6 NOTES STILL APPLY EXCEPT for the misstated "Tests" line clarified below.

### Bug in round-1 acceptance test (P1 from round 6)

Round-1 listed this Test:
> Integration: excludeDefaults excludes — Files left-tab not in PanelRegistry; LLM file ops not in agentTool list (server-side variant)

The second clause is WRONG. Verified against:
- `packages/workspace/src/plugin/bootstrap.ts:31` — bootstrap filters `defaults` by `excludeDefaults`, then fans contributions into registries.
- j9p7.9's locked v7.0 framing: filesystemPlugin is UI-ONLY (panels + catalog; `agentTools === undefined`).
- j9p7.11: createWorkspaceAgentApp passes file tools via the HARNESS (`createAgentApp` registers them by default), NOT via filesystemPlugin.

Therefore `excludeDefaults: ['filesystem']` removes ONLY the UI surface (Files left-tab + code-editor + markdown-editor panels + Files catalog). It does NOT — and cannot — remove LLM file ops, because those tools are not in the plugin's agentTools[] (which is undefined). They ship via the harness substrate and stay regardless.

### Corrected test text (REPLACE the round-1 line)

OLD:
> Integration: excludeDefaults excludes — Files left-tab not in PanelRegistry; LLM file ops not in agentTool list (server-side variant)

NEW:
> Integration (CLIENT): `<WorkspaceProvider plugins={[]} excludeDefaults={['filesystem']}>` → PanelRegistry does NOT contain 'files', 'code-editor', 'markdown-editor'; CatalogRegistry does NOT contain 'files'.
>
> Integration (SERVER, cross-checked by j9p7.11): `createWorkspaceAgentApp({ plugins: [], excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] })` → AgentToolRegistry STILL contains read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash (harness substrate is not gated by excludeDefaults). Files left-tab is excluded from PanelRegistry only.

### Why this matters

Conflating "excludeDefaults removes the UI plugin" with "excludeDefaults removes file tools" leads to test code that asserts a behavior the system cannot exhibit (and shouldn't — the harness owns tools). If a host wants to disable file tools entirely, the path is `createAgentApp({ disableDefaultFileTools: true })`, NOT `excludeDefaults: ['filesystem']`. Two switches, two layers (per j9p7 epic's "v7.0 framing — separation of concerns" table).

### Acceptance criteria additions (round 7)

- [ ] The round-1 test line about "LLM file ops not in agentTool list" is REPLACED by the corrected client/server pair above
- [ ] The bead's wording everywhere distinguishes:
  - excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] = UI off; tools STILL on
  - createAgentApp({ disableDefaultFileTools: true }) = tools off; UI orthogonal

title: [Plugin] Step 4b: createWorkspaceAgentApp({ plugins }) — plain wrap (no dual-registration)
description:
Expand createWorkspaceAgentApp to be the SERVER entry point for plugin-aware hosts: it wraps createAgentApp PLAINLY (no disableDefaultFileTools dance — v7.0 simplification), runs bootstrap() server-side, and registers substrate routes (UI bridge + file/tree/search) directly.

## v7.0 simplification

Earlier drafts had createWorkspaceAgentApp pass disableDefaultFileTools: true to createAgentApp so file tools came through filesystemPlugin's agentTools instead. v7.0 drops this: harness owns tools always; filesystemPlugin is UI-only. createWorkspaceAgentApp just wraps createAgentApp + runs bootstrap.

## API

```ts
import { createAgentApp } from '@boring/agent/server'
import { bootstrap } from '../plugin/bootstrap'
import { filesystemPlugin } from '../plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin'
import { uiTools } from './uiTools'
import { uiRoutes, fileRoutes } from './routes'

export interface CreateWorkspaceAgentAppOpts {
  workspaceRoot: string
  plugins?: Plugin[]
  excludeDefaults?: string[]
  // forwarded options
}

export async function createWorkspaceAgentApp(opts: CreateWorkspaceAgentAppOpts) {
  // PLAIN WRAP — harness registers file tools by default (per pi-tools-migration)
  const app = await createAgentApp({
    workspaceRoot: opts.workspaceRoot,
    extraTools: uiTools,                // get_ui_state, exec_ui — substrate
    // NO disableDefaultFileTools — harness owns file tools
    // …forward other opts
  })

  // Substrate routes — always-on
  await app.register(uiRoutes)          // /api/v1/ui/*
  await app.register(fileRoutes)        // /api/v1/files, /tree, /files/search

  // Server-side registries
  const registries = {
    panels: new PanelRegistry(),
    commands: new CommandRegistry(),
    catalogs: new CatalogRegistry(),
    agentTools: app.agentToolRegistry,
  }

  // filesystemPlugin (UI-only) goes through bootstrap; pluginAgentTools includes
  // any DOMAIN tools host plugins contribute (e.g., macro's execute_sql).
  bootstrap({
    plugins: opts.plugins ?? [],
    defaults: [filesystemPlugin],
    excludeDefaults: opts.excludeDefaults,
    registries,
  })

  return app
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] createWorkspaceAgentApp accepts plugins + excludeDefaults
- [ ] Internally calls createAgentApp WITHOUT disableDefaultFileTools (harness handles file tools)
- [ ] uiRoutes + fileRoutes registered as substrate (always-on)
- [ ] filesystemPlugin (UI-only) auto-mounts via bootstrap unless excludeDefaults excludes it
- [ ] User plugins from opts.plugins flow into bootstrap after defaults
- [ ] Server-side AgentToolRegistry includes harness file tools (read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash) AND plugin agentTools (e.g., macro's execute_sql)
- [ ] /api/v1/files/search responds correctly regardless of excludeDefaults setting
- [ ] excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] removes Files left-tab from PanelRegistry but does NOT remove file tools from AgentToolRegistry

## Tests

- Integration: createWorkspaceAgentApp({ plugins: [] }) → tool catalog includes read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash (defaults via harness)
- Integration: createWorkspaceAgentApp({ plugins: [], excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] }) → tool catalog STILL includes read/write/etc; Files left-tab not in PanelRegistry
- Integration: user plugin's domain agentTools appear in tool catalog endpoint
- Integration: /api/v1/files/search responds correctly even with excludeDefaults: ['filesystem']

## Dependencies

- B1 (UI bridge moved to workspace)
- B2 (file ops bundle wiring per pi-tools-migration)
- B7 (bootstrap)
- B9 (filesystemPlugin — plain const, UI-only)
- pi-tools-migration's harness/filesystem bundles ready in @boring/agent

## Considerations

- CLIENT and SERVER plugin tracks remain independent (separate registries per process). filesystemPlugin shape is identical on both sides.
- This bead is SIMPLER than v6.3 design — no dual-registration coordination, no disableDefaultFileTools indirection.
- For hosts that want to opt out of file tools entirely (rare), they use createAgentApp directly with disableDefaultFileTools: true (NOT createWorkspaceAgentApp).
- Substrate routes register BEFORE bootstrap so workspace UI HTTP plumbing is available even without plugins.

═══ Polish round 3 (v7.6) — cross-bead drift ═══

ROUND 2 NOTES STILL APPLY.

Round 3 additions:

### useDebouncedCatalogSearch hook is consumed, NOT shipped here

The hook itself ships in j9p7.10 (WorkspaceProvider's plugin-model-front machinery additions). This bead CONSUMES the hook from front/plugin/. Cleaner: .13 has zero plugin-model surface to add.

### Per-catalog error isolation (TWO layers, NOT one)

.13 implements ONE layer:
- try/catch around `await catalog.adapter.search(query, signal)`
- on rejection: catch error, surface as inline ErrorChip in the palette result group; other catalogs continue

j9p7.22 (per-plugin error boundaries) implements the SECOND layer:
- <PluginErrorBoundary pluginId={catalog.pluginId}> wraps each catalog's row renderer
- on a row throwing during render: boundary catches; ErrorChip per row; other rows continue

Both layers needed. .13's try/catch handles SEARCH failures (HTTP errors, adapter throws). .22's boundary handles RENDER failures (a row's renderer throws).

### Polymorphic Recent (j9p7.14) integration

.14's RecentEntry types live in front/components/CommandPalette.tsx (or front/components/recent/types.ts). .13 needs to:
- Import RecentEntry type from .14's location (after .14 lands)
- Render Recent section using the discriminated union
- On selection, push new RecentEntry to localStorage (atomic with existing addToRecent helper)

If .13 lands BEFORE .14: Recent shows files only (legacy behavior). If .14 lands BEFORE .13: Recent doesn't render until .13 wires it.

→ Sequence: .13 → .14 → .15 (migration). Polish round 3 keeps this order.

### Cross-review

Codex review of:
- AbortSignal propagation (signal threading from useDebouncedCatalogSearch into adapter.search → into fetch)
- per-catalog error isolation (one bad catalog doesn't kill the palette)

title: [Plugin] Step 5a-ii: Polymorphic Recent (catalog rows + commands)
description:
Replace the buggy Recent storage in CommandPalette with a discriminated union that handles BOTH catalog rows and commands. Fixes the type-mix bug where commands stored as 'cmd:foo' rendered through FilePathLabel.

## Why polymorphic (gemini P1)

Earlier drafts said 'Recent is catalog-only — commands don't appear in Recent.' Gemini correctly pushed back: every mature command palette (VS Code, Raycast, Linear) keeps recent commands. Re-running 'Toggle Theme' / 'Format JSON' is a primary use case.

## RecentEntry shape (final v6.3)

```ts
type RecentEntry =
  | {
      type: 'catalog'
      catalogId: string                  // ↔ CatalogConfig.id
      rowId: string                      // ↔ ExplorerRow.id
      rowSnapshot: ExplorerRow           // serialized at selection time
      selectedAt: number                 // unix ms
    }
  | {
      type: 'command'
      commandId: string                  // ↔ CommandConfig.id
      titleSnapshot: string              // for display if command is unregistered
      selectedAt: number
    }
```

## Storage invariant (gemini P1: ExplorerRow MUST be JSON-serializable)

rowSnapshot is round-tripped through JSON.stringify / JSON.parse via localStorage. Adapters whose ExplorerRow contains Date / Map / Set / functions / React nodes / class instances will be silently corrupted on save and crash on restore.

Document in §"Recent serialization invariant": any ExplorerRow participating in Recent MUST be 100% JSON-serializable. Adapters with non-serializable values must serialize at row construction time (e.g., Date → ISO string) and re-hydrate in their renderer.

NO deserializeRecent hook in Phase 1 — add as non-breaking optional if a real case appears.

## Render flow

1. Load entries from localStorage on palette open.
2. For each entry: look up source by id (catalog by catalogId, command by commandId).
3. If absent (plugin uninstalled, command unregistered) → DROP the entry.
4. If catalog:
   - Render via catalog.adapter.row renderer using rowSnapshot
   - On click → catalog.onSelect(rowSnapshot)
5. If command:
   - Render the title with a small 'command' chip (visual cue: this is an action, not data)
   - On click → command.run()
6. Add freshly-selected items to Recent at top; cap at e.g. 50 entries; persist back to localStorage.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] RecentEntry is a discriminated union by 'type' field
- [ ] Loading from localStorage handles both branches
- [ ] Saving from selection: catalog rows → catalog branch; command runs → command branch
- [ ] Orphan entries (source no longer registered) are dropped silently on render
- [ ] No 'cmd:foo' string entries possible in the new shape
- [ ] Recent renders both branches with appropriate visuals (catalog: adapter renderer; command: title + command chip)
- [ ] §"Recent serialization invariant" documented in inline JSDoc on RecentEntry type

## Tests

- Unit: RecentEntry discriminated union narrows correctly via switch (type: 'catalog' | 'command')
- Unit: addToRecent({type:'catalog', ...}) prepends to list; cap at 50; oldest evicted
- Unit: render orphan entry (catalogId not in registry) → not rendered; entry remains in storage but quietly skipped
- Integration: open palette, click a file in Files catalog → close + reopen → file appears in Recent rendered as file path
- Integration: open palette, run a command from Commands list → close + reopen → command appears in Recent with chip
- Integration: rapidly select 51 distinct items → only 50 remain in Recent (oldest evicted)

## Dependencies

- B13a (palette catalog-driven) — landing on the same component

## Considerations

- Don't conflate Recent (cross-plugin shared list) with per-catalog Recent (a future idea). One Recent for the whole palette is the v6.3 plan.
- The titleSnapshot on commands handles the case where a user runs a command, the plugin gets unregistered, and the user reopens the palette — they see the title text but clicking does nothing useful (so we drop the entry on click). Edge case but real.
- localStorage migration from old 'cmd:foo' / path strings is split into B13c.
- The cap (50) is arbitrary; tune later. localStorage is generously sized but not infinite.
