title: [Plugin] Step 5a-iii: localStorage Recent migration from legacy string entries
notes:
═══ Polish round 5 (v7.6) — fresh-eyes audit: previously skipped ═══

Round 5 — concrete content for Recent localStorage migration (rounds 2/3/4 missed this bead):

### v7.6 path

packages/workspace/src/front/components/recent/migrate.ts (NEW)

### Migration function (sketch from spec)

```ts
export function migrateRecent(): RecentEntry[] {
  const raw = localStorage.getItem('boring-ui-v2:command-palette:recent')
  if (!raw) return []
  let parsed: unknown
  try { parsed = JSON.parse(raw) } catch { return [] }
  if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return []
  // ... see j9p7.14 for full implementation
}
```

### Concrete tests (rounds 2-4 skipped this bead)

Unit (front/components/recent/__tests__/migrate.test.ts):
1. `migrateRecent with no localStorage entry → returns []`
2. `migrateRecent with corrupt JSON → returns []`
3. `migrateRecent with non-array (e.g., object) → returns []`
4. `migrateRecent with array of strings (legacy v6 format) → all strings parsed`
5. `migrateRecent with 'cmd:foo' string → maps to {type:'command', commandId:'foo', titleSnapshot:'foo'}`
6. `migrateRecent with plain path string → maps to {type:'catalog', catalogId:'files', rowId:path, rowSnapshot:{id:path,label:path}}`
7. `migrateRecent with mixed array (some strings, some objects) → handles each correctly; doesn't fail mid-iteration`
8. `migrateRecent on already-migrated shape (v7+ RecentEntry[]) → returns as-is (idempotent)`
9. `migrateRecent persists migrated shape back to localStorage on first run` (one-shot; subsequent runs skip)

### Idempotency invariant

Running migrateRecent() twice on the same input returns the same output. Test:
10. `migrateRecent() === migrateRecent()` (called twice in succession; second call sees the new shape)

### Best-effort recovery

If a legacy 'cmd:foo' entry can't be mapped (e.g., 'foo' command no longer registered), the entry is INCLUDED but RENDER drops it (j9p7.14's orphan handling). Migration doesn't pre-filter; orphan filtering is render-time.

### Logging assertions

- console.info('[j9p7.15] migrateRecent: legacy entries={N}, migrated={M}, dropped={N-M}')
- For each migration: console.info('[j9p7.15] migrate {entry} → {new shape}')

### Cross-review

Codex/cc review of:
- The migration's idempotency (the second run should be a no-op)
- Edge cases: empty array, single entry, MAX entries (50)
- Backward compat: an app that's been NEVER opened sees an empty list (no crash)

### When migration runs

On first palette open per session. Wired into useEffect inside CommandPalette mount lifecycle.
close_reason: Legacy Recent migration complete. Integrated into loadRecent(), idempotent, 951 tests pass.

title: [Plugin] Step 5b: <ChatCenteredShell /> drops  +  (KEEPS chatSuggestions; commands stay imperative)
description:
Migrate ChatCenteredShell off the legacy `data: DataPaneConfig` and `extraPanels: string[]` props. Workbench data tab and panel set both flow through registries now. CRITICALLY: KEEP the chatSuggestions prop and KEEP all in-component imperative useCommandRegistry().registerCommand() calls (gemini P0 — they close over local React state and cannot be hoisted to a module-scope plugin).

## What changes

DROP from ChatCenteredShellProps:
- `data: DataPaneConfig` — replaced by hosts contributing left-tab panels via plugins (e.g., macro's macroSeriesPanel or makeStaticDataPlugin)
- `extraPanels: string[]` — replaced by a new optional `allowedPanels?: string[]` for hosts that want to gate which registered panels appear in this shell instance

KEEP:
- `chatSuggestions: ChatSuggestion[]` — explicitly stays as a prop, NOT a plugin contribution. Per v6.3: chat suggestions fail the aggregation-honest test (UX cap forces curation; registry adds nothing).
- ALL existing useEffect-based command registration inside ChatCenteredShell (toggleSessions, toggleWorkbench, newChat, per-session quick-switch). Per gemini P0: toggleDrawer/toggleSurface close over `useState(drawerOpen)` defined inside the component; a module-scope 'internal chat-shell plugin' cannot reach instance state, and event-bridging just shifts the closure problem.

The previous draft (v6.2) said to move static commands into a module-scope 'internal chat-shell plugin'. v6.3 reverses that. ChatCenteredShell's commands are component-instance-bound — they stay imperative.

## Why coexistence is fine

The CommandRegistry subscribe retrofit (B6) ensures late-imperative registrations propagate to an open palette. The plugin model is for module-stable contributions; component-instance commands belong inside the component. Honest separation.

## extraPanels → allowedPanels semantics

```ts
interface ChatCenteredShellProps {
  // ...
  /** Optional whitelist of panel ids to allow in this shell's surface.
   *  Default: all registered panels are accepted. Hosts override when
   *  they want a stripped-down surface. */
  allowedPanels?: string[]
}
```

Internal plumbing: when SurfaceShell mounts a panel, check that its id is in allowedPanels (if defined). Otherwise refuse with a no-op + dev warn.

## Breaking change

ChatCenteredShellProps changes. Document in release notes (B19) as Breaking Change #2:
- Removed: `data?: DataPaneConfig` — migrate to a plugin contributing a left-tab panel OR `makeStaticDataPlugin({adapter})` for the simple case
- Removed: `extraPanels?: string[]` — migrate to allowedPanels (semantically narrower) OR drop entirely if unrestricted is fine
- KEPT: `chatSuggestions?: ChatSuggestion[]`, `withCommandPalette?: boolean`, all session-related props

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ChatCenteredShellProps no longer has `data` or `extraPanels`
- [ ] ChatCenteredShellProps gains `allowedPanels?: string[]` (optional)
- [ ] ChatCenteredShellProps STILL has `chatSuggestions?: ChatSuggestion[]`
- [ ] All in-component useEffect+registerCommand calls preserved (toggleSessions, toggleWorkbench, newChat, per-session)
- [ ] Workbench data tab content: comes from PanelRegistry's left-tab panels (filesystemPlugin's Files tab + any user plugin's contribution; macro's case = macroSeriesPanel)
- [ ] If allowedPanels defined, SurfaceShell refuses to mount panels not in the whitelist; dev warn

## Tests

- Unit: ChatCenteredShell renders without `data` prop; workbench data tab shows whichever left-tab panels are registered
- Unit: allowedPanels: ['code-editor', 'deck'] gates SurfaceShell to only those
- Integration: macro app passes makeStaticDataPlugin/macroSeriesPanel via WorkspaceProvider plugins; ChatCenteredShell shows the resulting left-tab — no `data` prop needed
- Regression: toggleDrawer keyboard shortcut still works (the imperative useEffect registration of chat-shell.toggleSessions still hooks the palette command)
- E2E: macro's existing chat-suggestions e2e spec still passes (chatSuggestions prop still works)

## Dependencies

- B5 (CatalogRegistry — for any catalog-driven left-tab content)
- B6 (CommandRegistry subscribe — propagates the imperative command registrations to the palette)
- B15 (WorkbenchLeftPane registry-driven — needs to be ready for left-tab panels to flow)

## Considerations

- DO NOT introduce a fictional 'chat-shell plugin' that re-emits events to handle these commands. That's the same closure problem — the event handler still closes over the React state and can't be module-scope.
- The cleanest mental model: 'commands inside ChatCenteredShell are LOCAL — they're only meaningful while this component is mounted. They register on mount, unregister on unmount. The plugin model is for GLOBAL contributions that exist across component lifecycle.'
- chatSuggestions could theoretically be a plugin contribution — but gemini correctly noted (and v6.3 spec explicitly states) that the UX cap (~6 cards) forces hosts to curate, defeating registry aggregation. Keep it as a prop.
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: scope-shifted to PLUGIN_MODEL Phase 1.5 (v7.4 merger). 'ChatCenteredShell drops data + extraPanels props' is OBSOLETE because Phase 1.5 deletes the entire shell. See j9p7.24 (Phase A — decompose shells into per-pane folders) and j9p7.29 (Phase G — delete ChatCenteredShell).

title: [Plugin] Step 5c: WorkbenchLeftPane registry-driven (excludeDefaults honesty)
description:
Refactor WorkbenchLeftPane to read its tab list from PanelRegistry (placement: 'left-tab') instead of hardcoding Files / Data tabs. Makes excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] truly remove the Files tab — closes the v6.2 P1 'real opt-out' hole.

## Why current code fails

Verified packages/workspace/src/components/chat/WorkbenchLeftPane.tsx:97, :174, :181 — Files and Data are hardcoded in the tab strip. excludeDefaults can suppress filesystem agent tools and catalogs but the dead Files tab still renders because it's not registry-driven. Contradicts the plan's 'real opt-out' promise.

## Refactor

```tsx
// packages/workspace/src/components/chat/WorkbenchLeftPane.tsx
import { useActivePanels } from "../../plugin/useActivePanels"
import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent } from "@radix/tabs"  // or whatever existing primitives

export function WorkbenchLeftPane(props: WorkbenchLeftPaneProps) {
  const allPanels = useActivePanels()
  const leftTabPanels = allPanels.filter(p => p.placement === 'left-tab')
  // Sort by registration order (already what useActivePanels returns)

  if (leftTabPanels.length === 0) {
    return <EmptyTabPlaceholder />   // or hide the whole pane
  }

  return (
    <Tabs defaultValue={leftTabPanels[0]?.id}>
      <TabsList>
        {leftTabPanels.map(p => (
          <TabsTrigger key={p.id} value={p.id}>{p.title}</TabsTrigger>
        ))}
      </TabsList>
      {leftTabPanels.map(p => (
        <TabsContent key={p.id} value={p.id}>
          <PanelHost panelId={p.id} />
        </TabsContent>
      ))}
    </Tabs>
  )
}
```

## Sort order

Registration order; defaults register first → Files appears before user-contributed left-tab panels. Sufficient determinism.

## 'right-tab' reservation

PanelConfig.placement gains 'right-tab' alongside 'left-tab'. No Phase 1 component consumes 'right-tab' (WorkbenchRightPane doesn't exist). Reserved in the contract for symmetry with the first plugin that needs a right-side registry-driven tab. Don't try to ship WorkbenchRightPane in Phase 1.

## Breaking change

WorkbenchLeftPane internal API changes — its previous prop interface (specific to hardcoded tabs) is replaced by a registry-driven render. Document as Breaking Change #3 in release notes (B19). External hosts unlikely to import WorkbenchLeftPane directly (it's used inside ChatCenteredShell), but document for completeness.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] WorkbenchLeftPane queries PanelRegistry for placement: 'left-tab' panels
- [ ] Renders one tab per registered left-tab panel
- [ ] Default sort: registration order (defaults first, user plugins after)
- [ ] excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] → Files tab not rendered
- [ ] Empty state: when zero left-tab panels are registered, sensible placeholder (or pane collapsed)
- [ ] Hardcoded references to 'files' / 'data' panel ids removed from WorkbenchLeftPane

## Tests

- Unit: render WorkbenchLeftPane with PanelRegistry containing only Files tab → one tab visible
- Unit: render with two left-tab panels → both tabs rendered in registration order
- Unit: render with zero left-tab panels → empty state (no broken tab strip)
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[]} excludeDefaults={['filesystem']}> → Files tab is NOT rendered in WorkbenchLeftPane
- E2E: macro app post-migration → workbench left pane shows Files tab + Macro Series tab (no generic 'Data' tab — dataCatalogPlugin was dropped from defaults)

## Dependencies

- B6 (PanelRegistry subscribe retrofit — useActivePanels needs to propagate)
- B9 (filesystemPlugin contributes the Files tab as placement: 'left-tab')

## Considerations

- The 'Data' tab in v5.x was hardcoded as a separate tab driven by ChatCenteredShell.data prop. v6.2 dropped dataCatalogPlugin from defaults. The 'Data tab' as a concept disappears — plugins that want a workbench data presence register their own left-tab panel (macro's macroSeriesPanel).
- 'right-tab' placement is in the contract but un-consumed; just don't strip it from the type union.
- If a plugin contributes a left-tab panel with the SAME id as filesystem's 'files', late-wins-on-id replaces (composition pattern). Useful for hosts that want to reskin the file browser.
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: scope-shifted to PLUGIN_MODEL Phase 1.5 (v7.4 merger). 'WorkbenchLeftPane registry-driven' is OBSOLETE because Phase 1.5 makes WorkbenchLeftPane just a registered core panel (no more hardcoded tab strip). See j9p7.25 (Phase B — wire core panel registrations in WorkspaceProvider).

title: [Plugin] Step 6-i: macro plugin module — split into client + server (same id 'boring-macro')
description:
Author the boring-macro-v2 plugin in two halves: client (panels, catalogs) and server (agent tools), both with id 'boring-macro'. Pattern lines up with Phase 2 npm sub-path distribution (./client + ./server exports). Honors the build invariant: NEVER cross-import server-only symbols into client.

## Why two halves with the same id

Per v6.2 codex round-3 P2 fix: macroAgentTools is server-only (DB queries, fs access). Importing it into client-facing plugin/index.ts violates the build invariant (would leak node:* into the browser bundle). Two halves, same id 'boring-macro', different bags of contributions, registered independently in client and server registries.

## File structure

```
apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/
├── index.ts            CLIENT — makeMacroClientPlugin + macroChatSuggestions const
├── server.ts           SERVER — makeMacroServerPlugin
├── plugin.shared.ts    (optional) shared constants like the plugin id literal
├── panels.ts           panel definitions: chartCanvasPanel, deckPanel, macroSeriesPanel
└── catalogs.ts         seriesCatalog (CatalogConfig)
```

## plugin/index.ts (client)

```ts
"use client"
import { definePlugin, type Plugin } from "@boring/workspace"
import type { ChatSuggestion } from "@boring/agent/front-shadcn"
import { chartCanvasPanel, deckPanel, macroSeriesPanel } from "./panels"
import { seriesCatalog } from "./catalogs"

export const macroChatSuggestions: ChatSuggestion[] = [
  { label: "Find a series", prompt: "Help me find a macro series." },
  { label: "Plot Real GDP", prompt: "Plot Real GDP since 1990." },
  // ...the existing ~8 suggestions from src/web/App.tsx
]

export const makeMacroClientPlugin = (): Plugin =>
  definePlugin({
    id: "boring-macro",
    label: "Macro",
    panels: [chartCanvasPanel, deckPanel, macroSeriesPanel],
    catalogs: [seriesCatalog],
  })
```

## plugin/server.ts (server)

```ts
"use server"
import { definePlugin, type Plugin } from "@boring/workspace"
import { macroAgentTools } from "../server/macroTools"

export const makeMacroServerPlugin = (): Plugin =>
  definePlugin({
    id: "boring-macro",
    label: "Macro",
    agentTools: macroAgentTools,
  })
```

## panels.ts — macroSeriesPanel (NEW; replaces the v5 dataPaneConfig wiring)

Macro's existing dataPaneConfig (verified apps/boring-macro-v2/src/web/App.tsx:96-127) wires DataExplorer with macroAdapter + onActivate that opens chart-canvas. v6.2 dropped dataCatalogPlugin → macro registers ITS OWN left-tab panel:

```tsx
// apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/panels.ts
import type { PanelConfig } from "@boring/workspace"
import { DataExplorer } from "@boring/workspace"
import { macroAdapter } from "../adapters/macroAdapter"
import { useSurfaceRef } from "@boring/workspace"  // hook providing surface ref

export const macroSeriesPanel: PanelConfig = {
  id: "macro-series",
  title: "Series",
  placement: "left-tab",
  source: "app",
  component: () => {
    const surface = useSurfaceRef()
    return (
      <DataExplorer
        adapter={macroAdapter}
        groupBy="frequency"
        facets={[/* the existing freq + source facets from App.tsx:103-118 */]}
        onActivate={(row) => surface.openPanel({
          id: `chart:${row.id}`,
          component: "chart-canvas",
          title: row.id,
          params: { seriesId: row.id },
        })}
        getDragPayload={(row) => ({ mimeType: "text/series-id", value: row.id })}
        emptyState="No series match"
      />
    )
  },
}
```

The `useSurfaceRef()` hook is provided by @boring/workspace — wraps the existing onSurfaceReady ref machinery so panels can imperatively openPanel without prop-drilling. (If this hook doesn't exist yet, B17 may need a small workspace-side addition.)

## chartCanvasPanel + deckPanel

Existing definitions (in macro today). Wrap in PanelConfig if they aren't already; both are `placement: 'center'` since they're opened on file/series activation.

## seriesCatalog (catalogs.ts)

```ts
import type { CatalogConfig } from "@boring/workspace"
import { macroAdapter } from "../adapters/macroAdapter"

export const seriesCatalog: CatalogConfig = {
  id: "macro-series",
  label: "Macro Series",
  adapter: macroAdapter,
  onSelect: (row) => { /* same chart-open behavior */ },
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/index.ts exports makeMacroClientPlugin + macroChatSuggestions
- [ ] apps/boring-macro-v2/src/plugin/server.ts exports makeMacroServerPlugin
- [ ] index.ts has NO server imports (verify: grep -E "from \\\"\\.\\./server" plugin/index.ts must be empty)
- [ ] server.ts imports macroAgentTools from ../server/macroTools (existing module)
- [ ] Both plugins use id 'boring-macro' (same id, different contributions)
- [ ] panels.ts defines chartCanvasPanel, deckPanel, macroSeriesPanel (PanelConfig shape)
- [ ] catalogs.ts defines seriesCatalog (CatalogConfig shape)
- [ ] macroChatSuggestions matches the existing list verbatim (no behavior change)

## Tests

- Unit: makeMacroClientPlugin() returns a Plugin; panels include macro-series, chart-canvas, deck; catalog includes macro-series
- Unit: makeMacroServerPlugin() returns a Plugin; agentTools include execute_sql, macro_search, get_series_data, persist_derived_series
- Unit: import { makeMacroClientPlugin } from plugin → no node:* in resulting module graph (vite test for client-cleanness)

## Dependencies

- B4 (Plugin contract + definePlugin)
- B11 (createWorkspaceAgentApp ready to consume server plugins)

## Considerations

- Same id 'boring-macro' on both — bootstrap dedupes per the duplicate-id check, BUT only within ONE registry context. Client and server tracks are separate processes; both register a 'boring-macro' plugin. That's fine.
- macroSeriesPanel REPLACES the old data: DataPaneConfig wiring. The onActivate behavior (open chart-canvas) is preserved verbatim.
- chatSuggestions exported as a const; consumed as a prop on ChatCenteredShell in B17 (NOT registered as a plugin contribution).
- DataExplorer is the existing workspace primitive — macroSeriesPanel wraps it with macro's adapter; minimal new code.

═══ Polish round 7 (v7.7) — P1: macro app path is src/front/, not src/web/ ═══

```
apps/boring-macro-v2/src/
├── front/          ← exists; this is where the React app lives

```

There is NO `src/web/` directory. Round-1 example code referenced `src/web/App.tsx` — that's stale (predates the v6.x rename). All references must read `src/front/App.tsx`.

### Path corrections (replace everywhere in this bead)

- "App entries: src/web/App.tsx → ..." → "App entries: src/front/App.tsx → ..."

title: [Plugin] Step 6-ii: macro app refactor (App.tsx + server/index.ts) + delete uiBridge.ts
description:
Refactor apps/boring-macro-v2/src/web/App.tsx and src/server/index.ts to use the plugin model. Delete src/server/uiBridge.ts (now in @boring/workspace core). Quantitative target: ~110 LOC of glue + 150 LOC inlined uiBridge → ~30 LOC plugin/* and ~10 LOC server/index.ts.

## src/web/App.tsx (BEFORE: ~80 LOC; AFTER: ~7 LOC)

```tsx
// AFTER
import { WorkspaceProvider, ChatCenteredShell } from "@boring/workspace"
import { makeMacroClientPlugin, macroChatSuggestions } from "../plugin"

export const App = () => (
  <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[makeMacroClientPlugin()]}>
    <ChatCenteredShell chatSuggestions={macroChatSuggestions} />
  </WorkspaceProvider>
)
```

DELETE from App.tsx:
- dataPaneConfig: DataPaneConfig (replaced by macroSeriesPanel registered via plugin)
- macroPanels: PanelConfig[] (panels now in plugin)
- chatSuggestions inline declaration (now imported from ../plugin)
- The whole `data={dataPaneConfig}` + `extraPanels={...}` props on ChatCenteredShell

## src/server/index.ts (BEFORE: ~30 LOC; AFTER: ~11 LOC)

```ts
// AFTER
import { createWorkspaceAgentApp } from "@boring/workspace/server"
import { makeMacroServerPlugin } from "../plugin/server"
import { registerMacroRoutes } from "./macroRoutes"
import { createClickHouseClient } from "../infra/clickhouse"

const clickhouse = await createClickHouseClient(env)
const app = await createWorkspaceAgentApp({
  workspaceRoot,
  plugins: [makeMacroServerPlugin()],
})
await app.register(registerMacroRoutes, { clickhouse, deckRoot })
await app.listen({ port })
```

DELETE from server/index.ts:
- import { uiTools, uiRoutes } from "./uiBridge" (deleted file)
- The `extraTools: [...macroAgentTools, ...uiTools]` line (workspace handles this)
- The separate `app.register(uiRoutes)` call (substrate now)
- Use createWorkspaceAgentApp (NOT createAgentApp) — workspace wraps agent

## DELETE: src/server/uiBridge.ts (~150 LOC)

The 9.3 KB inlined copy of @boring/workspace's UI bridge core. Now redundant — workspace's server export ships it. Just delete the file. References in server/index.ts are gone after this bead's other changes.

## LOC accounting (target)

| File | Before | After | Δ |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| src/web/App.tsx | 80 | 7 | -73 |
| src/server/index.ts | 30 | 11 | -19 |
| src/server/uiBridge.ts | 150 | 0 | -150 |
| src/plugin/index.ts (client) | 0 | 18 | +18 |
| src/plugin/server.ts | 0 | 10 | +10 |
| **Total** | **260** | **46** | **-214 (-82%)** |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] src/web/App.tsx is ~7 LOC: WorkspaceProvider with plugin + ChatCenteredShell with chatSuggestions
- [ ] src/web/App.tsx contains NO references to dataPaneConfig, DataPaneConfig, extraPanels, withCommandPalette
- [ ] src/server/index.ts uses createWorkspaceAgentApp (not createAgentApp)
- [ ] src/server/index.ts has ONE app.register call (for macro routes); UI bridge routes are substrate
- [ ] src/server/uiBridge.ts is DELETED (verify: ls src/server/uiBridge.ts → no such file)
- [ ] No imports from "./uiBridge" remain anywhere in apps/boring-macro-v2/
- [ ] App boots and renders ChatCenteredShell with the workbench, Files left-tab, and Macro Series left-tab visible

## Tests

- Smoke: `pnpm dev` from apps/boring-macro-v2/ → app responds on its port; /api/v1/files/search returns; /api/v1/macro/* returns
- Smoke: open localhost in browser; ChatCenteredShell visible; workbench shows Files + Macro Series tabs; cmd palette opens
- Unit: src/web/App.tsx has < 15 LOC (sanity check the reduction)

## Dependencies

- B1 (UI bridge moved to @boring/workspace)
- B11 (createWorkspaceAgentApp ready)
- B14 (ChatCenteredShell drops data + extraPanels props)
- B16 (macro plugin module exists)

## Considerations

- The clickhouse client is constructed by the host BEFORE makeMacroServerPlugin is called — server-side runtime deps live with the host, not the plugin. This is the factory pattern in action.
- `app.register(registerMacroRoutes, { clickhouse, deckRoot })` is the ONE LINE of route boilerplate per plugin (codex round-3 verdict: defensible). Phase 1 only macro has plugin routes.
- `await createWorkspaceAgentApp(...)` returns the Fastify app; everything after is standard Fastify.
- Don't accidentally drop chat-suggestions visual behavior — verify the existing chat-suggestions e2e spec still passes after this change.

title: [Plugin] Step 1b: File ops bundle wiring (pi factories + custom additions)
description:
Wire `@boring/agent`'s file-ops tool catalog through pi-tools-migration's bundle factories: `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle)` and `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)`. v7.0 simplification: these are HARNESS substrate, not plugin contributions. No dual-registration with filesystemPlugin.

## Context: this is downstream of pi-tools-migration

`packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md` SHIPS FIRST and produces:
  - `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle)` → `[bash, executeIsolatedCode]`
  - `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)` → `[read, write, edit, find, grep, ls]` (pi factories + Operations adapters)
  - `vercelGrepTool` (custom AgentTool for vercel-sandbox mode)
  - DELETION of all 6 hand-rolled tool files

This bead does NOT extract from hand-rolled tools — those are deleted by pi-tools-migration. This bead just ensures the bundle factories exist and are wired into createAgentApp per the migration plan.

## Tool name and schema changes

- find_files → find (pi convention)
- grep_files → grep (pi convention)
- New: ls (pi default; useful affordance)
- Schemas: pi's are richer supersets

## v7.0 separation: tools are harness-level

The earlier 'shared bundle, dual registration' arrangement is gone. v7.0:
  - Substrate file tools always-on via createAgentApp (or opt-out with disableDefaultFileTools: true)
  - filesystemPlugin has NO agentTools field — it's UI-only (panels + catalog)
  - createWorkspaceAgentApp wraps createAgentApp without disableDefaultFileTools: true. No coordination dance.

## Custom non-pi additions allowed

buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle) returns pi factory tools PLUS any project-specific filesystem tools we add (watch_files, stat, git-aware ops, multi_edit, …). These are substrate alongside pi's defaults; they live in @boring/agent/server/tools/filesystem/. Plugin authors don't see them — they flow through the harness automatically.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle): AgentTool[] exported from @boring/agent/server/tools/harness
- [ ] buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle): AgentTool[] exported from @boring/agent/server/tools/filesystem
- [ ] createAgentApp registers both bundles by default
- [ ] disableDefaultFileTools: true opts out of the filesystem bundle (harness bundle remains)
- [ ] find_files/grep_files references gone (renamed to find/grep)
- [ ] ls tool added (pi default)
- [ ] HTTP routes (/api/v1/files, /tree, /files/search) moved to @boring/workspace/server (substrate path stays)
- [ ] No standalone-agent tool registration goes through any plugin path
- [ ] Bundle factories accept and pass through optional custom AgentTool additions

## Tests

- Unit: buildFilesystemAgentTools(stubBundle) returns 6 pi tools + any registered customs; find (not find_files) appears
- Unit: disableDefaultFileTools: true removes filesystem bundle; harness bundle remains
- Integration: createAgentApp default config exposes read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash/executeIsolatedCode

## Dependencies

- pi-tools-migration Phase 0 + Phase 1 complete (existing boring-ui-v2-uhwx.* bead chain)

## Considerations

- This bead is small if pi-tools-migration is already done. Mostly verification + naming + ensuring bundle factories exist as exported symbols at the expected paths.
- Custom non-pi additions are NOT added in this bead — that's per-need future work. The bead just ensures the extension point exists.
- v6.x's createFilesystemAgentTools(deps) name is gone; pi-tools-migration prescribes buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle).
- The standalone agent CLI keeps file tools by default — that's the point of v7.0's 'harness owns tools.'
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: file ops bundle wiring complete per pi-tools-migration. Verified: packages/agent/src/server/tools/filesystem/index.ts (3.4 KB) exports buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle); packages/agent/src/server/tools/harness/index.ts (4.9 KB) exports buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle); standardCatalog uses both via createAgentApp.ts:48,93; disableDefaultFileTools flag wired.

title: [Plugin] Step 6-iii: macro e2e suite (10 specs) ACCEPTANCE GATE
description:
Run all 10 existing boring-macro-v2 e2e specs against the post-migration app. THIS IS THE PHASE 1 ACCEPTANCE GATE — if any spec fails, Phase 1 is incomplete; if all pass, the plugin model is shippable.

## The 10 specs (verified live)

Located at /home/ubuntu/projects/boring-macro-v2/e2e/:

1. composer-border.spec.ts — empty composer border state
2. deck.spec.ts — deck/*.md opens DeckPane (path-aware resolver test)
3. catalog-to-chart.spec.ts — click series row → chart panel opens
4. catalog.spec.ts — workbench data tab shows series
5. split-no-clip.spec.ts — surface split layout doesn't clip
6. layout-persistence.spec.ts — workbench layout persists across reloads
7. chat-suggestions.spec.ts — empty-state suggestions render and submit
8. chart-tabs.spec.ts — chart panel tab interactions
9. topbar.spec.ts — top bar elements
10. agent.spec.ts — chat → LLM → tool calls round-trip

## Why these are the gate

The specs are BEHAVIOR-LEVEL — they don't reference deleted props (`data`, `extraPanels`, `withCommandPalette`) by name. Verified codebase scan: only `apps/boring-macro-v2/src/server/index.ts` and `src/web/App.tsx` reference these props. The specs interact with the rendered UI; if migration preserves behavior, specs pass.

If any spec FAILS post-migration, the regression is real and must be fixed before Phase 1 ships.

## Pre-flight check

Before running, verify:
- macro app builds cleanly (pnpm build from apps/boring-macro-v2/)
- TypeScript: pnpm typecheck — must be zero errors
- Lint: pnpm lint — must pass

## Run

```bash
cd /home/ubuntu/projects/boring-macro-v2
pnpm exec playwright test
```

Or per-spec:
```bash
pnpm exec playwright test e2e/catalog-to-chart.spec.ts --reporter=line
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] composer-border.spec.ts passes
- [ ] deck.spec.ts passes — confirms path-aware resolver (deck/*.md → DeckPane, not generic MarkdownEditor)
- [ ] catalog-to-chart.spec.ts passes — confirms macroSeriesPanel onActivate → chart-canvas open
- [ ] catalog.spec.ts passes — confirms workbench data tab shows series via macroSeriesPanel left-tab
- [ ] split-no-clip.spec.ts passes
- [ ] layout-persistence.spec.ts passes — confirms dockview layout still persists across reload
- [ ] chat-suggestions.spec.ts passes — confirms chatSuggestions prop still works
- [ ] chart-tabs.spec.ts passes
- [ ] topbar.spec.ts passes
- [ ] agent.spec.ts passes — confirms LLM tool round-trip (read/write/edit/find_files via filesystemPlugin)

## If any spec fails

1. Run that spec with --headed --debug to inspect.
2. Diagnose: is it a real regression (behavior changed) or a stale selector (test artifact)? Real regression → fix the implementation.
3. DO NOT modify the spec to make it pass unless the user-visible behavior change is explicitly approved (it shouldn't be — Phase 1 promises behavior preservation).

## Dependencies

- ALL preceding beads (B1 through B17) — this is the final integration test
- Specifically blocks on: B17 (macro app refactor); transitively blocks on the entire Phase 1 chain

## Considerations

- These specs are part of macro's existing test suite; they were passing before the migration. Failures point at the migration, not the specs.
- Run the full suite, not a subset. Some interactions are subtle (e.g., topbar layout depends on workbench presence).
- If any test infrastructure changed in macro (playwright config, CI), reconcile before running.
- Run twice to detect flakes — true regressions reproduce.
- This bead's success is the GO/NO-GO signal for closing the epic (j9p7).

title: [Plugin] Step 7: Release notes for 3 breaking changes
description:
Author release notes for the three breaking changes in @boring/workspace introduced by Phase 1. External hosts (today: only boring-macro-v2; tomorrow possibly others) need a clear migration guide.

## The three breaking changes

### 1. CommandPaletteProps — removed fileSearchFn / onOpenFile (B13a)

Old:
```tsx
<CommandPalette
  open={open}
  onOpenChange={setOpen}
  fileSearchFn={(q) => myFileSearch(q)}
  onOpenFile={(path) => mySurface.openFile(path)}
/>
```

New: file search comes from CatalogRegistry. WorkspaceProvider mounts CommandPalette without these props; hosts that custom-mount CommandPalette must contribute a catalog.

Migration: drop the props. If the host has a custom file search function, register it as a CatalogConfig.adapter on a Plugin. If the host had an onOpenFile, move that logic to catalog.onSelect.

### 2. ChatCenteredShellProps — removed data / extraPanels / withCommandPalette (B14)

Old:
```tsx
<ChatCenteredShell
  data={dataPaneConfig}
  extraPanels={["chart-canvas", "deck"]}
  withCommandPalette={true}    // also dropped
  chatSuggestions={...}
/>
```

New:
```tsx
<ChatCenteredShell chatSuggestions={...} />
```

Migration:
- `data: DataPaneConfig` → register a left-tab Panel via a Plugin (e.g., custom panel like macro's macroSeriesPanel). For simple cases, use `makeStaticDataPlugin({adapter})` — the convenience factory exported from @boring/workspace.
- `extraPanels: string[]` → use `allowedPanels?: string[]` (semantically narrower — gates which registered panels appear in this shell). Or drop entirely if unrestricted is fine.
- `withCommandPalette: boolean` → CommandPalette is mounted by WorkspaceProvider; gate at provider level if needed.
- `chatSuggestions: ChatSuggestion[]` → KEPT verbatim. No migration.

### 3. WorkbenchLeftPane — internal tab API changed (B15)

WorkbenchLeftPane no longer has hardcoded Files / Data tabs. Tabs are read from PanelRegistry (placement: 'left-tab'). Hosts that imported WorkbenchLeftPane directly (rare; usually mounted inside ChatCenteredShell) need to contribute their tab content via plugins.

Migration: register left-tab panels via plugins. excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] now actually removes the Files tab.

## File for release notes

Where the project keeps release notes (TBD by maintainer): possibly `packages/workspace/CHANGELOG.md` or `docs/releases/0.x.md` — coordinate with the project's release convention.

Format suggestion:

```md
## @boring/workspace 0.x.0 (Plugin Model Phase 1)

### Breaking changes

#### `<CommandPalette>`: removed `fileSearchFn` and `onOpenFile` props
...

#### `<ChatCenteredShell>`: removed `data`, `extraPanels`, `withCommandPalette` props
...

#### `<WorkbenchLeftPane>`: tabs are now driven by `PanelRegistry`
...

### New
- `Plugin` contract + `definePlugin()` factory
- Registry-driven workbench tabs (placement: 'left-tab' panels)
- `<WorkspaceProvider plugins={...}>` and `createWorkspaceAgentApp({plugins})`
- `excludeDefaults` for opt-out
- `makeStaticDataPlugin` convenience factory
- Polymorphic Recent (catalogs + commands)
- Path-aware file-pattern resolver
- `@boring/workspace/events` package subpath

### Migration

For each of the three breaking changes above, see the migration block. The boring-macro-v2 migration is the canonical worked example — see apps/boring-macro-v2/src/{plugin/, web/App.tsx, server/index.ts} in commit <hash> for the diff.
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Release notes file created (per project convention) with the three breaking changes documented
- [ ] Each breaking change has BEFORE / AFTER code blocks
- [ ] Each breaking change has a migration recipe
- [ ] New features section enumerates: Plugin contract, registry-driven tabs, plugins prop, excludeDefaults, makeStaticDataPlugin, polymorphic Recent, path-aware resolver, events subpath export
- [ ] Migration section points at the boring-macro-v2 commit as the canonical example

## Tests

N/A — documentation-only bead. Lint: spell-check + markdown-lint pass.

## Dependencies

- B13a (CommandPalette breaking change details)
- B14 (ChatCenteredShell breaking change details)
- B15 (WorkbenchLeftPane breaking change details)
- B17 (macro migration commit available as canonical example)

## Considerations

- Don't ship release notes BEFORE B17 lands — the migration example needs to exist for the link to be valid.
- Coordinate with maintainer on the version bump (probably 0.x → 0.x+1, since these are breaking — semver pre-1.0 allows minor bumps for breaking).
- If beads-based code review surfaces additional breaking changes during implementation, append them here.

title: [Plugin] Phase 1.5-B: Wire core panel registrations in WorkspaceProvider
description:
Phase 1.5-B from PLUGIN_MODEL v7.4. Make WorkspaceProvider register the 4 core workspace panels (chat, session-list, workbench-left, artifact-surface) BEFORE running the plugin bootstrap.

## Why

After Phase A (j9p7.24), each core panel has a definition.ts exporting a PanelConfig. Phase B aggregates them and registers them as substrate.

This is what 'chat is core, not a plugin' means concretely: the chat panel ships in the registry whether or not any plugin is registered. ChatLayout / IdeLayout / Tier 2 / Tier 3 hosts can all reference 'chat' / 'session-list' / 'workbench-left' / 'artifact-surface' by id and the panels resolve.

## Implementation

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/registry/coreRegistrations.ts (NEW)
import { chatPanel } from "../panes/chat/definition"
import { sessionListPanel } from "../panes/session-list/definition"
import { workbenchLeftPanel } from "../panes/workbench-left/definition"
import { artifactSurfacePanel } from "../panes/artifact-surface/definition"

export const coreWorkspacePanels: PanelConfig[] = [
  chatPanel,
  sessionListPanel,
  workbenchLeftPanel,
  artifactSurfacePanel,
]
```

```tsx
// packages/workspace/src/WorkspaceProvider.tsx (UPDATE)
import { coreWorkspacePanels } from './registry/coreRegistrations'
import { bootstrap } from './plugin/bootstrap'
import { filesystemPlugin } from './plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin' // when j9p7.9 lands

function WorkspaceProvider({ plugins, excludeDefaults, children }) {
  const panelRegistry = new PanelRegistry()
  const commandRegistry = new CommandRegistry()
  const catalogRegistry = new CatalogRegistry()
  
  // Substrate: always register the 4 core panels
  for (const panel of coreWorkspacePanels) {
    panelRegistry.register(panel.id, panel)
  }
  
  // Default plugins + user plugins
  const defaults = [/* filesystemPlugin */] // when j9p7.9 lands
  bootstrap({
    plugins: plugins ?? [],
    defaults,
    excludeDefaults,
    registries: { panels: panelRegistry, commands: commandRegistry, catalogs: catalogRegistry },
  })
  
  // ...rest of provider
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] registry/coreRegistrations.ts exists; exports coreWorkspacePanels: PanelConfig[]
- [ ] WorkspaceProvider registers core panels BEFORE bootstrap()
- [ ] Test: render <WorkspaceProvider> with no plugins; assert PanelRegistry contains 'chat', 'session-list', 'workbench-left', 'artifact-surface'
- [ ] Test: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[filesystemPlugin]}> registers core panels + 'files' from plugin
- [ ] Test: ordering — core panels register BEFORE plugin panels (verify by checking registration timestamps or registration-order array)

## Tests

- New test in WorkspaceProvider.test.tsx asserting the 4 core panel ids are registered post-mount
- Test that core panels keep their 'builtin' source (so app-source plugins win on tie-break)
- Smoke test: render WorkspaceProvider + check useActivePanels() returns at least the 4 core ids

## Dependencies

- j9p7.24 (Phase A — per-pane folders with definition.ts files)
- j9p7.7 (bootstrap function — DONE; needs v7.2 systemPromptAppend delta)
- j9p7.9 (filesystemPlugin) — needed for the 'defaults' array if filesystem ships at the same time

## Considerations

- coreWorkspacePanels is NOT a plugin — it's substrate registered directly. Don't accidentally make it a Plugin object (no 'id', no 'commands', no 'agentTools' field for the bundle).
- The 4 core panels go into the SAME PanelRegistry as plugin-contributed panels. They differ only in source ('builtin' vs 'app') for tie-break purposes.
- chat is core but the chat IMPLEMENTATION lives in @boring/agent — the workspace's chatPanel is a thin wrapper. Don't conflate.
- Phase B does NOT introduce filesystemPlugin into the defaults array if j9p7.9 hasn't shipped; coordinate with j9p7.9 author.

title: [Plugin] Step 2a: Extract validateTool from pluginLoader.ts to @boring/agent/shared (node-clean)
description:
Extract the validateTool function from packages/agent/src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/pluginLoader.ts into packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts so workspace's client bundle can import it without dragging node:* modules into the browser. P0 fix flagged by codex round-2.

## Why this is a P0

pluginLoader.ts (verified line 1-4) imports node:fs/promises, node:path, node:os, node:url at module scope. The plugin model's validateAgentTool re-uses validateTool — but if it imports from pluginLoader.ts, the entire node-only module gets pulled into any client bundle that calls definePlugin (which validates contributions at definition time). That breaks the workspace's client bundle.

## Solution

Move just the validateTool function into a new node-clean module:

```ts
// packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts (NEW)
import type { AgentTool } from "./tool"
export function validateTool(tool: unknown): AgentTool | null {
  if (typeof tool !== 'object' || tool === null) return null
  const t = tool as Record<string, unknown>
  if (typeof t.name !== 'string' || t.name.length === 0) return null
  if (typeof t.description !== 'string') return null
  if (typeof t.parameters !== 'object' || t.parameters === null) return null
  if (typeof t.execute !== 'function') return null
  return t as unknown as AgentTool
}
```

Then update pluginLoader.ts to import the extracted version (function body deleted from pluginLoader; just a re-export or direct usage):

```ts
import { validateTool } from "../../../shared/validateTool"
```

@boring/workspace's plugin/validators.ts then re-exports validateTool as validateAgentTool from @boring/agent/shared (which is browser-safe).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts exists, exports validateTool(tool: unknown): AgentTool | null
- [ ] packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts has NO node:* imports (verify with grep -n "node:" — must be empty)
- [ ] pluginLoader.ts imports validateTool from ../../../shared/validateTool — does NOT define it inline
- [ ] @boring/agent/shared barrel exports validateTool
- [ ] All existing pluginLoader tests still pass

## Tests

- Unit: import validateTool from a Vite-bundled context (simulated browser) without dragging node:* — bundle resolves cleanly
- Unit: existing validateTool semantics unchanged (well-formed AgentTool returns the tool; missing field returns null) — port the existing test cases
- Integration: pluginLoaderSmoke test still passes against the new import location

## Why this lands BEFORE Step 2b

B4 (Plugin type + definePlugin + validators) needs to import validateAgentTool. If B3 hasn't landed first, the import either (a) leaks node:* into client OR (b) duplicates the validator implementation. Both unacceptable. Hence B3 is a hard prerequisite for B4.

This is a one-file refactor; should land in 30-60 minutes of focused work.
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: implementation verified. validateTool now lives in @boring/agent/shared/validateTool.ts; pluginLoader.ts and definePlugin.ts both import from there. Node-clean per requirements. See packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts:1.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.5): ship. Commit 6f044bb

title: [Plugin] Step 0 (v7.6): Workspace reorg into front/+server/+shared/+plugins/
description:
v7.6 — gemini r2 P0 + codex r5 P1 fix. The mechanical workspace reorg that lets ALL subsequent phases assume the v7.6 layout. NO behavior change. NO new functionality. ONE-go file moves to FINAL destinations.

## Why

PLUGIN_MODEL v7.5 had Phase 1.5 Phase A doing both the workspace reorg AND the chat-shell decomposition. Both reviewers (gemini P0; codex P1) flagged this as wasted work — Phase 1 would build the new plugin machinery into the OLD flat src/ layout, then Phase A would rip up and re-arrange. Step 0 makes the reorg a one-time event before anything else.

## Meta-rule (locked v7.6)

When files move, they move DIRECTLY to their final v7.6 destinations. No intermediate placements. This is the ONE place where existing src/ files reorg; from now on, every subsequent phase assumes the v7.6 layout exists.

## Mechanical moves (one git mv per pair)

Source → Destination:

```
# Workspace's own front
src/components/{ui, DataExplorer, CommandPalette, SessionList,
                PluginErrorBoundary?}     → src/front/components/
src/registry/                              → src/front/registry/
src/dock/                                  → src/front/dock/
src/events/                                → src/front/events/
src/hooks/                                 → src/front/hooks/
src/layouts/                               → src/front/layout/
src/WorkspaceProvider.tsx                  → src/front/WorkspaceProvider.tsx

# Plugin model machinery split
src/plugin/{types, definePlugin, bootstrap}.ts → src/shared/plugin/
src/plugin/{CatalogRegistry, useCatalogs, useCommands,
            useActivePanels, index.ts}     → src/front/plugin/

# Existing panes/ — most go INTO plugins later (Step 3 + Phase E)
# Only ones that move in Step 0:
#   (none yet — code-editor/markdown-editor/file-tree go to
#    plugins/filesystemPlugin/ in Step 3 directly)

# Server (already at src/server/; minor renames if needed)
src/server/                                stays
src/shared/                                stays (gains plugin/ subdir)

# Audit + decide:
src/store/, src/testing/, src/types/       → consolidate into shared/ if minimal;
                                             delete if dead
src/panes/data-catalog/                    → audit (orphaned post-v6.2);
                                             delete OR move to front/components/
```

## tsconfig changes (codex P1)

- tsconfig.front.json: add excludes for `src/plugins/**/server/**`
  (any future plugin's server half) — prevents it from being typechecked as front
- tsconfig.shared.json (if exists): exclude DOM lib so shared/ types stay platform-neutral
- Verify: `pnpm typecheck` from packages/workspace passes after the reorg

## Tests

- ALL existing tests pass after vi.mock paths follow moved files (mechanical update)
- No NEW tests required — pure refactor
- Run full vitest suite + e2e smoke to confirm zero regressions
- Spot-check imports throughout the monorepo (`grep -r "@boring/workspace/components/" packages apps`) and update consumers if any exposed paths shifted

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] src/ has front/, server/, shared/, plugins/ at top level
- [ ] All formerly-flat directories (components/, registry/, dock/, events/, hooks/, layouts/) live under front/
- [ ] src/plugin/{types, definePlugin, bootstrap}.ts moved to src/shared/plugin/
- [ ] src/plugin/{CatalogRegistry, hooks} moved to src/front/plugin/
- [ ] tsconfig.front.json excludes src/plugins/**/server/**
- [ ] tsconfig.shared.json (if exists) excludes DOM lib
- [ ] vitest all green; typecheck clean
- [ ] No NEW exports added; package barrel still exports same public API (just from new internal paths)

## Dependencies

- None. This is the FIRST bead in the chain after Step 1's already-closed reorgs (UI bridge / file ops bundle).
- All other beads (especially j9p7.4/.5/.6/.7 deltas, j9p7.8, j9p7.9, j9p7.10, j9p7.11) DEPEND ON this completing.

## Considerations

- This is the ONLY destructive structural pass; downstream phases won't move files again. The "do it one go" principle is locked.
- Tests update mechanically: vi.mock("../../components/CommandPalette") → vi.mock("../../front/components/CommandPalette"). One pass with sed/jscodeshift, not per-file.
- `src/index.ts` (package barrel) keeps the SAME public exports but points at new internal paths. External consumers see no change.
- Files ALREADY in src/server/ stay where they are — they're already in the right place.
- Files ALREADY in src/shared/ stay; gain plugin/ subdir from the plugin model parts.
- src/panes/data-catalog/ audit: probably DELETE since dataCatalogPlugin was cut in v6.2 and nothing else uses it. Verify no consumer first.
- ETA: 1-2 days of focused work.
notes:
═══ Polish round 7 (v7.7) — P1: replace broad sed; clarify deletions ═══

Round-6 (codex) flagged TWO issues: the round-5 sed cascade violates AGENTS.md "no broad rewrite scripts" (Safety section), and the round-1/round-5 deletion decisions for `src/store/`, `src/types/`, `src/panes/data-catalog/` were ambiguous. Both fixed.

### Replace broad sed with explicit, audited Edit operations

The `find ... -exec sed -i ...` cascade in round-5 is REJECTED. Per AGENTS.md: "No broad rewrite scripts (codemods, 'fix everything') without approval." The agent doing j9p7.30 MUST use targeted per-file Edit operations, not sed.

Workflow:
1. After file moves complete, run `pnpm test` to discover broken vi.mock paths.
2. For each failing test file, READ the file, IDENTIFY the specific vi.mock string, and use the Edit tool to replace ONLY that string with the new path.
3. Re-run that test alone before moving to the next.
4. Repeat until all tests pass.

Acceptable shortcut: a CURATED list of `grep -l 'old/path/' file_pattern | head -5` to identify candidates, then per-file Edit. NOT `find ... -exec sed`.

The pre/post grep counts (`/tmp/vi-mocks-pre.txt` vs `/tmp/vi-mocks-post.txt`) ARE still valid as a sanity check — counts must match — but the rewrites between them are explicit Edits, not sed.

### Concrete deletion vs move decisions (round 6 ambiguity resolved)

Each item below has a LOCKED disposition. No agent discretion at implementation time:

| Path | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `src/panes/data-catalog/` | **DELETE** | Orphaned post-v6.2 (dataCatalogPlugin was cut). Verify zero consumers with `grep -r 'data-catalog' packages apps` BEFORE deletion. If any consumer hits, MOVE to `front/components/data-catalog/` and reopen this decision. |
| `src/store/validation.ts` | **MOVE** to `src/lib/validation.ts` | 70 lines, not storage-specific despite the folder name. `src/store/` then becomes empty → delete (Rule 1: written permission GIVEN here). |
| `src/types/boring-core-front.d.ts` | **MOVE** to `src/shared/types/boring-core-front.d.ts` | Type-only ambient module declarations belong in shared, NOT app-internal. |
| `src/testing/` | **STAY PUT** | Already a separate package.json entry point (`./testing`). Don't touch. |
| `src/lib/` | **STAY PUT** | Generic utilities; no front/server bias. |
| `src/data/` | **STAY PUT** | HTTP client; consumed only from front/. May relocate in a future bead, but NOT in Step 0. |
| `src/toast/` | **STAY PUT** | Same reasoning as src/lib/. |
| `src/theme/` | **STAY PUT** | Front-only stylesheet but tsup already handles it; moving costs more than it gains. |
| `src/panes/{ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx, EmptyPane.tsx}` | **STAY PUT** in this bead | They move to `front/chrome/{artifact-surface, EmptyPane}/` during j9p7.24 (Phase A). NOT this bead's job. |
| `src/panes/{code-editor, markdown-editor, file-tree}/` | **STAY PUT** in this bead | They move to `plugins/filesystemPlugin/front/` during j9p7.9. NOT this bead's job. |

### Per-file Edit operation list (post-move)

The vi.mock translations to apply per file are LOCKED to this exact set (no others):
- `"../../components/ui/...` → `"../../front/components/ui/...`
- `"../../components/DataExplorer/...` → `"../../front/components/DataExplorer/...`
- `"../../components/CommandPalette` → `"../../front/components/CommandPalette`
- `"../../components/SessionList` → `"../../front/components/SessionList`
- `"../../registry/...` → `"../../front/registry/...`
- `"../../dock/...` → `"../../front/dock/...`
- `"../../events/...` → `"../../front/events/...`
- `"../../hooks/...` → `"../../front/hooks/...`
- `"../../layouts/...` → `"../../front/layout/...`
- `"../../bridge/...` → `"../../front/bridge/...`
- `"../../WorkspaceProvider` → `"../../front/WorkspaceProvider`
- `"../../plugin/types"` → `"../../shared/plugin/types"` (TYPE module split)
- `"../../plugin/definePlugin"` → `"../../shared/plugin/definePlugin"`
- `"../../plugin/bootstrap"` → `"../../shared/plugin/bootstrap"`
- `"../../plugin/CatalogRegistry"` → `"../../front/plugin/CatalogRegistry"`
- `"../../plugin/useCatalogs"` → `"../../front/plugin/useCatalogs"`
- `"../../plugin/useCommands"` → `"../../front/plugin/useCommands"`
- `"../../plugin/useActivePanels"` → `"../../front/plugin/useActivePanels"`

For each pattern, the workflow is: `grep -l '<old-pattern>' packages/workspace -r --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx'` → for each match, READ + targeted Edit. Document the count of files touched per pattern in the bead's commit message.

### Acceptance criteria additions

- [ ] No `find ... -exec sed -i` (or equivalent) anywhere in the implementation diff
- [ ] Each file move + Edit is in git history as a discrete change OR as part of one mechanical commit with clear message
- [ ] Deletion decisions match the table above; nothing else is deleted without follow-up bead approval
- [ ] `src/panes/data-catalog/` deletion is preceded by a `grep -r data-catalog` zero-hit confirmation

title: [Plugin] v7.2 deltas: Plugin.systemPrompt field + bootstrap concatenation
description:
v7.2 spec addition that's small enough to delay but separate enough to track. Split out of j9p7.4 (Plugin contract) + j9p7.7 (bootstrap) status notes for explicit ownership.

## Why a separate bead

j9p7.4 and j9p7.7 are ALREADY CLOSED (the v7.0 Plugin contract + bootstrap shipped in code). v7.2 adds an OPTIONAL `Plugin.systemPrompt?: string` field + bootstrap concatenation logic. This is small (~30 LOC total) but doesn't belong in 'closed' beads. Tracking explicitly so an agent can mechanically pick it up.

## Scope

### Part A — Plugin contract (in shared/plugin/types.ts)

Add `systemPrompt?: string` field after `label?` and before `panels?`:
```ts
export interface Plugin {
  id: string
  label?: string
  /** Optional context prepended to the agent's system prompt at boot.
   *  Use to tell the LLM what your plugin's domain is and when its
   *  agent tools apply. Concatenated across all registered plugins
   *  (in registration order) and joined with double-newlines. Plain
   *  Markdown. ~200-500 chars per plugin recommended; longer eats
   *  context window. v7.2 addition. */
  systemPrompt?: string
  panels?: PanelConfig[]
  commands?: CommandConfig[]
  catalogs?: CatalogConfig[]
  agentTools?: AgentTool[]
}
```

### Part B — definePlugin validation

definePlugin should pass-through systemPrompt without validation beyond 'string-or-undefined'. Don't enforce length cap (recommendation in JSDoc only).

### Part C — bootstrap concatenation (in shared/plugin/bootstrap.ts)

bootstrap() result becomes:
```ts
export interface BootstrapResult {
  registered: string[]
  systemPromptAppend: string  // NEW — concat of all plugins' systemPrompt
}
```

Implementation:
```ts
const systemPromptAppend = finalSet
  .filter(p => p.systemPrompt && p.systemPrompt.trim())
  .map(p => p.systemPrompt!.trim())
  .join('\n\n')

return { registered: finalSet.map(p => p.id), systemPromptAppend }
```

### Part D — plumb through to harness

createWorkspaceAgentApp passes the bootstrap result's systemPromptAppend to the underlying createAgentApp via the existing `systemPromptAppend` option (verified at packages/agent/src/server/createAgentApp.ts:46-51). Single line:

```ts
const result = bootstrap({ plugins: opts.plugins ?? [], defaults: [filesystemPlugin], registries })
const app = await createAgentApp({
  ...opts,
  systemPromptAppend: result.systemPromptAppend,
})
```

### Part E — macro example sets systemPrompt

Per v7.2 spec example (in §Concrete before/after):
```ts
makeMacroServerPlugin = () => definePlugin({
  id: 'boring-macro',
  systemPrompt: [
    '## Macro database',
    'You have access to a ClickHouse database of FRED time series',
    '(GDP, CPI, employment, etc.; ~87k series). Use:',
    '- macro_search(query) to find series by name or topic',
    '- get_series_data(seriesId, range?) for time-series points',
    '- execute_sql(query) for analytical questions across series',
    'Always cite the FRED series id when reporting numbers.',
  ].join('\\n'),
  agentTools: macroAgentTools,
})
```

This is the canonical example. Boring-macro-v2's plugin/server.ts adds it during j9p7.18.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Plugin type includes `systemPrompt?: string` field with JSDoc
- [ ] definePlugin accepts and passes through systemPrompt; validation tolerant of undefined/empty
- [ ] bootstrap result includes `systemPromptAppend: string`
- [ ] Empty plugins → empty string; one plugin with systemPrompt → that string trimmed; two plugins → joined with double-newline
- [ ] createWorkspaceAgentApp plumbs systemPromptAppend into createAgentApp's option
- [ ] No regression in existing closed beads' tests

## Tests (per AGENTS.md: comprehensive unit + integration with detailed logging)

Unit (in shared/plugin/__tests__/bootstrap.test.ts):
- bootstrap with no plugins → systemPromptAppend === ''
- bootstrap with one plugin (systemPrompt: 'Hello') → systemPromptAppend === 'Hello'
- bootstrap with two plugins ('A', 'B') → 'A\\n\\nB' (registration order)
- bootstrap with one plugin (systemPrompt: undefined) → ''
- bootstrap with one plugin (systemPrompt: '   ') → '' (trim filter)
- bootstrap with mix of present/absent → only present joined

Integration (createWorkspaceAgentApp):
- App with macro plugin → /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/system-prompt response includes the macro systemPrompt prose
- App with no plugins → no plugin systemPrompt in response

## Dependencies

- j9p7.4 (Plugin contract — closed; this extends)
- j9p7.7 (bootstrap — closed; this extends)
- j9p7.30 (Step 0 reorg — for paths)

## Cross-review (per AGENTS.md §3)

Mandatory before close: ask Codex (if Claude Code agent) or Claude Code (if Codex agent) to review staged diff. Verdicts: ship/revise/reject. Cap 3 rounds.

## Considerations

- MUST NOT break existing bootstrap callers — adding a field to the result object is non-breaking; consumers that don't use systemPromptAppend ignore it.
- Don't enforce length cap programmatically; document recommendation in JSDoc only. Plugin authors are trusted.
- The macro example's systemPrompt content is plan-recommended but tunable — content lives in the macroPlugin definition, not in this bead's spec.
- Order matters: bootstrap concatenates in registration order (defaults first, then user plugins). Document this so plugin authors know what they're appending after.
notes:
═══ Polish round 7 (v7.7) — P1: ordering reaffirmed (cross-ref j9p7.11) ═══

Round-6 (codex) flagged that j9p7.11 and this bead disagreed on bootstrap-vs-createAgentApp ordering. Resolution: this bead's Part D has the correct sequence; j9p7.11 has been updated to match.

### Locked ordering (canonical — matches j9p7.11 round-7)

Inside `createWorkspaceAgentApp`:
1. Construct registries (with a staging list for agentTools — app doesn't exist yet)
2. **Run `bootstrap()`** — produces `systemPromptAppend` and fans plugin tools into the staging list
3. Call `createAgentApp({ systemPromptAppend, extraTools: [...uiTools, ...stagedAgentTools] })`
4. Register substrate routes on the returned app

Bootstrap CANNOT run after createAgentApp because its OUTPUT (systemPromptAppend) is one of createAgentApp's INPUTS. Same reason agentTools must be staged in step 1 then handed to step 3 — they need to be present at agent construction time, not bolted on after.

### BootstrapResult shape (this bead extends)

```ts
export interface BootstrapResult {
  registered: string[]
  systemPromptAppend: string  // NEW — concat of plugins' systemPrompt
}
```

The `agentTools` field of registries is still pluggable (tests pass a fake; createWorkspaceAgentApp passes a staging-list adapter); bootstrap doesn't see the live AgentToolRegistry, just SOMETHING with `.register(tool, pluginId)`.

### Acceptance criteria additions (round 7)

- [ ] j9p7.11's example code matches this bead's Part D ordering verbatim
- [ ] No code path constructs createAgentApp before bootstrap returns
- [ ] The staging-list adapter pattern is documented in either bead's description (one canonical example, cross-referenced)
close_reason: Parts A-C complete: types.ts + bootstrap concat + 6 new tests. Part D deferred to j9p7.11 (createWorkspaceAgentApp doesn't call bootstrap yet).

title: [Plugin] Step 0 verification: cross-package build smoke + public API integrity
description:
v7.6 — explicit verification gate AFTER j9p7.30 (Step 0 workspace reorg) closes. Catches: (a) cross-package build breakage, (b) accidentally-dropped public API exports.

## Why

j9p7.30 is mechanical. Tests pass when vi.mock paths follow files. But cross-package linkages (apps importing from @boring/workspace) might break if:
- A previously-exported symbol is dropped from src/index.ts
- The new tsup config emits to different paths than apps expect
- Apps' tsconfig.json paths references break (rare but possible)

Without an explicit gate, an agent picks up Phase A (j9p7.24) on a workspace whose package.json compiled but whose external consumers fail. Surfaces late = expensive.

## Scope

### Part A — Cross-package build smoke

```bash
# After j9p7.30 closes:
pnpm -w build                           # build the WORLD
pnpm -w typecheck                       # all packages typecheck against new workspace
```

Must pass green for: @boring/core, @boring/agent, @boring/workspace, all apps (workspace-playground, full-app, boring-macro-v2).

### Part B — Public API integrity

Compare pre-Step-0 and post-Step-0 exports:

```bash
# Capture pre-state (run BEFORE j9p7.30 starts)
cd packages/workspace
pnpm pack --dry-run | grep "dist/" > /tmp/workspace-exports-pre.txt

# Capture post-state (after j9p7.30 closes)
pnpm pack --dry-run | grep "dist/" > /tmp/workspace-exports-post.txt

# Diff
diff /tmp/workspace-exports-{pre,post}.txt
```

Acceptable diff: paths within `dist/` may rename (front/server/shared subdirs). UNACCEPTABLE: a previously-exported symbol disappears.

Or programmatically:
```bash
# Compare TypeScript declaration exports
grep '^export' packages/workspace/dist/workspace.d.ts | sort > /tmp/exports-pre.txt
# (after j9p7.30):
grep '^export' packages/workspace/dist/workspace.d.ts | sort > /tmp/exports-post.txt
diff /tmp/exports-{pre,post}.txt
```

### Part C — App-runtime smoke

For each app:
```bash
# workspace-playground:
cd apps/workspace-playground && pnpm dev &
sleep 5
curl -s http://localhost:5173/ | grep -q 'workspace-playground'  # OR equivalent
kill %1

# full-app:
# (similar smoke — app boots, /api/v1/agent/sessions returns 200)

# boring-macro-v2:
# (similar)
```

If any app fails to boot, j9p7.30's reorg is incomplete — fix before this bead closes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm -w build` passes green across all packages and apps
- [ ] `pnpm -w typecheck` passes green
- [ ] Public API diff: zero symbols dropped from packages/workspace's exports
- [ ] All three apps boot via `pnpm dev` and respond to root URL
- [ ] No new lint warnings introduced

## Tests (per AGENTS.md: comprehensive)

This bead IS itself a test bead — its scope is verification, not new feature implementation. Output: a green CI run.

Add (one-time): a check script at `packages/workspace/scripts/verify-public-api.ts` that grep-compares exports between two refs (HEAD and HEAD~1) and fails if any drop. Can be called manually or hooked into pre-merge CI.

## Dependencies

- j9p7.30 (Step 0 reorg — must close first)
- Blocks: j9p7.8, .9, .10, .11, .24 (Phase A) all assume the reorg + verification gate passed before they start

## Cross-review (per AGENTS.md §3)

Mandatory: codex/claude review of the verification script + any spec for the verification process. Verdicts: ship/revise/reject.

## Considerations

- This is a SAFETY bead, not a feature bead. Closes when verification scripts exist + run green once. Doesn't itself implement features.
- The verify-public-api.ts script becomes reusable — useful BEFORE every workspace package release in the future.
- If j9p7.30 surfaces issues that can't be fixed without scope expansion, this bead reopens j9p7.30 instead of closing.
- Don't add this gate retroactively — runs ONCE at j9p7.30 close, not per phase.
notes: Build gate GREEN after 8b2e88c: full-app vite aliases fixed. All 3 apps build. EmeraldSpring can now complete API integrity + runtime smoke (Parts B+C).
close_reason: Build gate green: all 3 apps build. Typecheck green (workspace + core). 312 public API exports verified, 0 dropped. Agent BoundFs issue pre-existing.

title: [Plugin] Step 2b: Plugin contract + definePlugin + validators
description:
Define the v6.3 Plugin TypeScript contract — six fields, all data — plus the definePlugin() factory that validates at definition time. Lays the foundation that all subsequent plugin work builds on.

## The contract (locked in v6.3)

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/shared/plugin.ts
import type { ComponentType } from "react"
import type { AgentTool } from "@boring/agent/shared"
import type { ExplorerAdapter, ExplorerRow } from "../components/DataExplorer/types"
import type { PanelConfig } from "../registry/types"

export interface Plugin {
  /** Stable id. Used for late-wins-on-id, debug provenance. */
  id: string
  /** Human-readable label, defaults to id. */
  label?: string
  panels?: PanelConfig[]
  commands?: CommandConfig[]
  catalogs?: CatalogConfig[]
  agentTools?: AgentTool[]    // server-side only
}

// PanelConfig: existing discriminated union in registry/types.ts.
// Plugin model only ADDS 'left-tab' | 'right-tab' to placement
// + pluginId?: string (auto-set by registry; do not assign manually).
// Existing fields (lazy, requiresCapabilities, essential, chromeless,
// source: 'builtin' | 'app') preserved verbatim.

// CommandConfig: already exists; add pluginId?: string field.

// CatalogConfig: NEW.
export interface CatalogConfig {
  id: string
  label: string
  adapter: ExplorerAdapter
  onSelect: (row: ExplorerRow) => void
  pluginId?: string
}
// NOTE: NO recentKind field (cut in v6.1 — was dead code after orphan-drop policy).
```

## The factory

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts
export function definePlugin(spec: Plugin): Plugin {
  validatePlugin(spec)   // throws PluginValidationError on bad input
  return Object.assign({}, spec)   // shallow clone, no Object.freeze (over-engineering)
}
```

## Validators (per v6.1 §"What definePlugin validates")

1. id: non-empty string
2. panels: each id unique within plugin; placement is one of the allowed enum values; if lazy:true, component is a thunk; if !lazy, component is a ComponentType
3. commands: each id unique within plugin; run is a function
4. catalogs: each id unique within plugin; adapter.search is a function; onSelect is a function
5. agentTools: delegate to validateAgentTool (re-export of @boring/agent/shared/validateTool)

Errors throw immediately with shape:
```
PluginValidationError: plugin "boring-macro": catalogs[0].adapter.search must be a function (got: undefined)
```

## Error model — ONE class with kind

```ts
export type PluginErrorKind =
  | 'validation'
  | 'duplicate-id'        // two plugins share Plugin.id (throws at registration)
  | 'mount'               // not used in Phase 1 (no onMount); reserved for Phase 2+
  | 'contribution'        // runtime per-contribution failure (catalog.search throws, etc.)

export class PluginError extends Error {
  constructor(public readonly kind: PluginErrorKind, message: string) { super(message) }
}
```

(v5/v5.2 had 5 classes — collapsed to one with a kind field per v6 simplification. Less surface, same diagnostic info.)

## Plugin id collision policy (v6.1)

- TWO plugins sharing Plugin.id → throw PluginError({kind: 'duplicate-id'}) at bootstrap registration. Not a composition pattern; an authoring bug.
- TWO contributions sharing a panel/command/catalog id ACROSS plugins → late-wins-on-id with dev-mode console.warn. THIS IS the composition pattern (host overrides default).

## Files

- packages/workspace/src/shared/plugin.ts — Plugin + CatalogConfig types
- packages/workspace/src/registry/types.ts — extend existing PanelConfig with 'left-tab'/'right-tab' + pluginId; CommandConfig adds pluginId
- packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts — factory + PluginError + validators

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Plugin / PanelConfig / CommandConfig / CatalogConfig all type-check
- [ ] definePlugin({id: 'foo'}) returns a Plugin
- [ ] definePlugin({id: ''}) throws PluginValidationError
- [ ] definePlugin with duplicate panel ids within the SAME plugin throws PluginValidationError
- [ ] definePlugin with malformed agentTool (no execute fn) throws PluginValidationError citing the bad index
- [ ] PanelConfig still supports 'left' | 'center' | 'right' | 'bottom' AND new 'left-tab' | 'right-tab'
- [ ] PanelConfig still supports lazy: true via the discriminated union (no regressions)
- [ ] @boring/workspace barrel re-exports definePlugin and Plugin

## Tests

- Unit: definePlugin happy path returns input
- Unit: each validator branch: bad id, bad panel, bad command, bad catalog, bad agentTool — all throw
- Unit: error message contains plugin id + contribution kind + bad field
- Unit: PluginError instanceof Error; .kind set correctly

## Dependencies

- B3 (validateTool extraction) — required because validateAgentTool re-exports validateTool from @boring/agent/shared. Without B3 first, this would import a node-leaky module into client.

## Considerations

- Validation runs synchronously at definePlugin call time — plugin module fails to import on bad input. This is the right time: dev sees errors at module load, not later at registration.
- Cross-PLUGIN id collisions (panel id from plugin A == panel id from plugin B) are NOT validated here; that's a runtime registration concern handled at bootstrap.
- Do NOT freeze the Plugin object (Object.freeze) — considered and rejected in v6.1 as over-engineering.
notes:

- packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts (factory + validation + PluginError)
- packages/workspace/src/plugin/__tests__/definePlugin.test.ts (tests green)
