title: [Plugin] Step 2d: Path-aware file-pattern resolver upgrade
description:
Replace the basename-only file-pattern matcher in PanelRegistry.resolve + SurfaceShell.openFile with a path-aware micromatch-based resolver, plus a concrete specificity-scoring formula. Required so domain-specific patterns like 'deck/**/*.md' actually beat generic '**/*.md'.

## Why current resolver fails

Verified at packages/workspace/src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts:91 — matchGlob() handles only `*suffix` and exact match. SurfaceShell.tsx:98 strips path to filename via filename.split('/').pop() before lookup. Patterns like 'deck/**/*.md' would be stored but never match because the lookup never sees the full path.

This blocks the canonical compositional example in PLUGIN_MODEL.md: opening 'deck/labor/labor.md' should pick macro's DeckPane (specific) over filesystem's MarkdownEditor (generic).

## Design

### Step 1 — accept full paths

SurfaceShell.openFile and resolvePanelForPath pass the FULL path to PanelRegistry.resolve(path). Don't pre-strip to basename.

### Step 2 — path-aware micromatch

Replace matchGlob with micromatch:
```ts
import micromatch from "micromatch"
function matches(path: string, pattern: string): boolean {
  return micromatch.isMatch(path, pattern, { matchBase: false, dot: true })
}
```

### Step 3 — concrete specificity formula

```ts
function specificity(pattern: string): number {
  const segmentCount = pattern.split('/').filter(Boolean).length
  // Count non-wildcard chars (everything that isn't *, ?, !)
  const nonWildcardChars = pattern.replace(/[*?!]/g, '').length
  return segmentCount * 10 + nonWildcardChars
}
```

Higher score wins. Examples:
  '**/*.md'             → segments=2, non-wildcard=3  ('.md') → 23
  'deck/**/*.md'        → segments=3, non-wildcard=8  ('deck/.md') → 38
  'deck/labor/*.md'     → segments=3, non-wildcard=12 ('deck/labor/.md') → 42

deck/**/*.md beats **/*.md. As intended.

### Step 4 — preserve source tie-breaker

Current PanelRegistry.resolve has 'app source beats builtin' on equal-specificity ties. PRESERVE this. Order:
  1. Path-aware micromatch filter
  2. Higher specificity wins
  3. source: 'app' beats source: 'builtin'
  4. Registration order, late wins

### Step 5 — late-wins via registration order

Tied on all of the above → return the latest-registered panel.

## Files affected

- packages/workspace/src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts:91 — replace matchGlob, add specificity scoring, ordered tiebreakers
- packages/workspace/src/components/chat/SurfaceShell.tsx:98 — pass full path to resolve, drop filename.split

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PanelRegistry.resolve(path) takes full path, returns best PanelConfig or undefined
- [ ] micromatch is added as a dep of @boring/workspace (check pnpm-lock)
- [ ] Specificity formula matches the documented one (segments × 10 + non-wildcard chars)
- [ ] source: 'app' beats source: 'builtin' on equal specificity (preserved behavior)
- [ ] Tie-on-everything → registration order, late wins (preserved)
- [ ] SurfaceShell.openFile passes full path (not basename) to resolve

## Tests (CRITICAL — make this bullet-proof since the resolver is foundational)

- Unit: matches('deck/labor/labor.md', 'deck/**/*.md') === true
- Unit: matches('notes.md', '**/*.md') === true
- Unit: specificity('deck/**/*.md') > specificity('**/*.md')
- Unit: register {id: 'markdown', patterns: ['**/*.md']} + {id: 'deck', patterns: ['deck/**/*.md']} → resolve('deck/labor/labor.md') returns 'deck'; resolve('notes.md') returns 'markdown'
- Unit: register {id: 'a', source: 'builtin', patterns: ['**/*.ts']} + {id: 'b', source: 'app', patterns: ['**/*.ts']} → resolve('foo.ts') returns 'b' (app wins on tie)
- Unit: register two with identical specificity + source → later registration wins
- Unit: PanelConfig with no filePatterns is never returned by resolve
- Integration: SurfaceShell.openFile('deck/labor/labor.md') opens DeckPane (full e2e through the workbench)
- Integration: opening a *.foo file with no matching panel returns undefined (paves the way for B12's EmptyFilePanel fallback)

## Dependencies

- None hard. (B6 retrofits PanelRegistry but the matcher upgrade is independent of subscribe semantics.) Can run in parallel with B5/B6/B7.

## Considerations

- micromatch is a substantial dependency (~50KB minified). Verify it's already used elsewhere or budget the add.
- The 'matchBase: false' option is what makes us PATH-aware. Don't accidentally enable matchBase or basename-only behavior creeps back.
- Don't lose the source tie-breaker — that's an existing user-meaningful behavior. App-supplied panels win over builtins.
- Consider exposing the resolve algorithm via a pure function for unit testing without instantiating PanelRegistry.
notes:
═══ Polish round 2 (v7.6) — read EPIC notes first ═══

v7.6 path corrections SPECIFIC to this bead:
- 'PanelRegistry.ts:91' → packages/workspace/src/front/registry/PanelRegistry.ts (post-Step-0)
- 'SurfaceShell.tsx:98' → DOOMED. After Phase A (j9p7.24), SurfaceShell moves to front/chrome/artifact-surface/. Path-resolver upgrade lives in front/registry/PanelRegistry.ts ONLY.
- micromatch dep added to packages/workspace/package.json (NOT a peer dep — workspace owns the resolver)

Test logging (per epic notes):
- console.info('[j9p7.8] specificity({segments}seg, {nonWildcard}chars) = {score}') in resolveBest()
- Test names cover compositional examples from §Plugin composability:
  - 'deck/labor/labor.md beats notes.md path-aware'
  - 'specificity formula: deck/**/*.md > **/*.md'
  - 'app source beats builtin on tie'
  - 'late-wins-on-id within identical specificity + source'

Architectural invariants:
- Inv #4 (path validation in adapter) — resolver does NOT validate paths; SurfaceShell.openFile is the path-bounding layer (already enforced)
- Inv #1 (no node:* in shared) — micromatch is browser-safe; verify by import-from-vite-test

Cross-review: codex/cc review of micromatch options ({matchBase: false, dot: true}) + specificity formula. Verdicts: ship/revise/reject.

Acceptance gate: opening 'deck/labor/labor.md' in macro post-migration → DeckPane (not generic markdown-editor). Tested as part of j9p7.20.
close_reason: micromatch resolver + specificity scoring + 8 new tests. All 970 tests pass.

title: [Plugin] Step 3: filesystemPlugin (UI-only — panels + catalog, no agentTools)
description:
Implement filesystemPlugin as a v7.0 UI-only Plugin: panels + catalog, NO agentTools. File tools belong to the harness (per pi-tools-migration); the plugin is purely the UI surface.

## v7.0 framing

Earlier drafts had filesystemPlugin via a makeFilesystemPlugin(deps) factory carrying agentTools from a shared bundle. v7.0 drops that entirely:
  - File ops tools (read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash/executeIsolatedCode) are HARNESS substrate registered by createAgentApp via pi-tools-migration's bundle factories. ALWAYS-on (opt out with disableDefaultFileTools: true).
  - filesystemPlugin contributes ONLY UI: FileTree (left-tab), CodeEditor + MarkdownEditor (center, with filePatterns), Files catalog (cmd palette).
  - No factory; plain module-scope const. No runtime bundle dependency.

## Implementation

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin.ts
import { definePlugin } from '../definePlugin'
import { FileTreePanel, CodeEditorPanel, MarkdownEditorPanel } from '../../panels'
import { filesCatalog } from './filesystemCatalog'

export const filesystemPlugin = definePlugin({
  id: 'filesystem',
  label: 'Filesystem',
  panels: [
    { id: 'files', title: 'Files', component: FileTreePanel, placement: 'left-tab', source: 'builtin' },
    { id: 'code-editor', title: 'Code', component: CodeEditorPanel, placement: 'center',
      filePatterns: ['**/*.ts','**/*.tsx','**/*.js','**/*.jsx','**/*.py','**/*.rs','**/*.go','**/*.json','**/*.yml','**/*.yaml'],
      source: 'builtin' },
    { id: 'markdown-editor', title: 'Markdown', component: MarkdownEditorPanel, placement: 'center',
      filePatterns: ['**/*.md', '**/*.mdx'], source: 'builtin' },
  ],
  catalogs: [filesCatalog],
  // NO agentTools — file tools live with the harness
})
```

## filesCatalog adapter

Wires /api/v1/files/search to a CatalogConfig.adapter (cmd palette search).

## Excludes ONLY UI

excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] removes:
- Files left-tab from WorkbenchLeftPane
- code-editor / markdown-editor panel registrations (file types fall through to EmptyFilePanel per B12)
- Files catalog from the cmd palette

It does NOT remove:
- LLM file tools (still in @boring/agent's harness substrate)
- /api/v1/files/search route (substrate)
- Any harness-level capability

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] filesystemPlugin exported as a plain const (NOT a factory)
- [ ] No runtime bundle dependency (no deps argument)
- [ ] Plugin shape: id='filesystem', panels (3), catalogs (1), agentTools=undefined
- [ ] Files panel uses 'builtin' source
- [ ] excludeDefaults removes UI; LLM file tools remain

## Tests

- Unit: filesystemPlugin shape (panel ids, catalog id, no agentTools)
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[]}> shows Files left-tab
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider excludeDefaults={['filesystem']}> hides Files left-tab
- Integration: opening 'foo.ts' opens code-editor when default mounted; opens EmptyFilePanel when excluded
- Integration (server): createWorkspaceAgentApp registers harness file tools; cmd palette catalog hits /api/v1/files/search

## Dependencies

- B4 (Plugin contract + definePlugin)
- B5 (CatalogRegistry — fan target)
- B6 (PanelRegistry retrofit — fan target)
- pi-tools-migration's bundle factories must exist for filesystem agent tools to flow through createAgentApp

## Considerations

- WAY simpler than v6.x's factory shape. Plugin is a static const; module-load is instant.
- Files catalog and Files panel both have id 'files' but in different registries — no collision.
- /api/v1/files/search route is SUBSTRATE (always-on), not part of the plugin.
- Component imports (FileTreePanel etc.) come from existing workspace primitives.
notes:
═══ Polish round 7 (v7.7) — P2: post-Step-0 path note ═══

Round-6 (codex bead-level review) flagged minor path drift. Round-1 example code references `packages/workspace/src/plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin.ts` and `src/panels/` — those paths are PRE-Step-0 (j9p7.30 hasn't run yet). After Step 0 / j9p7.30 closes, the canonical paths are:

- Plugin module: `packages/workspace/src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/index.ts` (NOT `src/plugin/defaults/`)
- Panes (file-tree, code-editor, markdown-editor): `packages/workspace/src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/front/panes/<id>/`
- Catalog: `packages/workspace/src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/front/catalogs/filesCatalog.ts`
- No server.ts (UI-only plugin, gemini r2 P1 confirmed)

The round-1 example lines like `import { definePlugin } from '../definePlugin'` translate to `from '@boring/workspace'` (or `'../../../shared/plugin/definePlugin'` for an internal import; prefer the public barrel).

The round-1 import `from '../../panels'` (FileTreePanel etc.) is OBSOLETE; the components LIVE INSIDE this plugin's own `front/panes/` directory after the move. Inline them or import from the local `front/panes/` neighbours, NOT a workspace-level `panels/` directory (which won't exist post-Step-0).

### Acceptance criteria addition

- [ ] Final implementation paths match `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/{index.ts, front/panes/, front/catalogs/}` exactly
- [ ] No imports from `src/plugin/defaults/` or `src/panels/` survive (those locations don't exist post-Step-0)

title: core-M2: routes — workspace invites (create with email send + raw token, list, revoke, accept)
description:
## Goal
Invite endpoints. createInvite gets the raw token from store + sends WorkspaceInvite email with the accept URL. acceptInvite throws 4 specific HttpError codes.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites`: requireWorkspaceMember(); list invites for workspace.
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); body `{ email, role }`; calls `WorkspaceStore.createInvite` → gets `{ invite, rawToken }`. Renders WorkspaceInvite email with `acceptUrl: ${BETTER_AUTH_URL}/api/v1/workspaces/${invite.workspaceId}/invites/${invite.id}/accept?invite_token=${rawToken}`. Sends via mail transport. Response: `{ invite }` (does NOT include rawToken — it's already in the email).
  - If mail not configured (no MAIL_FROM): returns `{ invite, warning: 'mail_disabled' }`.
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites/:inviteId/accept`: auth-only (NOT requireWorkspaceMember — accepter isn't a member yet); reads `?invite_token` query, validates against stored hash, calls `WorkspaceStore.acceptInvite`. On 4 error codes (HttpError thrown by acceptInvite) → 404/410/409/403 with code. Success: 200 `{ workspaceId, role }`.
- `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites/:inviteId`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); calls revokeInvite.

## Considerations
- The raw token NEVER persists outside the email body. Server logs MUST NOT include it (redaction list covers `token`).
- `expiresAt` defaults to 7 days from server time at creation.

## Tests
- Owner creates invite → email sent (mock transport asserts), invite row exists, raw token NOT in DB.
- Non-owner POST invites → 403.
- Accept with valid token → user joins workspace.
- Accept with expired token → 410 invite_expired.
- Accept twice → second call 409 invite_already_accepted.
- Accept with wrong-email user → 403 invite_email_mismatch.

## Refs
CORE.md §API reference §HTTP surface; §DB §createInvite, acceptInvite.
close_reason: done

title: M2 — Remote mode (Vercel Sandbox)
description:
**~1 week.** Prove the core architectural claim: swapping adapters flips from local to remote with zero changes elsewhere.

**Deliverables:**
- `createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox)` — 30 LOC adapter against `sandbox.fs.*` + `sandbox.writeFiles` + path validation.
- `createVercelSandboxExec(sandbox)` — 30 LOC adapter wrapping `sandbox.runCommand`.
- `SandboxHandleStore` interface + `FileHandleStore` default impl at `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Cloud package injects DB-backed impl. ~60 LOC.
- `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` — create-or-get-or-resume-from-snapshot + in-process cache, persists via SandboxHandleStore.
- `vercel-sandbox` `RuntimeModeAdapter` — workspace + sandbox share a single Sandbox handle.
- Snapshot-bake flow (src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/bake.ts): creates seed sandbox + pip install + snapshot + caches snapshotId. One-time per `python_packages` hash. ~150 LOC.
- Periodic snapshot cron (every 10 min or on-idle).
- Circuit breaker around Vercel SDK (exponential backoff, fast-fail). ~30 LOC.
- Snapshot retention policy (keep-last-2). ~15 LOC.
- Config: `BORING_AGENT_MODE`, `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_TEAM_ID`.

**Gate:** `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` — same chat UI, same tools, files now living in a Firecracker VM. No other code changes. Switching modes mid-development is an env-var flip.

## Success Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip — no code changes.
acceptance_criteria: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip — no code changes.
close_reason: M2 Remote Mode complete — all children shipped: jru.1 (find_files, f318c84), jru.2 (grep_files, b5898ff), jru.3 (tree/stat cache, 04db555). Plus n1o (mode-flip E2E be82e7d), 082 (tarball e640081), 4vl (pricing 790e5af), 96h (cold-start fcc7f65), f4f (fs latency baca373), xzr (leak monitor b5898ff).

title: M2: Add find_files tool over FileSearch (mitigate vercel fs latency)
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec find(100 files) p50 ~297ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x). Implement a first-class find_files tool that uses FileSearch abstraction instead of shelling out to find. Acceptance: chat can enumerate matching files without invoking bash find; docs note this as preferred path for vercel-sandbox mode.
close_reason: shipped in 1cb50cb; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added find_files tool over FileSearch + catalog wiring + tests + vercel-sandbox docs note

title: M2: Add grep_files batched tool to avoid per-file read loops
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec grep(50 files) p50 ~292ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x), and per-file read/stat ops are 180-1000x slower than local node fs. Implement grep_files tool that executes one batched search in runtime adapter (prefer ripgrep where available) and returns structured matches. Acceptance: tool can search 50+ files with one invocation and includes file/path/line metadata for UI rendering.
close_reason: shipped — grep_files batched tool: sandbox.exec() with rg||grep fallback, structured match output, 14 unit tests passing

title: M2: Add short-lived tree/stat cache for vercel-sandbox workspace
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-fs stat p50 ~299ms vs local node stat ~0.3ms (~996x), readFile/writeFile/mkdir also 200x+ slower. Add bounded in-process cache for readdir/stat results in vercel-sandbox workspace adapter with invalidation on write/rename/unlink/mkdir. Acceptance: repeated stat/readdir in same turn avoid redundant remote calls; cache invalidation correctness covered by unit tests.
close_reason: shipped in 04db555; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added short-lived stat/readdir cache invalidation hook for sandbox.exec + cache correctness tests

title: core-M2: safeRedirect util + integration with better-auth callback paths
description:
## Goal
`safeRedirect(url: string, config: CoreConfig): string` — strips CRLF/HTML chars, accepts relative paths, accepts absolute URLs only if origin is in `config.cors.origins`, falls back to `/`.

## Why it matters
Open-redirect + CRLF injection are real vectors for OAuth callback abuse. better-auth doesn't enforce; core wraps every redirect emission point.

## Acceptance criteria
- `safeRedirect(url, config): string` exported from `@boring/core/server`.
- Rejection rules:
  - Contains `[\0\r\n<>"'` + backtick `]` → fallback `/`.
  - Absolute URL with origin NOT in `config.cors.origins` → fallback `/`.
- Acceptance:
  - Relative paths starting with `/` (no scheme).
  - Absolute URLs with origin in allowlist.
- Wired into better-auth callback handlers (any redirect URL passed to `reply.redirect()` runs through this).
- Wired into invite-accept page redirect.

## Considerations
- Trailing-slash handling: `https://app.com` and `https://app.com/` both accepted if origin matches.
- Magic-link `next` query param explicitly handled.

## Tests
- Unit: 12+ cases covering CRLF chars, relative, absolute-allowed, absolute-disallowed, empty string, javascript: URL, data: URL.
- Integration: GitHub OAuth callback with `state` containing a malicious next URL → user redirected to `/`, not the malicious target.

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Security additions §Redirect URI sanitization.
close_reason: shipped in scaffold commit 96346a0 — safeRedirect util + 24 unit tests covering CRLF, open-redirect, scheme injection. Integration wiring deferred to M2 better-auth bead. Reviewed by codex.

title: core-M4: dependency-graph CI check (agent has zero runtime import of core)
description:
## Goal
CI step that verifies `@boring/agent` has zero RUNTIME imports of `@boring/core`. Type-only imports are allowed (erased by tsc); runtime imports are forbidden.

## Why it matters
The standalone-CLI promise relies on agent being core-free. A regression where someone imports a core symbol (even accidentally) would break `npx @boring/agent` for self-hosters.

## Why not `tsc --listFiles`

`tsc --listFiles` lists files visited by the TS compiler, including type-only files — false positives. We need a check on the EMITTED JS, where only runtime imports remain.

## Acceptance criteria
- Script `scripts/check-agent-isolation.ts`:
  1. Run `pnpm --dir packages/agent build` to produce `dist/`.
  2. Static-walk every emitted `.js` and `.mjs` file under `packages/agent/dist/` (use `es-module-lexer` or `acorn` to parse).
  3. Collect every `import` / `require` specifier — whether bare module spec or relative path that escapes via node resolution.
  4. Filter for any specifier matching `^@boring/core(/|$)`.
  5. Assert the filtered list is empty.
- Wired into the CI workflow as a separate step running AFTER the agent build job.
- Fails CI with a clear error message naming the offending source file (resolved from the dist sourcemap).

## Considerations
- Type-only imports MUST be stripped by tsup/tsc — verify build config has `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` or equivalent so `import type` is genuinely erased.
- `package.json`'s `"sideEffects"` field in agent should NOT include any core-imported file.
- Dynamic imports (`await import('@boring/core/...')`) are also caught by the parser walk.

## Tests
- **Self-test fail**: temporarily add `import { createCoreApp } from '@boring/core/server'` to a non-test file in agent → run isolation check → assert exits non-zero with the file path in the error.
- **Self-test pass**: remove the import → re-run → exits zero.
- **Type-only allowed**: add `import type { WorkspaceStore } from '@boring/core/shared'` to agent's plugin → run check → exits zero (type-only erased from JS output).
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-ka07'`.

## Refs
CORE.md §Dependency position; §Milestones §M4; M4_REGISTER_AGENT bead (cross-package type sharing).
close_reason: scripts/check-agent-isolation.ts walks all .js/.mjs in agent dist, extracts import specifiers via regex, asserts zero @boring/core matches. Wired into invariants.yml post-build step. Self-tested with injected import — caught at file:line. Commit dddedc4.

title: M1: buildBwrapArgs (port — security-critical)
description:
**Goal:** Port the bwrap argument builder. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/buildBwrapArgs.ts`.

**Source:** Old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/adapters/bwrap.ts` lines ~22–58.

**SECURITY REVIEW REQUIRED:** This bead MUST be reviewed by a second agent or human before merge. It defines the sandbox boundary — subtle flag errors create sandbox escapes.

**Behavior:** Given `{workspaceRoot}`, returns `string[]` of `bwrap` flags creating a sandbox where:
- `/workspace` → bind-mounted from `workspaceRoot` (writable)
- Read-only binds: `/usr`, `/lib`, `/lib64`, `/bin`, `/etc/ssl`, `/etc/ca-certificates`, etc. (see `RO_BIND_DIRS`)
- `--unshare-all --share-net` (proc/user/ipc/uts isolated; keep network)
- `--die-with-parent` — so if our Node process dies, sandbox terminates.
- `--chdir /workspace`
- `--setenv HOME /workspace`
- `--proc /proc --dev /dev --tmpfs /tmp`

**Why flag order matters:** bwrap applies binds in order. Read-only binds must come BEFORE `--chdir`. Port verbatim where possible.

**Constants:**
- `BWRAP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30`
- `KILL_GRACE_SECONDS = 5`
- `RO_BIND_DIRS` — list of host paths to bind read-only.

## Security invariants
- `--unshare-all` MUST always be present (no exception, no config to disable)
- `--die-with-parent` MUST always be present
- workspaceRoot bind is writable; ALL other binds are read-only
- No user-controlled string appears unescaped in the args array
- workspaceRoot is validated before reaching this function (realpath resolved, no symlink chasing outside allowed root)

## Unit tests (vitest)

### Snapshot + structure
1. Snapshot test: args array for known workspaceRoot matches expected output
2. `--unshare-all` present in every generated args array
3. `--share-net` present (network allowed)
4. `--die-with-parent` present
5. `--bind workspaceRoot /workspace` present exactly once
6. All RO_BIND_DIRS have `--ro-bind` entries
7. RO binds appear before `--chdir` in the array (ordering invariant)
8. `--chdir /workspace` present
9. `--setenv HOME /workspace` present

### Adversarial inputs (security-critical)
10. workspaceRoot with spaces: `/home/user/my project` — args correctly quoted/separated
11. workspaceRoot with shell metacharacters: `/tmp/$(whoami)` — treated as literal path, no shell expansion
12. workspaceRoot with newlines: `/tmp/foo\nbar` — rejected or escaped
13. workspaceRoot with null bytes: `/tmp/foo\x00bar` — rejected
14. workspaceRoot as symlink: should be pre-validated by caller (test that buildBwrapArgs does NOT resolve symlinks — that is tx7 responsibility)
15. workspaceRoot that does not exist: function still builds args (existence check is caller responsibility), but assert no crash
16. Empty workspaceRoot string: throws or returns error (not silent empty bind)
17. Very long workspaceRoot (>4096 chars): rejected

### Negative tests
18. Removing `--unshare-all` from RO_BIND_DIRS does NOT affect its presence (it is hardcoded, not derived)
19. No `--bind-try` in output (we want hard failures, not silent skips)

## Acceptance criteria
- Snapshot test for args array
- Required flags present (`--unshare-all`, `--die-with-parent`, `--share-net`)
- `--bind` for workspaceRoot present exactly once
- RO binds precede `--chdir`
- All 19 adversarial + structural tests pass
- Security review completed before merge
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot test for args array; required flags present; --bind for workspaceRoot present exactly once; RO binds precede --chdir.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; commit a98c004; targeted test: pnpm exec vitest run src/server/sandbox/bwrap/__tests__/buildBwrapArgs.test.ts

title: core-M1: schema — workspaces table + soft-delete + default-per-user index
description:
## Goal
Define the `workspaces` table matching v1 column-for-column, with the partial unique index that enforces one default workspace per (user, app).

## Why it matters
Workspaces are the central tenancy unit. The default-per-user partial unique index was a v1 footgun (concurrent creates can hit unique violation); we preserve it for v2 with a documented retry-on-violation in createWorkspace.

## Acceptance criteria
- `workspaces` table per CORE.md §DB Schema overview "Core-owned":
  - `id` uuid primary key
  - `appId` text NOT NULL
  - `name` text NOT NULL (1-100 chars validated at app layer, not DB)
  - `createdBy` text references `users.id`
  - `createdAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
  - `deletedAt` timestamp NULL (soft-delete marker)
  - `isDefault` boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false
  - `machineId` / `volumeId` / `flyRegion` text NULL (legacy v1 fields preserved for agent provisioning)
- Partial unique index `idx_workspaces_default_per_user_app` ON `(createdBy, appId) WHERE isDefault = true` (matches v1 schema.ts:67).
- B-tree index on `(createdBy)` for `WorkspaceStore.list` lookups.

## Considerations
- v1 has a known race on default-workspace creation under unique violation — documented as carried-forward known issue (CORE.md §Traps from v1 → Default-workspace promotion). Don't try to fix in this bead; just preserve the index.
- `createdBy` FK has NO cascade — see core-M1-schema-workspace-members bead for the user-deletion cascade policy. Workspaces created by a deleted user are NOT auto-deleted.

## Tests
- Integration: insert a default workspace; insert a second default for same (user, app); assert unique violation.
- Insert a default for (userA, appX) AND (userA, appY); assert both succeed (cross-app isolation).

## Refs
CORE.md §DB Schema; v1 schema.ts:42-78; §Traps from v1 → Accepted as known issues.
close_reason: already landed in commit b722851

title: Gov: Single source of truth for env + config schema
description:
**Goal:** One canonical Zod schema for all env vars + config keys. File: `src/shared/config-schema.ts`.

**Contents:**
```ts
export const ConfigSchema = z.object({
  // Runtime
  BORING_AGENT_MODE: z.enum(['direct','local','vercel-sandbox']).optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID: z.string().default('default'),
  BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_AUTH_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),

  // Providers
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: z.string(),

  // Vercel mode
  VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),
  VERCEL_TEAM_ID: z.string().optional(),

  // TOML-sourced (runtime/sandbox sections)
  runtime: z.object({ mode: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  workspace: z.object({ template_path: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  sandbox: z.object({
    python_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    system_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    vercel: z.object({
      runtime: z.enum(['node24','node22','python3.13']).optional(),
      snapshot_id: z.string().optional(),
    }).optional(),
  }).optional(),
})
```

**Precedence rules:**
1. CLI flag
2. Env var
3. `boring.app.toml` / `.boring-agent/config.toml`
4. Default

**Documented in:** `docs/CONFIG.md` with full table.

**Acceptance:** all env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`; invalid configs fail fast with clear errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

All env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`. Invalid configs fail fast. `docs/CONFIG.md` has full table.

title: fix(agent): ChatPanel should render via ai-elements primitives (Message/Tool/CodeBlock), not plain divs
description:
**Problem:** `ChatPanel.tsx` currently renders messages with bare `
`s: ```tsx
{message.role}
{text ?
{text}
: null} {toolParts.map(...)}
``` The ai-elements primitives ``, ``, ``, ``, `` exist in `src/front/primitives/` (copied via `7hv` M3b) but are not imported or used. Result: chat looks unstyled — spec section §4 Tool rendering + §UI export pattern expect these primitives to be used by the default ``. **Fix scope:** - Import `Message`, `Reasoning`, `Tool`, `Terminal`, `CodeBlock` from `./primitives/`. - Wrap each assistant + user message in ``. - Wrap text deltas properly (streaming handled by useChat). - Replace the plain tool-part `
` wrapper with `` or the renderer registry (already exists — `resolveToolRenderer`). - Wrap reasoning deltas with `` primitive when present. - Ensure theme.css vars drive visible styling (bg, fg, accent, bubble colors). **Verification (manual + programmatic):** - `pnpm --filter with-custom-tool dev` → open browser → send a message → visible message bubbles with dark theme, assistant differentiated from user, tool calls rendered by the Tool primitive, tool output boxed. - Existing ChatPanel tests still pass. **Reference files:** - `packages/agent/src/front/primitives/*.tsx` (ai-elements copies) - `packages/agent/src/front/toolRenderers/` (already merges by tool name) - Old boring-ui reference: `packages/agent/src/front/components/chat/AiChat.jsx` (structure only; v2 uses useChat directly) ## Acceptance Criteria ChatPanel renders user + assistant bubbles via ``; tool calls via `` + specialized renderers where applicable; reasoning via ``. Default dark theme from theme.css is visibly applied. Existing tests + types green. Manual browser test shows a distinctly styled chat (not plain-div wall of text).

title: M1: createNodeWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** Node fs-backed `Workspace` adapter. File: `src/server/workspace/createNodeWorkspace.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createNodeWorkspace(root: string): Workspace {
  return {
    root,
    async readFile(rel) { const p = validatePath(root, rel); await assertRealPath...(root,p); return fs.readFile(p,'utf8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... ensureWritable ... fs.writeFile(p, data) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... fs.rm(p, { recursive: false }) },   // file OR empty dir
    async readdir(rel) { ... fs.readdir(p, { withFileTypes: true }) → Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... fs.stat(p) → Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { validate both; fs.rename(abs(from), abs(to)) },
  }
}
```

**Uses:** path helpers on every call (no exceptions).

**Shared by:** `direct` and `local` modes (both use same host fs).

**Tests:**
- Happy path for all 7 ops.
- Path traversal rejections on every op.
- `rename` within root (move file, rename file, rename dir).
- `unlink` on empty dir succeeds; on non-empty dir rejects.
- `readdir` returns `{name, kind}` — no extra fields.

**Done when:** Passes shared `Workspace` conformance suite (in infra epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.

title: core-M2: mail transport layer (resend default, smtp via nodemailer, console fallback)
description:
## Goal
`createMailTransport(url): MailTransport` dispatches by URL scheme. Three implementations: resend (default REST), smtp (nodemailer), console (dev-only).

## Why it matters
Email delivery underpins verification, reset, magic-link, and invites. Resend is the recommended default; SMTP is the escape hatch for self-hosters; console is for dev/test.

## Acceptance criteria
- `MailTransport` interface: `{ send(email: RenderedEmail): Promise<{ id: string }> }`.
- `RenderedEmail = { to: string; subject: string; html: string; text: string }`.
- `createMailTransport(url: string, env: 'production' | 'development' | 'test'): MailTransport`:
  - **`resend://<api-key>` (default recommended)** — direct `fetch POST https://api.resend.com/emails` with Bearer auth. NO npm dep on `resend`.
  - **`smtp://`, `smtps://`** — nodemailer SMTP transport.
  - **`console://`** — logs at INFO level. **Logging policy pinned by env:**
    - `env === 'development'`: log full body `{to, subject, html, text}` (so devs can read template output).
    - `env === 'test'`: log full body AND write to `/tmp/test-mail-<pid>.log` for assertion harnesses.
    - `env === 'production'`: REFUSED — `console://` rejected at boot via ConfigValidationError ("console mail transport is not allowed in production").
  - **`console-capture://` (test-only)** — same as console:// in test mode but writes JSON-line to a configurable path; M7_E2E uses this. Refused outside test env.
  - Unknown scheme → throws `ConfigValidationError` at boot.
- Each impl handles errors: 4xx / 5xx from Resend → throws `MailDeliveryError`; SMTP timeouts → throws same.
- Retry logic: 3 retries with exponential backoff (500ms, 1s, 2s) for transient failures (5xx, network errors). 4xx errors do NOT retry (e.g. invalid recipient).

## Considerations
- For Resend: `from: config.auth.mail.from` is required. Resend rejects sends without a verified domain.
- The test-mode mailbox observer in `createTestApp` (M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS) is the canonical way to capture emails in tests.
- DO NOT log email body in production logs even via redaction — entire body NEVER goes to pino in production mode.

## Tests
- Each transport dispatches correctly given its URL scheme.
- Mock Resend API → `transport.send` returns the message id.
- Bad scheme → throws.
- Retry on 503 → succeeds on second attempt.
- 4xx → no retry, throws immediately.
- `console://` in production env → throws ConfigValidationError at boot.
- `console-capture://` in non-test env → throws.
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-ma63'`.

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Email sending §Transport.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.3): 4 issues fixed, re-verified, ship

title: Phase 4: Playwright E2E Tests
description:
## Goal
Golden-path E2E tests + bridge protocol E2E test. ~2 min CI.

## What to implement

### Golden paths (Playwright, workspace-playground app)

#### File workflow
1. Click file in FileTree → editor tab opens with correct content
2. Edit file in CodeMirror → dirty indicator (dot) appears on tab
3. Cmd+S → file saved (dirty indicator disappears)
4. Close tab → reopen same file → content matches saved version

#### Layout persistence
5. Drag sidebar wider → reload page → sidebar width restored
6. Open 3 tabs → close middle tab → reload → 2 tabs restored in correct order
7. Collapse sidebar → reload → sidebar still collapsed

#### Panel lifecycle
8. Open file → panel registers in tab bar
9. Close panel via X button → tab removed, panel unmounted
10. Open same file again → panel re-created (not cached)

#### Chat layout
11. Switch chat session → messages update
12. Open artifact from chat → artifact panel opens adjacent

#### Markdown workflow
13. Open .md file → tiptap editor renders rich content
14. Toggle bold → text formatted → save → reload → formatting preserved

### Bridge protocol E2E (~50 LOC, critical)
Proves the full command-based bridge flow end-to-end:

```
Test flow:
1. Playwright opens workspace-playground at localhost
2. Separate fetch() client simulates agent:
   - POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "openFile", params: { path: "/src/main.ts" } }
3. Playwright asserts: new editor tab appears in workspace within 2s
4. Agent client: POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "showNotification", params: { msg: "Hello", level: "info" } }
5. Playwright asserts: toast notification appears
6. Playwright: user closes file tab
7. Agent client: GET /api/v1/ui/state → verify openPanels no longer includes main.ts
8. Playwright: SSE event stream test — open EventSource, verify events arrive with v:1 protocol version
```

This test validates:
- Command POST → SSE delivery → workspace execution
- Workspace state → PUT to server → agent reads via GET
- Protocol version field present
- End-to-end latency <2s

### CI integration
- Run on every PR (~2 min target)
- Playwright config: 1 worker (sequential, avoids port conflicts)
- Full suite (chromium + firefox + webkit) on merge to main only
- Retry: 1 retry per test (flake protection)

## Implementation files
- tests/e2e/golden-path.spec.ts — scenarios 1-14
- tests/e2e/bridge-protocol.spec.ts — scenario 15 (bridge E2E)
- tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts — workspace-playground server setup

## Success criteria
- All 15 E2E scenarios pass
- Bridge E2E proves command flow works end-to-end
- No flaky tests (0 flakes over 10 consecutive runs)
- <2 min total runtime on CI
- Test output includes detailed step logging for debugging failures

title: core-M1: integration tests for accepted-bug races (TOCTOU, last-owner, default-promotion)
description:
## Goal
Write integration tests that DOCUMENT the three accepted v1 race conditions as failing (or `.fails()`-marked) tests, with clear comments referencing the v1.1 fix bead. Use a deterministic concurrency harness so failures reproduce reliably in CI.

## Why it matters
Silent known-issues rot. Explicit failing-tests-as-documentation make the bug visible to anyone running the suite. When v1.1 fixes the races, the implementer flips `.fails()` off and the tests pass. Without a deterministic harness, races are flaky in CI — sometimes triggering, sometimes not — which is worse than no test.

## Acceptance criteria
- Three test files (one per race), each marked `test.fails()` (vitest's "expected to fail").
- **Deterministic concurrency harness** `__tests__/_concurrency.ts`:
  - Provides `runConcurrent<T>(fns: Array<() => Promise<T>>, opts: { barrier: number }): Promise<T[]>`.
  - Uses `Promise.allSettled` + an explicit barrier (all functions wait for the barrier to release before proceeding to the racy operation).
  - Optional `instrument` hook that wraps Drizzle's `transaction()` to inject sleeps at chosen points so the race window is FORCED open in tests (the test harness reaches into Drizzle internals only in test mode).
- **Files**:
  - `inviteAcceptToctou.test.ts` — 2 concurrent acceptInvite calls on same invite; both succeed (the race we DON'T fix in v1).
  - `lastOwnerRemoveRace.test.ts` — 2 concurrent removeMember on two owners; both can succeed, leaving 0 owners.
  - `defaultWorkspacePromotion.test.ts` — soft-delete the default workspace; assert no auto-promotion happens.
- Each test has a comment block linking to the bead ID for the v1.1 fix and to the §Traps from v1 section in CORE.md.
- Comment includes mitigation note for ops (e.g. "if a workspace ends up with 0 owners, run admin SQL ...").

## Considerations
- `test.fails()` makes the suite pass when the race triggers. If the race accidentally doesn't trigger (e.g. faster CI), the test fails with "expected race did not occur" — instrument hook prevents this.
- When v1.1 fixes the races, this bead's tests are flipped to `expect`-style (no `.fails`).

## Tests
- Self-referential: the suite passes when these tests "fail in the documented way."
- Run the suite 100× in a CI loop; assert race triggers each time (no flakiness).
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-mo58'`. Each race test logs:
  - `race.test.start { race, beadId, v1.1FixBead: '...' }`
  - `race.barrier.released { participants: 2 }`
  - `race.test.outcome { race, observed: 'both_succeeded' | 'one_failed' | 'unexpected', mitigation: '...' }`
- Failure logs explicitly state the operator-recovery SQL.

## Refs
CORE.md §Traps from v1 → Accepted as known issues; M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS bead.
close_reason: shipped in f6dec1c: race-safe invite/member/account-delete semantics + Postgres integration tests

title: v1 Migration Context + Implementation Reference
description:
## Goal
Reference document for the implementing agent. Contains ALL v1→v2 migration context, files NOT to port, per-app compatibility, and shared knowledge the implementer needs. This bead is read-only context — no code to write, but every other bead should be understood in the context of this information.

## v1 → v2 Reference Map

### Phase 1: Foundation
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| DockviewShell.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx (1718 lines, focus lines 1–80, 400–500) | Dockview init: DockviewReact setup, onReady callback, component map registration, api.fromJSON() for restore. SIMPLIFY: v1 is 1718 lines — most is panel lifecycle that belongs in hooks. |
| PanelChrome.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/DockTab.jsx (143 lines) | Tab rendering with file icons, close button, active state. Replace all custom CSS with shadcn/tailwind. |
| dockview-reset.css | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx — .dockview-* overrides in imported CSS | Identify which dockview classes need zeroing. Also check node_modules/dockview-react/dist/styles/dockview.css for full default theme. |
| store/index.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/persistence/LayoutManager.js (618 lines) | Layout save/restore: dockApi.toJSON() serialization, localStorage read/write, validation. DROP: version migration system, lastKnownGoodLayout backup, multi-key storage. KEEP: toJSON/fromJSON round-trip, validation. |
| PanelRegistry.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/registry/panes.jsx (484 lines) | Registration: register(id, { component, title, icon, placement, constraints, requires }). Lazy loading via React.lazy(). Capability gating. DROP: requiresRouters, requiresFeatures (legacy). KEEP: requiresCapabilities. |
| IdeLayout.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx (full file) | v1 is 1718 lines because layout logic, hooks, and panel lifecycle are inlined. v2: IdeLayout is ~30 lines building a LayoutConfig. Study for group arrangement (sidebar left locked, center tabs, right rail) and constraints. |
| ChatLayout.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) | Same simplification. Study group arrangement + SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) for artifact surface dockview. |
| useDockLayout.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/useDockLayout.js (337 lines) | Sidebar discovery (findSidebarGroup()), collapse toggle, center group tracking, panel activation, group constraints. |
| usePanelActions.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/usePanelActions.js (16 KB) | Open/close/activate panel. File-to-panel routing. Editor tab management. HEAVY — extract only core open/close/activate, leave file-specific logic to panes. |

### Phase 2: Built-in Panes
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| FileTreePane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/FileTree.jsx (1129 lines) + panels/FileTreePanel.jsx (344 lines) | Tree rendering, expand/collapse, click-to-open, context menu. REWRITE with react-arborist + shadcn. |
| MarkdownEditor.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/Editor.jsx (912 lines) | PORT directly. Keep tiptap. Restyle with shadcn/tailwind. Port toolbar, task lists, code blocks. |
| CodeEditorPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/CodeEditor.jsx (176 lines, react-simple-code-editor + Prism) + panels/EditorPanel.jsx (417 lines) | File loading (useFileContent()), save (useFileWrite()), dirty state, external change detection. FULL REWRITE: react-simple-code-editor → CodeMirror 6. Port load/save/dirty pattern. |
| DataCatalogPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/panels/DataCatalogPanel.jsx (87 lines) | Simple list. Port with shadcn Card/Badge. |
| EmptyPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/panels/EmptyPanel.jsx (44 lines) | Placeholder. Trivial rewrite. |
| useFileData.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/providers/data/queries.js (10 KB) + providers/data/httpProvider.js (8.4 KB) | React Query hooks, HTTP client, auth header injection, 401 retry. DROP: useGitStatus, lightningFs, isomorphicGit, pyodide providers. |

### Phase 3: Agent Integration

|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| bridge/client.ts + bridge/commands.ts | packages/workspace/src/server/services/uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) + packages/workspace/src/front/utils/frontendState.js (2.6 KB) | Current UI state bridge: panel snapshots, command queuing. v2: bridge reads/writes single Zustand store. |
| bridge/client.ts | packages/workspace/src/server/http/uiStateRoutes.ts (289 lines) | Current HTTP endpoints. REPLACE with command-based SSE+POST bridge. Port command vocabulary. |

| Tool → artifact routing | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/utils/toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4 KB) + hooks/useToolBridge.js (5.3 KB) | How tool results become panels. SIMPLIFY: v2 agent calls bridge.openPanel() explicitly. Keep as optional convenience. |

## Files Explicitly NOT to Port

|---------|--------|

| panels/TerminalPanel.jsx (384 lines) | Same |
| panels/ShellTerminalPanel.jsx (298 lines) | Same |
| components/SyncStatusFooter.jsx (489 lines) | Git sync UI — agent responsibility |

| providers/data/lightningFsProvider.js | Browser FS — dropped |
| providers/data/isomorphicGitProvider.js | Browser git — dropped |
| providers/data/pyodideRunner.js | Browser Python — dropped |
| hooks/useGitHubConnection.js | Auth — agent/core responsibility |
| hooks/useFrontendStatePersist.js | Replaced by bridge in v2 |
| components/FrontmatterEditor.jsx | YAML editing — not in v2 scope |
| chrome/UserMenu.jsx | App chrome — app shell, not workspace |
| server/adapters/* (nodeFs, directBash, bwrap) | Moved to agent |
| server/http/fileRoutes.ts, execRoutes.ts, gitRoutes.ts | Moved to agent |
| server/jobs/execJob.ts | Moved to agent |
| server/workspace/paths.ts, helpers.ts | Moved to agent |
| All *.d.ts, *.d.ts.map, *.js build artifacts | v1 build artifacts, not source |

## Per-App v2 Compatibility
| App | Compat | Key gaps |
|-----|--------|----------|
| minimal | ~95% | FileTree needs prop-based variant (solved by Tier 3 standalone) |
| custom-layout | ~90% | Same — 3-column CSS grid + standalone components |
| chat | ~20% (by design) | 80% is agent code. Workspace provides layout shell + artifact surface. |
| agent-backend | ~15% (by design) | Layout toggle dropped (app implements). Capability fetching is app shell's job. |
| ide | ~5% (by design) | 95% is app-shell code. Auth, routing, cloud features not workspace's concern. |
| agent-frontend | DROPPED (permanent) | Offline mode permanently dropped. |

Low percentages are correct — they reflect auth, routing, cloud, and agent logic moving to proper owners.

## Custom Events Inventory (v1 → v2)

|----------|----------------|
| boring-ui:user-settings-open | App shell responsibility |
| boring-ui:sync-interval-changed | Dropped (agent owns sync) |
| boring-ui:agent-prompt | bridge.sendMessage() |
| boring-ui:shell-state | store.subscribe() |
| theme-toggle-request | useTheme() hook |
| bui:openFile | bridge.openFile() |
| PI session events (4) | Agent package responsibility |

## localStorage Key Inventory (Collision Avoidance)
| v1 key pattern | v2 status |
|----------------|-----------|
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:layout | → boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:tabs | Dropped (derived from layout) |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:lastKnownGoodLayout | Dropped |
| boring-ui-theme / kurt-web-theme | → boring-ui-v2:preferences |
| boring-ui:chat-sessions:v1 | Agent package responsibility |
| boring-ui:terminal-* | Dropped (terminal → agent) |

## CSS Breakpoints to Parameterize

|---------------|-----------|-------------|
| 56px | Sidebar collapse width | collapsedWidth in GroupConfig |
| 420px | Surface min-width | constraints.minWidth in GroupConfig |
| 1180px | Compact layout trigger | tailwind lg breakpoint |
| 960px | Mobile layout trigger | tailwind md breakpoint |

## IDE Boot Flow Boundary
| Step | v2 owner |
|------|----------|
| Capabilities fetch | App shell |
| Auth flow (OIDC, session) | App shell |
| Workspace routing | App shell |
| Data provider scope | Workspace (WorkspaceProvider) |
| Resolved capabilities | Workspace (registry filters) |
| Layout selection | App shell |
| Layout rendering | Workspace |

## @boring/core Dependency Contract
Every import workspace v2 makes from @boring/core:

### HTTP transport (@boring/core/front)
- apiFetchJson(url, opts), buildApiUrl(path), getHttpErrorDetail(error), routes constant

### UI utilities (@boring/core/front)
- cn() (tailwind class merger), copyTextToClipboard(text), formatShortcut(shortcut), useViewportBreakpoint()

### Keyboard shortcuts (@boring/core/front)
- useKeyboardShortcuts(shortcuts), DEFAULT_SHORTCUTS

### Accessibility (@boring/core/front)
- announceToScreenReader(message), trapFocus(containerEl), useReducedMotion()

### Theme (@boring/core/front)
- useTheme(), ThemeToggle

### Configuration (@boring/core/front)
- useConfig(), useCapabilities()

### Design tokens (@boring/core/front)
- ICON_SIZE_INLINE, ICON_SIZE_TOOLBAR, ICON_SIZE_ACTIVITY

### UI components (@boring/core/ui — 13 vendored)
- Button, Input, Badge, Separator, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Select, Dialog, Avatar, Switch, Label, Textarea
- v2 imports these, does NOT re-vendor

### Server (@boring/core/server)
- createAuthHook, ServerConfig type

### Shared (@boring/core/shared)
- CapabilitiesResponse type, Git types (GitStatus, GitChange, etc.)

## Deep Import Anti-Pattern (v1 → v2 fix)
v1 apps use 11+ deep imports bypassing the public API. v2's index.ts exports everything apps need — zero deep imports required. See Public API bead for full export list.
