title: M3b: /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes + tracker
description:
**Goal:** Per-session change summary endpoint + server-side tracker. File: `src/server/http/routes/sessionChanges.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Tracker:**
- Hook writeTool / editTool / bash-with-write to record `{path, op, size}` per session.
- Store in-memory keyed by `sessionId` (persistent in M3b.polish or later).

**Route:** `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` → `{files: [{path, op, size}, ...]}`.

**Why:** powers post-turn summary ("Claude modified 3 files") like Claude Code.

**Tests:** multiple writes + edits accumulate; deletes recorded; response shape stable.

## Acceptance Criteria

Multiple writes+edits accumulate. Deletes recorded. Response shape stable.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple writes+edits accumulate. Deletes recorded. Response shape stable.
close_reason: Implemented per-session file-ops tracker + endpoint: added in-memory SessionChangesTracker, wired chat route to record data-file-changed chunks by session, and added GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes returning { files: [...] }. Hooked app wiring so chat + changes route share tracker. Added bash tool file-change inference for successful write/edit/unlink/rename/mkdir patterns (including delete tracking) so bash-originated mutations flow into tracker. Added focused tests for route accumulation/session isolation/deletes, tracker parsing+bounds, and bash inference edge-cases. Validation: vitest (49 tests across chat/sessionChanges/tracker/bash/createAgentApp), package typecheck, invariants pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 494e498.

title: core-M1: schema — workspace_invites + tokenHash + expiresAt default + RESTRICT FK
description:
## Goal
Define `workspace_invites` with sha256 token hash storage (raw token never persisted), 7-day default expiry, and `createdBy → users.id ON DELETE RESTRICT`.

## Why it matters
Invites are the multi-user onboarding seam. Storing only the token hash limits blast radius if the DB is dumped. The 7-day default matches v1 UX.

## Acceptance criteria
- Table `workspace_invites`:
  - `id` uuid primary key
  - `workspaceId` text NOT NULL
  - `email` text NOT NULL (lowercased + trimmed by app layer before insert)
  - `tokenHash` text NOT NULL UNIQUE (sha256 of raw token; raw never stored)
  - `role` text NOT NULL CHECK IN ('owner', 'editor', 'viewer')
  - `expiresAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT (now() + interval '7 days')
  - `acceptedAt` timestamp NULL
  - `createdBy` text NULL references `users.id` ON DELETE RESTRICT
  - `createdAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
- Unique index on `tokenHash` (used by `getInviteByTokenHash` for post-signup lookup).
- B-tree index on `(workspaceId)` for `listInvites`.
- FK `workspaceId → workspaces.id` (no cascade — invites outlive soft-delete).

## Considerations
- `createdBy` is NULL for system-generated invites (e.g. during signup-via-invite flow if the inviter user was already deleted). RESTRICT only fires when the value is non-null and references a real user.
- The 7-day default is set at the DB layer so the app doesn't need to pass `expiresAt` on insert; can be overridden at app layer if a different TTL is needed.

## Tests
- Integration: insert invite without `expiresAt`; assert it's `now() + 7 days` (within 1 minute tolerance).
- Insert two invites with same token hash; assert unique violation.

## Refs
CORE.md §DB §Schema; §Workspace semantics & defaults.
notes: unclaiming, conflict
close_reason: workspace_invites schema shipped — tokenHash unique, RESTRICT FK, 7-day expiresAt default, 6 integration tests

title: Phase 4: Performance Budget Enforcement
description:
## Goal
CI enforcement of bundle budgets + performance benchmarks.

## Performance budgets (from plan Section 4.7)
| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Initial JS (shell + registry) | <150KB gz | vite build + gz analysis |
| Total all-loaded ceiling | <800KB gz | vite build + gz analysis |
| Shell render (empty layout) | <500ms TTI | Playwright performance.mark() |
| File tree (10K files) | <120ms first view | Vitest benchmark (virtualized) |
| First CodeMirror render | <500ms | Playwright performance.mark() |
| First tiptap render | <500ms | Playwright performance.mark() |
| Layout persistence round-trip | <50ms | Vitest benchmark |
| Bridge command latency | <100ms (SSE), <2s (poll) | Vitest benchmark |

## What to implement

### Bundle size analysis (CI)
- Post-build script: vite build → analyze chunks with rollup-plugin-visualizer or custom script
- Check two numbers: initial chunk <150KB gz, total <800KB gz
- Output human-readable report to stdout
- CI step: run build, compare against budgets, fail PR if exceeded
- PR comment with bundle diff (optional, nice-to-have)

### Runtime performance benchmarks (CI)
- Vitest bench files for synchronous operations:
  - Zustand store hydration from localStorage (target: <50ms)
  - File tree rendering 10K entries (target: <120ms for visible viewport)
  - Bridge command serialization round-trip (target: <10ms)
- Playwright performance tests for browser operations:
  - Shell TTI with empty layout (target: <500ms)
  - First CodeMirror render with 1000-line file (target: <500ms)
  - First tiptap render with 500-word document (target: <500ms)

### Performance marks API
- usePerformanceMark(name) hook: calls performance.mark on mount
- Wrap DockviewShell mount, CodeMirror first render, tiptap first render
- Marks readable by Playwright: page.evaluate(() => performance.getEntriesByType('mark'))

### CI integration
- GitHub Actions step after build
- Exit code 1 if any budget exceeded
- Summary table in CI output showing metric / actual / budget / pass/fail

## Implementation files
- scripts/check-bundle-size.ts — post-build budget checker
- src/hooks/usePerformanceMark.ts — performance.mark wrapper
- tests/bench/ — vitest benchmark files
- tests/perf/ — playwright performance test files

## Unit tests (vitest)

### Bundle budget checker
1. Passes when initial chunk <150KB gz
2. Fails (exit 1) when initial chunk >150KB gz
3. Passes when total <800KB gz
4. Fails (exit 1) when total >800KB gz
5. Outputs human-readable table with metric names and sizes

### usePerformanceMark hook
6. Calls performance.mark(name) on mount
7. Does not call performance.mark on re-render (only once)
8. Cleans up mark on unmount (optional, verify no memory leak)

### Vitest benchmarks
9. Store hydration benchmark completes under 50ms (threshold assertion)
10. File tree 10K render benchmark completes under 120ms
11. Bridge command serialization under 10ms

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
12. Shell TTI: navigate to workspace-playground → measure time to first interactive → <500ms
13. CodeMirror render: open file tab → performance.mark('cm-first-render') fires → <500ms
14. Tiptap render: open markdown tab → performance.mark('tiptap-first-render') fires → <500ms

## Success criteria
- CI reports bundle sizes on every PR
- CI fails if initial >150KB or total >800KB
- Performance benchmarks run in CI
- All benchmarks pass within defined budgets
- All 14 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude-cli: ship

title: Phase 2: HTTP Data Provider
description:
## Goal
Typed fetch client + React Query hooks for all agent-hosted HTTP endpoints. DataProvider context so panes access data without knowing transport.

## What to implement

### DataProvider (src/providers/DataProvider.tsx)
- React context wrapping React Query client + typed fetch
- Configured via WorkspaceProvider props: apiBaseUrl, authHeaders
- Provides hooks for panes to use
- Internal QueryClient instance (no need for apps to provide one)

### Typed fetch client (all endpoints are agent-hosted)
- GET /api/v1/tree?dir=<path> → FileEntry[]
- GET /api/v1/files?path=<path> → { content: string, mtime: number }
- POST /api/v1/files { path, content } → void (create/update file)
- DELETE /api/v1/files?path=<path> → void (delete file)
- GET /api/v1/stat?path=<path> → { isDir: boolean, size: number, mtime: number }
- GET /api/v1/files/search?q=<glob>&limit=<n> → string[]
- POST /api/v1/dirs { path } → void (create directory)
- POST /api/v1/files/move { from, to } → void (rename/move file or directory)
- No git routes in v1 (git UI dropped per decisions table)

### React Query hooks
- useFileContent(path): { data: { content, mtime }, isLoading, error, refetch }
- useFileList(dir): { data: FileEntry[], isLoading, error } — with refetchInterval: 3000ms
- useFileWrite(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/files — invalidates useFileContent cache on success
- useFileSearch(query, limit): search hook with debounce
- useStat(path): { data: { isDir, size, mtime }, isLoading, error }
- useCreateDir(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/dirs
- useMoveFile(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/files/move — invalidates tree + content caches
- useDeleteFile(): useMutation for DELETE /api/v1/files — invalidates tree cache

### Query key patterns
- ['files', path] for file content
- ['tree', dir] for directory listing
- ['stat', path] for file metadata
- ['search', query, limit] for search results
- Mutations invalidate related query keys on success

### Error handling
- 401/403 → fire onAuthError callback (app shell handles redirect to login)
- 404 → return null/undefined (component-level "file not found" UI)
- 5xx → auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff (React Query retry config)
- Network error → auto-retry 3x
- Timeout: 10s default, configurable per route. Show toast on timeout.
- Auth header injection: authHeaders from WorkspaceProvider props injected into every request

## v1 references
- queries.js (10KB) — React Query hooks, query key patterns
- httpProvider.js (8.4KB) — HTTP client, auth header injection, 401 retry
- DROP: useGitStatus, lightningFs, isomorphicGit, pyodide providers

## Considerations
- DataProvider ONLY used by pane wrappers (FileTreePane, CodeEditorPane, etc.)
- Standalone components (FileTree, CodeEditor) accept all data via props — NO DataProvider needed
- DataProvider is a React context wrapper for convenience, NOT an abstraction for swappable backends (decision: HTTP only, no DataProvider SPI)
- Polling paused during active edits (refetchInterval disabled when dirty)
- React Query staleTime: 0 for file content (always refetch), tree can be stale for 3s

## Unit tests (providers/DataProvider.test.ts)

### Typed fetch client
- Test: GET /api/v1/tree returns FileEntry array
- Test: GET /api/v1/files returns { content, mtime }
- Test: POST /api/v1/files sends { path, content }
- Test: DELETE /api/v1/files sends path param
- Test: POST /api/v1/dirs creates directory
- Test: POST /api/v1/files/move sends { from, to }
- Test: authHeaders injected into every request
- Test: apiBaseUrl prepended to all endpoint paths

### React Query hooks
- Test: useFileContent(path) fetches and returns content
- Test: useFileList(dir) returns directory listing
- Test: useFileList polling: refetchInterval fires every 3000ms
- Test: useFileWrite invalidates useFileContent cache on success
- Test: useMoveFile invalidates tree + content caches
- Test: useDeleteFile invalidates tree cache
- Test: useFileSearch debounces query

### Error handling
- Test: 401 response → onAuthError callback fired
- Test: 403 response → onAuthError callback fired
- Test: 404 response → no retry, returns error
- Test: 500 response → retries 3x with backoff
- Test: network error → retries 3x
- Test: timeout (10s) → shows toast notification

### Query key isolation
- Test: different paths produce different cache entries
- Test: mutations invalidate correct keys (not unrelated paths)

## Success criteria
- All hooks work with mock API responses
- Error handling: 401 fires callback, 5xx retries, timeout shows toast
- Query keys properly scoped (path-based cache invalidation)
- Hooks usable inside WorkspaceProvider context
- All file tree operations (create, rename, delete, move) have typed wrappers

Dependencies:
  -> boring-ui-v2-zwp (blocks) - Phase 1: WorkspaceProvider
  -> boring-ui-v2-dwe (blocks) - Phase 1: Project Scaffold

Dependents:
  <- boring-ui-v2-fds (blocks) - Phase 4: Error Handling + Recovery
  <- boring-ui-v2-8qd (blocks) - Phase 2: Markdown Editor Pane (Tiptap)
  <- boring-ui-v2-0ld (blocks) - Phase 2: Code Editor Pane (CodeMirror 6)
  <- boring-ui-v2-6el (blocks) - Phase 2: File Tree Pane
close_reason: done

title: M2: E2E — mode flip works with zero code changes
description:
**Goal:** Prove the architectural claim. `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same UI, same tools; agent runs in a Firecracker VM instead of host/bwrap.

**Test approach (Playwright against a real or mocked Vercel account):**
1. Boot with `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox`.
2. Send "list files in /" via chat.
3. Assert response mentions `/vercel/sandbox` or similar VM paths, NOT host paths.
4. Write a file via the UI's FileTree "new file" action.
5. Send "cat the new file" → assert contents match.

**Logging:** heavy test logs tagged `[m2-modeflip]` for CI debugging. Print sandbox ID, API call counts, cold-start duration.

**Done when:** flipping env vars works both ways; no code changes needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` works same as local. Flipping env vars both ways works. No code changes.
acceptance_criteria: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` works same as local. Flipping env vars both ways works. No code changes.

title: core-M1: schema — workspace_runtimes (full v1 columns + auto-create-on-read semantics)
description:
## Goal
Define `workspace_runtimes` with the full v1 column set including `spriteName`, `updatedAt`, `provisioningStep`, `stepStartedAt`. Document the auto-create-on-read semantic (matches v1).

## Why it matters
Runtime state is what the agent provisioning state machine reads. Missing columns (codex pass 1 caught this) would leave provisioning unable to track sub-steps. Auto-create-on-read is the v1 invariant — every valid workspace has a runtime row, even if our migration left a gap.

## Acceptance criteria
- Table `workspace_runtimes`:
  - `workspaceId` text PRIMARY KEY references `workspaces.id`
  - `spriteUrl` text NULL
  - `spriteName` text NULL
  - `state` text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK IN ('pending', 'provisioning', 'ready', 'error')
  - `lastError` text NULL
  - `updatedAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
  - `provisioningStep` text NULL (e.g. 'creating_machine', 'mounting_volume')
  - `stepStartedAt` timestamp NULL

## Considerations
- Auto-create-on-read: `WorkspaceStore.getWorkspaceRuntime(id)` inserts a `state='ready'` row if the workspace exists but no runtime row does. NOT done via DB trigger (Postgres + Local stores have to behave identically). Done in store-layer code.
- `workspaces.create` seeds runtime as a side-effect inside the same transaction, so well-formed workspaces always have a runtime.
- The auto-create-on-read covers migration drift, not normal flow.

## Tests
- Integration: insert workspace WITHOUT runtime row (bypassing store); call `getWorkspaceRuntime`; assert row was inserted with `state='ready'`.
- Integration: workspace.create + getWorkspaceRuntime; assert single row, no duplicates from race.
- Local store: same auto-create behavior; conformance suite covers both impls.

## Refs
CORE.md §DB Schema "workspaceRuntimes"; §Workspace semantics & defaults.
close_reason: shipped in main via commits 01bf41f + ec1cc4f (workspace_runtimes schema/migration/tests)

title: Deferred: GitHub Connect + /api/v1/git/* routes (v1.x)
description:
**Scope:** Git UI dropped from agent v1 AND workspace v1. Neither package has a git consumer → `/api/v1/git/*` routes would be dead code. Agent runs git via the `bash` tool (`git status`, `git commit`, etc.).

**Design already documented (for future):**
- Git routes ~200 LOC of thin `sandbox.exec('git …')` wrappers with output parsing.
- Credentials in remote mode: token-in-URL for HTTPS (`https://x-access-token:$TOKEN@github.com/…`).
- SSE invalidation: `data-git-changed` events after write ops.
- VM persistence: git config (`user.name`, `user.email`) seeded on sandbox create.

**Trigger to implement:** any UI consumer emerges (agent v1.x status bar, or workspace reviving git badges).

**Acceptance:** stays deferred until consumer lands; design notes preserved in this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria

Stays deferred until UI consumer lands. Design notes preserved in this bead.
close_reason: Added explicit v1.x deferral notice for GitHub Connect + /api/v1/git/* in docs (API.md and KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md), including scope, rationale, trigger, and migration path. Cross-review via claude -p: SHIP.

title: core-M3: AuthProvider + useSession (better-auth React client wrap) + UserIdentityProvider
description:
## Goal
React-side auth: wrap better-auth's React client. `useSession()` returns `SessionState`. `UserIdentityProvider` hydrates `useUser()` from `/api/v1/me` after session resolves.

## Acceptance criteria
- `<AuthProvider>` wraps `better-auth/react` client.
- `useSession(): SessionState` exported. Returns `{ data, isPending, error: HttpError | null }`.
- `signIn(provider?: 'github' | 'email', credentials?)` exported — delegates to better-auth client.
- `signOut(): Promise<void>` exported with explicit behavior:
  1. Calls `authClient.signOut()` — server invalidates session.
  2. Clears TanStack Query cache (`queryClient.clear()`) — prevents stale workspace/user data in next session.
  3. Navigates to `/auth/signin` (uses react-router's navigate hook).
  4. Resolves the promise after navigation completes.
- `<UserIdentityProvider>` calls `/api/v1/me` once a session exists; populates `useUser()` with `{ user, settings }`.
- `useUser(): User | null` returns null while pending.

## Considerations
- Identity caching: better-auth's session is the source of truth. If a transient 401 happens (e.g. backend deploy), useSession may flip to `data: null` momentarily — UI should NOT immediately redirect to signin. Use a 30s grace via a thin debounce in the redirect-trigger inside AuthGate (handled by M3_AUTH_GATE bead, not here).
- `useUser` query is cached via TanStack Query with staleTime 60s; invalidated by mutations to /me/settings.
- Logging: client-side `signOut` logs to a structured logger (e.g. console.info with `{event: 'signOut', reason: 'user-initiated'}`) for production observability via injected error reporter.

## Tests
- Mock better-auth client → useSession reflects mocked state.
- After session resolves, useUser fetches `/api/v1/me` and returns combined data.
- 401 burst test: session flips to null briefly; UI does not show signin until 30s sustain (covered by M3_AUTH_GATE).
- signOut → assert authClient.signOut called, queryClient.clear called, navigate('/auth/signin') called.
- After signOut, calling /api/v1/me would 401 (covered by integration tests in M2).

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §React client; §Traps from v1 → Identity caching.

title: Frontend: InvitesPage (list + create with Idempotency-Key + revoke)
description:
## Context

Invites management page: list pending invites for the workspace, create new invites, revoke. Builds on P3B (resolve/accept) and P3C (Idempotency-Key middleware).

## Scope

1. Create `packages/core/src/front/workspace/InvitesPage.tsx`:
   - Use `useQuery` to load `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites`.
   - Render a list: email, role, createdAt, expiresAt, "Revoke" button.
   - **Create form**: email input, role dropdown, "Send invite" button. On submit:
     - Generate a fresh `Idempotency-Key` (UUID) per submission.
     - Call `POST /api/v1/invites` with the header.
     - On 200: invalidate the invites query, clear form, show success toast "Invite sent to {email}".
     - On 422 (duplicate email or invalid role): show inline form error.
   - **Revoke button**: calls `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites/:inviteId`. On success: invalidate query.
   - **Status indicators**: pending, expired (compute from expiresAt), accepted (if accepted_at present).

2. Mount in `BoringApp.tsx` at `/w/:id/invites`.

3. Update `routes` table with `workspaceInvites: '/w/:id/invites'`.

4. Tests at `workspace/__tests__/InvitesPage.test.tsx`:
   - **Renders existing invites**.
   - **Create invite**: submits form, calls POST with Idempotency-Key, invalidates query.
   - **Idempotency-Key changes per submission**: spy on the header values across two submissions; assert different.
   - **Revoke invite**: calls DELETE, invalidates query.
   - **Duplicate email**: shows inline form error.
   - **Status badge**: invite past expiresAt shows "Expired"; accepted shows "Accepted".
   - **Non-owner cannot see this page**: route guard.

## Why this exists

Owners need to invite collaborators. The Idempotency-Key prevents duplicate emails on client retry (network blip, double-click, etc.).

## Acceptance criteria

- All seven tests pass.
- Manual: send an invite to a real email; click the link; complete the accept flow; member appears in MembersPage.

## Considerations

- Generate fresh UUID per form submission; do NOT reuse across reloads (otherwise a user resubmitting after seeing an error would replay the cached response).
- Show the expiration badge clearly so admins know when to resend.
- The route DELETE for invites already exists in core; reuse.

## Tests

Listed above.

## Out of scope

- Bulk invite (CSV upload).
- Invite resend (would require a new endpoint or just delete-and-recreate).
- Custom message in invite email.

## Spec reference

"Workspace UI pages" + Decision #5 (Idempotency on POST /invites) in v7 spec. Phase 3. Depends on P3B (endpoints), P3C (middleware).

title: core-M3: ConfigProvider (3 retries with backoff, fail-closed to AppErrorBoundary)
description:
## Goal
`<ConfigProvider>` fetches `/api/v1/config` once, retries 3× with backoff (500ms, 1s, 2s), throws to AppErrorBoundary on persistent failure.

## Why it matters
v1 fail-open behavior masked outages. v2 fail-closed is honest: if the server is unreachable, the user sees a clear error UI, not a hung blank page.

## Acceptance criteria
- `ConfigProvider` fetches via `apiFetchJson('/api/v1/config')` on mount.
- Blocks render until first response or all retries exhausted.
- Retries 3× with delays 500ms, 1s, 2s.
- On persistent failure: throws `ConfigFetchError extends Error` (catchable by AppErrorBoundary).
- `useConfig()` returns `RuntimeConfig` synchronously after load.
- `useConfigLoaded()` returns `boolean`.

## Considerations
- The "Cannot reach server" boundary UI shows a refresh button + the requestId from the failed response (if any).
- v1 had a fail-open-to-defaults pattern; intentionally dropped.

## Tests
- Mock fetch to fail 3× → AppErrorBoundary catches ConfigFetchError.
- Mock fetch to succeed on first call → useConfig returns data.
- Mock fetch to fail twice then succeed → useConfig returns data on third try.

## Refs
CORE.md §Config §RuntimeConfig.

title: core-M3: AuthGate redirect logic (with 30s identity-cache grace)
description:
## Goal
`<AuthGate>` redirects unauthenticated users to `/auth/signin` for non-public routes. Honors the 30s identity-cache to avoid bouncing users on transient 401s.

## Acceptance criteria
- Inside `<BoringApp>` after `<Routes>`.
- Public paths (no redirect): `/auth/*`, public route children (default empty list).
- For non-public routes:
  - If session.data is null and was null for >30s → navigate to `/auth/signin?redirect=<current-path>`.
  - If session.data is null but was non-null within 30s → render last-known UI (cached identity).
- After successful signin, navigate back to `?redirect` if present, else `/`.

## Considerations
- The 30s grace period prevents redirect storms during backend deploys.
- The `redirect` query param must pass through `safeRedirect` on the server post-signin to prevent open-redirect.

## Tests
- Unauth user navigates to `/dashboard` → redirected to `/auth/signin?redirect=/dashboard`.
- Auth user with valid session → no redirect.
- Brief network blip → no redirect for 30s.

## Refs
CORE.md §Layer 2 §BoringApp §AuthGate; §Traps from v1 → Identity caching.
close_reason: shipped — cross-review attempted (claude CLI timeout), local gates green

title: Update core/docs/CORE.md + README + changelog with v7 surface area
description:
## Context

`packages/core/docs/CORE.md` is the authoritative reference for app authors using core. After v7 lands, it needs to reflect the new shape: provisioner SPI, FS driver, workspace UI pages, command-palette builder, encryption rotation procedure, etc.

## Scope

1. Update `packages/core/docs/CORE.md` with new sections:

   **Workspace settings encryption**
   - Algorithm (per audit from P1A).
   - Rotation procedure: lock writes (feature flag) → run rotation script (decrypt with old key, re-encrypt with new key) → swap config → unlock writes.
   - Operational guidance: estimated downtime per 1000 rows, how to test the script in staging.

   **Workspace provisioner SPI**
   - Interface contract (`provision`, `destroy`).
   - The two modes: managed (provisioner present) vs unmanaged (no provisioner).
   - How to wire `createFsProvisioner({ rootDir })` in an app's `createCoreApp` call.
   - Future driver guidance: when shipping an async driver, the spec calls for re-introducing state machine + worker + fencing — see archived v5 spec for reference.

   **Workspace UI pages**
   - List of routes BoringApp registers (`/w/:id/settings`, `/w/:id/members`, `/w/:id/invites`, `/invites/:token`).
   - Auth requirements per route.

   **Command palette integration**
   - How apps call `getWorkspaceCommands(workspaceId, navigate)` and register with `@boring/workspace`'s palette. Code snippet.

   **Migration notes**
   - The 0007 migration drops `machine_id`/`volume_id`/`fly_region` from `workspaces` (Fly was deferred). If existing apps had data there, it's gone — document this clearly.
   - The state-check constraint narrowed; pre-existing rows in `provisioning`/`destroying`/`destroyed` must be cleaned up before migration. Provide a cleanup query.

   **Configuration**
   - New config keys: `features.inviteTtlDays`, `encryption.workspaceSettingsKey` (already existed but now load-bearing).
   - `provisioner` is configured at `createCoreApp` call site, not via env config.

   **Error codes added in v7**
   - `PROVISION_FAILED`, `DESTROY_FAILED`, `INVALID_RETRY_STATE`, `RUNTIME_UNMANAGED`, `LAST_OWNER`, `INVITE_NOT_FOUND`, `WRONG_USER_EMAIL`.

2. Update the README in `packages/core/README.md` with a one-paragraph summary of v7's surface.

3. Add a "v7 changelog" section to CORE.md or to `core/CHANGELOG.md` summarizing what shipped vs what was deferred (with a link to the spec at `docs/plans/core-gap-closure-spec.md`).

## Why this exists

Without docs, app authors guess at the shape. Worse, the next person picking up this codebase has to re-derive the design. CORE.md is the authoritative interface; it must reflect what's actually built.

## Acceptance criteria

- CORE.md updated with all six new sections.
- README updated.
- Changelog entry exists.
- Code snippets compile (or are clearly marked pseudo-code).

## Considerations

- Don't duplicate the spec. Reference it for design intent; CORE.md is for usage.
- Keep code snippets tested where possible (e.g. extract from actual test fixtures).

## Tests

- Markdown linter passes.
- Code snippets in CORE.md, where reproduced from real source, match the implementation (manual review).

## Out of scope

- Migration guide for users coming from v0/early-v1 (no in-flight users to migrate).
- Tutorial-style content (CORE.md is reference).

## Spec reference

All v7 decisions. Phase 4. Depends on all Phase 1–3 implementation beads being done so the docs accurately reflect what shipped.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met: CORE.md/README/changelog updated for shipped v7 surface area

title: core-M6: helmet + CSP strict + Playwright CSP test
description:
## Goal
Strict helmet defaults including CSP. CSP allows CM6's `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` exception. Playwright test verifies headers + CSP enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- `@fastify/helmet` registered with strict defaults: HSTS (max-age 31536000, includeSubDomains, preload), X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin.
- CSP: `default-src 'self'`; `script-src 'self'`; `style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'`; `img-src 'self' data: blob:`; `connect-src 'self'` plus `BETTER_AUTH_URL`'s origin; `font-src 'self'`; `object-src 'none'`; `frame-ancestors 'none'`.
- Helmet config exposed via `CoreConfig.helmet` for child apps to extend (e.g. add Stripe.js domain to `script-src`).
- Playwright test in apps/full-app: navigate to /; assert response headers include all helmet defaults; assert inline `<script>` injection is blocked (page raises CSP violation logged via `securitypolicyviolation` event).

## Considerations
- CM6 (CodeMirror) uses style-mod which produces inline styles. Without 'unsafe-inline', editors don't render. The exception is documented + tested.

## Tests
- **Unit**: register helmet plugin onto a test Fastify; GET /health; assert all 7 headers present with expected values via `expect(res.headers['strict-transport-security']).toEqual(...)`.
- **CSP integration**: serve a static HTML with an inline `<script>alert(1)</script>`; load via Playwright; listen for `page.on('console')`; assert a CSP violation message appears + script did not execute (no alert dialog).
- **CM6 sanity**: load a Storybook page that renders a CodeMirror instance; assert no CSP violations in console (style-mod inline styles permitted by `'unsafe-inline'` exception).
- **Header override**: register with `CoreConfig.helmet.contentSecurityPolicy.directives['script-src'] = ["'self'", 'https://js.stripe.com']`; assert merged correctly.

title: core-M5: wrap apps/workspace-playground in BoringApp
description:
## Goal
Migrate the existing `apps/workspace-playground` to use `<BoringApp>` from `@boring/core`. Remove any ad-hoc auth/config wiring. Smoke-test scripted in CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/workspace-playground/src/main.tsx` mounts `<BoringApp>` with the playground's existing routes inside.
- `apps/workspace-playground/package.json` adds `@boring/core: workspace:*` dep.
- Old provider stack (if any) removed.
- `apps/workspace-playground/src/server/main.ts` boots `createCoreApp` with `CORE_STORES=local`, `MAIL_TRANSPORT_URL=console://` so the playground works without external services.
- `pnpm --filter workspace-playground build` succeeds.
- `pnpm --filter workspace-playground dev` boots without console errors.

## Tests
- **Scripted smoke** (Playwright, runs in CI):
  - `apps/workspace-playground/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` boots dev server via `pnpm --filter workspace-playground dev` (Playwright `webServer` fixture).
  - Navigate to `/`; assert HTTP 200 + `<title>` matches expected app name.
  - Sign in as `dev@local` (auto-seeded by LocalUserStore) with password `dev`.
  - Assert landed on `/workspace/:id` of the auto-created default workspace.
  - Open the workspace switcher; assert at least one workspace listed.
- **No manual-only acceptance** — anything that requires a human to verify is OUT.
- All test steps log pino events tagged with `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-ofhw'`.

## Considerations
- The playground may use mock data; if so, the dev server runs with stubs but the smoke test still goes through real createCoreApp boot.

## Refs
CORE.md §Milestones §M5; M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS bead.

title: core-M1: schema — workspace_settings (bytea + pgcrypto) + key rotation behavior
description:
## Goal
Define `workspace_settings` with bytea-encrypted values via pgcrypto, plus the application-layer policy for key rotation (decrypt failure = `configured: false` + warn, no throw).

## Why it matters
This table holds secrets (GitHub installation tokens, OAuth refresh tokens, etc. via typed accessors). Encryption at rest is non-negotiable. The key-rotation policy keeps the system survivable when keys change without re-encrypting all rows.

## Acceptance criteria
- Table `workspace_settings`:
  - `(workspaceId, key)` composite primary key
  - `key` text NOT NULL (1-128 chars, app-layer validated)
  - `value` bytea NOT NULL (always pgcrypto-encrypted; raw plaintext never written)
  - `updatedAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
- pgcrypto extension installed (`CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto`).
- FK `workspaceId → workspaces.id` ON DELETE NO ACTION.

## Considerations
- Encryption key from `WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var (32-byte hex).
- Key-rotation policy (CORE.md §DB §Encrypted settings): on decrypt failure, `getWorkspaceSettings` returns the affected key with `configured: false` + warn log. Typed accessors return null on decrypt failure. NEVER throw — survival over correctness for a key-rotation event.
- Re-encryption on key rotation is an operator concern (one-shot script); core does not auto-migrate.

## Tests
- Integration: insert via `pgp_sym_encrypt`, read via `pgp_sym_decrypt`, assert roundtrip.
- Integration: insert with key A, read with key B; assert decrypt error caught and `configured: false` returned (not thrown).
- Unit: validate that the SQL helpers in `PostgresWorkspaceStore` use parameterized pgcrypto calls (no SQL injection vector).

## Refs
CORE.md §DB §Encrypted settings; §Traps from v1 → Encrypted workspace_settings.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: core-M1: PostgresWorkspaceStore impl (CRUD + members + invites + runtime + settings)
description:
## Goal
Implement `PostgresWorkspaceStore` matching the full interface — workspace CRUD, membership, invites (with tokenHash + raw token return), settings (metadata-only contract), runtime (auto-create-on-read), UI state, `getWorkspacesWhereSoleOwner` for the user-deletion orchestrator.

## Why it matters
This is the central data-access surface for everything multi-tenant. Most M2 routes are thin wrappers over this store. Get this wrong and the whole product breaks.

## Sub-PR roadmap (this bead = 3 PRs in sequence)

This bead is intentionally broad (~3 days of work). Implementer SHOULD land it as 3 PRs — bead stays open until all 3 merge. Each sub-PR is independently reviewable + testable:

**Sub-PR 1: Workspaces + Members + sole-owner**
- workspace CRUD (`create`, `list(userId, appId)`, `get`, `update`, `delete` returning `{removed, code?}`).
- Auto-membership: `create` inserts the calling user as `role='owner'` in same tx.
- Members API (`isMember`, `getMemberRole`, `listMembers` enriched with user JOIN, `upsertMember`, `removeMember` with code union).
- `getWorkspacesWhereSoleOwner(userId)` for orchestrator.
- Conformance suite covers all of the above.

**Sub-PR 2: Invites**
- `createInvite` returns `{ invite, rawToken }`. Raw token via `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url')`, sha256 hashed for storage.
- `listInvites`, `getInvite`, `getInviteByTokenHash` (used by post-signup hook), `revokeInvite`.
- `acceptInvite` THROWS HttpError with one of 4 codes (NOT_FOUND/EXPIRED/ALREADY_ACCEPTED/EMAIL_MISMATCH); success returns both fields populated.
- v1 race conditions (TOCTOU) carried forward — see M1_RACE_TESTS bead.

**Sub-PR 3: Settings + Runtime + UI state**
- `getWorkspaceSettings` metadata-only contract (NEVER decrypted values).
- `putWorkspaceSettings` returns refreshed metadata array.
- `decryptSetting(workspaceId, key)` and `encryptAndPut(...)` low-level primitives.
- `getWorkspaceRuntime` auto-creates `state='ready'` if missing.
- `putWorkspaceRuntime`, `retryWorkspaceRuntime`.
- `getUiState(userId, workspaceId)` returns null if no row.
- `putUiState(userId, workspaceId, state)` upsert.

## Acceptance criteria
- All methods declared in `WorkspaceStore` interface (CORE.md §DB §Stores) implemented.
- pgcrypto calls parameterized via Drizzle (no SQL injection vector).
- All methods take a Drizzle transaction or fall back to default db.
- `acceptInvite` runs in a transaction.
- All known v1 race conditions carried forward, documented in M1_RACE_TESTS.

## Tests
- Conformance suite identical to LocalWorkspaceStore (M1_STORE_CONFORMANCE bead provides shared `describeWorkspaceStoreConformance`).
- Per-PR test gate: each sub-PR's tests must pass independently before merge.
- `acceptInvite` 4 error paths.
- `delete` returns `workspace_provisioning` when runtime is provisioning.
- `getInviteByTokenHash` returns null for non-matching hash.
- `list` filters by appId (cross-app isolation test).
- `listMembers` returns user emails + names from JOIN.
- **Workspace listing order**: `list()` returns `is_default=true` first, then `createdAt DESC` — explicit ordering test.
- `getWorkspacesWhereSoleOwner` returns empty / sole-owner / co-owned cases.
- `getUiState` returns null for fresh workspace; returns persisted state after `putUiState`.

## Refs
CORE.md §DB §WorkspaceStore; §Workspace semantics & defaults; §Traps from v1.
close_reason: All 3 sub-PRs committed. PostgresWorkspaceStore complete: CRUD + members + invites + settings + runtime + UI state.

title: Phase 4: Responsive Layout
description:
## Goal
Mobile sidebar as sheet, tablet auto-collapse, minimum panel sizes enforced.

## What to implement

### Mobile layout (<768px)
- Sidebar collapses to shadcn Sheet (slide-in overlay from left)
- Sheet triggered by hamburger menu button (top-left)
- Sheet dismisses on outside click or file selection
- Main content area takes full width when sidebar is sheet

### Tablet layout (<1024px)
- Auto-collapse sidebar below breakpoint
- Sidebar collapses to icon-only rail (40px wide)
- Click sidebar icon to expand temporarily (overlay mode)
- User can pin sidebar open (persisted to preferences)

### Minimum panel sizes
- Enforced via GroupConfig constraints (DockviewShell handleReady)
- Sidebar: min 200px, max 50% viewport
- Editor: min 300px
- Agent panel: min 250px
- Prevents panels from being dragged to zero width

### Breakpoint detection
- Use @boring/core's useViewportBreakpoint() hook
- Responsive changes are CSS-first where possible (media queries)
- JS-driven changes (Sheet vs inline sidebar) via hook

## v1 references
- useResponsiveSidebarCollapse.js (1.5KB) — breakpoint logic for auto-collapse

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Mobile layout
1. Viewport <768px: sidebar renders as Sheet (not inline)
2. Hamburger button visible at <768px, hidden at >=768px
3. Click hamburger → Sheet opens with file tree
4. Click file in Sheet → Sheet closes + file opens
5. Click outside Sheet → Sheet dismisses

### Tablet layout
6. Viewport <1024px: sidebar auto-collapses to icon rail
7. Collapsed sidebar shows icons only (40px width)
8. Click collapsed sidebar icon → expands as overlay
9. Pin button in expanded overlay → keeps sidebar open (persisted)

### Panel constraints
10. Sidebar cannot be resized below 200px (drag stops at minimum)
11. Sidebar cannot exceed 50% of viewport width
12. Editor panel maintains minimum 300px width
13. Agent panel maintains minimum 250px width

### Breakpoint transitions
14. Resize from desktop → tablet: sidebar auto-collapses (smooth transition)
15. Resize from tablet → mobile: sidebar switches to Sheet mode
16. Resize from mobile → desktop: sidebar returns to inline mode

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
17. Set viewport to 375x667 (mobile) → hamburger visible, sidebar is Sheet
18. Set viewport to 800x600 (tablet) → sidebar collapsed to rail
19. Set viewport to 1440x900 (desktop) → sidebar inline, full width
20. Drag panel divider → verify minimum size enforced

## Success criteria
- Sidebar becomes Sheet on mobile
- Sidebar auto-collapses on tablet
- Panel sizes never go below minimums
- Transitions are smooth (no layout flash)
- All 20 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude-cli: ship

title: core-M3: BoringApp provider stack (correct order: ErrorBoundary → QueryClient → Config → Theme → Auth → UserIdentity → Router → WorkspaceAuth → Routes → AuthGate)
description:
## Goal
The `<BoringApp>` React shell with all providers in the documented order. Mounts default routes for `/auth/*` and `/me`; renders child routes inside.

## Why it matters
Provider order is load-bearing: WorkspaceAuthProvider depends on URL params (must be inside Router), Auth depends on Config (apiBase from runtime config), etc. Reordering breaks providers silently. This bead enforces the order via test.

## Acceptance criteria
- `BoringApp` React component exported from `@boring/core/front`.
- Provider order matches CORE.md §Layer 2 §`<BoringApp>` shell:
  1. `<AppErrorBoundary>` (outermost)
  2. `<QueryClientProvider>` (TanStack Query)
  3. `<ConfigProvider>`
  4. `<ThemeProvider>`
  5. `<AuthProvider>`
  6. `<UserIdentityProvider>`
  7. `<BrowserRouter>` (BEFORE WorkspaceAuthProvider)
  8. `<WorkspaceAuthProvider>` (reads URL params)
  9. `<Routes>` with default routes + `children`
  10. `<AuthGate>` (redirect logic)
- Default routes: `/auth/signin`, `/auth/signup`, `/auth/callback/github`, `/auth/verify-email`, `/auth/forgot-password`, `/auth/reset-password`, `/me`.
- `<BoringApp>` accepts `children` (additional `<Route>` elements).
- `authPages` prop overrides individual auth pages: `{ signIn?, signUp?, forgotPassword?, resetPassword?, verifyEmail? }`.

## Considerations
- TanStack Query client created once with default options (staleTime 60s for /me, /capabilities).
- `<AppErrorBoundary>` catches everything including provider boot errors (e.g. ConfigFetchError).

## Tests
- Render BoringApp; assert provider order via React DevTools-like introspection (use a `__verifyProviderOrder` test util that inspects context shape).
- Reordering providers in source breaks the order-assertion test.

## Refs
CORE.md §Layer 2 §`<BoringApp>` shell.
close_reason: Committed f016180, codex reviewing

title: core-M3: ForgotPasswordPage + ResetPasswordPage (client-side password match)
description:
## Goal
Forgot-password and reset-password flows wired to better-auth's client.

## Acceptance criteria
- `<ForgotPasswordPage>`: email input form. Calls `authClient.forgetPassword({ email, redirectTo: '/auth/reset-password' })`. Always shows "Check your inbox" success (don't leak whether the email exists).
- `<ResetPasswordPage>`: reads `?token=` from URL. Two password fields + confirm password. Client-side validation: passwords must match; first password must meet basic policy (≥8 chars).
- Calls `authClient.resetPassword({ token, newPassword })`. On success → redirect to `/auth/signin` with success toast.
- 410 / invalid token → show "Link expired" with a "Request new link" button to ForgotPasswordPage.

## Considerations
- Don't enumerate accounts: forgot-password ALWAYS shows success even if email isn't registered.
- Server-side weak_password (NIST blocklist) gives 400; client renders that as an inline error on the password field.

## Tests
- Forgot with any email → success state shown (no enumeration).
- Reset with mismatched passwords → client-side error, no fetch.
- Reset with expired token → shows expired UI.
- Reset with weak password (server rejects) → inline error.

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Sign-in / sign-up / reset / verify pages.
close_reason: ForgotPasswordPage + ResetPasswordPage implemented with 8 tests, codex SHIP (385494e)

title: Risk: Multi-tab concurrency (document + observe)
description:
**Risk class:** Accepted risk (spec: silent last-write-wins on multi-tab file writes).

**Scope for v1:** DOCUMENT, don't mitigate.

**Deliverable:**
- `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md` entry: "Opening two tabs and editing the same file simultaneously: the later write wins silently. The agent has no awareness of concurrent edits."
- Future mitigation seam: optimistic write with `If-Match` header + 409 on conflict.

**Why accept:** dev-tool posture; mitigations cost >> user exposure for v1.

**Trigger to re-evaluate:** first bug report where a user loses edits.

## Acceptance Criteria

Documented in `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md`. Future mitigation seam identified.
close_reason: shipped — documented multi-tab last-write-wins behavior and added characterization test; reviewed by claude: ship; landed in commit 8042f80 (shared-worktree mixed commit)

title: M1: editTool factory (string-replace)
description:
**Goal:** `edit` tool — string-replace edits on existing files. File: `src/server/catalog/tools/editTool.ts`.

**Parameters:** `{ path: string, oldString: string, newString: string, replaceAll?: boolean }`.

**Behavior:**
- Reads file via workspace.readFile.
- If `oldString` not present → error (agent must use exact content).
- If `replaceAll: false` and `oldString` appears more than once → error ("ambiguous; add context or replaceAll").
- Replaces + writes back via workspace.writeFile.
- Returns `{ path, replacements: number }`.

**Tests:** unique match replace; ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll; replaceAll succeeds; no-match rejects.

**Why NOT a diff-based edit tool:** Claude Code pattern. Exact-string-replace is simpler for LLMs than unified-diff; fewer parse errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: Risk: Pi SDK version pinning + release monitoring (no semver guarantee)
description:
**Goal:** Lock down pi-coding-agent version strategy + monitoring. Spec §Risks: "Pi SDK API instability (v0.68.x, no semver)."

**Strategy:**
1. **Pin exact version in package.json** — no caret, no tilde. `"@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent": "0.68.3"` (or whatever exact version we target at M1).
2. **Pin via `overrides` in workspace root** (pnpm-workspace.yaml or package.json overrides) so transitive deps can't bump it.
3. **Monitor upstream releases:** RSS feed / GitHub Releases subscription on `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Ops channel (email or Slack) on new release.
4. **Manual upgrade protocol:**
   - Read release notes before upgrading.
   - Run full e2e suite (including risk.pi-abort-propagation-test + risk.pi-session-mapping-conformance) on new version locally.
   - If tests pass: upgrade in a dedicated PR with before/after test output + CHANGELOG-style notes.
   - If tests fail: open issue + pin stays; consider `PiAgentCoreHarness` fallback.

**No semver guarantee = no auto-upgrades:**
- Dependabot/Renovate config: IGNORE pi-coding-agent (document rationale in comment).
- CI has a scheduled weekly job that tries the latest pi version against our test suite; reports to a quarantine channel if tests fail. **Does not auto-PR.**

**Fallback readiness:**
- Keep `PiAgentCoreHarness` as a design-seam in the Harness interface (already there).
- If a pi-coding-agent breaking change lands + we can't work around: implement PiAgentCoreHarness in ~400 LOC (lower-level pi-agent-core API, no plugin ecosystem).

**Rationale (why accept the risk):** pi-coding-agent is single-maintainer; rewriting the harness is expensive. Pinning is the conservative default. Monitoring catches breaks early.

**Tests:**
- `package.json` audit: assert `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` version is exact (no range specifier). Lint in CI.
- Weekly CI job: try latest pi; report failures separately from main CI.

**Logging:** `[pi-version]` prefix in startup log: print exact version for forensics (`pi-coding-agent@0.68.3 loaded`).

## Acceptance Criteria

Exact-version pin in package.json + workspace overrides. CI lints for the exact specifier. Weekly canary job exists + reports failures. CONTRIBUTING.md has upgrade protocol. Fallback plan (PiAgentCoreHarness) tracked as design seam.

title: M3b: Tool renderers — ai-elements generic + DiffView for edit
description:
**Goal:** Render bash/read/write with ai-elements generic `Tool` + `Terminal` + `CodeBlock`. **One custom renderer: `DiffView` for `edit`.** Files: `src/front/toolRenderers/*`.

**Note on spec discrepancy:** spec §Options we ship > LOC table (line 462) says "no DiffView in v1" but spec §M3b roadmap (line 1354) says "Only 1 specialized renderer authored: a unified-diff view for edit". Resolution: **DiffView IS in v1** — the LOC row was stale. DiffView adapted from old boring-ui is ~40 LOC; small enough to ship.

**Mapping:**
- `bash` → `<Tool>{<Terminal>{stdout, stderr, exitCode}</Terminal>}</Tool>` (input=command shown at top)
- `read` → `<Tool>{<CodeBlock language={guessFromPath}>{content}</CodeBlock></Tool>` — truncated view for long files with "show all" affordance
- `write` → `<Tool>` with summary `"Wrote N bytes to <path>"` (no content render — too much for writes that could be large; delegate to Editor if user wants to inspect)
- `edit` → `<DiffView oldString={input.oldString} newString={input.newString} path={input.path} replaceAll={input.replaceAll} />` — custom unified-diff UI.
- `get_ui_state` → `<Tool>` with pretty-printed JSON of returned state. Collapsible.
- `exec_ui` → `<Tool>` with a compact "→ openFile(foo.md)" style summary. Matches the `data-ui-command` display card pattern.

**DiffView implementation:**
- Reference: `packages/agent/src/front/components/chat/EditToolRenderer.jsx` from old boring-ui.
- Library: `diff` (jsdiff) for computing unified diff; render with our primitives + CSS vars.
- Shows: file path header, +/- lines with color coding (via `--boring-chat-diff-add`/`-remove` vars).
- Collapsed view for long diffs (>30 lines) with "expand" button.

**Override seam:** `<ChatPanel toolRenderers={{ my_custom_tool: MyRenderer }} />`. Default map inlined; override merges deep. Workspace package uses this for workspace-specific tools.

**Tests:**
- Each renderer renders a snapshot for representative input.
- DiffView: multi-line addition, multi-line removal, mixed changes, empty-file diff, identical strings (renders "no change").
- Override via prop: custom renderer takes precedence.
- Fallback: unknown tool name → generic `<Tool>` with JSON.stringify.

**Logging:** none at render time (UI perf). `[tool-renderer]` only for dev warnings (e.g., malformed tool output that doesn't match expected shape).

## Acceptance Criteria

All 6 shipped tools have a renderer: bash, read, write, edit (custom DiffView), get_ui_state, exec_ui. Override via `toolRenderers` prop works with deep merge. Fallback generic renderer renders unknown tools legibly. DiffView handles edge cases (empty, identical, very long). Visual snapshots captured for regression.
acceptance_criteria: Each built-in tool has a renderer snapshot. DiffView renders multi-line changes cleanly. Override via `toolRenderers` prop merges deep.

title: M4: Verify plugins bypass Sandbox as designed (local mode only)
description:
**Goal:** Automated verification of spec §Risks > "Plugin × sandbox mismatch" design-time mitigation: **plugin loading is local-mode-only; plugin tools run in Node host (bypass Sandbox by design).**

**Why this test matters:** a future refactor could accidentally wire plugins through the Sandbox, silently changing the security model. A test catches this.

**Scenarios:**

1. **Direct mode loads plugins:** set `BORING_AGENT_MODE=direct`, drop a test extension into `~/.pi/agent/extensions/testplugin.ts` that registers a tool calling `process.cwd()`. Invoke tool via chat. Assert the tool reports the BACKEND process cwd (plugin bypassed Sandbox ✓).

2. **Local mode loads plugins:** same as above but `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local`. Plugin still calls `process.cwd()` — reports backend cwd (NOT bwrapped). Bypass confirmed.

3. **Vercel-sandbox mode SKIPS plugin discovery:** set `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox` with the same extension present. Invoke via chat — expect 404/not-found. Plugin discovery disabled in remote mode.

4. **Warning log on plugin load:** log should say `[plugin] Loaded testplugin (host-process; bypasses sandbox)` with level=warn. Verify in log capture.

5. **Plugin + our default name collision:** drop a plugin registering `bash` → assert last-registered wins, log warning, chat still functional (covered in m4.name-collision; cross-link).

**Implementation:** test file `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/pluginSandboxBypass.test.ts`. Fixture `test-fixtures/plugins/testplugin.ts` — minimal pi extension.

**Tests ARE the enforcement** — this is a design-time invariant that only code guarantees.

**Logging:** capture `[plugin]` logs for each load/skip event.

## Acceptance Criteria

4 scenarios pass: plugins load in direct mode, plugins load in local mode, plugins DON'T load in vercel-sandbox mode, warning logged on each load. Test file documents the invariant with a large comment block. Regression = test fails = PR blocked.

title: core-M2: post-signup hook (invite-accept-in-tx OR default-workspace create + cookie signal)
description:
## Goal
Better-auth post-signup hook that runs in the same transaction as user creation. Branches on `invite_token` presence: invite-accept (validates + acceptInvite) OR default-workspace create. Optionally sends the Welcome email.

## Why it matters
The "every signed-in user has at least one workspace" invariant — agent embed mode depends on it. Also handles signup-via-invite-link UX. Welcome email is the first non-transactional touch; setting it up here keeps the post-signup logic centralized.

## Acceptance criteria
- Hook registered with better-auth via `databaseHooks.user.create.after`.
- If signup request carried `x-invite-token` header (forwarded by SignUpPage from `?invite_token` URL):
  1. Hash via sha256, call `WorkspaceStore.getInviteByTokenHash(hash)`.
  2. Validate: not expired, not accepted, `invite.email === user.email` case-insensitively.
  3. Call `WorkspaceStore.acceptInvite(workspaceId, inviteId, userId)` in same tx.
  4. On any validation failure: set `boring_invite_failed` cookie (60s, non-HttpOnly, value = one of `'invite_expired' | 'invite_already_accepted' | 'invite_email_mismatch' | 'invite_not_found'` — matches ERROR_CODES enum verbatim); fall through to step 5.
  5. (Failure path) Continue to default-workspace create.
- Else (no invite token):
  - `WorkspaceStore.create(userId, 'My Workspace', appId)` with `isDefault: true`.
- **Welcome email dispatch (always after the above, only if signup did NOT come through a successful invite-accept)**:
  - If `config.features.sendWelcomeEmail !== false` AND mail is configured: render `<Welcome appName={config.appName} getStartedUrl={...}/>` template, send via mail transport.
  - If invite was accepted: do NOT send Welcome (the invite already provides context).
  - Mail send failures are logged but never break the signup. Pino warn `event: welcome_email.send_failed`.

## Considerations
- The cookie signaling avoids leaking failure state into the URL, doesn't require an `/api/v1/me` round-trip just to detect.
- Default workspace creation matches v1 UX; users never see an empty `<WorkspaceSwitcher>`.
- Email verification (if enabled) is orthogonal to this hook — better-auth handles it via its own hook.
- Welcome email is feature-flagged via `features.sendWelcomeEmail` in CoreConfig (default true).

## Tests
- Signup with valid invite token → user joins invited workspace, no default created, NO Welcome email.
- Signup without token → default 'My Workspace' created + Welcome email sent (mock transport asserts).
- Signup with expired token → default created + cookie value 'invite_expired' set + Welcome email sent.
- Signup with email-mismatch token → default created + cookie 'invite_email_mismatch' + Welcome email.
- features.sendWelcomeEmail = false → no Welcome.
- Mail config missing → no Welcome (graceful skip + warn log).

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Post-signup side effects; §Auth §Email sending §Templates.

title: fix(agent): history replay drops user messages — add JSON messages endpoint
description:
**Symptom (reported by user):** On page refresh, past message formatting changes — user bubbles vanish, only assistant text comes back.

**Root cause:** packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/chat.ts line 154 — the replay branch filters to `msg.role === 'assistant'` and emits chunks via AI SDK's createUIMessageStream. Two issues:

1. User messages are silently dropped (the `continue`).
2. AI SDK v6 UIMessageChunk stream is shaped for ONE assistant response; it has no native `type: 'user-message'` or cross-role equivalent. Multi-message history replay is a stretch of the stream protocol.

**Fix (idiomatic AI SDK v6):**
1. Add `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/messages` → returns `UIMessage[]` JSON (full history including user turns).
2. In `useAgentChat`, on mount, fetch the endpoint and call `setMessages(fetchedMessages)` to hydrate. Keep `resume: true` for in-flight stream recovery, but drop the stream-based history replay.
3. Delete the history-replay branch in chat.ts' stream endpoint — let it return 204 when no active stream exists.
4. Tests: verify refresh shows same user + assistant sequence as live stream.

**Scope:** packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/chat.ts + hooks/useAgentChat.ts + one new route + 1–2 unit tests.

**Why P1:** visible UX regression, found post-v1 close. Small scope, high impact.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; refresh now hydrates full UIMessage history via /messages, resume endpoint is live-stream-only 204 fallback, and regression tests updated

title: Research: Tailwind v4 conflict with workspace styles
description:
**Question:** When `@boring/workspace` also uses Tailwind v4, do our utility classes and theirs compose cleanly, or do we get preflight resets conflicting with the workspace's global styles?

**Investigation:**
1. Set up a minimal workspace-like host that imports both packages.
2. Render ChatPanel inside a workspace layout that also has Tailwind v4.
3. Check DevTools for specificity conflicts, unexpected resets, class-name collisions.

**Expected issues:**
- Two preflight injections → double-reset.
- Scoped utility classes bleed.

**Mitigation candidates (pick one):**
- Use CSS Modules inside primitives (bypass Tailwind for agent-internal styling).
- Ship a scoping config (prefix `agt-` on all our classes).
- Document "don't enable Tailwind preflight in both packages" as a hard requirement.

**Acceptance:** integration test or documented constraint.

## Acceptance Criteria

Integration test OR documented 'don't enable preflight in both packages' constraint.
close_reason: shipped — research doc + 7 guard tests. Three conflicts found (CSS var collision, double @layer base, utility identity), all mitigated by current architecture. Documented constraints: don't import both globals.css files; only one @import tailwindcss per document.

title: core-M6: graceful shutdown — clean drain + 30s timeout test
description:
## Goal
SIGTERM/SIGINT handler installed by createCoreApp. Drains in-flight up to 30s, exits 0 on clean drain or 1 on timeout. Two integration tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- Fastify configured with `closeGraceDelay: 30_000`.
- Process listens for SIGTERM and SIGINT; on receipt:
  1. Stop accepting new requests.
  2. Wait for in-flight to drain (up to 30s).
  3. Close DB pool.
  4. exit(0) on success, exit(1) with `shutdown:grace-exceeded` warn on timeout.
- `options.manageShutdown === false` skips handler installation (for tests).

## Tests
- Clean-drain test: spawn app with vitest's spawn, SIGTERM, assert exit 0 within 30s.
- Slow-route test: inject slow route (40s), SIGTERM during in-flight request, assert exit 1 + `shutdown:grace-exceeded` log line after 30s.
- (SIGKILL not testable from Node — documentation only.)

## Refs
CORE.md §Milestones §M6 §Graceful shutdown.
close_reason: shipped: graceful shutdown signal handling + integration timeout test

title: Invite TTL: features.inviteTtlDays config + drop SQL default + store-layer compute
description:
## Context

`workspace_invites.expires_at` has a SQL default of `now() + interval '7 days'`. There's no way for an app operator to change this without modifying the schema. v7 wants a single global config knob.

## Scope

1. Add `features.inviteTtlDays: number` to `coreConfigSchema` (default 7). Validate with `z.number().int().min(1).max(30)` (sensible bounds: 1 day minimum, 30 max).
2. **Drop the SQL default** on `workspace_invites.expires_at` (keep `notNull`). This must be part of migration 0007 (depends on migration bead).
3. Update `PostgresWorkspaceStore.createInvite` and `LocalWorkspaceStore.createInvite` to compute `expiresAt = now() + ttlDays * 86400000` from `app.config.features.inviteTtlDays`.
4. Tests:
   - Config validation: `inviteTtlDays = 0` rejected; `= 31` rejected; `= 7` accepted.
   - Invite creation: TTL is honored from config (set config to 3 days; invite expires in 3 days from creation).
   - Default behavior: with no config override, default TTL is 7 days.

## Why this exists

App operators may have policy requirements (some want 24h TTL for security; some want 14d for laggy onboarding). One-knob config is right level of flexibility for v1; per-invite TTL is explicitly out of scope.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config schema updated.
- Migration drops SQL default on `expires_at` (column stays `notNull`).
- `createInvite` calls in both stores read from config.
- All four test cases pass.

## Considerations

- Conflicts/coordination with the migration bead — the SQL default drop must land in the same migration.
- Document that out-of-band SQL inserts of invites must specify `expires_at` (no fallback default).

## Out of scope

- Per-invite TTL override.
- Bulk extend-existing-invites operation.

## Spec reference

Decision #8 in v7 spec. Phase 1. Migration coordination with the Phase 2-A migration bead.

title: Implement WorkspaceBridge idempotency and replay protection
description:
Goal:
    Implement atomic semantic idempotency/replay protection after token/AuthPolicy identity and capability context exists.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ## Idempotency and replay policy

Replay/idempotency protection is required in MVP for mutation ops.

Token `jti` prevents blind token replay but is not enough for semantic retries. Every mutating op that can be retried by browser, agent, SDK, or sandbox code must declare an operation-level idempotency policy.

Required policies:

| Operation | Policy |
| --- | --- |
| `human-input.v1.request` | idempotent by `requestId` / `toolCallId` |
| `human-input.v1.answer` | one-shot idempotency key; conflicting replay rejected |
| `human-input.v1.cancel` | one-shot idempotency key; conflicting replay rejected |
| `macro.v1.transform.persist` | required idempotency key |

Replay/idempotency storage must support atomic check-and-set.

For mutation ops, key scope must include:

- workspaceId;
- sessionId if present;
- pluginId/runtimeId if present;
- op;
- idempotencyKey/requestId/toolCallId;
- normalized input hash.

Behavior:

- same key + same hash returns prior pending/final result when safe;
- same key + different hash rejects with `BRIDGE_REPLAY_REJECTED`;
- missing required key returns `BRIDGE_IDEMPOTENCY_REQUIRED`;
- answer/cancel state transitions must be atomic so double-submit cannot resolve the same waiter twice;
- idempotency records must never store bearer tokens;
- workspace package may provide bounded file/in-memory storage; core/cloud may inject persistent storage later;
- file-backed defaults must use lock/transaction semantics, not naive read/modify/write;
- multi-worker/serverless/core deployments must inject DB/Redis-style atomic storage;
- if no atomic store is available for required mutation ops, those ops must be disabled with a stable diagnostic;
- records have TTL/garbage collection so workspace-local state cannot grow forever.

### Phase 3C — Idempotency/replay primitives

Implement operation-level idempotency store and replay checks after identity/capability context is defined by tokens and BridgeAuthPolicy.

Acceptance:

- Missing idempotency key for required mutation returns `BRIDGE_IDEMPOTENCY_REQUIRED`.
- Same key + same payload returns previous result where safe.
- Same key + different payload returns `BRIDGE_REPLAY_REJECTED`.
- One-shot answer/cancel replay is handled according to explicit policy.
- Concurrent answer/cancel resolves exactly one final state.
- Token value never appears in idempotency records or diagnostics.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Tests cover missing key, same-payload replay, conflicting replay, concurrent answer/cancel, normalized hash, TTL/GC, file-backed lock/transaction behavior.
- Required mutations disabled with stable diagnostic if no atomic store.
- Idempotency records never store bearer token.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/workspaceBridge
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.
close_reason: shipped — Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP; implemented in-memory/file idempotency stores, normalized hashes, replay rejection, TTL/GC, no-store diagnostic, and token-free records; gates passed: workspaceBridge tests and workspace typecheck

title: Add workspace data catalog plugin foundation
description:
# Add workspace data catalog plugin foundation

Implement a reusable data catalog plugin package that other domain plugins, especially boring-macro, can build on top of.

## Context

User requested a data catalog plugin that contributes:
- a left workbench tab
- a visualization data tab/panel
- a command-palette catalog
- an agent skill/system prompt and agent tool

Coordinate with `packages/workspace/docs/plans/PLUGIN_OUTPUTS_ISOLATION_PLAN.md`: another agent is extracting explicit plugin outputs, so keep this implementation scoped and plugin-owned on the current plugin contract (`panels`, `catalogs`, `commands`, `agentTools`, `systemPrompt`) and avoid hardcoding new behavior into workspace chrome.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Add a reusable data catalog plugin/factory under `packages/workspace/src/plugins/`.
- Plugin contributes a Data left-tab based on DataExplorer.
- Plugin contributes a visualization-data center panel.
- Plugin contributes a command-palette catalog backed by the same catalog adapter.
- Plugin contributes system-prompt skill text plus an agent tool for querying the catalog.
- Export the plugin/factory and relevant types from the workspace public API.
- Add focused tests for plugin shape, catalog search/select behavior, and agent tool behavior.
- Run relevant workspace checks.

title: Docs for workspace-local runtime provisioning
description:
Document the workspace-local runtime provisioning contract for users and plugin authors.

Background / intent:
- The feature is user-visible: workspaces get a generated `.boring-agent` directory, plugin skills are readable as files, and package authors use package.json#pi/#boring plus trusted server provisioning.
- Good docs reduce confusion and help users understand what is generated, what is safe to delete, and what reload does.

Scope:
- Add/update docs in the appropriate package docs/README locations for:
  - zero-setup behavior on workspace load and CLI modes
  - `$WORKSPACE/.boring-agent` generated layout and `.gitignore` policy
  - user-owned `.agents/skills` vs generated `.boring-agent/skills`
  - package authoring shape: package.json#pi.skills/systemPrompt/packages/extensions/prompts and package.json#boring.front/server
  - trusted server plugin provisioning for SDKs/templates/routes/server-stateful tools
  - `/api/v1/agent/reload` behavior and when to use it
  - `provisionWorkspace: false` behavior
  - direct/local/Vercel consistency and known first-pass concurrency limitation
- Do not document nonexistent doctor/status/job endpoints.

Acceptance criteria:
- Docs include a concise macro-style example showing `bm`, mirrored skills, and template files.
- Docs explain that `.boring-agent` is generated/disposable and should not be hand-edited.
- Docs mention future workspace-installed plugins as deferred and not first-pass behavior.
- Docs include verification commands for the relevant unit/e2e smoke tests.
close_reason: docs added; Gemini 3.5 Flash review round 2: SHIP

title: D1 move file-tree panes into filesystemPlugin
description:
# Goal
Move file tree implementation from root panes into `plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/`.

# Background and reasoning
File tree panes are owned by the filesystem plugin. Keeping them under `src/panes/file-tree/` hides plugin ownership and forces imports from chrome to reach sideways into panes.

# Exact moves
Use `git mv`:
- `src/panes/file-tree/FileTree.tsx` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTree.tsx`
- `src/panes/file-tree/FileTreeView.tsx` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTreeView.tsx`
- `src/panes/file-tree/__tests__/FileTree.test.tsx` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/__tests__/FileTree.test.tsx`
- `src/panes/file-tree/__tests__/FileTreePane.test.tsx` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/__tests__/FileTreePane.test.tsx`
- `src/panes/file-tree/__tests__/copyToClipboard.test.ts` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/__tests__/copyToClipboard.test.ts`

# Exact import updates inside moved files
- `FileTreeView.tsx`: data imports -> `../../../front/data`; data types -> `../../../front/data/types`; bridge types -> `../../../front/bridge/types`; `PanelChrome` -> `../../../front/dock`; UI primitives -> `../../../front/components/ui`; `cn` -> `../../../front/lib/utils`; toast -> `../../../front/toast`; events -> `../../../front/events`.
- `FileTree.tsx`: `getFileIcon` -> `../../../front/registry/getFileIcon`; `cn` -> `../../../front/lib/utils`.
- File tree tests: keep local `../FileTree` and `../FileTreeView`; update toast import in `FileTreePane.test.tsx` to `../../../../front/toast`.

# Exact external updates
- `src/front/chrome/workbench-left/WorkbenchLeftPane.tsx`: `../../../panes/file-tree/FileTreeView` -> `../../../plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTreeView`.
- `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/index.ts`: `../../panes/file-tree/FileTreeView` -> `./file-tree/FileTreeView`.
- `src/panes/index.ts` compatibility exports for FileTreePane/FileTreeView should point to `../plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTreeView`.
- `src/index.ts`: FileTreePane/FileTreeView and FileTree exports/types should point to `./plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/...`.
- `stories/FileTree.stories.tsx`: `../src/panes/file-tree/FileTree` -> `../src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTree`.
- `stories/storybook-mocks.tsx`: FileTreeNode type import -> `../src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree/FileTree`.

# Scope / non-goals
- Do not change tree behavior, ignore patterns, clipboard fallback, or API calls.

# Acceptance checklist
- [ ] File tree tests pass from new folder.
- [ ] WorkbenchLeftPane imports FileTreeView from filesystemPlugin.
- [ ] Public API still exports `FileTree`, `FileTreePane`, `FileTreeView`, and related types.

# Gate commands
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace test -- src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/file-tree`
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace typecheck`

# Evidence
Record moved paths, import diff summary, and gate output.
close_reason: shipped — codex review: revise→ship (staging fix)

title: Move Python SDK provisioning to .boring-agent/venv with runtime smoke checks
description:
Goal: make Python SDKs and console scripts reliable in the executing runtime.

Scope:
- Create venv at `.boring-agent/venv`.
- Prefer `python3 -m venv --copies .boring-agent/venv`.

- Vercel installs/checks Python in remote sandbox.
