title: A2.5 move testing subpath into front/testing and update build entry
description:
# Goal
Move published testing helpers from `packages/workspace/src/testing/` to `packages/workspace/src/front/testing/`, then update the Vite lib entry so `@boring/workspace/testing` still publishes.

# Background and reasoning
Testing helpers are React/front-end helpers and published through the `./testing` package subpath. The critical trap is `vite.config.ts`: the `testing` lib entry must move from `src/testing/index.ts` to `src/front/testing/index.ts` or `dist/testing.js` and `dist/testing.d.ts` silently break. This bead depends on data and store moves because testing helpers import both.

# Exact moves
Use `git mv` for all files under `src/testing/`:
- `TestWorkspaceProvider.tsx`, `createLocalStorageSessions.ts`, `createMockBridge.ts`, `createMockPaneProps.ts`, `createMockRegistry.ts`, `createMockSessions.ts`, `e2e.ts`, `index.ts`, `mockApi.ts`, `renderPane.tsx` -> same names under `src/front/testing/`.
- `src/testing/__tests__/testing.test.tsx` -> `src/front/testing/__tests__/testing.test.tsx`.

# Exact import updates
Inside moved testing files:
- `TestWorkspaceProvider.tsx`: `../front/registry/PanelRegistry` -> `../registry/PanelRegistry`; `../front/WorkspaceProvider` -> `../WorkspaceProvider`; `../front/data/DataProvider` -> `../data/DataProvider`.
- `createMockBridge.ts`: `../front/store/types` -> `../store/types`.
- `mockApi.ts`: `../front/data/types` -> `../data/types`.
- `__tests__/testing.test.tsx`: `../../front/data/hooks` -> `../../data/hooks`.
External imports to update:
- `src/panes/code-editor/__tests__/CodeEditorPane.test.tsx`: `../../../testing/createMockPaneProps` -> `../../../front/testing/createMockPaneProps`.
- `src/panes/markdown-editor/__tests__/MarkdownEditorPane.test.tsx`: same update.
- `packages/workspace/vite.config.ts`: `testing: resolve(__dirname, "src/testing/index.ts")` -> `testing: resolve(__dirname, "src/front/testing/index.ts")`.

# Scope / non-goals
- Do not alter package `exports` in `package.json`; it already points to `dist/testing.*`.
- Do not change helper behavior or test fixtures.

# Acceptance checklist
- [ ] No tracked files remain under `packages/workspace/src/testing/`.
- [ ] `vite.config.ts` uses `src/front/testing/index.ts` for lib entry `testing`.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace build` produces `dist/testing.js` and `dist/testing.d.ts`.
- [ ] Subpath smoke succeeds: `node -e 'import("@boring/workspace/testing").then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m).sort()))'`.

# Gate commands
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace test -- src/front/testing`
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace build`
- `node -e 'import("@boring/workspace/testing").then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m).sort()))'`

# Evidence
Record Vite entry diff, dist artifact names, smoke output, and gate results.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; testing moved to front/testing, build entry updated, typecheck/test/build/smoke green

title: WorkspaceBridge RPC v1 epic: emitUiEffect, ask-user without server plugin, Macro SDK bridge
description:
Goal:
    Deliver the full WorkspaceBridge v1 program: ask-user without required plugin-owned server routes, and Macro/runtime SDK bridge transport across direct/local/vercel.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ## Goal

Remove plugin-owned server routes where a plugin only needs protected communication with the workspace host.

This plan targets two concrete migrations:

1. **`@hachej/boring-ask-user`**
   - Long-term package shape: `front` + `agent` + `shared` only.
   - No custom ask-user server plugin/routes required by normal setup.
   - Pending-question/runtime coordination becomes a workspace-owned `human-input` bridge service.

2. **Plugin/runtime SDK calls such as boring-macro**
   - SDKs running inside direct/local/remote agent runtimes call host-owned operations through protected WorkspaceBridge RPC.
   - SDKs no longer need hardcoded app URLs, localhost assumptions, or ad-hoc auth headers.
   - Macro browser/product routes may remain; bridge is required first for SDK-from-runtime/sandbox portability.

## Non-goals

- Do not remove regular trusted app routes wholesale. Macro-style `/api/macro/*` product/browser APIs remain valid when the app owns and reviews them.
- Do not create a generic route proxy such as `bridge.call('/api/anything')`.
- Do not keep backward-compatible aliases for the UI side-effect rename. This plan intentionally replaces `postCommand` with `emitUiEffect`.
- Do not remove `@hachej/boring-ask-user/server` in the same release that introduces the replacement path.
- Do not couple `@hachej/boring-agent` to workspace. Agent may expose generic runtime env/context seams; workspace/core inject workspace bridge specifics.

## Core idea

Replace the existing UI bridge concept with a broader `WorkspaceBridge` that has two lanes:

1. **UI effect lane** — `emitUiEffect` behavior.
   - Opens panes.
   - Focuses files.
   - Shows toasts/banners.
   - Publishes UI-only effects.
   - Remains the single dispatch source for UI side effects.

2. **RPC/request lane** — `call/registerHandler` behavior.
   - Bounded request/response calls.
   - Capability-scoped and schema-validated.
   - Used by agent tools, runtime SDKs, iframe/runtime plugins, and browser UI submissions.

UI side effects caused by RPC handlers must still flow through `emitUiEffect`; `call()` must not become a second UI effect dispatcher.

## Package boundary rules

These rules protect the existing package architecture.

- `packages/workspace/src/shared/**` remains platform-neutral: no `node:*`, no `Buffer`.
- Workspace and agent remain DB-free. Persistence uses injected stores; core/cloud can inject DB-backed stores.
- `@hachej/boring-agent` must not import workspace-specific bridge code. It may expose generic runtime env/context hooks.
- Workspace/core owns injection of `BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_*` env vars and bridge tokens.
- Workspace base front/shared code must not value-import `@hachej/boring-agent`.
- Routes/tools/bridge handlers that touch files operate through `Workspace` / runtime adapters, not raw root paths.
- Path validation remains adapter-owned.
- Workspace + Sandbox remain paired through `RuntimeModeAdapter`; bridge env injection must respect the active pair.
- Stable bridge errors must use the canonical error-code import site; no scattered raw string codes.

## Success criteria

- `ask_user` works in a workspace with only ask-user front + Pi extension surfaces.
- Ask-user no longer needs plugin-owned server routes for the new path.
- Workspace-owned pending-question coordinator is DB-free with store injection seam.
- Runtime/sandbox SDK calls use scoped bridge token and no localhost assumption.
- Macro SDK can call data/persist operations via bridge in direct/local/vercel modes.
- Existing Macro browser/product routes continue to work during migration.
- Workspace file-like plugin calls use workspace-files bridge ops through adapter validation.
- No bridge token appears in logs, workspace files, diagnostics, or tool outputs.
- `WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect` is the canonical UI side-effect dispatch path, with no old alias.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - All child WorkspaceBridge beads closed.
- br dep cycles reports no cycles and bv cycle insight is null.
- Ask-user and Macro success criteria proven by child evidence.
- No bridge token/logging/invariant regression.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - br dep cycles # no cycles
- bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles' # null
- pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm lint:invariants
- ask-user e2e and Macro SDK smoke commands from child beads pass or have explicit provider skip.

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: D4 move defaultEditorPanels into filesystemPlugin
description:
# Goal
Move `defaultEditorPanels.ts` from root panes to `plugins/filesystemPlugin/defaultEditorPanels.ts`.

# Background and reasoning
`defaultEditorPanels` is consumed by apps through the public barrel and uses `source: "app"` panel registrations. It is related to filesystem editor panes but intentionally distinct from `filesystemPlugin` built-ins, which use `source: "builtin"`. Keep behavior as-is; dedupe is a separate follow-up.

# Exact move
Use `git mv`:
- `src/panes/defaultEditorPanels.ts` -> `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/defaultEditorPanels.ts`

# Exact import updates inside moved file

- `./markdown-editor/MarkdownEditorPane` stays local under new plugin root if markdown-editor already moved.

# Exact external updates
- `src/panes/index.ts` compatibility export for `defaultEditorPanels` should point to `../plugins/filesystemPlugin/defaultEditorPanels`.
- `src/index.ts` public export for `defaultEditorPanels` should point to `./plugins/filesystemPlugin/defaultEditorPanels`.
- Search all packages for direct imports of `src/panes/defaultEditorPanels`; update only if present. Known consumers should use `@boring/workspace` barrel, so no app changes should be required.

# Scope / non-goals
- Do not change panel ids, titles, placement, `source: "app"`, or filePatterns.
- Do not remove this export even if filesystemPlugin has similar built-in panels.

title: Define WorkspaceBridge RPC shared contracts and canonical bridge errors
description:
Goal:
Add platform-neutral shared contracts for WorkspaceBridge.call and operation definitions.


WorkspaceBridge v1 context to preserve:
- emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- callerClass means transport/source boundary (`browser`, `runtime`, `server`). Actor attribution is separate observability data: `actorKind` (`human`, `agent`, `system`, `service`), redacted `performedBy`, and redacted `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is audit/log context only in v1. Do not complicate rate-limit keys or create a new auth layer from it.
- Browser auth policy derives human attribution; runtime tokens/env derive agent attribution; trusted server calls choose system/service; request bodies cannot spoof attribution.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local; no local dev token.
- No generic `workspace-files.v1.*` bridge API in v1.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Ask-user is a hard cutover to new bridge path; no compatibility window for old server route/export behavior.


Scope:
- Shared request/response DTOs, operation metadata, bridge context, idempotency policy, resource scopes, canonical errors.
- Add lightweight actor attribution types: `BridgeActorKind = human | agent | system | service`, `performedBy`, `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is derived at trusted boundaries and included in audit/log context; request input cannot set it.
- Stable bridge errors stay in one canonical import site.

Non-goals:
- No generic `workspace-files.v1.*` operation definitions.
- No artifact service types.

Acceptance/tests:
- [ ] Type tests cover sample op and actor attribution context.
- [ ] Shared code has no node:* or Buffer.
- [ ] No value imports from @hachej/boring-agent in workspace base front/shared.
- [ ] No raw bridge error strings outside canonical enum/tests.

Gate commands:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test
- pnpm lint:invariants


Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation/test/doc changes are scoped to this bead.
- [ ] Tests include detailed logs with tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands pass or skips are explicit with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.
close_reason: shipped — Gemini review: SHIP; added platform-neutral WorkspaceBridge RPC contracts, actor attribution, idempotency policy, file-asset pointer type, and canonical bridge error enum; gates passed: workspace typecheck, workspace build+test, lint:invariants

title: Add minimal authenticated workspace warmup coordinator
description:
Goal: add the minimal authenticated workspace warmup coordinator/effect that lets the keyed workspace shell render chat while sessions/tree/sandbox/workbench preparation happens off the blocking path.

Context:
- Unified plan uses a simple readiness model: auth/draft gate, workspace identity gate, workbench/agent-ready gate.
- This bead is about browser workspace warmup after a valid authenticated workspace identity, not about .boring-agent runtime provisioning internals.
- Runtime provisioning is server-side and synchronous per /tmp/boring-ui-runtime-provisioning-simplified/...; this warmup may request/observe agent runtime preparation but must not implement package-manager/provisioning logic or expose package install statuses.
- Current WorkspaceBootGate blocks children while preloading /api/v1/tree and /api/v1/agent/sessions. Default composed app should stop blocking chat on those preloads.
- Keep this small: one coordinator/effect keyed by workspace.id. Do not design a configurable preload framework unless the code genuinely needs a helper.
- Depends on boring-ui-v2-reorg-7tvg for canonical WORKSPACE_NOT_READY/substrate readiness vocabulary.

Scope:
- Add WorkspaceBackgroundBoot/startWorkspaceWarmup (name chosen by implementation) in workspace app/front only if useful; otherwise implement the effect directly in WorkspaceAgentFront.
- Start/observe sessions/tree/sandbox preparation after workspace identity is valid.
- Key work by workspace.id and abort/ignore stale work on workspace switch.
- Reuse/extract tree preload cache behavior from WorkspaceBootGate where sensible.
- Expose simple UI readiness for the workbench/composer: preparing | ready | failed plus workspace substrate requirement where available.
- Treat WORKSPACE_NOT_READY from preload endpoints as retryable workspace substrate preparing state, not terminal failure.
- Honor provisionWorkspace === false: do not request server-side runtime preparation and do not cause .boring-agent writes in that mode.
- Keep WorkspaceBootGate behavior intact for callers that still want blocking boot.
- Do not run pre-auth, do not own .boring-agent layout, and do not run frontend package-manager/provisioning work.

Acceptance:
- Coordinator/effect returns null or otherwise never blocks children.
- It starts warmup for the target workspace only after identity is valid.
- Tree/session warmup can be delayed while chat shell remains mounted.
- Old workspace warmup responses are aborted/ignored and cannot seed cache/status for the new workspace.
- Successful tree responses seed setPreloadedTreeEntries exactly like WorkspaceBootGate.
- Retryable WORKSPACE_NOT_READY remains preparing; non-retryable/exhausted failures become local failed status.
- provisionWorkspace === false prevents runtime-preparation request and .boring-agent writes from this path.
- WorkspaceBootGate compatibility test proves it still blocks children until preload success.
- Tests cover success, retryable preparing, failure, abort/stale response, cache seeding, and provisionWorkspace false.

Validation:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/app/front/__tests__/WorkspaceBackgroundBoot.test.tsx
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/app/front/__tests__/WorkspaceAgentFront.test.tsx
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/app/front/__tests__/WorkspaceBootGate.test.tsx

If implementation keeps warmup as an effect instead of a named WorkspaceBackgroundBoot helper, use the WorkspaceAgentFront warmup-effect test file and update this validation command accordingly.
notes: Polish notes: Treat WORKSPACE_NOT_READY from preload endpoints as pending/retryable workspace substrate readiness, not failedRequirement. Background warmup may retry/backoff or observe workspace substrate readiness where available; do not treat /api/v1/ready-status as a runtime provisioning/package status endpoint. Tree preload success clears workspace-fs; tree preload WORKSPACE_NOT_READY keeps workspace-fs pending. Honor provisionWorkspace === false by not requesting server-side runtime preparation or causing .boring-agent writes in that mode. If implementation keeps warmup as an effect instead of a named WorkspaceBackgroundBoot helper, tests may live under WorkspaceAgentFront warmup-effect tests instead of WorkspaceBackgroundBoot.test.tsx.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by Gemini reviewer: SHIP

title: Mirror plugin skills into .boring-agent/skills
description:
Implement plugin skill mirroring into generated workspace state.

Background / intent:
- Skills are docs/context the model may read. Plugin-owned skills must be readable through normal workspace read tools, including in Vercel.
- Package plugins declare skills through package.json#pi.skills. Trusted app/internal server plugins may also expose a small skills field if needed for server-owned skill sources.
- User-owned .agents/skills is separate and must never be overwritten or pruned by provisioning.
- No special boring-skill:// read tool in the first pass.

Scope:
- Add packages/agent/src/server/workspace/provisioning/skills.ts.
- Mirror plugin skills from structural plugin input:
  plugins[].skills[].source -> .boring-agent/skills/<plugin-id>/<skill-name>/...
- Support file and directory sources:
  - file source becomes .boring-agent/skills/<plugin-id>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md
  - directory source copies recursively into .boring-agent/skills/<plugin-id>/<skill-name>/
- Nuke-and-pave only generated .boring-agent/skills during provisioning. Missing old directory is success.
- Do not write to or prune .agents/skills.
- Return skillPaths including:
  - $BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT/.boring-agent/skills
  - $BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT/.agents/skills
- Prompt can reference $BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT/.boring-agent/skills, but this module does not build prompts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests cover file skill source, directory skill source, recursive copy, missing generated dir, removed/renamed skill pruning, duplicate plugin/skill namespace behavior, and failure causing runtime not-ready.
- Tests assert .agents/skills is untouched.
- Tests assert path layout uses plugin id + skill name for stable generated locations.
- Tests assert returned skillPaths include generated plugin skills and user skill root in deterministic order.
- Tests assert /api/v1/agent/skills and chat harness later consume the same returned skillPaths.
close_reason: implemented in worktree; Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP

description:

title: Provision Python SDKs with uv and expose bm-style CLIs
description:
Implement Python/uv runtime package provisioning under .boring-agent/venv.

Background / intent:
- Plugin CLIs such as macro's bm should be available in every runtime mode without user setup.
- Python virtualenv console scripts may bake absolute paths into shebangs, so completed venvs must not be moved.

Scope:
- Add packages/agent/src/server/workspace/provisioning/python.ts.
- Implement ensureUv(adapter, paths): use system uv if present; otherwise install workspace-local uv into .boring-agent/sdk/uv, preferably official standalone binary marked executable.
- Avoid relying on python3 -m venv && pip install uv as primary bootstrap path; many systems lack python3-venv/pip.
- Install all desired Python packages for the runtime in one batch with uv venv and uv pip install.
- Install directly into .boring-agent/venv; do not move completed venvs.
- Build command argument arrays, not interpolated shell strings; package paths may contain spaces.
- PATH result includes .boring-agent/venv/bin and .boring-agent/sdk/uv/bin.
- Support RuntimePythonSpec fields from the plan: id, projectFile, packageName, packageRoot, version, extraLibs, env, expectedBins.
- Include extraLibs in the uv pip install batch after the main package sources.
- Use adapter.resolveInstallSource(sourceRoot, { kind: "python", id, fingerprint }) for local package roots/project roots. The provisioning engine must not call uv build/upload directly.
- Fingerprint includes desired python specs, extraLibs, and python/uv versions; skip only when fingerprint matches and expected bins exist.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests with fake pyproject expose a fake bm bin.
- Tests prove extraLibs are passed to uv pip install and affect the composite fingerprint.
- Tests prove env contributions are returned for harness command env without leaking secrets to logs.
- Tests prove unchanged composite fingerprint + present bin skips install.
- Tests prove missing bin or changed SDK/python/uv fingerprint reinstalls.
- Tests prove downloaded uv is marked executable.
- Tests include package paths with spaces.
- Tests prove venv is installed at final .boring-agent/venv path and no venv tmp move is used.

title: Questions browser-to-server command routes and auth checks
description: Implement askUserPlugin/server/questionsRoutes.ts and questionsBridge.ts for the narrow Questions browser -> server command channel. Routes/handlers must mount through askUserServerPlugin defineServerPlugin routes on the Workspace Fastify app and cover questions.submit, questions.cancel, and questions.opened. This is not the generic UiBridge.postCommand queue; UiBridge remains server/agent -> UI for openSurface/focus only. Commands use shared types QuestionsSubmitCommand, QuestionsCancelCommand, QuestionsOpenedCommand. Handlers must use authenticated request context and CSRF/origin protections for state-changing commands; never trust browser-supplied sessionId alone. Submit/cancel validate questionId, sessionId, answerToken with constant-time comparison, ownerPrincipalId, status, and immutable finalized schema before accepting values. Status transitions: only ready->answered or ready->cancelled resolve waiter. Duplicate same submit may return prior accepted result; submit after cancel returns ASK_USER_ALREADY_CANCELLED; cancel after answer returns ASK_USER_ALREADY_ANSWERED; concurrent submit CAS first-wins and losing tabs render accepted answer from state. questions.opened records per-question open ack for runtime UI availability. Tests must cover auth/session mismatch, bad token, constant-time compare helper, invalid answer payload, duplicate/idempotent submit, cancel after answer, submit after cancel, concurrent submit race, opened ack, and CSRF/origin integration where available.

title: Confirm no generic workspace-files bridge API in v1
description:
Goal:
Guard against building `workspace-files.v1.read/write/list` in v1. Existing deck/file/raw/upload endpoints already cover current product needs.


WorkspaceBridge v1 context to preserve:
- emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- callerClass means transport/source boundary (`browser`, `runtime`, `server`). Actor attribution is separate observability data: `actorKind` (`human`, `agent`, `system`, `service`), redacted `performedBy`, and redacted `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is audit/log context only in v1. Do not complicate rate-limit keys or create a new auth layer from it.
- Browser auth policy derives human attribution; runtime tokens/env derive agent attribution; trusted server calls choose system/service; request bodies cannot spoof attribution.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local; no local dev token.
- No generic `workspace-files.v1.*` bridge API in v1.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Ask-user is a hard cutover to new bridge path; no compatibility window for old server route/export behavior.


Scope:
- Do not implement or register `workspace-files.v1.*` bridge operations.
- Existing `/api/v1/files/*`, raw-file, upload, and Macro deck endpoints remain product-owned and path-validated.
- Large bridge outputs use unified upload/file-asset/raw-file pipeline, not generic bridge file ops.

Acceptance/tests:
- [ ] grep/tests prove no `workspace-files.v1.*` operations registered.
- [ ] existing file/raw/upload endpoint tests remain green.
- [ ] Macro deck endpoints remain product-owned; no bridge mapping added.
- [ ] path validation coverage remains in existing adapter/file/deck tests.

Gate commands:
- rg "workspace-files\.v1" packages docs # no live bridge op registration/docs except explicit guardrail text
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent run test src/server/http/routes
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test
- pnpm typecheck

- [ ] Tests include detailed logs with tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands pass or skips are explicit with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Implement workspace pending-question schemas/runtime/store
description:
Goal:
Move only the generic ask-user coordination backend into workspace-owned pending-question infrastructure. Do not build a broad human-input platform.


WorkspaceBridge v1 context to preserve:
- emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- callerClass means transport/source boundary (`browser`, `runtime`, `server`). Actor attribution is separate observability data: `actorKind` (`human`, `agent`, `system`, `service`), redacted `performedBy`, and redacted `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is audit/log context only in v1. Do not complicate rate-limit keys or create a new auth layer from it.
- Browser auth policy derives human attribution; runtime tokens/env derive agent attribution; trusted server calls choose system/service; request bodies cannot spoof attribution.

title: Define agent workspace readiness source and requirement metadata
description:
Goal: define the small server/runtime readiness source and tool readiness metadata needed to return prompt, stable readiness responses instead of hanging or leaking infrastructure errors.

Context:
- Chat shell may render before the selected workspace's agent runtime is ready.
- Runtime provisioning is server-side and synchronous before declaring agent runtime ready. Browser UI only needs preparing/ready/failed; do not duplicate provisioning package status internals here.
- registerAgentRoutes currently stores runtime binding promises. Runtime-dependent routes can hang if modeAdapter.create() is slow; we need a non-awaiting slot/status model.
- Tool/workspace substrate readiness requirements remain small: workspace-fs, sandbox-exec, ui-bridge.
- Important distinction: WORKSPACE_NOT_READY is only for workspace substrate readiness. Agent runtime/provisioning readiness must use a separate stable readiness/error path.

Scope:
- Add canonical WORKSPACE_NOT_READY to packages/agent/src/shared/error-codes.ts and export through shared barrels.
- Define ToolReadinessRequirement = "workspace-fs" | "sandbox-exec" | "ui-bridge" in shared agent/workspace tool types.
- Add readinessRequirements?: ToolReadinessRequirement[] to AgentTool and workspace structural AgentTool types.
- Track request/workspace runtime binding slots around registerAgentRoutes: creating | ready | failed, with promise/binding/error/readyTracker as needed.
- Add internal peek helpers so routes can respond promptly without always awaiting runtime creation.
- Wire /api/v1/ready-status to request/workspace slot/tracker in request-scoped mode for workspace substrate/runtime-slot readiness; do not turn it into a runtime provisioning package status endpoint.
- Workspace-substrate-dependent routes return stable retryable WORKSPACE_NOT_READY only for workspace-fs/sandbox-exec/ui-bridge readiness.
- If /api/v1/agent/chat cannot start because selected workspace agent runtime/provisioning is still preparing or failed, return a separate stable agent-runtime/provisioning readiness envelope/code and render it as local Preparing agent… or actionable agent-runtime failure. Reuse runtime provisioning stable errors when available; if no shared code exists yet, introduce the minimal canonical code(s) needed (for example AGENT_RUNTIME_NOT_READY for preparing and RUNTIME_PROVISIONING_FAILED/RUNTIME_PROVISIONING_LOCKED for failures). Do not label runtime provisioning as WORKSPACE_NOT_READY.
- Auth/session/model metadata routes that do not need workspace runtime remain available.
- Keep this narrow: no DB schema, no broad lifecycle system, no runtime provisioning package status endpoint.

Acceptance:
- ErrorCode.enum.WORKSPACE_NOT_READY exists and route/tool envelopes use it only for workspace-fs/sandbox-exec/ui-bridge substrate readiness.
- Shared types compile and are exported where needed.
- With delayed or never-resolving request-scoped modeAdapter.create(), runtime-dependent routes respond promptly rather than awaiting forever.
- /api/v1/agent/chat accepts/streams when harness is ready.
- If /api/v1/agent/chat is blocked by workspace substrate readiness, it returns stable retryable WORKSPACE_NOT_READY.
- If /api/v1/agent/chat is blocked by agent runtime/provisioning readiness/failure, it returns a separate stable runtime/provisioning readiness/error envelope and never calls it WORKSPACE_NOT_READY.
- /api/v1/tree, /api/v1/agent/sessions, /api/v1/agent/chat, and one file/search route have creating/ready/failed tests.
- WORKSPACE_NOT_READY from preload/session/tree routes is retryable preparing state for clients.
- /api/v1/ready-status uses request/workspace slot tracker, not singleton always-ready state, and does not pretend to expose runtime package install status.

Validation:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test src/server
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace typecheck
notes: Runtime/provisioning readiness must not be mislabeled as WORKSPACE_NOT_READY. WORKSPACE_NOT_READY is reserved for workspace substrate readiness only: workspace-fs, sandbox-exec, ui-bridge. If runtime provisioning fails, use/reuse stable provisioning errors from the runtime provisioning plan.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by reviewer: SHIP; focused agent route tests pass (full typecheck blocked by unrelated untracked telemetry test)

title: Implement Vercel install-source and workspace transport adapter
description:
Implement Vercel sandbox provisioning adapter behavior.

Background / intent:
- Host plugin source paths are not visible inside remote /workspace. Vercel-specific artifact creation/upload must be hidden behind resolveInstallSource.
- The shared provisioning engine must stay mode-agnostic and never call npm pack, uv build, or upload APIs directly.

Scope:
- Vercel adapter owns host path invisibility, artifact preparation, upload, and runtime-visible install source resolution.
- resolveInstallSource(local node root, { kind: "node", id, fingerprint }):
  - checks .boring-agent/tmp/<id>-<fingerprint>.tgz in workspace
  - if missing, creates node artifact in a non-source temp location and uploads it
  - returns remote workspace path
- resolveInstallSource(local python root, { kind: "python", id, fingerprint }):
  - checks .boring-agent/tmp/<id>-<fingerprint>.tar.gz in workspace
  - if missing, creates Python artifact in a non-source temp location and uploads it
  - returns remote workspace path
- Vercel generated skills/templates must land in /workspace-visible paths through workspaceFs.copyFromHost.
- Vercel should prefer an ephemeral cache root such as /tmp/boring-agent-cache, not synced workspace caches.
- Cold installs are acceptable first pass because installs happen only on fingerprint changes.
- Do not require real Vercel/cloud credentials for unit tests; use fakes for artifact/upload behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests prove host source trees are not polluted with .tgz, dist, or sdist artifacts.
- Tests prove unchanged artifact fingerprint avoids repack/reupload.
- Tests prove skills are visible at /workspace/.boring-agent/skills.
- Tests prove the shared provisioning engine never imports Vercel APIs or calls pack/build/upload directly.
- Tests prove provisioning engine tests can use a fake adapter without Vercel credentials.
- Tests include useful artifact path/log output on failure.
close_reason: implemented in worktree; Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP

title: Integrate pending-question runtime with WorkspaceBridge composition
description:
Goal:
Wire the pure pending-question runtime/store into WorkspaceBridge composition before registering human-input.v1 handlers.


WorkspaceBridge v1 context to preserve:
- emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- callerClass means transport/source boundary (`browser`, `runtime`, `server`). Actor attribution is separate observability data: `actorKind` (`human`, `agent`, `system`, `service`), redacted `performedBy`, and redacted `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is audit/log context only in v1. Do not complicate rate-limit keys or create a new auth layer from it.
- Browser auth policy derives human attribution; runtime tokens/env derive agent attribution; trusted server calls choose system/service; request bodies cannot spoof attribution.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local; no local dev token.
- No generic `workspace-files.v1.*` bridge API in v1.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Ask-user is a hard cutover to new bridge path; no compatibility window for old server route/export behavior.


Scope:
- One injected pending-question runtime/store per workspace/server instance.
- Testable without HTTP route.
- Multiple workspaces do not share pending state.
- No duplicate store in dev/hot reload.
- Core/cloud can inject DB-backed store later; workspace remains DB-free.

Acceptance/tests:
- [ ] in-process composition passes one pending-question runtime to handler factory.
- [ ] no HTTP transport needed for runtime tests.
- [ ] duplicate registration fails clearly or reuses expected instance.
- [ ] logs include actor attribution and redacted ids.

Gate commands:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/humanInput src/server/workspaceBridge
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck


Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation/test/doc changes are scoped to this bead.
- [ ] Tests include detailed logs with tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands pass or skips are explicit with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Ask-user file-backed store with pending state and transcript event log
description: Implement the plugin-owned AskUserStore interface and default file-backed adapter. The store owns pending state and append-only transcript event persistence for askUserPlugin; core/cloud can later inject a DB adapter without changing callers. Interface must include getPending(sessionId), getByQuestionId(questionId), createPending(question), applyPatch(questionId, patch, expectedVersion?), finalize(questionId, submitLabel?, expectedVersion?), answer(questionId, answer), cancel(questionId), markAbandoned(questionId), clearPending(sessionId), appendTranscriptEvent(event), listTranscriptEvents(sessionId), and getTranscriptEventsForQuestion(questionId). Pending invariant: only one draft/ready question per session; second createPending returns ASK_USER_PENDING_EXISTS. Store generates or accepts only server-generated question records containing answerToken, ownerPrincipalId, draftVersion, status, timestamps, draftFields/schema. Apply patch must enforce patchId idempotency (same patchId no-op, no draftVersion advance), expectedVersion CAS, reject stale patches with ASK_USER_PATCH_STALE, reject patches after non-draft status, forbid update_field.patch.type, and emit store change events after every mutation. Finalize must atomically validate assembled schema and flip draft->ready. Answer/cancel must enforce terminal transitions and idempotency rules. Transcript events are append-only created/patched/ready/answered/cancelled/abandoned and replayable. File-backed adapter must be safe for reload survival, write atomically, and stay under plugin server code. Tests must cover one-pending enforcement, patch idempotency, stale patch rejection, atomic finalize, duplicate submit/cancel behavior, reload persistence, transcript append/list/replay, and abandoned handling.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by subagent reviewer after clearPending fix: ship

title: Ask-user coordinator and host-side runtime
description: Implement plugin-owned AskUserCoordinator and AskUserRuntime under askUserPlugin/server. The coordinator has default in-process waiter map keyed by questionId: registerWaiter(questionId, sessionId, signal?), resolveAnswered(questionId, answer), resolveCancelled(questionId, reason). Keep it injectable for future pub/sub-backed coordinators but ship in-process v1. Runtime owns create-ready complete-schema flow and beginAskUserStream draft flow, using AskUserStore, coordinator, answerToken generation, ownerPrincipalId derivation, timeout handling, abort handling, rate limiting, operational events, and UI-open acknowledgement. Important: ask_user runs in the host Pi/runtime process for direct/local/vercel-sandbox; execution modes affect workspace/filesystem isolation, not where ask-user waiter runs. If host ask-user runtime is missing, fail fast with ASK_USER_RUNTIME_UNAVAILABLE/runtime_unavailable, not sandbox transport logic. Runtime must generate cryptographically random answerToken (>=128 bits entropy) server-side at pending creation and never accept it from agent/browser. ownerPrincipalId comes from request/session auth context, with sentinel anonymous for no-auth v1. Rate limits: configurable per-session/per-principal createPending (suggested 6/min/session and 30/hour/principal), returning ASK_USER_RATE_LIMITED/rate_limited. Operational events are no-op-by-default and exclude answer values. Tests must cover answered/cancelled resolution, abort, timeout, runtime unavailable, rate limiting, answerToken generation, anonymous principal fallback, and exactly-once waiter resolution. Add explicit startup abandonment/orphan tests: on server/runtime startup, persisted draft/ready pending questions without rehydrated waiters are marked abandoned; if a waiter is discovered missing during submit/cancel handling, the question is abandoned and any live waiter resolves with reason abandoned exactly once.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: Update direct adapter to use host workspace path as runtime cwd
description:
Goal: make direct mode conform to the unified runtime cwd contract.

In direct mode, there is no sandbox or path alias. The agent-visible file tree root and command cwd are the real host workspace path.

Scope:
- Set `runtimeContext.runtimeCwd = ctx.workspaceRoot` in the direct runtime bundle.
- Ensure Workspace.root and Sandbox.runtimeContext match.
- Ensure exec default cwd is the host workspace path.

Acceptance criteria:
- direct mode `pwd`, `$PWD`, `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT`, and model-visible cwd equal the host workspace path.
- No `/workspace` is introduced in direct mode unless the actual host path is `/workspace`.
- Unit tests cover direct RuntimeBundle and exec defaults.

title: Epic: implement simplified workspace-local runtime provisioning
description:
Goal: implement the simplified workspace-local runtime provisioning system.

Self-contained summary:
- Opening a workspace should be zero setup. The agent should immediately know/use plugin skills, boring-ui scaffold-plugin/verify-plugin, plugin CLIs such as macro's bm, and starter files such as deck/intro.md.
- The agent always operates from BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT. Generated runtime state lives under BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT/.boring-agent.
- First-pass .boring-agent dirs: node, venv, sdk, skills, cache, tmp, plus generated .gitignore containing "*". No state/, logs/, lock dir, or root state file.
- Package plugins keep the regular package.json#pi and package.json#boring namespaces. Do not invent a third public manifest namespace.
- Workspace owns plugin/package discovery and passes structural plugin input to agent. Agent reads only id, skills, provisioning and must not import @hachej/boring-workspace types.
- Agent owns provisionWorkspaceRuntime({ plugins, adapter, runtimeLayout }), skill mirror, workspace file seeding, node/python runtime installs, fingerprints, and adapter interface.
- Runtime mode adapters own command execution, workspace file transport, cache roots, host-vs-runtime path visibility, and Vercel artifact packaging/upload.
- No WorkspaceSetupPlan, RuntimeProvisioningPlugin, async job registry, lock manager, cleanup manager, doctor route, legacy migration, or broad package-manager framework.
- Synchronous provisioning succeeds or throws stable canonical provisioning errors. Future multi-user/cloud locking is deferred to a DB-backed workspace lease outside this first pass.

Implementation order:
1. runtime layout + structural contract
2. fingerprints/skills/workspace files/adapters/node/python modules
3. provisionWorkspaceRuntime orchestration
4. workspace + prompt + CLI + reload integration
5. macro example + errors/logging + e2e + final verification

All child beads are intended to be self-contained; implementation agents should not need the original markdown plan.
close_reason: shipped; all implementation child beads closed and final Gemini epic review: SHIP

title: B2 move generic EmptyPane into front chrome
description:
# Goal
Move generic `EmptyPane` from root panes into `front/chrome/empty-pane/` and keep it separate from the registered empty file panel.

# Background and reasoning
There are two empty concepts. `front/chrome/empty-file-panel/` is the registered panel used when no file renderer matches. `EmptyPane.tsx` is a generic empty state component. Keep both, but put the generic component under chrome ownership.

# Exact moves

- `src/panes/__tests__/EmptyPane.test.tsx` -> `src/front/chrome/empty-pane/__tests__/EmptyPane.test.tsx`

# Scope / non-goals

- Do not merge it into `empty-file-panel`.

# Evidence

title: Add out-of-process WorkspaceBridge HTTP transport
description:
Goal:
    Add POST /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call for callers outside host process while keeping registry as abstraction.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ## Transport shape

A protected backend transport is required for callers outside the host process, especially SDKs/CLIs running in the workspace sandbox. It replaces plugin-owned transport routes for SDK/runtime code; it does not replace all trusted app product APIs.

Important distinction: this transport is **not** required for in-process bridge callers such as the ask-user Pi extension when it has an explicit bridge context. In-process callers can use `bridge.call(...)` directly. The HTTP route exists for out-of-process callers unless a different transport is introduced.

### HTTP endpoint

```http
POST /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer <bridge-token>   # runtime mode only
```

Request:

```json
{
  "op": "macro.v1.series.data",
  "input": { "seriesId": "GDPC1" },
  "idempotencyKey": "optional-for-mutations"
}
```

Response:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "output": {}
}
```

Error response:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "BRIDGE_CAPABILITY_DENIED",
    "message": "capability denied",
    "details": {}
  }
}
```

Transport hardening:

- Accept `POST` only.
- Require `Content-Type: application/json`.
- Reject simple form-compatible content types for mutations.
- Enforce `maxInputBytes` before parsing large bodies where possible.
- Set `Cache-Control: no-store`.
- Runtime bearer calls do not use cookies.
- Browser calls do not use runtime bearer tokens.
- Browser mutation calls require normal app/session auth plus CSRF/origin checks.
- CORS is same-origin/default-deny unless an embedding mode explicitly opens it.
- Do not set permissive `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`.
- Do not allow browser credentials cross-origin.
- Rate limit by workspace, principal/session, plugin/runtime id, caller class, and op.
- Error responses include stable codes but never raw tokens, secrets, stack traces, full payloads, or host filesystem paths.

### Browser bridge mode requirements

- Accept only authenticated same-origin browser requests unless local CLI no-auth policy explicitly says otherwise.
- Require `Content-Type: application/json`.
- Validate `Origin` and/or `Sec-Fetch-Site`.
- Require CSRF token for mutation ops if the app uses cookie auth.
- Enforce workspace membership/role before dispatch.
- Browser callers can only invoke ops whose metadata includes `browser`.
- Browser caller cannot spoof `callerClass`, workspaceId, or sessionId in body.

### Bridge URL derivation

Runtime bridge URL must be environment-aware:

- CLI/local direct mode may use the externally reachable CLI server URL.
- Local/bwrap mode must use a URL reachable from inside the sandbox namespace.
- Vercel sandbox mode must use a public/tunnel/proxy URL, never host-local `localhost`.
- Full-app/core deployments derive the URL from trusted server config, not from untrusted request headers unless explicitly allowlisted.
- Remote runtime bridge URLs must be HTTPS unless explicitly in local/dev mode.
- Runtime tokens must not be sent to plain HTTP endpoints outside localhost/dev.
- If no safe reachable bridge URL is available, runtime SDK bridge capability must be disabled with a stable diagnostic rather than injecting a broken URL.

### Runtime env for subprocess SDKs/CLIs

Inject at execution time:

```bash
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_URL="https://host/api/v1/workspace-bridge/call"
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_TOKEN="..."
BORING_WORKSPACE_ID="..."
BORING_AGENT_SESSION_ID="..."
```

Rules:

- Inject per command/tool/runtime execution.
- Prefer not to inject bridge env for commands that do not need bridge capabilities.
- Do not persist into `.boring-agent/`, generated plugin files, workspace templates, logs, or diagnostics.
- Direct/local/vercel modes must all use the paired workspace+sandbox runtime mapping.
- Remote sandbox gets an externally reachable HTTPS bridge URL, not server-local `localhost`.
- Env-injected tokens are inherited by child processes unless scrubbed. SDKs/tools should avoid passing bridge env to child processes unless needed.

### Pi extension context seam

Ask-user's Pi extension needs a clean bridge path without hidden globals.

V1 decision:

- Ask-user's `human-input.v1.request` blocking wait is supported through an explicit in-process Pi extension bridge context seam.
- If the Pi extension is loaded in a mode where that context is unavailable, the `ask_user` tool must be disabled or return a stable diagnostic rather than falling back to an unbounded blocking HTTP request.
- Env/HTTP bridge client is for subprocess SDKs/CLIs and bounded runtime calls, not for long-running ask-user waits in v1.
- External subprocess blocking human input can be added later via explicit `operationId` + poll/long-poll protocol.

Provide a helper such as:

```ts
createWorkspaceBridgeClient(ctx)
```

It must be explicit, typed, testable, and compatible with hot reload. It must not rely on hidden module globals.

### Phase 4B — Out-of-process HTTP bridge transport

Add `POST /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call` for callers that cannot use the in-process bridge.

This phase is needed for sandbox/runtime SDKs, browser bridge submissions, and future external runtimes. It is not required for the in-process ask-user path.

Acceptance:

- Browser-auth handler call succeeds for browser-allowed op.
- Browser call rejected for runtime-only op.
- Runtime token call succeeds for scoped op.
- Runtime bearer mode cannot call browser-only op unless explicitly allowed.
- Expired/missing-capability tokens rejected.
- CSRF/origin mutation behavior covered where host auth exists.
- Route tests include mutation idempotency behavior.
- Response headers include no-store.
- CORS is default-deny.
- Error responses redact tokens/secrets/host paths.
- Tests prove in-process ask-user/human-input flow does not require this HTTP route.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Browser allowed op succeeds; browser runtime-only denied.
- Runtime token scoped op succeeds; browser-only denied unless explicit.
- Expired/missing capability rejected.
- CSRF/origin/content-type/no-store/CORS default-deny/idempotency covered.
- Error responses redact secrets/host paths/full payloads.
- Test proves in-process ask-user/human-input flow does not require HTTP route.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/workspaceBridge
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Agent provisioning entrypoint accepts structural plugins
description:
Add the minimal agent-owned provisioning input contract and public entrypoint without importing workspace package types.

Background / intent:
- The runtime provisioning engine lives in @hachej/boring-agent because standalone agent/CLI must work without @hachej/boring-workspace.
- Workspace collects plugins and passes them to agent. Agent only reads id, skills, and provisioning structurally.
- This is not a new plugin model and not a named RuntimeProvisioningPlugin abstraction.

Scope:
- In @hachej/boring-agent/server, define/export provisionWorkspaceRuntime(opts):
  provisionWorkspaceRuntime({ plugins, adapter, runtimeLayout }): Promise<WorkspaceProvisioningResult>
- The plugins option is structural:
  Array<{ id: string; skills?: PluginSkillSource[]; provisioning?: RuntimeProvisioningContribution }>
- Export/own only minimal agent provisioning types:
  - PluginSkillSource { name: string; source: string | URL }
  - RuntimeProvisioningContribution { templateDirs?, python?, nodePackages? }
  - RuntimeTemplateContribution { id, path, target? }
  - RuntimePythonSpec { id, projectFile, packageName?, packageRoot?, version?, extraLibs?, env?, expectedBins? }
  - RuntimeNodePackageSpec { id, packageName, packageRoot?, version?, expectedBins? }
  - WorkspaceProvisioningResult { changed, env, pathEntries, skillPaths }
- Export WorkspaceProvisioningAdapter type with:
  - exec(command, args, opts)
  - resolveInstallSource(source, { kind, id, fingerprint })
  - workspaceFs { exists, rm, mkdir, writeText, readText, copyFromHost }
  - getRuntimeCacheRoot()
- workspaceFs.copyFromHost accepts string | URL and recursively copies directories.
- workspaceFs.rm treats missing path as success; workspaceFs.mkdir is recursive.

Constraints:
- @hachej/boring-agent must not import WorkspaceServerPlugin or any @hachej/boring-workspace value/type.
- Do not introduce RuntimeProvisioningPlugin, WorkspaceSetupPlan, or any separate plan lifecycle object.
- Keep package plugin authoring in package.json#pi and package.json#boring; no third public manifest namespace.

Acceptance criteria:
- Type/compile tests prove WorkspaceServerPlugin-like objects are structurally assignable from workspace code without agent importing workspace.
- Grep/invariant test ensures packages/agent does not import @hachej/boring-workspace for provisioning types.
- Tests cover copyFromHost URL input, recursive directory copies, rm missing success, and mkdir recursion through a fake adapter.

title: Render composed workspace chat before preload after identity match
description:
Goal: render the target authenticated workspace chat shell as soon as workspace identity is valid, without waiting for tree/session/sandbox/workbench warmup or server-side agent runtime preparation.

Context:
- The only global blocker after auth is workspace identity safety. We must never show workspace A files/panels/sessions under workspace B.
- Once currentWorkspace.id matches the route target, mount the keyed workspace shell and show chat shell immediately.
- Real Send may still show composer-local Preparing chat/agent/workspace if chat backend or agent runtime is not ready, but the shell/draft should be visible.
- Runtime provisioning is server-side and synchronous before agent runtime ready; this bead should request/observe readiness only through existing server/app seams, not implement provisioning.

Scope:
- Update CoreWorkspaceAgentFront route logic so transition page is only for missing/loading/mismatched workspace identity or terminal identity errors.
- Update WorkspaceAuthProvider/exports as needed to expose minimal route workspace status without breaking useCurrentWorkspace callers.
- Remove blocking WorkspaceBootGate from the default composed route path.
- Mount WorkspaceAgentFront keyed by workspaceId once identity matches.
- Start the warmup coordinator/effect from boring-ui-v2-reorg-31w6 after identity match.
- Preserve custom workspaceRoute/workspaceIdParam behavior.
- Handle terminal route states: not-found, forbidden, switch-failed.

Acceptance:
- Missing/mismatched/loading identity still shows identity fallback/transition.
- 404 renders not-found recovery, 403 renders forbidden recovery, other detail-query errors render switch-failed recovery.
- Once identity matches, WorkspaceAgentFront renders chat shell before /api/v1/tree or /api/v1/agent/sessions resolves.
- Direct initial load and workspace switch both get chat-first behavior after identity match.
- WorkspaceAgentFront remounts on workspaceId change.
- No stale workspace A panels/files/sessions show under workspace B route.
- Non-default route/param configuration is covered in tests.

- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/app/front/__tests__/WorkspaceAgentFront.test.tsx

- Add coverage for a non-default route/param, e.g. /projects/:workspaceSlug, proving loading/mismatch/404/403 recovery and keyed remount use the configured param.
- Do not globally break existing useCurrentWorkspace() callers; add a new hook/state or backwards-compatible provider value.

title: Add workbench-local warmup overlay for unready workspace surfaces
description:
Goal: while authenticated workbench surfaces are preparing, show a workbench-local overlay/static state instead of mounting FileTree/editor/plugin panes against an unready or wrong workspace.

Context:
- Chat shell and restored draft must stay visible and usable.
- Readiness belongs in composer/workbench/tool status, not global top bar or full-app loader.
- Pre-auth public workbench empty state is handled by chat-entry/auth work; this bead is for authenticated workspace surfaces after identity is valid.
- Real FileTree/editor/plugin panes may mount only when their backing state is ready for the current workspace id.

Scope:
- Add a minimal ChatLayout seam such as surfaceOverlay?: ReactNode or surfaceBlocked?: ReactNode.
- Render overlay/static state instead of the workbench PanelSlot while provided; do not mount blocked panel components beneath it.
- Ensure overlay covers/suppresses the whole workbench region, including any left-tab/sidebar plugin surface that would expose workspace state.
- Wire WorkspaceAgentFront warmup/readiness state to this seam.
- Copy maps simply:
  - workspace-fs => Preparing files…
  - sandbox-exec => Waking sandbox…
  - ui-bridge => Connecting workspace UI…
  - generic agent/workspace preparing => Preparing workspace… / Preparing agent…
- Error overlay is workbench-local with retry/reload copy.

Acceptance:
- ChatLayout exposes a minimal overlay/blocking prop.
- Overlay fills only workbench/artifact region and does not cover chat/composer.
- FileTree/editor/plugin/left-tab components do not mount while readiness is preparing/failed for that workspace.
- Overlay disappears and real workbench panels mount when ready.
- WORKSPACE_NOT_READY preparing states are not rendered as fatal errors.
- Tests include registered left-tab/plugin component and assert it does not mount while blocked.

Validation:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/front/layout/__tests__/ChatLayout.test.tsx
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test src/app/front/__tests__/WorkspaceAgentFront.test.tsx
notes:
Polish fixes from bead review:

- WORKSPACE_NOT_READY is retryable pending state, not terminal error overlay. Error overlay is only for non-retryable failures or exhausted retry policy.

title: Ask-user e2e through front + Pi extension + human-input bridge
description:
Goal:
    Prove ask-user works end-to-end through front + Pi extension + human-input bridge with no plugin-owned answer/cancel route in new path.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ### Phase 13 — Ask-user e2e

Update playground/test composition to prefer front+agent+human-input.

Acceptance:

- E2E logs: extension registration, tool call, bridge request, UI effect, pending question, browser answer, waiter resolution.
- Model/tool calls `ask_user`.
- Questions pane opens.
- Browser answer goes through bridge.
- Tool resolves.
- Test asserts `agentTools` path is not used for new path.
- Test asserts no ask-user custom route receives answer/cancel.

## Test matrix

Each phase must include tests with detailed, token-redacted logs. The final security phase aggregates; it must not be the first place core behavior is tested.

| Area | Required tests |
| --- | --- |
| Shared contracts | typecheck front/shared/server; no node imports in shared; no value imports from agent |
| Rename/invariants | no public `postCommand` alias; AGENTS/invariant docs updated; UI effects still work |
| Registry | success, unknown op, duplicate op, schema failure, timeout, input/output size limit |
| Tokens | valid, expired, wrong audience, workspace/session/plugin mismatch, redaction |
| Out-of-process HTTP transport | browser auth, runtime bearer, caller allowlist, CSRF/origin, no-cache, CORS default-deny |
| Idempotency | same payload replay, conflicting replay, missing key, one-shot answer/cancel, concurrent double submit |
| Runtime env | direct/local/vercel env visibility, no persisted tokens, HTTPS/public URL for remote |
| Human input | request, pending, answer, cancel, timeout, abort, duplicate request, nonce checks |
| Ask-user e2e | tool registration, UI opens, browser answers, waiter resolves, no old route hit |
| Workspace files | adapter path validation, traversal rejection, capability denial, write idempotency |
| Macro SDK | route audit, direct/local/vercel smoke, no localhost, stable bridge errors |
| Payload/workspace-cache | oversized rejection, workspace-cache fallback path validation, no host paths |
| Security | cross-workspace/session/plugin denial, token redaction, invariant lint, rate limits |

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - E2E logs extension registration, tool call, bridge request, UI effect, pending question, browser answer, waiter resolution.
- Questions pane opens via emitUiEffect for correct workspace/session.
- Browser answer goes through bridge with nonce.
- Tool resolves typed answer/cancel.
- Assert agentTools path not used and old route not hit.
- Trace/screenshots/log artifacts redacted.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace build && pnpm --filter workspace-playground test
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test
- pnpm typecheck

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Install/link nodePackages into .boring-agent/node and .boring-agent/bin
description:
Goal: make nodePackages[] produce runnable CLI bins in the agent runtime PATH.

Scope:
- Install Node CLI packages into `.boring-agent/node` using npm/pnpm internally.
- Do not build a custom package manager.
- For `packageRoot`, pack local package and install the tarball so remote runtimes work.
- Keep npm/pnpm cache under `.boring-agent/cache/node` when practical.
- Stage package/tarball work under `.boring-agent/tmp`.
- Link bins into `.boring-agent/bin`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `boring-ui --help` can run from PATH after provisioning.
- Works in direct, bwrap, and Vercel matrix tests.
- Remote runtimes do not depend on local packageRoot paths after provisioning.
close_reason: Implemented nodePackages pack/install/link into .boring-agent/node/bin with cache/tmp, single npm install for multiple packages, remote tarball install, bin shims/stale cleanup, fingerprint bin targets, and runnable tests; reviewer verdict ship and validation passed.

title: Pass runtimeCwd through Pi/session cwd mechanism without conflicting prompt lines
description:
Goal: ensure model-visible cwd is the same root as the file tree and shell cwd.

Scope:
- Find all Pi/session cwd call sites.
- Supply `runtimeCwd` to the existing Pi/session cwd mechanism.
- Avoid appending a second conflicting cwd line.
- Ensure the base/current working directory line is correct.
- Keep host/storage writes routed through Workspace/sandbox APIs, not by passing `/workspace` to host filesystem operations.

Acceptance criteria:
- Model-visible cwd equals runtimeCwd in direct, local/bwrap, and Vercel.
- No prompt/session line exposes host path in sandboxed modes.
- Tests cover the existing Pi cwd line behavior.
close_reason: Plumbed runtimeCwd into harness/Pi session cwd while preserving storage cwd for host resources; added prompt/session tests; reviewer verdict ship and focused validation passed.

title: Add runtime doctor and sandbox path leak detection
description:
Goal: make runtime state inspectable and prevent regressions that leak internal paths to the model.

Scope:
- Add runtime doctor/smoke output: runtimeCwd, PATH first entries, BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT, VIRTUAL_ENV, artifact roots, Python status, provisioning version/fingerprint.
- Do not expose secrets.
- Add dev/test-only leak detector for sandboxed modes.
- Fail if host workspace root appears in model-facing prompt/observations for bwrap.
- Fail if `/vercel/sandbox` appears in model-facing prompt/observations for Vercel.

Acceptance criteria:
- Doctor output helps debug cwd/env/provisioning state.
- Leak detector catches deliberate test leaks.
close_reason: Added runtime doctor route/report with cwd/env/PATH/Python/artifact/provisioning diagnostics and test/dev sandbox path leak detector for bwrap host-root and Vercel internal-root model-facing leaks; reviewer verdict ship and validation passed.

title: Make reload rerun provisioning and refresh resources
description:
Ensure /api/v1/agent/reload reruns provisioning consistently.

Background / intent:
- No file watcher in first pass. /reload is the explicit reconciliation trigger.
- Skill/doc changes should be cheap and should not trigger npm/uv installs when runtime fingerprints are unchanged.

Scope:
- Reload reruns plugin scan / future .boring/plugins.json read.
- Reload rereads plugin definitions and package metadata.
- Reload recopies .boring-agent/skills every time.
- Reload seeds only missing workspace files.
- Reload checks runtime fingerprints and expected outputs.
- Reload must not run slow npm/uv install on skill-only changes when runtime fingerprints/expected outputs are unchanged.
- Reload rebuilds system prompt from plugin metadata + runtimeLayout + provisioningResult.
- Reload refreshes Pi/harness resources.
- No file watcher in first pass.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests prove edited skill files appear in .boring-agent/skills after reload without npm/uv install.
- Tests prove runtime package code change causes reprovision/reupload when fingerprint changes.
- Tests prove removed/renamed plugin skills are pruned from generated mirror.
- Tests prove prompt and /api/v1/agent/skills use refreshed skill paths after reload.
- Tests prove user-owned .agents/skills survives reload untouched.
- Tests include detailed logs/counters proving whether npm/uv install ran.
close_reason: implemented in worktree; Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP

title: fresh-eyes: simplify ui-shadcn useToast import
description: Fresh-eye follow-up after boring-ui-v2-reorg-8q24. full-app build warns that src/front/toast is dynamically imported by ui/use-toast but also statically imported elsewhere, so the dynamic import is ineffective and noisy. Replace with a static import and re-run targeted gates.
