title: Future: workspace-installed plugin declarations and reload
description:
Future follow-up: support workspace-installed plugin declarations while preserving the same provisioning architecture.

Background / intent:
- The first runtime provisioning pass focuses on app-bundled/default plugins and CLI defaults.
- The plan explicitly preserves a future path where users can run `boring-ui plugin add @boring/macro` and have that write a durable declaration rather than copying source trees.
- This must remain reloadable and must not introduce a third manifest namespace.

Future scope:
- Add/read `$WORKSPACE/.boring/plugins.json` declarations such as:
  `{ "plugins": [{ "source": "@boring/macro", "version": "0.2.0" }] }`.
- On workspace load and `/api/v1/agent/reload`:
  1. read `.boring/plugins.json`
  2. resolve/install plugin package enough for server/control metadata
  3. scan package.json#pi and package.json#boring metadata
  4. expose resolved plugin as the same structural plugin definition shape
  5. call the same provisionWorkspaceRuntime path for skills/runtime packages/templates
- Do not copy plugin source trees into the workspace during collection.
- Do not add a WorkspaceSetupPlan, plugin provisioning DSL, or root runtime state file.
- If package-manifest-only runtime provisioning is required later, extend package.json#boring deliberately and validate it in the workspace manifest schema.

Future acceptance criteria:
- Tests prove `.boring/plugins.json` declarations survive reload and resolve into structural plugin input.
- Tests prove package skill updates are reread and mirrored after `/reload`.
- Tests prove generated mirrors remain disposable while `.boring/plugins.json` is durable workspace state.
- Tests prove no package source tree is copied into user workspace during collection.
- Tests prove the agent package still does not import workspace package types.

title: Expose child-app chat entry hooks for auth-first and chat-first
description:
Goal: expose the minimal package seams and app-owned example/test path for child apps to implement auth-first or chat-first entry without packages owning the funnel.

Context:
- Unified plan source: packages/core/docs/plans/chat-first-auth-workspace-boot-plan.md.
- Exactly two modes: auth-first (route/page signin before app) and chat-first (public product-shaped shell; first Send opens auth overlay).
- Auth overlay defaults to signin; signup is secondary mode in the same overlay.
- Pre-auth chat is local-only. Auth/session checks are allowed, but no agent/model/private-workspace/session-history calls happen before auth.
- Pre-auth top bar has no workspace dropdown/list/switcher.
- Pre-auth workbench is only a static public explainer; it is not a real workspace and must not mount WorkspaceProvider/FileTree/editor/plugin panels.
- Auth may redirect/reload, so pending draft + returnTo + intended workspace id/context must be saved in a simple child-app-owned sessionStorage blob before auth. Nonce/OAuth-state hardening is optional, not required for v1.
- Post-auth transition is lean: same shell layout, Sign in -> avatar/account menu, draft restored/focused, no auto-send, workspace/agent readiness continues locally.
- Default workspace record creation is handled by boring-ui-v2-reorg-edcw and must not be confused with .boring-agent runtime provisioning.

Scope:
- Add only the minimal chat seams if missing: initialDraft and onBeforeSubmit (or equivalent). Do not add usage thresholds, trigger predicates, or auth policy config to packages.
- Ensure onBeforeSubmit runs before any agent/model/session-history network call and returning false cancels backend submission.
- Ensure normal submit, empty-state suggestions, and slash/skill send paths pass through the same submit interception path.
- Provide an app-owned composition/example or test seam that can mount public chat + static workbench explainer without WorkspaceAuthProvider/private workspace queries.
- Preserve draft through signin, signup, failed auth, overlay close, OAuth/email redirect, and returnTo. Restore/focus draft after auth but never auto-send.
- Do not globally weaken CoreFront/AuthGate defaults.

Acceptance:
- Default auth-first behavior remains unchanged.
- Packages do not force auth-first vs chat-first.
- First Send in chat-first opens signin overlay and does not call /api/v1/agent/chat.
- Top-bar Sign in CTA before sending opens same signin overlay and preserves any draft.
- Closing overlay keeps local draft; later Send reopens signin overlay.
- Public pre-auth shell makes no /api/v1/workspaces, /api/v1/workspaces/:id, /api/v1/agent/*, /api/v1/tree, /api/v1/ui/*, bridge, tool, or backend agent/model calls.
- Public pre-auth /workspace/:id does not reveal workspace name, owner, availability, not-found, or other existence metadata.
- Public workbench shows only static explanatory empty state copy.
- After auth, top bar switches Sign in -> avatar/account menu, draft is restored/focused, and no full-screen app loader or auto-send occurs.
- If chat/agent/workspace is still preparing after auth, composer/workbench shows local preparing status and keeps draft.

Validation:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test src/front
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test src/app/front
- pnpm --filter full-app e2e -- e2e/chat-entry-auth.spec.ts
notes:
Additional required plan details to preserve:
- chat-first public shell is only for app/workspace-shaped routes, not unrelated marketing pages.
- pending chat entry state is a simple child-app-owned sessionStorage JSON blob in v1: draft, returnTo, intendedWorkspaceId, tiny publicContext, createdAt.
- validate returnTo as same-origin/app-relative before using it.
- keep publicContext tiny/public only; v1 only needs prompt/category state if anything.
- do not store attachment data URLs in pending draft state in v1.
- if OAuth/email verification returns on another device/browser or saved sessionStorage state is missing/expired, continue to authenticated default workspace without draft restore and do not treat it as an error.
- pre-auth /workspace/:id must not reveal workspace name, owner, availability, not-found/access-denied, repo, members, or existence metadata.
- tests should cover invalid returnTo sanitization and missing/expired pending draft fallback.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by subagent/reviewer: SHIP

title: Ask-user plugin shared contracts, schema, and error registry
description: Implement the self-contained shared layer for askUserPlugin under packages/workspace/src/plugins/askUserPlugin/shared. This bead owns all browser-safe ask-user contracts from ASK_USER_QUESTIONS_PLUGIN_SPEC.md and must not place ask-user types in generic @boring/workspace/shared barrels. Deliver shared/types.ts, shared/schema.ts, shared/error-codes.ts, and shared/constants.ts. Include AskUserQuestion, AskUserRequest, AskUserToolInput, AskUserFormSchema with wireVersion: 1, AskUserField union including text/textarea/select/multiselect/checkbox/radio/number, AskUserOption, AskUserAnswer, AskUserToolResult, AskUserCancelReason, AskUserFormPatch with patchId, QuestionsSubmitCommand, QuestionsCancelCommand, QuestionsOpenedCommand, transcript event types, plugin ids, surface kinds, command kinds, UI-state slot names, and ASK_USER_SCHEMA_LIMITS. Validation must be Zod or equivalent runtime validation derived from the wire contract, fail fast at tool boundaries, reject unknown wire versions, reject duplicate field names and duplicate option values, enforce field-name regex and reserved-key rejection (__proto__/prototype/constructor), enforce size limits, timeout bounds/defaults, defaultValue correctness, finite number validation, RE2-safe text patterns, and multiselect constraints. Export a plugin-scoped canonical error enum from shared/error-codes.ts, and ensure ask-user code imports codes from that one file, not raw string codes. Tests must cover schema limits, unknown wireVersion, duplicate fields/options, bad defaults, dangerous field names, invalid number values, timeout bounds, pattern safety, and command payload validation. Acceptance: shared code has no node:* imports, no Buffer, no @boring/agent value imports, and generic workspace shared does not import ask-user plugin contracts.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by opus reviewer: ship

title: Wire provisioning result into prompt, Pi resources, and harness
description:
Integrate provisioning result with system prompt, Pi resources, skills API, and harness command env.

Background / intent:
- Provisioning and system prompt building are siblings over runtimeLayout.
- Provisioning guarantees generated files/env/PATH exist. Prompt builder points the model at canonical locations but never mutates files.
- The model should read plugin skills through normal workspace file reads, not host app/server source paths and not a special boring-skill:// tool.

Scope:
- Prompt builder runs after provisionWorkspaceRuntime and may reference canonical paths:
  $BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT/.boring-agent/skills
- Prompt builder must not mutate filesystem.
- registerAgentRoutes/harness receives:
  - cwd = runtimeLayout.workspaceRoot
  - env/PATH from provisioningResult.env/pathEntries
  - pi.additionalSkillPaths += provisioningResult.skillPaths
  - piPackages/extensionPaths unchanged from plugin collection/native Pi loader path
- /api/v1/agent/skills and chat harness must use the same skillPaths.
- Preserve package.json#pi.systemPrompt as prompt-builder input, not provisioning input.
- Do not introduce a new harness resource protocol for plugin skills in this first pass.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests prove prompt references mirrored skill paths, not host app/server source paths.
- Tests prove env/PATH are passed to tool command execution and preserve existing env precedence safely.
- Tests prove /api/v1/agent/skills and chat harness agree on skill paths.
- Tests prove prompt builder cannot create/delete/copy files.
- Tests prove no boring-skill:// read tool is needed for plugin skills.
- Tests include logging/assertion output showing final cwd, skillPaths, and PATH entries on failure.
close_reason: implemented in worktree; Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP

title: C1 move DataCatalog presentation components into front/components
description:
# Goal
Move DataCatalog presentation components from root panes into `front/components/data-catalog/`.

# Background and reasoning
`DataCatalog` and `DataCatalogPane` are generic presentation components. They are not a built-in data plugin because they have no concrete data source. Real catalogs come from `plugins/factories/makeStaticDataPlugin.tsx`. This move puts DataCatalog next to `DataExplorer`, its presentation peer.

# Exact moves
Use `git mv`:
- `src/panes/data-catalog/DataCatalog.tsx` -> `src/front/components/data-catalog/DataCatalog.tsx`
- `src/panes/data-catalog/DataCatalogPane.tsx` -> `src/front/components/data-catalog/DataCatalogPane.tsx`
- `src/panes/data-catalog/__tests__/DataCatalog.test.tsx` -> `src/front/components/data-catalog/__tests__/DataCatalog.test.tsx`
- `src/panes/data-catalog/__tests__/DataCatalogPane.test.tsx` -> `src/front/components/data-catalog/__tests__/DataCatalogPane.test.tsx`

# Exact import updates inside moved files
- `DataCatalog.tsx`: `../../front/lib/utils` or old `../../lib/utils` -> `../../lib/utils`.
- `DataCatalogPane.tsx`: `../../front/dock` -> `../../dock`; `../../front/components/DataExplorer/adapters` -> `../DataExplorer/adapters`; `../../front/components/DataExplorer` -> `../DataExplorer`; dynamic import `../../front/components/DataExplorer/DataExplorer` -> `../DataExplorer/DataExplorer`.
- `DataCatalogPane.test.tsx`: mock path `../../../front/dock` -> `../../../dock`.

# Scope / non-goals
- Do not create a data-catalog plugin.
- Do not change `makeStaticDataPlugin`.
- Do not change DataExplorer behavior.

# Acceptance checklist
- [ ] New folder `src/front/components/data-catalog/` contains both components and tests.
- [ ] No tracked files remain under `src/panes/data-catalog/`.
- [ ] DataCatalog tests pass from new folder.

# Gate commands
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace test -- src/front/components/data-catalog`
- `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace typecheck`

# Evidence
Record moved paths, import update summary, and test/typecheck output.
close_reason: shipped — codex review: ship

title: Question form primitives and validation UI
description: Implement reusable front primitives under askUserPlugin/front/primitives: QuestionFormProvider, QuestionForm, QuestionFields, QuestionField, QuestionSubmitButton, QuestionCancelButton, useQuestionForm, validateQuestionValues(schema, values): QuestionValidationResult, QuestionFieldRendererRegistry, QuestionFormState, and unsupported field renderer. Default renderer supports text, textarea, select, multiselect, checkbox, radio, and number. Primitives must support disabled, draft, submitting, errors, renderer overrides, option descriptions, validation messages, default values, required markers, min/max/pattern, number constraints, multiselect constraints, and unknown future fields with graceful unsupported-field message. Accessibility requirements: ARIA live region for new questions/terminal outcomes, focus first focusable field when ready, focus first invalid field after failed submit, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submit when valid, Escape cancel with dirty confirmation, labels/help/errors connected via aria-describedby/aria-invalid/aria-errormessage, no global focus trap. Preserve user-entered dirty values across draft patches where possible; if a touched field is patched, keep user value and show non-blocking agent-updated hint. Tests must cover each field renderer, validation result shape, unsupported field, accessibility attributes/focus, keyboard shortcuts, dirty preservation during patches, renderer override, and submit disabled until ready/valid.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude after revisions: ship

title: Set env and PATH from runtimeCwd and .boring-agent layout

Add small helper for trusted app/internal domain handlers.

title: B gate verify chrome pane relocation
description:
# Goal
Verify Phase B after `ArtifactSurfacePane` and `EmptyPane` move into `front/chrome/`.

# Expected state
- `src/front/chrome/artifact-surface/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx` exists beside `SurfaceShell.tsx`.
- `src/front/chrome/artifact-surface/__tests__/ArtifactSurfacePane.test.tsx` and `ArtifactSurfacePane.persistence.test.tsx` exist.

# Acceptance checklist
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @boring/workspace test` passes.
- [ ] Public API test passes and exports `ArtifactSurfacePane` plus `EmptyPane`.

title: Agent guidance and ask-user prompt/skill integration
description:
Add ask-user agent guidance owned by askUserPlugin, inspired by pi-ask-user but not depending on or wrapping it. The guidance teaches one LLM-visible ask_user tool, decision handshake (detect boundary, gather evidence, synthesize context, ask one focused question, commit decision, reopen only on new ambiguity), and anti-overasking guardrails. MUST ask only for material decisions not determined by user instruction or repo conventions: user-visible architecture/schema/API/deploy/security blast radius, costly-to-undo migrations/destructive/data-loss/prod changes, unclear requirements that materially change implementation, and preference-dependent tradeoffs with no convention. SHOULD proceed and state assumption when repo docs/tests/conventions decide it. Question budget max one normal, max two if unclear/cancelled. Include mapping examples for radio/select/multiselect/textarea and wireVersion: 1. Ensure plugin contributes prompt/system guidance via the new ask-user Pi extension backed by `human-input.v1.request` and explicit in-process WorkspaceBridge context, not the old server plugin/agentTools path and not generic workspace shared. Tests should snapshot generated prompt/guidance and verify it does not mandate over-asking for trivial/repo-convention decisions.

Bridge-plan reconciliation:
- Guidance must reference the new ask_user Pi extension path from `boring-ui-v2-reorg-vdox`.
- Guidance must not require the old `WorkspaceServerPlugin.agentTools` ask-user composition.

Gate commands:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test
- pnpm typecheck

Definition of done:
- [ ] Prompt/guidance snapshots updated for bridge-backed ask_user.
- [ ] No old server plugin/agentTools requirement remains in guidance.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Add agent-neutral runtime env/context contribution seam
description:
Goal:
    Add generic runtime env/context contribution hooks in @hachej/boring-agent without importing workspace.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ### Phase 6A — Agent-neutral runtime env/context contribution seam

Add generic seams in `@hachej/boring-agent` without importing workspace.

Acceptance:

- Agent exposes generic runtime env/context contribution hooks.
- Standalone agent behavior is unchanged when no contributor is supplied.
- No workspace-specific env names or imports are hardcoded in generic agent layers.
- Tests prove existing direct/local/vercel execution still works without bridge.

### Runtime env for subprocess SDKs/CLIs

Inject at execution time:

```bash
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_URL="https://host/api/v1/workspace-bridge/call"
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_TOKEN="..."
BORING_WORKSPACE_ID="..."
BORING_AGENT_SESSION_ID="..."
```

Rules:

- Inject per command/tool/runtime execution.
- Prefer not to inject bridge env for commands that do not need bridge capabilities.
- Do not persist into `.boring-agent/`, generated plugin files, workspace templates, logs, or diagnostics.
- Direct/local/vercel modes must all use the paired workspace+sandbox runtime mapping.
- Remote sandbox gets an externally reachable HTTPS bridge URL, not server-local `localhost`.
- Env-injected tokens are inherited by child processes unless scrubbed. SDKs/tools should avoid passing bridge env to child processes unless needed.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Existing direct/local/vercel execution tests pass without contributor.
- Test contributor env reaches exec.
- No workspace-specific env names/imports in generic agent layers.
- Standalone agent unchanged.
- Redacted logs show env contribution class/name only.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent run test
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent run typecheck
- pnpm lint:invariants

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: Stable provisioning errors and diagnostic logging
description:
Add stable provisioning errors and concise diagnostic logging for the simplified runtime provisioning flow.

Background / intent:
- The plan intentionally has no async job registry, no provisioning status endpoint, no doctor route, and no lock manager in the first pass.
- Because provisioning is synchronous, startup/reload either succeeds or throws a stable provisioning error. Existing readiness/health may report not-ready while synchronous provisioning runs.
- Failures must be understandable from thrown errors/logs without exposing secrets.

Scope:
- Add/extend canonical error codes in the existing error-code enum/import path for runtime provisioning failures. Do not use raw string error codes at throw sites.
- Cover at least these failure categories:
  - layout/write failure under .boring-agent
  - skill mirror failure
  - workspace template seeding failure
  - node/npm preflight failure
  - npm install failure
  - uv bootstrap/preflight failure
  - uv venv/pip install failure
  - adapter resolveInstallSource/artifact failure
- Add structured logs around each synchronous provisioning phase:
  - layout
  - skill mirror
  - workspace files
  - node packages
  - python packages
  - adapter source resolution/artifact prep
  - reload resource refresh
- Logs should include workspace root, plugin id, package id, runtime kind, fingerprint prefix, and changed/skipped/install decisions where useful, but never secrets or full env dumps.
- Do not add a doctor route, async job table, lock state, or root state file.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests assert thrown provisioning errors carry stable canonical codes.
- Unit tests assert failures include enough context for workspace root, plugin id/package id/runtime kind.
- Tests/log snapshots or logger fakes prove useful phase logs exist and do not include secret-like env values.
- Tests prove readiness/harness start is not declared successful after provisioning failure.
- Verification bead can grep for raw provisioning error-code strings outside the canonical enum/import path.
close_reason: implemented in worktree; Gemini 3.5 Flash review: SHIP

title: Add ask-user Pi extension using in-process WorkspaceBridge context
description:
Goal:
    Add ask-user agent/Pi extension surface so new path no longer requires WorkspaceServerPlugin.agentTools.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ### Pi extension context seam

Ask-user's Pi extension needs a clean bridge path without hidden globals.

V1 decision:

- Ask-user's `human-input.v1.request` blocking wait is supported through an explicit in-process Pi extension bridge context seam.
- If the Pi extension is loaded in a mode where that context is unavailable, the `ask_user` tool must be disabled or return a stable diagnostic rather than falling back to an unbounded blocking HTTP request.
- Env/HTTP bridge client is for subprocess SDKs/CLIs and bounded runtime calls, not for long-running ask-user waits in v1.
- External subprocess blocking human input can be added later via explicit `operationId` + poll/long-poll protocol.

Provide a helper such as:

```ts
createWorkspaceBridgeClient(ctx)
```

It must be explicit, typed, testable, and compatible with hot reload. It must not rely on hidden module globals.

## Ask-user migration

### UI effect targeting and rehydration

Every UI effect must include or derive the target workspace id and session/user scope where relevant.

Rules:

- human-input UI effects are ephemeral hints to open/focus UI;
- human-input store is the source of truth;
- reconnect/page refresh rehydrates through `human-input.v1.pending`, not replayed frontend-only effects;
- effects must not open ask-user UI in the wrong workspace/session/browser context.

### Target package shape

```txt
plugins/ask-user/
  src/front/
  src/agent/
  src/shared/
```

Long-term no required `src/server/` surface.

### New flow

1. Pi extension registers `ask_user`.
2. Tool calls `human-input.v1.request` through explicit in-process bridge context.
3. Workspace pending-question coordinator creates pending question and emits a UI effect.
4. Ask-user front plugin renders the pending form.
5. Browser submits `human-input.v1.answer` through bridge using browser auth and one-shot nonce.
6. Waiting tool resolves with typed result.

### Ask-user migration safety

During compatibility window, old and new paths must share one coordination backend or be mutually exclusive.

Do not allow:

- old `createAskUserServerPlugin()` store and new `human-input` store running independently in the same workspace;
- duplicate `ask_user` tool registration from both `agentTools` and Pi extension;
- browser UI submitting to old routes while agent waits on new bridge runtime.

Preferred compatibility shape:

1. new pending-question coordinator is canonical;
2. old server export delegates to human-input where possible;
3. mixed-mode detection produces clear diagnostics;
4. docs mark old server export deprecated for one release before removal.

### Compatibility window

Do not delete old server path immediately.

Steps:

1. Add workspace-owned human-input bridge path.
2. Keep `@hachej/boring-ask-user/server` export.
3. Make old `createAskUserServerPlugin()` delegate to human-input or make old/new paths mutually exclusive.
4. Migrate playground/full-app to new front+agent path.
5. Stop requiring `WorkspaceServerPlugin.agentTools` for ask-user.
6. Deprecate server export for at least one release.
7. Remove only in a later breaking/minor release after downstream migration.

### Phase 11 — Ask-user agent extension

Add ask-user Pi extension agent surface.

Acceptance:

- Pi extension registers `ask_user`.
- Extension uses explicit in-process bridge context when loaded in host process.
- If context unavailable, tool disabled or returns stable diagnostic; no hidden globals.
- Invalid input fails locally.
- Valid input calls `human-input.v1.request` with request/tool call id.
- Tool cancellation aborts waiter.
- New path does not require `WorkspaceServerPlugin.agentTools`.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Pi extension registers ask_user.
- Explicit typed in-process bridge context; no hidden globals.
- Context unavailable disables tool or stable diagnostic; no unbounded blocking HTTP fallback.
- Invalid input fails locally.
- Valid input calls human-input.v1.request with request/tool/session/schema/title/context.
- Tool cancellation aborts waiter.
- Logs registration/tool/bridge request with answer/token redacted.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/humanInput
- ask-user package test/typecheck command if package exists, else document N/A
- pnpm typecheck

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

Prior-art/replacement note:
- Replaces/adapts prior ask-user Pi extension work from `boring-ui-v2-reorg-97g3` if present. Existing extension factory must be rewired to explicit in-process WorkspaceBridge context and must not depend on the old server plugin/agentTools path.

title: Register human-input.v1 bridge handlers
description:
Goal:
Register human-input.v1.request/answer/cancel/pending/transcript against the pending-question coordinator.


WorkspaceBridge v1 context to preserve:
- emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- callerClass means transport/source boundary (`browser`, `runtime`, `server`). Actor attribution is separate observability data: `actorKind` (`human`, `agent`, `system`, `service`), redacted `performedBy`, and redacted `onBehalfOf`.
- Actor attribution is audit/log context only in v1. Do not complicate rate-limit keys or create a new auth layer from it.
- Browser auth policy derives human attribution; runtime tokens/env derive agent attribution; trusted server calls choose system/service; request bodies cannot spoof attribution.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local; no local dev token.
- No generic `workspace-files.v1.*` bridge API in v1.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Ask-user is a hard cutover to new bridge path; no compatibility window for old server route/export behavior.


Scope:
- request: runtime/server caller, in-process wait only in v1, validates capability/schema, emits UI effect, waits for answer/cancel/timeout, abort abandons/cancels waiter.
- answer/cancel: browser/server mutation, requires workspace/session/question and one-shot nonce; browser cannot answer by questionId alone.
- pending: returns pending question/nonce only for authorized caller.
- transcript: super-admin/debug only; runtime and normal browser denied.
- Logs must distinguish agent requested input and human answered/cancelled.

Acceptance/tests:
- [ ] request emits UI effect and waits.
- [ ] nonce missing/wrong/reused/wrong action/session/question rejected.
- [ ] answered/user_cancelled/timed_out/tool_aborted/server_restart mappings pinned.
- [ ] transcript access policy tested.
- [ ] callerClass, actorKind, performedBy/onBehalfOf logged redacted.

Gate commands:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/humanInput src/server/workspaceBridge
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck


Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation/test/doc changes are scoped to this bead.
- [ ] Tests include detailed logs with tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands pass or skips are explicit with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.

title: PostHog telemetry v1: env-only opt-in from core
description:
Umbrella bead for PR 65 PostHog telemetry v1. Purpose: make telemetry boring to enable for core-composed boring-ui apps while keeping implementation simple, privacy-safe, and package boundaries intact. Common user path is env only:

BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true
POSTHOG_KEY=phc_...
POSTHOG_HOST=https://us.i.posthog.com        # optional
BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT=full-app           # optional shared-account prefix

If telemetry is not wanted, users do nothing or set BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false. Telemetry is explicit opt-in: POSTHOG_KEY alone must not enable telemetry. Core owns createPostHogTelemetryFromEnv(); child apps do not write PostHog setup code in the common path. Core may pass a tiny structural TelemetrySink into agent/workspace composition so those packages remain PostHog-free.

V1 event scope is intentionally small: core app.opened/server.request.failed; workspace workspace.opened/panel.opened/command.executed; agent chat started/message submitted/completed/failed and tool completed/failed. Privacy policy: no prompts, assistant output, file contents, command strings, stdout/stderr, raw paths, stack traces, headers/cookies/tokens, env dumps, or secrets. Shared PostHog account/project support uses BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT as both event prefix and boringProject property.

Non-goals/deferred: Sentry, OTLP, billing/metering, per-tenant or runtime multi-account routing, analytics database, content capture, complex consent, browser-originated telemetry, auth hook telemetry, plugin-error telemetry, UI-command telemetry, tool-started events, direct browser PostHog, and mandatory lifecycle flush wiring. Deferred follow-up beads preserve those ideas without blocking v1.
acceptance_criteria:
- Core-composed apps can enable PostHog with env vars only; no child-app setup code on the common path.
- Telemetry is off by default; POSTHOG_KEY without BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true is no-op.
- BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT prefixes event names and is also sent as boringProject; raw eventName is also preserved.
- Agent/workspace packages remain free of PostHog imports and do not depend on core for telemetry.
- Central sanitization prevents content or secret-bearing data from being captured by default.
- Unit tests cover contract/no-op/safe capture/helper behavior; integration tests cover core wiring/event emission; e2e/smoke tests cover env-only enable/disable and prefixing with detailed redacted logs.
- V1 scope stays simple: no browser telemetry endpoint/runtime config, no Sentry/OTLP, no tenant routing.
notes:
Polish status:
- Beads embed the PR 65 plan context and should be implementable without reopening the markdown plan.
- V1 dependency chain is x0ek.1 -> x0ek.2 -> x0ek.3 -> x0ek.4 -> x0ek.6 -> x0ek.5 -> x0ek.
- Deferred follow-up beads preserve omitted plan ideas without expanding v1: boring-ui-v2-reorg-xot2 and boring-ui-v2-reorg-3odt.

Final validation expected before closing umbrella:
- Focused unit tests from x0ek.1/x0ek.2.
- Core wiring tests from x0ek.3.
- Event/privacy tests from x0ek.4.
- E2E/smoke from x0ek.6 with detailed redacted logging.
- Docs from x0ek.5 match implementation.
- pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm lint:invariants, or scoped equivalents with explicit rationale if full gates are too broad.
close_reason: shipped — final reviewer: SHIP; all child telemetry beads reviewed/closed and focused validation passed

title: Telemetry contract and no-op sink
description:
Create the tiny structural telemetry seam used by core, agent, and workspace. This is not a provider framework; it exists only so core can pass a PostHog-backed sink into composed packages without agent/workspace importing PostHog or core. Define a compatible TelemetrySink shape: capture(event): void | Promise<void>, optional flush(): void | Promise<void>, and TelemetryEvent { name: string, distinctId?: string, properties?: Record<string, unknown> }. Provide noopTelemetry and safeCapture(). safeCapture must handle both synchronous throws and rejected promises so telemetry can never break product behavior.

Design guidance: choose the smallest placement that avoids dependency tangles. It is acceptable for packages to use structurally compatible local/shared types rather than forcing agent to depend on core. Keep shared-layer invariants: no node:* imports and no Buffer in src/shared/**. No PostHog types or imports appear in this bead. This bead should not add event emitters or env parsing; it only creates the seam and tests it.

Likely tests: focused Vitest tests for noop capture, normal capture call, sync-throw capture, async-reject capture, and optional flush type/behavior if a helper wraps it. Include clear assertion messages/log output so CI failures identify which safety case failed.
acceptance_criteria:
- TelemetrySink, TelemetryEvent, noopTelemetry, and safeCapture exist where needed by core/agent/workspace without introducing PostHog imports outside core server code.
- safeCapture catches sync throws and rejected promises from capture().
- Optional flush(): void | Promise<void> is represented for sinks that need shutdown, but no broad lifecycle work is added in this bead.
- Unit tests cover noop, normal capture, sync throw, async rejection, and optional flush shape/compatibility where implemented.
- Test output/assertion names clearly distinguish noop, sync failure, async rejection, and success cases.
- Invariant checks remain clean: no node:* imports or Buffer in src/shared/**.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <telemetry contract/noop test path>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test <telemetry contract/noop test path if agent-local type exists>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test <telemetry contract/noop test path if workspace-local type exists>
- pnpm lint:invariants

Implementation hints:
- Prefer one tiny shared contract per package or an existing neutral shared location; do not make agent depend on core.
- safeCapture should use Promise.resolve(telemetry.capture(event)).catch(() => {}) inside try/catch.
- Tests should intentionally make capture throw synchronously and return Promise.reject asynchronously.
- Do not introduce PostHog, env parsing, event emitters, runtime config, or frontend endpoints in this bead.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP; focused telemetry tests and lint:invariants passed

title: Core PostHog env helper
description:
Implement the core-owned PostHog integration for v1 in packages/core/src/server/telemetry/posthog.ts. The helper is createPostHogTelemetryFromEnv(env = process.env). It is explicit opt-in: BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED must equal true and POSTHOG_KEY must be present. If telemetry is disabled, unset, or missing the key, return noopTelemetry; POSTHOG_KEY alone must not enable telemetry. Support POSTHOG_HOST defaulting to PostHog Cloud US and BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT as a safe slug used both to prefix event names and to populate boringProject.

Important privacy requirement: implement central sanitizeTelemetryProperties() before data reaches PostHog. Allowed default property keys are only workspaceId, sessionId, requestId, runtimeMode, modelProvider, toolName, panelId, commandId, status, durationMs, errorCode, packageName, and packageVersion. Drop unknown keys so a mistaken emitter cannot leak prompts, assistant output, file contents, command strings/output, raw paths, headers, cookies, tokens, stack traces, env vars, or secrets. Use a safe auth/user id as distinctId only if already available to callers; do not send emails by default.

Project prefix rule: if BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT=full-app, PostHog event names become full-app.<event.name>; properties include boringProject: full-app and eventName: <raw event.name>. If project is unset, send raw event names. parseTelemetryProject() must accept only a safe slug such as full-app or customer-portal; invalid values safely disable prefixing or warn once.

Tests must mock/fake PostHog; do not call the network or require real credentials. Logs/assertion names should show redacted env state, enabled/disabled branch, event name, project prefix, and sanitized property keys.
acceptance_criteria:
- createPostHogTelemetryFromEnv() lives in packages/core/src/server/telemetry/posthog.ts.
- BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED unset or false returns noopTelemetry.
- POSTHOG_KEY without BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true returns noopTelemetry.
- BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true with missing POSTHOG_KEY returns noopTelemetry and may warn safely without printing secrets.
- BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true with POSTHOG_KEY captures to mocked/fake PostHog.
- POSTHOG_HOST override is passed to PostHog; otherwise default host is used.
- BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT accepts only a safe event-prefix slug; invalid values safely disable prefixing or warn.
- Captured PostHog event name is `${project}.${event.name}` when project is set, else raw event.name; properties include boringProject and eventName.
- sanitizeTelemetryProperties() keeps only the planned low-cardinality metadata and drops unsafe/unknown keys.
- Unit tests cover disabled, missing key, POSTHOG_KEY-alone no-op, enabled, host override, prefix, invalid prefix, property sanitization, no real network, and no secret leakage in warnings/logs.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <posthog telemetry helper test path>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core typecheck

Implementation hints:
- Mock the PostHog constructor/client; no real network and no real secrets.
- Redacted test logging may print: telemetry enabled boolean, host used, event names, sanitized property keys, captured count.
- Redacted test logging must not print: POSTHOG_KEY, full env, prompt/content samples, raw errors/stacks, headers/cookies/tokens.
- parseTelemetryProject should reject empty/unsafe values; safe example slugs: full-app, customer-portal.
- sanitizeTelemetryProperties is a security boundary, not cosmetic cleanup; default-deny unknown keys.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP; core PostHog helper tests and typecheck passed

title: Wire telemetry through core-composed server
description:
Wire the resolved telemetry sink through core-composed server entrypoints. Core should resolve telemetry as options.telemetry ?? createPostHogTelemetryFromEnv(process.env), then pass that sink into composed workspace/agent server pieces that need it. Child apps should not need to call the helper in normal core-composed usage; env vars are enough. A custom telemetry option remains useful for tests and advanced composition, but do not market/build a provider framework.

Keep scope tight and surgical. Do not add runtime config, browser telemetry endpoint, frontend HTTP sink, Sentry/OTLP, tenant routing, or provider routing. Do not move unrelated app setup. If an existing core close hook makes it a tiny change, call optional telemetry.flush() on shutdown; otherwise leave lifecycle flush wiring deferred as the plan says.

Tests should exercise the core-composed path rather than only the helper: default env helper is used when no telemetry option is supplied; supplied telemetry overrides env helper; disabled env produces no captured events. Use a test sink/fake helper and clear diagnostic logging for which sink was selected, without printing secrets.
acceptance_criteria:
- Core-composed server entrypoint resolves options.telemetry ?? createPostHogTelemetryFromEnv(process.env).
- Child app common path is env-only; no app-level PostHog setup code is required.
- A custom telemetry option still overrides env helper for tests/advanced composition.
- Resolved sink is passed into composed agent/workspace server code only through the structural TelemetrySink seam.
- No PostHog imports are introduced in agent/workspace.
- No frontend runtime config, browser endpoint, frontend HTTP sink, Sentry/OTLP, tenant routing, or provider-routing abstraction is added in v1.
- Tests cover env helper being used by default, custom telemetry override, disabled env no-op behavior through the core-composed entrypoint, and redacted diagnostic logging for sink selection.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <core-composed server telemetry wiring test path>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core typecheck
- pnpm lint:invariants

Implementation hints:
- Keep changes surgical in the core-composed app/server factory.
- Resolve telemetry once and pass the same sink down; avoid hidden global/singleton state.
- Do not require child apps to import the helper for the common path.
- Do not add frontend runtime config or telemetry HTTP routes in v1.
- If flush lifecycle wiring is not obviously one small existing hook, leave it for the deferred lifecycle bead.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP; focused wiring test passed; agent/workspace typechecks and lint:invariants passed; core typecheck blocked by unrelated dirty WorkspaceAuthProvider test changes

title: Emit minimal safe telemetry events
description:
Add the v1 event emitters with the smallest useful event set that is reachable from server/core-composed code without adding browser telemetry infrastructure. Emit core app.opened and server.request.failed; agent agent.chat.started, agent.chat.message.submitted, agent.chat.completed, agent.chat.failed, agent.tool.completed, and agent.tool.failed. Workspace opened/panel/command telemetry is still desired, but exact events are browser/frontend facts and are deferred to the browser-originated telemetry bridge bead unless a zero-touch server hook already exists. Do not add runtime config, frontend HTTP sink, or a browser telemetry endpoint in this bead.

Use only allowed low-cardinality properties: workspaceId, sessionId, requestId, runtimeMode, modelProvider, toolName, panelId, commandId, status, durationMs, errorCode, packageName, packageVersion. Identity may use a safe auth/user id as distinctId when already available, otherwise anonymous. Do not capture prompts, assistant output, file contents, command strings, stdout/stderr, raw paths, stack traces, raw errors, headers, cookies, tokens, or env vars. All event properties should pass through central sanitization before PostHog. Avoid hot-path awaits and swallow/debug-log telemetry failures so product behavior is unchanged.

Natural hook rule: add emit calls where the code already knows the event happened. Do not broad-refactor just to create telemetry hooks. Deferred unless zero-touch: auth hook telemetry, browser/frontend events, workspace panel/command events, UI-command events, plugin-error events, and tool-started events.

Tests should use in-memory test sinks and clear event logs. Cover representative core and agent paths; telemetry sink failure; and privacy redaction. Test names/logs should print event names and sanitized keys only, never content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Core emits app.opened and server.request.failed at natural existing server hook points without broad refactors.
- Agent emits agent.chat.started, agent.chat.message.submitted, agent.chat.completed, agent.chat.failed, agent.tool.completed, and agent.tool.failed at natural existing route/harness hook points.
- Workspace opened/panel/command events are either implemented only if a zero-touch server hook exists or explicitly left to the deferred browser telemetry bead; no runtime config, frontend endpoint, or frontend HTTP sink is added in v1.
- Deferred events remain deferred unless zero-touch: auth hook telemetry, browser/frontend events, UI-command events, plugin-error events, and tool-started events.
- Event properties are restricted to the allowlist and pass through central sanitization before PostHog.
- Unit/integration tests assert expected events are emitted for representative core app/server failure and agent chat/tool completed/failed paths.
- Privacy tests prove prompt text, assistant output, file contents, command output, command strings, raw paths, stack traces/raw errors, headers/cookies/tokens, and env vars are not emitted.
- Telemetry failures do not change product behavior or break streaming paths.
- Tests/logging identify event names and sanitized property keys without printing secret/content values.
- Package boundary invariant holds: workspace base front/shared code has no value imports from @hachej/boring-agent and agent/workspace have no PostHog imports.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <core telemetry event tests>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test <workspace telemetry event tests>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test <agent telemetry event tests>
- pnpm lint:invariants

Implementation hints:
- Use in-memory test sinks that collect event names, distinctId, and sanitized property keys.
- Emit only at natural existing hooks. If a hook is not natural, document why the event is deferred instead of refactoring broadly.
- For chat/tool tests, include fake prompt/content/command/stdout/stderr/raw path values and assert none appear in emitted event payloads or logs.
- For failure tests, include raw Error objects/stacks and assert only stable errorCode/status-style metadata is emitted.
- Avoid awaiting telemetry in streaming/hot paths; verify sink rejection does not fail the user path.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP after fixes; focused core/agent telemetry tests, agent typecheck, and lint:invariants passed

title: Document PostHog telemetry env setup
description:
Document the v1 PostHog telemetry behavior after implementation. Keep docs aligned with the simplified scope: core owns the helper, child apps enable telemetry with env vars only, telemetry is off by default, POSTHOG_KEY alone is not enough, BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT distinguishes several apps sharing the same PostHog account/project, agent/workspace stay PostHog-free, and browser-originated telemetry is deferred. Include the event list, allowed properties, privacy exclusions, and validation/e2e smoke command so users and future agents know what is expected.

Docs should explain the common path in copy-pasteable env blocks, plus disabled behavior. Include a shared PostHog account example: BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT=full-app produces full-app.agent.chat.started while properties include boringProject and eventName. Do not reintroduce Sentry, OTLP, runtime routing, tenant routing, browser endpoint setup, direct browser PostHog setup, or content capture in docs.

Docs should be updated only after implementation and tests land, so they match reality rather than the old plan.
acceptance_criteria:
- Docs show env-only enablement: BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true, POSTHOG_KEY, optional POSTHOG_HOST, optional BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT.
- Docs explicitly say telemetry is off by default and POSTHOG_KEY alone does not enable it.
- Docs explain shared PostHog account/project usage with prefixed event names and boringProject/eventName properties.
- Docs list initial event names and allowed properties.
- Docs list privacy exclusions: no prompts, assistant output, file contents, command strings/output, raw paths, stack traces/raw errors, headers/cookies/tokens, or env vars.
- Docs mention deferred scope: browser-originated telemetry, auth hook telemetry, plugin-error telemetry, UI-command telemetry, tool-started events, direct browser PostHog, and mandatory lifecycle flush wiring.
- Docs include the focused unit/integration/e2e smoke validation commands added by implementation beads.
- Docs match actual implementation and tests.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <telemetry helper + smoke paths>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test <telemetry event paths if touched>
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test <telemetry event paths if touched>
- pnpm typecheck

Documentation placement guidance:
- Prefer the canonical core docs and app composition docs already used for env/config behavior.
- Include a short copy-paste env block and a short shared PostHog account example.
- Link or mention deferred beads/features only as deferred; do not document browser telemetry as shipped.
- Docs should describe tested behavior, not aspirational plan text.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP; docs match implementation and focused telemetry validations passed

title: Telemetry v1 e2e smoke and logging
description: Add an end-to-end style telemetry smoke test for the complete v1 path after the contract, PostHog helper, core wiring, and minimal event emitters exist. Purpose: prove a core-composed app can be configured by env vars only, emit safe events through the resolved sink, apply BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT prefixing, and remain silent when disabled. Keep this small and deterministic: use a fake/mock PostHog transport/client or local test double, not the real PostHog network. The test should have detailed diagnostic logging for env state (redacted), event names, distinctId shape, sanitized property keys, and captured event count, without printing secrets or raw payload values that might contain content. This bead is validation only; do not add browser telemetry runtime config, a frontend endpoint, or external SaaS calls.
acceptance_criteria:
- E2E/smoke test boots or exercises the core-composed telemetry path with env-only setup: BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true, POSTHOG_KEY set to a fake value, optional POSTHOG_HOST, and BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT.
- Test uses a fake/mock PostHog client or transport; it must not call real PostHog or require network/secrets.
- Enabled run captures expected representative events with prefixed event names and boringProject/eventName properties.
- Disabled run with BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED unset or false captures zero events, even if POSTHOG_KEY is present.
- Sanitization assertions prove unsafe keys/content are dropped before capture.
- Logs are detailed enough for CI debugging: redacted env summary, captured event count, event names, and sanitized property keys. Logs must not print POSTHOG_KEY, prompts, assistant output, file contents, command output, raw paths, headers/cookies/tokens, stack traces, or env dumps.
- Validation command is documented in the bead close comment, e.g. pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <telemetry e2e/smoke test path> plus relevant package tests if event emitters span packages.
notes:
Validation commands for implementer:
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core test <telemetry e2e/smoke test path>
- If the smoke spans package event emitters, also run the focused package tests from x0ek.4.

E2E/smoke shape:
- Use a fake PostHog client/transport installed through dependency injection or module mock.
- Scenario A: BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true, fake POSTHOG_KEY, BORING_TELEMETRY_PROJECT=full-app => captured events are prefixed full-app.* and include boringProject/eventName.
- Scenario B: BORING_TELEMETRY_ENABLED unset/false with POSTHOG_KEY present => zero captures.
- Scenario C: unsafe sample properties/content are supplied => captured event contains only allowlisted keys.
- Logs: print redacted env summary, captured count, event names, sanitized property keys. Never print key/content/raw paths/headers/tokens/stacks/env dump.
close_reason: shipped — reviewer: SHIP; telemetry smoke test and focused telemetry tests passed

title: Future: browser-originated telemetry bridge
description: Deferred follow-up intentionally excluded from PostHog telemetry v1 to preserve simplicity. If browser-originated telemetry becomes necessary later, add non-secret runtime config such as { telemetry: { enabled, endpoint } }, an authenticated telemetry endpoint, a small frontend HTTP sink, endpoint body limits, known-event validation, and central property sanitization before forwarding to the server PostHog sink. This is where exact workspace.opened, workspace.panel.opened, workspace.command.executed, browser/frontend error telemetry, and other browser facts should land. Do not expose POSTHOG_KEY/VITE_POSTHOG_KEY as the default path unless a later design explicitly chooses direct browser PostHog. This bead exists so the idea from the plan is not lost, but it must not block v1 env-only server telemetry.
acceptance_criteria:
- Non-secret runtime config communicates telemetry enabled/endpoint without exposing PostHog secrets.
- Browser HTTP sink sends only known safe event names to an authenticated core endpoint.
- Endpoint enforces body size, event allowlist, auth, and central property sanitization.
- Workspace frontend events include exact workspace.opened, workspace.panel.opened, and workspace.command.executed from natural frontend hooks.
- Disabled telemetry causes no browser POSTs and no user-visible failures.
- E2E/browser tests include detailed redacted logging and verify no prompts/content/secrets are sent.
- Docs explain why this is separate from v1.

title: Implement in-process WorkspaceBridge registry and call dispatch
description:
Goal:
    Implement the trusted in-process call/registerHandler registry. Registry is the abstraction used by server/internal and Pi extension paths.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ## Transport versus abstraction

WorkspaceBridge is the abstraction. The backend route is only one transport.

| Caller | Transport | Expected frontend involvement |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Server/internal code | Direct in-process function call | None unless handler emits UI effect |
| Pi extension in host process | Explicit bridge context seam; direct in-process bridge client | None unless handler emits UI effect |
| SDK/CLI in direct/local/remote runtime | Protected backend route | None unless handler emits UI effect |
| Browser UI | Backend route with browser auth/CSRF | The browser is the caller |
| Iframe plugin | Future postMessage bridge to host, then bridge call | Iframe only; shell UI effects still use `emitUiEffect` |

Most bridge calls should not route to the frontend. For example, `macro.v1.series.data` is SDK/runtime → host service → response. The frontend is not involved.

Some bridge calls intentionally create UI work. For example, `human-input.v1.request` is agent/runtime → host pending-question coordinator → `emitUiEffect` opens the Questions UI → browser answers via `human-input.v1.answer` → waiting tool resolves.

## Operation registry

All bridge operations are registered capabilities. Operation names are versioned from day one.

Examples:

```txt
human-input.v1.request
human-input.v1.answer
human-input.v1.cancel
human-input.v1.pending
human-input.v1.transcript
macro.v1.catalog.search
macro.v1.series.data
macro.v1.transform.persist
```

Every operation declares:

```ts
interface WorkspaceBridgeOperationDefinition {
  op: string
  version: number
  owner: string
  callerClassesAllowed: Array<'browser' | 'runtime' | 'server'>
  requiredCapabilities: string[]
  resourceScopeSchema?: unknown
  inputSchema: unknown
  outputSchema?: unknown
  timeoutMs: number
  maxInputBytes: number
  maxOutputBytes: number
  idempotencyPolicy: 'none' | 'required' | 'request-id' | 'tool-call-id'
  auditCategory: string
}
```

Rules:

- Unknown ops return `BRIDGE_OP_NOT_FOUND`.
- Duplicate op registration is rejected unless explicitly replacing the same owner in a controlled test/dev path.
- Browser callers cannot call runtime-only ops even if authenticated.
- Runtime tokens derive workspace/session/plugin identity from token claims, not request body.
- If body-provided workspace/session conflicts with auth/token context, reject.

### Phase 2 — In-process bridge registry

Add server-side registry/dispatch for trusted in-process calls.

Acceptance:

- Register/call demo op.
- Unknown op rejected.
- Duplicate op rejected.
- callerClassesAllowed validated for server/runtime/browser contexts.
- Missing capability rejected.
- Input and output schemas validated.
- `maxInputBytes` and `maxOutputBytes` enforced.
- Timeout enforced.
- Handler-triggered UI effects call `emitUiEffect`, not an RPC shortcut.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Tests cover demo success, unknown op, duplicate op, caller denied, missing capability/resource scope, schema invalid, timeout, input/output size, handler failure redaction.
- Handler-triggered UI effects call emitUiEffect, never RPC shortcut.
- Logs include op/caller/workspace/request IDs with secrets redacted.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server/workspaceBridge
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck
- pnpm lint:invariants

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.
close_reason: shipped — Gemini review: SHIP; implemented in-process WorkspaceBridge registerHandler/call registry with auth/capability/schema/size/timeout checks, emitUiEffect passthrough, and redacted failure logging; gates passed: workspaceBridge tests, workspace typecheck, lint:invariants

title: Inject WorkspaceBridge env from workspace/core into direct/local/vercel runtimes
description:
Goal:
    Use the generic agent seam to inject BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_* from workspace/core composition only when runtime SDK bridge access is needed.

    WorkspaceBridge v1 global context every worker must preserve:
- Two lanes: WorkspaceBridge.emitUiEffect is UI side effect only; WorkspaceBridge.call/registerHandler is bounded host capability RPC.
- Hard rename postCommand/postUiCommand canonical API to emitUiEffect; no public compatibility alias.
- HTTP /api/v1/workspace-bridge/call is only an out-of-process transport, not the abstraction and not an arbitrary API proxy.
- Browser callers use BridgeAuthPolicy/app auth/CSRF/origin; runtime/sandbox SDK callers use scoped short-lived bearer tokens; server/internal callers use trusted in-process context.
- Capabilities are host-owned, default-deny, versioned, and resource-scoped. Plugins never self-grant.
- @hachej/boring-agent stays workspace-neutral; workspace/core inject bridge env/context through generic seams. Workspace/core stay DB-free through injected stores.
- Generated/runtime plugin RPC manifest is deferred from v1. Guardrail remains: generated/runtime plugins should not add custom Fastify routes or host-process handlers.
- Large bridge outputs reuse existing upload/file-asset/raw-file mechanisms (`/api/v1/files/upload`, `/api/v1/files/raw`, Workspace binary write/read helpers), not a separate artifact/cache service.
- Local CLI/no-auth remains trusted-local/lightweight; no separate local dev token in v1.
- human-input transcript is super-admin/debug only; runtime tokens and normal browser users cannot read it.
- Tests must log enough detail to debug failures while redacting tokens, answers, file contents, host paths, and full payloads.

    Plan excerpts and requirements embedded for this bead:
    ### Bridge URL derivation

Runtime bridge URL must be environment-aware:

- CLI/local direct mode may use the externally reachable CLI server URL.
- Local/bwrap mode must use a URL reachable from inside the sandbox namespace.
- Vercel sandbox mode must use a public/tunnel/proxy URL, never host-local `localhost`.
- Full-app/core deployments derive the URL from trusted server config, not from untrusted request headers unless explicitly allowlisted.
- Remote runtime bridge URLs must be HTTPS unless explicitly in local/dev mode.
- Runtime tokens must not be sent to plain HTTP endpoints outside localhost/dev.
- If no safe reachable bridge URL is available, runtime SDK bridge capability must be disabled with a stable diagnostic rather than injecting a broken URL.

### Runtime env for subprocess SDKs/CLIs

Inject at execution time:

```bash
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_URL="https://host/api/v1/workspace-bridge/call"
BORING_WORKSPACE_BRIDGE_TOKEN="..."
BORING_WORKSPACE_ID="..."
BORING_AGENT_SESSION_ID="..."
```

Rules:

- Inject per command/tool/runtime execution.
- Prefer not to inject bridge env for commands that do not need bridge capabilities.
- Do not persist into `.boring-agent/`, generated plugin files, workspace templates, logs, or diagnostics.
- Direct/local/vercel modes must all use the paired workspace+sandbox runtime mapping.
- Remote sandbox gets an externally reachable HTTPS bridge URL, not server-local `localhost`.
- Env-injected tokens are inherited by child processes unless scrubbed. SDKs/tools should avoid passing bridge env to child processes unless needed.

### Phase 6B — Workspace bridge env injection

Inject bridge env into direct/local/vercel runtime execution from workspace/core composition.

Acceptance:

- Direct exec sees bridge env when capability requires it.
- Local/bwrap exec sees bridge env when capability requires it.
- Vercel sandbox exec sees public HTTPS/reachable bridge URL or stable disabled diagnostic.
- Commands/tools that do not need bridge access do not receive token where feasible.
- Token not written to `.boring-agent/`, workspace files, provisioning output, logs, or diagnostics.
- Tests log redacted token presence, not token value.

    Scope / non-goals:
    - Preserve all plan behavior relevant to this bead; do not broaden into unrelated refactors.
- Do not weaken security, package-boundary, or test requirements to simplify implementation.

    Acceptance / tests:
    - Direct/local/vercel smoke sees env when needed.
- Commands not needing bridge do not receive token where feasible.
- Vercel/remote gets public HTTPS reachable URL or stable disabled diagnostic.
- Token never persisted to .boring-agent/workspace files/generated files/provisioning/logs/diagnostics/tool output.
- Logs redact token value.

    Gate commands and pass criteria:
    - pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run test src/server
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent run test
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace run typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent run typecheck

    Definition of done:
- [ ] Implementation is complete and scoped to this bead only.
- [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests named here are added or updated; logs are detailed and token/answer/content redacted.
- [ ] Gate commands below pass, or any provider/downstream skip is explicitly justified with diagnostic evidence.
- [ ] Evidence captured in bead/PR: commands run, pass/fail summary, important redacted logs/screenshots/traces.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes before close.
close_reason: shipped — Gemini gemini-3.5-flash review: SHIP; gates: workspace server tests, agent test, agent/workspace typecheck, lint:invariants

title: workspace src folder reorg: canonical four-zone layout
description:
# Goal
Reorganize `packages/workspace/src/` so root-level workspace code follows canonical zones: `front/`, `server/`, `shared/`, `plugins/`. Preserve behavior and public API.

# Background and reasoning
Current root has stray directories: `data/`, `lib/`, `panes/`, `store/`, `testing/`, `theme/`, `toast/`. `src/__tests__/` intentionally stays at root because those are package integration tests. Pane families are plugin-owned or chrome-owned but live under `src/panes/`, which hides ownership and makes imports misleading. Final state: React UI utilities under `src/front/*`; filesystem pane implementation under `src/plugins/filesystemPlugin/*`; generic data catalog UI under `src/front/components/data-catalog/*`; no tracked files under `src/panes/`.

# Scope
- Pure file moves and import updates only.
- No behavior changes.
- Top-level `src/index.ts` exports remain source-compatible.
- `@boring/workspace/testing` subpath must keep building through `dist/testing.js` and `dist/testing.d.ts`.

# Non-goals
- No plugin behavior redesign.
- No dedupe of `defaultEditorPanels` versus built-in filesystem plugin panels.
- No unrelated Storybook cleanup except imports broken by these moves.

# Acceptance checklist
- [ ] All dependent phase beads A1 through F are closed.
- [ ] `packages/workspace/src/` has only `front/`, `server/`, `shared/`, `plugins/`, `__tests__/`, `globals.css`, and `index.ts` at root, with `src/__tests__/` still present.
- [ ] Public API test still passes and all exported names from before reorg still resolve.
- [ ] Evidence from final verification bead shows typecheck, test, build, subpath smoke, panes grep, and Storybook decision.

# Gate commands
See final verification bead. This epic is complete only after final gate passes.

# Evidence
Attach or summarize final command results in closing comment. No plan doc lookup should be needed; implementation details are in child beads.
close_reason: src reorg complete — final gate bzk8 green: root zones canonical, public API/test/typecheck/build/smoke/grep pass

title: fix app testing aliases after workspace testing reorg
description: Workspace testing helpers moved from packages/workspace/src/testing to packages/workspace/src/front/testing, but app Vite/Vitest aliases still point at the old source path. Update app aliases and prove pnpm -w build passes.
close_reason: shipped — app aliases already point to src/front/testing, pnpm -w build passes

title: A2.1 move data hooks into front/data
description:
# Goal
Move data fetching layer from `packages/workspace/src/data/` to `packages/workspace/src/front/data/` and update imports while preserving public API exports.

# Background and reasoning
The data layer is React client data fetching (`DataProvider`, `FetchClient`, hooks, file event invalidation). It is front-end code, not root package infrastructure. This move is part of A2 and depends on A1 because data consumers also use `front/lib`.

# Exact moves
Move the whole folder with `git mv`:

title: A2.2 move workspace store into front/store
description:

title: Research: Plugin tool name collision precedence
description:
**Question:** When a pi plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, who wins?

**Investigation:**
1. Write a synthetic plugin that registers a tool named `bash` with behavior "return 'FROM PLUGIN'".
2. Load standard catalog + plugin → invoke `bash` via chat → observe output.
3. Vary registration order: ours-then-plugin vs plugin-then-ours.
4. Try pi's namespace mechanisms if any.

**Document:** exact precedence rule in `docs/PLUGINS.md`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins (plugins after defaults = plugin wins).

**Future:** namespace defaults as `boring.bash` — deferred unless collision reports pile up.

**Acceptance:** rule documented with reproducible test.

## Acceptance Criteria

Precedence rule documented with reproducible test.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship. Documented plugin-vs-plugin same-name precedence in PLUGINS.md and added mergeTools unit test proving later plugin wins with warning and no hard error.

title: Phase 3a: Agent Pane Slot
description:
## Goal
Register ChatPanel from @boring/agent as a panel in the workspace registry. Workspace does NOT import from agent — the app shell passes the component in.

## What to implement
- ChatPanel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop (app shell provides it)
- Default placement: 'right' (IdeLayout) or center slot (ChatLayout)
- Lazy-loaded with React.lazy + Suspense wrapper
- requiresCapabilities: ['agent.chat'] — only available when capability is set

## How it works (no circular dependency)
- App shell imports ChatPanel from @boring/agent
- App shell passes it to WorkspaceProvider panels prop
- Workspace never imports from @boring/agent
- Types (WorkspaceBridge, CommandResult, etc.) in @boring/core

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Registration
1. Panel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop appears in registry
2. Panel has correct default placement (IdeLayout: 'right', ChatLayout: 'center')
3. Panel component lazy-loads (Suspense fallback shown, then component renders)

### Capability gating
4. Panel visible when capabilities include 'agent.chat'
5. Panel hidden when capabilities do NOT include 'agent.chat'
6. Adding 'agent.chat' capability dynamically makes panel appear

### Isolation (no circular dependency)
7. WorkspaceProvider renders without @boring/agent imported (no hard dependency)
8. Passing null/undefined for panels prop works (no agent panel)
9. Custom panel component (not ChatPanel) can be registered the same way

### Placement
10. In IdeLayout: agent panel renders in right group
11. In ChatLayout: agent panel renders in center group

## Success criteria
- Agent pane renders inside IdeLayout right rail
- Agent pane renders inside ChatLayout center
- Pane lazy-loads correctly
- Capability gating works (hidden when 'agent.chat' not in capabilities)
- All 11 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: M0: Interface — SandboxHandleStore (DB injection seam)
description:
**Goal:** Lock the sandbox-handle persistence interface. File: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface SandboxHandleRecord {
  workspaceId: string
  sandboxId: string
  snapshotId?: string
  createdAt: string
  lastUsedAt: string
}
export interface SandboxHandleStore {
  get(workspaceId: string): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord | null>
  put(record: SandboxHandleRecord): Promise<void>
  delete(workspaceId: string): Promise<void>
  list(): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord[]>
}
```

**Why:** Agent has no DB dependency. Default impl (`FileHandleStore`) writes `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Future `@boring/cloud` injects a DB-backed impl via `createAgentApp({ sandboxHandleStore })`. Same pattern as `SessionStore`.

**Used by:** `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` (M2).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.

**Design note — no M1 impl by design:** Bwrap sandboxes in M1 are ephemeral (no handle to persist — each sandbox is a fresh process). First implementation is M2: FileHandleStore, which persists Vercel sandbox handles for snapshot resume.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.
