title: [Phase 1.9.5] CI invariants — lock in migration outcome (forbidden-patterns + renderer coverage + schema parity)
description:
# Phase 1.9.5 — CI invariants for the migrated tool surface

## Why

Once the hand-rolled tools are deleted (1.9), nothing in CI prevents a future contributor from re-introducing them. The same kind of grep-enforced guard the project already uses (per AGENTS.md "Critical architectural invariants" — `bash scripts/check-invariants.sh`) should be extended to lock in the migration outcome.

This is the "make the change un-undoable accidentally" bead.

## What to add

### 1. Forbidden-pattern grep rules

Add to `scripts/check-invariants.sh` (or equivalent invariant runner):

```bash
# Forbid hand-rolled tool re-introduction
forbid_pattern \
  "Hand-rolled tool re-introduction (use pi factories)" \
  "Pi-tools-migration locked in: bash/read/write/edit/find/grep are pi factories. Use Operations adapters." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src" -g "!**/operations/**" \
  -e "function createBashTool|function createReadTool|function createWriteTool|function createEditTool|function createFindFilesTool|function createGrepFilesTool"

# Forbid old tool names in code (post-rename)
forbid_pattern \
  "Old tool name find_files / grep_files" \
  "Renamed in step 1.10: use pi names \`find\` / \`grep\`." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src" \
  -e "find_files|grep_files"

# Forbid the per-tool guideline pattern in tool-adapter.ts
forbid_pattern \
  "Per-tool guideline bullets violate Principle 1" \
  "Pi-built tools self-advertise. Do not add \`Prefer the X tool when...\` guidelines." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src/server/harness" \
  -e "Prefer the .* tool when"

# Forbid the double-dash promptSnippet pattern
forbid_pattern \
  "Double-dash promptSnippet leaks into system prompt" \
  "promptSnippet should be the description verbatim (no leading dash + backticks)." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src/server/harness" \
  -e "promptSnippet:.*- \`\\\$\\{tool\\.name\\}\`"
```

### 2. Tool-renderer coverage check

Add a script `scripts/check-tool-renderer-coverage.sh` (or a vitest test in `__tests__/`) that:
- Boots `createAgentApp({ mode: "direct" })`.
- Lists registered tool names.
- Asserts every tool name has a renderer entry in `packages/agent/src/ui-shadcn/workspaceToolRenderers.tsx` (and `packages/workspace/...` if separate).

This catches the case where someone adds a new pi tool to the bundle but forgets the renderer (model uses the tool, UI shows "unknown tool").

### 3. Snapshot test for vercelGrepTool schema parity

Add `packages/agent/src/server/tools/__tests__/grep-schema-parity.test.ts`:
- Compares `JSON.stringify(vercelGrepTool(mockSandbox).parameters)` to `JSON.stringify(createGrepTool(mockCwd).parameters)`.
- Fails if pi changes its schema and we have not updated ours. **Principle 1 enforcement gate.**

### 4. Bead → CI doc

Update `AGENTS.md` "Critical architectural invariants" to add a 9th: "Pi-tools-migration: bash/read/write/edit/find/grep flow through pi factories. Custom AgentTools require justification against Principle 3 (epic uhwx)." Or add as 10th if 9 is taken.

## Tests this bead defines

| Test | What it asserts |
|---|---|
| `check-invariants.sh` (extended) | No hand-rolled `createXxxTool` factories. No `find_files`/`grep_files`. No per-tool guideline pattern. No double-dash promptSnippet. |
| `check-tool-renderer-coverage.sh` | Every registered tool name has a UI renderer entry. |
| `grep-schema-parity.test.ts` | `vercelGrepTool.parameters === createGrepTool().parameters` (deep equal). |

**Detailed logging:** each invariant violation prints `[invariants] ERR <file>:<line>: <message>\n  Invariant: <name>\n  Fix: <hint>` (matches existing pattern in `check-invariants.sh`). Schema parity test on failure prints a unified diff.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `bash scripts/check-invariants.sh packages/agent` exits 0 on a clean tree.
- [ ] Running it on a synthetic violation (e.g., I add `function createBashTool()` to a tool file) exits non-zero with a clear message.
- [ ] Tool-renderer coverage check passes.
- [ ] Schema parity test passes.
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated with the invariant.
- [ ] CI workflow runs `check-invariants.sh` on every PR.

## Depends on

- 1.10 (uhwx.14) tool-name rename done — invariant for `find_files`/`grep_files` only makes sense after.

## Why this gates `uhwx.13` (delete hand-rolled)

The CI invariants should land BEFORE the hand-rolled deletion lands, so the grep-check that "no `function createBashTool` in `tools/`" is already passing on a clean tree (the file is still there but the invariant ignores it via the path glob). After 1.9 deletes the file, the invariant catches re-introductions.

Order: 1.18 lands → 1.13 deletes the hand-rolled → invariants now actively guard the deletion.

## References

- AGENTS.md § "Critical architectural invariants" + the existing `scripts/check-invariants.sh` for the pattern.
- Plan § "Design principles" — Principle 1 anti-patterns are what these invariants enforce.

title: [Phase 1.10.5] Update agent-package-spec.md decisions #4 #6 + CHANGELOG + README
description:
# Phase 1.10.5 — Update agent-package-spec.md decision records

## Why

The original spec at `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` locks two decisions that this migration explicitly supersedes:

- **Decision #4 (line 631):** "v1 harness: @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent — Ships loop + plugin API; we pass our own `tools: [...]` to skip pi defaults."
- **Decision #6 (line 633):** "Catalog: 4 base tools: `bash, read, write, edit`. +1 conditional `execute_isolated_code` if sandbox declares `isolated-code` capability. Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred."

After this migration, both are wrong:
- We do NOT pass our own tools — we use pi's factories with Operations adapters.
- We DO have dedicated find/grep tools (six tools, not four).

Future contributors reading the spec will be confused unless we update the decision records.

## What to change

In `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md`:

### 1. Update decisions #4 and #6 in place

```diff
- | 4 | v1 harness | `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` | Ships loop + plugin API; we pass our own `tools: [...]` to skip pi's defaults. |
+ | 4 | v1 harness | `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` | Pi's factory tools + `XxxOperations` adapters per mode. Our backends plug in via Operations; pi owns tool definitions, schemas, prompt snippets. See `pi-tools-migration.md` (epic uhwx). |

- | 6 | Catalog | 4 base tools: `bash, read, write, edit`. +1 conditional `execute_isolated_code` ... Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred. |
+ | 6 | Catalog | Standard tools (via pi factories): `bash, read, write, edit, find, grep, ls`. Custom tools (via `customTools`): `execute_isolated_code` (capability-gated), `vercelGrepTool` (vercel-sandbox only — pi's GrepOperations cannot redirect rg spawn). See `pi-tools-migration.md` "Design principles" for the full rationale. |
```

### 2. Add a "Supersedes" note to the spec

At the top of the spec, add:

```markdown
> **Note (2026-Q2):** Decisions #4 and #6 below describe the v1-original
> design. The pi-tools-migration (`docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md`,
> epic uhwx) supersedes these. The migration leverages pi's tool
> factories rather than supplying our own, and adds dedicated find/grep
> tools rather than routing them via bash. The old decisions remain
> in this table for historical reference.
```

### 3. Update the LOC budget estimate (line 490)

```diff
- | 4 tool factories (bash → sandbox; read/write/edit → workspace) | ~200 |
+ | Operations adapters (bash + 6 file ops, mode-specific) + bundle factories | ~250 |
+ | Custom tools (vercelGrepTool, executeIsolatedCodeTool) | ~250 |
```

### 4. Update the package-layout reference (line 540+)

Change `src/server/catalog/tools/{bashTool,readTool,...}.ts` block to reflect the new shape: `src/server/tools/{operations,filesystem,harness}/`. Match the actual post-migration tree.

### 5. Update README if relevant

If `packages/agent/README.md` documents tool names or the "we pass tools to pi" model, update it. Likely a one-paragraph change.

### 6. CHANGELOG entry

Add to `packages/agent/CHANGELOG.md` (create if missing):

```markdown
## [Unreleased] — Pi tools migration (epic uhwx)

### Changed (breaking)
- Tool names: `find_files` → `find`, `grep_files` → `grep` (pi conventions).
- Tool schemas: read uses `offset`/`limit` (was `lineOffset`/`lineCount`); edit takes `edits[]` array (was `oldString`/`newString`/`replaceAll`); write drops `createDirs` (always recursive); bash gains optional `timeout`. Grep gains `ignoreCase`/`literal`/`context`.
- File ops registered through `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)` instead of `standardCatalog`. Standalone path: default-on with `disableDefaultFileTools` opt-out. Workspace path: via `filesystemPlugin`.

### Added
- Streaming bash output via `Sandbox.exec({ onStdout, onStderr })` callbacks.
- Genericized `Sandbox` interface (`SandboxResources`, `vendorHints`, `placement: "remote"`) for future Modal/Docker/Apple-container backends.

### Removed
- Six hand-rolled tool implementations under `src/server/catalog/tools/`.
- Per-tool guideline bullets in tool-adapter (system prompt -~700 chars).
- Double-dash `promptSnippet` bug (system prompt cosmetic fix).
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spec lines 631 and 633 updated.
- [ ] "Supersedes" note added at top of spec.
- [ ] LOC budget table updated.
- [ ] Package-layout reference updated to match post-migration tree.
- [ ] README updated if it mentions tools.
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry written.
- [ ] No remaining "we pass our own tools" or "Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred" references in any doc under `packages/agent/`.

## Depends on

- 1.14 (uhwx.14) renames done — CHANGELOG can reference final names.

## References

- Spec: `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` decisions #4, #6.
- Migration plan: `packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md`.
- AGENTS.md "Where to Find What" table for the doc-owner convention.

title: [Phase 0.2] Refactor 6 tool files to use _shared.ts
description:
# Phase 0.2 — Refactor all 6 tool files to use _shared.ts

## Why

Eliminate duplicated `makeError`/`bytesWritten`/`nowIso`/`normalizeLimit`/`decoder`/`FileChangeMetadata` declarations across `bashTool.ts`, `readTool.ts`, `writeTool.ts`, `editTool.ts`, `findFilesTool.ts`, `grepFilesTool.ts`. Reduces LOC ~30-40% per file with no behavior change.

## What to change

For each of the six tool files in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/`:

1. Replace local `function makeError(...)` with `import { makeError } from "./_shared"`.
2. Replace local `bytesWritten`/`nowIso` with `_shared` imports (edit + write tools only).
3. Replace local `normalizeLimit` with `_shared` import (find + grep tools only).
4. Replace local `TextDecoder` instances + `decode` helper with `_shared` imports (bash + grep tools).
5. Replace local `FileChangeMetadata` declarations with `_shared` import. Use the canonical `existsBefore?` field (universal — see Phase 0.3 for the universal-emit decision).
6. Replace local constants (`DEFAULT_LIMIT`, `MAX_LIMIT`, `MAX_*_LENGTH`) with `_shared` imports.

Keep all existing tests passing. Each file should shrink by 10-25 LOC.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- All 6 tool tests pass unchanged.
- A `grep -rn "function makeError" packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/" returns zero hits except in `_shared.ts`.
- Same for `function bytesWritten`, `function nowIso`, `function normalizeLimit`.
- No `new TextDecoder` outside `_shared.ts` in this directory.
- No `interface FileChangeMetadata` outside `_shared.ts` in this directory.

## References

- Plan § "Phase 0 - audit findings - Cross-file duplication" lists items 1-7 covered here.

title: [Phase 0.3] Standardize schemas, abort checks, error phrasing
description:
# Phase 0.3 — Standardize schemas + error phrasing

## Why

The six tool files diverge in small, fixable ways: only `findFilesTool` + `grepFilesTool` use `additionalProperties: false`; only `grepFilesTool` has param descriptions; `bashTool` skips the abort check at start; `editTool` does not emit `existsBefore` even though the field belongs there universally. These divergences cause subtle differences in model behavior + workspace bridge events. This bead aligns them.

## What to change

In each of the six tool files in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/`:

1. **Add `additionalProperties: false`** to every JSON-schema `parameters` block. This tightens the model-facing contract — small breaking change worth flagging in the PR description.
2. **Add `if (ctx.abortSignal.aborted) return makeError("X aborted")` at the top of every `execute`**, including bashTool which currently relies only on `sandbox.exec`'s signal forwarding.
3. **Standardize error phrasing**: `"<name> failed: <msg>"` for execution failures, `"<name> aborted"` for aborts. No `"unknown <X> failure"` variants.
4. **Standardize param-typing approach**: every `execute(input, ctx)` body starts with `const params = input as Record<string, unknown>`. Drop the local `XxxParams` interfaces in `editTool` + `writeTool`.
5. **Universal `existsBefore` emission**: `editTool` starts emitting `existsBefore: true` on its `FileChangeMetadata` so the workspace bridge sees a consistent shape. Workspace bridge already accepts the field; this just makes it always-present.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- All 6 tool tests pass; update tests for any outputs that change shape (mainly `editTool` for the new `existsBefore` field).
- A grep for `additionalProperties` in this directory shows it on all 6 schemas.
- A grep for `signal.aborted` shows the abort-at-start check in all 6 files.
- Workspace bridge / sessionChangesTracker tests still green.

## Notes

The `toolCallId` sanitization in `writeTool.ts:75-79` (regex `[^A-Za-z0-9_-]`) stays as defense-in-depth with a one-line comment explaining the intent. Pi guarantees UUIDs today but keeping the regex is essentially free.

## References

- Plan § "Phase 0 - audit findings - Inconsistencies" lists items 8-14.
- Plan § "Phase 0 standardization decisions" makes the universal-existsBefore + additionalProperties decisions explicit.

title: [Phase 0.4] Drop stale TODOs, inline single-use helpers, clean descriptions
description:
# Phase 0.4 — Drop dead code + cleanup

## Why

Final pass: remove stale comments, unused helpers, leaked descriptions. No behavior change.

## What to change

1. **`readTool.ts:97`** — drop the stale `// TODO(m1): add max-bytes guard once Workspace exposes bounded reads.` comment. M1 has long shipped.
2. **`bashTool.ts:9-11`** — inline the single-use `decode(bytes)` helper at its one callsite (or import from `_shared` after Phase 0.2).
3. **`editTool.ts:46-66`** — replace the two near-identical `replaceFirstOccurrence` + `replaceAllOccurrences` functions with one helper that takes a count.
4. **`findFilesTool.ts:54`** — remove the leaked `vercel-sandbox mode` reference from the description. The tool has no sandbox knowledge; the description should be sandbox-mode-agnostic.
5. **`bashTool.ts:234-244`** — add a one-line comment explaining the intentional asymmetry (model sees JSON-stringified `output` in `content[0].text`; UI consumes structured `details`). Worth documenting because a future reader could mistake it for redundancy.

## Out of scope

- `bashTool.ts:23-194` (170 LOC of `inferBashFileChanges` shell-parsing) — wrong file but **deferred to a separate PR**. Independent of this plan.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- All tests pass.
- A grep for `TODO(m1)` returns zero hits in this directory.
- The `decode` helper exists only in `_shared.ts`.
- `findFilesTool` description does not mention sandbox modes.

## References

- Plan § "Phase 0 - audit findings - Dead/questionable code" lists items 15-21 (item 20 is out of scope).

title: [Phase 1.1] boundFs Operations adapter (read/write/edit/ls + symlink-safe bounding)
description:
# Phase 1.1 — boundFs Operations adapter (with symlink resolution)

## Why

Pi's standard tools take **absolute paths** in their `XxxOperations` callbacks (pi resolves model input via `resolveToCwd(filePath, cwd)` before dispatch — see pi's `dist/core/tools/path-utils.js`). Pi does NO path-bounding — the Operations adapter is 100% responsible for refusing paths outside the workspace. This bead builds that adapter for `direct` and `local` modes (where the workspace is the host fs).

`agent-package-spec.md` decision #7a says path-bounding is a workspace responsibility via `validatePath` / `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`. We move the bounding from inside our hand-rolled tool into a thin Operations wrapper around pi's default fs primitives.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/bound.ts` exporting:

```ts
import type { ReadOperations, WriteOperations, EditOperations, LsOperations } from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
import { realpath, readFile as fsReadFile, writeFile as fsWriteFile, mkdir as fsMkdir, access, readdir, stat } from "node:fs/promises"
import { constants } from "node:fs"
import { isUnderWorkspace, assertRealPathWithinWorkspace } from "../../workspace/paths"

export interface BoundFs {
  read: ReadOperations
  write: WriteOperations
  edit: EditOperations
  ls: LsOperations
}

export function boundFs(workspaceRoot: string): BoundFs { ... }
```

Each method:
1. Calls `fs.realpath(absPath)` to resolve symlinks.
2. Throws `Error("path \"${rel}\" is outside workspace")` if resolved path is not under `workspaceRoot`.
3. Delegates to the underlying `node:fs` primitive on the happy path.

Port `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace` and `isUnderWorkspace` helpers from `packages/agent/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` — they already exist for our hand-rolled tools. Reuse them.

## Tests

`packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/__tests__/bound.test.ts` covering:

- happy path: `read("/workspace/foo.txt")` succeeds.
- absolute escape: `read("/etc/passwd")` rejects with the standard message.
- relative escape via `..`: pi resolves to absolute before calling Operations, so this test must construct an absolute path that points outside the root.
- symlink escape: create a file in the workspace that symlinks to `/etc/passwd`; assert `read` is rejected. **Critical** — pi's `resolveToCwd` does NOT resolve symlinks; we must.
- error shape: thrown error reaches the model as a useful text message (not "ENOENT") via pi's tool-invocation error path. Verify by running through `createReadTool(cwd, { operations: boundFs(root).read })` and asserting the resulting `ToolResult`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- `boundFs(root).read.readFile("/etc/passwd")` rejects.
- Symlink escape test passes.

## References

- Plan § "Phase 1 — Operations adapters" + R7 (path-bounding error message) + R8 (symlink resolution).
- Spec line 390 (`assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`).

title: [Phase 1.2] vercel*Ops Operations adapters (bash/read/write/edit/find/ls)
description:
# Phase 1.2 — vercel*Ops Operations adapters

## Why

For `vercel-sandbox` mode, every file/exec operation must route to the remote VM, not the agent host. Pi's `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` (the canonical reference per pi-mono#564) is the template: replace the SSH backend with our `Sandbox` interface.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/vercel.ts` exporting:

```ts
import type { Sandbox } from "../../../shared/sandbox"
import type { BashOperations, ReadOperations, WriteOperations, EditOperations, FindOperations, LsOperations } from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"

export function vercelBashOps(sandbox: Sandbox): BashOperations
export function vercelReadOps(sandbox: Sandbox): ReadOperations
export function vercelWriteOps(sandbox: Sandbox): WriteOperations
export function vercelEditOps(sandbox: Sandbox): EditOperations
export function vercelFindOps(sandbox: Sandbox): FindOperations
export function vercelLsOps(sandbox: Sandbox): LsOperations
```

### `vercelBashOps`

Pi calls `exec(command, cwd, { onData, signal, timeout, env })` and expects `Promise<{ exitCode: number | null }>`. After bead `uhwx.16` lands, our `Sandbox.exec` accepts `onStdout`/`onStderr` callbacks; this adapter forwards them to pi's `onData`:

```ts
export function vercelBashOps(sandbox: Sandbox): BashOperations {
  return {
    exec(command, cwd, { onData, signal, timeout, env }) {
      return sandbox.exec(command, {
        cwd, env, signal,
        timeoutMs: timeout && timeout * 1000,
        onStdout: (chunk) => onData(Buffer.from(chunk)),
        onStderr: (chunk) => onData(Buffer.from(chunk)),
      }).then((result) => ({ exitCode: result.exitCode }))
    },
  }
}
```

**Streaming is empirically verified** (see `uhwx.16` and `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`): 80 incremental Writable.write() calls over a 1.3s/100-line command. No further investigation needed.

### `vercelReadOps` / `vercelWriteOps` / `vercelEditOps`

Forward `(absPath, ...)` to `sandbox.fs.read` / `sandbox.writeFiles` / `sandbox.fs.access`. Document the path-translation contract: pi resolves the model's input via `resolveToCwd(filePath, cwd)` before calling Operations; our adapter receives the resolved absolute path. The VM uses `/vercel/sandbox/...` as cwd — absolute paths the model emits already match.

### `vercelFindOps`

`glob(pattern, cwd, { ignore, limit })` runs `fd --glob --no-require-git --max-results <limit> <pattern> <cwd>` inside the VM via `sandbox.exec`, parses stdout into a `string[]`. Honor `ignore` patterns (default + caller-supplied) by appending `--exclude` flags.

### `vercelLsOps`

`exists(absPath)`, `stat(absPath)`, `readdir(absPath)` → forward to sandbox.fs methods.

## Tests

`packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/__tests__/vercel.test.ts` mocking `Sandbox` and asserting:
- Each adapter forwards args correctly.
- `vercelBashOps` propagates abort + timeout (smoke confirmed 12ms abort latency).
- `vercelFindOps` parses fd output correctly (incl. truncation).
- Error responses propagate with usable messages.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- All six adapters compile against pi's `XxxOperations` types.
- `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` regression check still passes.

## Depends on

- 1.0 (uhwx.16) — Sandbox.exec needs `onStdout`/`onStderr` callbacks before vercelBashOps can wire pi's `onData`.

## References

- Plan § "Phase 1 — vercel-sandbox mode".
- Smoke script: `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`.
- Pi reference: `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` lines 47-112.
- Pi RFC: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564.

title: [Phase 1.3] buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle) factory (bash + isolated-code, mode-aware)
description:
# Phase 1.3 — buildHarnessAgentTools bundle factory

## Why

Bash + execute_isolated_code stay harness-level (per PLUGIN_MODEL.md non-goal line 122). They are NOT part of the filesystemPlugin path. `createAgentApp` always registers them directly. The bash Operations are mode-specific (direct: pi default; local: pi default + bwrap spawnHook; vercel-sandbox: vercel ops). This bead consolidates that switch into one factory.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/harness/index.ts` exporting:

```ts
export function buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle: RuntimeBundle): AgentTool[]
```

Internal `bashOptionsForMode(bundle: RuntimeBundle): BashToolOptions` returns:
- `direct`: `{ operations: createLocalBashOperations() }`
- `local`: `{ operations: createLocalBashOperations(), spawnHook: bwrapSpawnHook(bundle.workspace.root) }`
- `vercel-sandbox`: `{ operations: vercelBashOps(bundle.sandbox) }`

`bwrapSpawnHook(root)` is a tiny (~15 LOC) helper that returns a `BashSpawnHook` rewriting `command` to `bwrap ${bwrapArgs(root)} -- bash -lc ${shellEscape(command)}`. Reuse our existing `BwrapSandbox` flag-set as `bwrapArgs`.

The bundle returns:
1. `createBashTool(bundle.workspace.root, bashOptionsForMode(bundle))`
2. If `bundle.sandbox.capabilities.includes("isolated-code")`: also `executeIsolatedCodeTool(bundle.sandbox)` (existing tool, unchanged).

## Tests

`__tests__/harness.test.ts`:
- direct mode: returned array contains a tool named `bash`. The bash tool's execute matches pi's default behavior on a mock `cwd`.
- local mode: bash tool exists; spawning it routes through bwrap (assert via spawn-hook spy).
- vercel-sandbox: bash tool exists; spawning it forwards to a mock sandbox.exec.
- isolated-code: present when `sandbox.capabilities` includes `"isolated-code"`, absent otherwise.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle).map(t => t.name)` returns the right names per mode.

## Depends on

- 1.2 (vercel*Ops) for the vercel-sandbox branch.

## References

- Plan § "buildHarnessAgentTools".
- PLUGIN_MODEL.md line 122 (bash + isolated stay harness-level).
- Pi sandbox extension reference: `examples/extensions/sandbox/index.ts:132-198` (spawnHook pattern).

Bead required existing tool unchanged; fix by extracting/reusing one implementation. (2) packages/agent/src/server/tools/harness/__tests__/harness.test.ts:54 only asserts local-mode tool name; bead required asserting spawn routes through bwrap via spawn-hook spy. Add a test that exercises local bash and observes the bwrap command path. Gates run by reviewer: harness vitest pass, tsc --noEmit pass, invariants pass.

title: [Phase 1.4] buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle) factory (read/write/edit/find/grep/ls, mode-aware)
description:
# Phase 1.4 — buildFilesystemAgentTools bundle factory

## Why

The six file-ops tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `find`, `grep`, `ls`) are workspace-level. They flow through both the standalone path (`createAgentApp` default-on, opt-out via `disableDefaultFileTools`) and the workspace path (`filesystemPlugin.agentTools` per PLUGIN_MODEL.md). One factory function, called by both consumers, with mode-specific Operations injected.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/filesystem/index.ts` exporting:

```ts
export function buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle: RuntimeBundle): AgentTool[]
```

Per-mode behavior:

- **direct + local**: workspace IS the host fs; pi defaults are correct.
  ```ts
  const ops = boundFs(cwd)
  return [
    createReadTool(cwd, { operations: ops.read }),
    createWriteTool(cwd, { operations: ops.write }),
    createEditTool(cwd, { operations: ops.edit }),
    createFindTool(cwd),                     // pi default fd, .gitignore-aware
    createGrepTool(cwd),                     // pi default rg
    createLsTool(cwd, { operations: ops.ls }),
  ]
  ```
- **vercel-sandbox**: route Operations to the VM. Grep is a custom `vercelGrepTool` (1.5) because pi's `GrepOperations` cannot redirect the actual rg spawn.
  ```ts
  return [
    createReadTool(cwd, { operations: vercelReadOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createWriteTool(cwd, { operations: vercelWriteOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createEditTool(cwd, { operations: vercelEditOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createFindTool(cwd, { operations: vercelFindOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    vercelGrepTool(bundle.sandbox),          // custom AgentTool
    createLsTool(cwd, { operations: vercelLsOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
  ]
  ```

## Tests

`__tests__/filesystem.test.ts`:
- direct + local: returned tools have names `[read, write, edit, find, grep, ls]`.
- vercel-sandbox: same names but `grep` is the custom `vercelGrepTool` instance, not pi's factory output.
- Switching modes returns different objects (no stale closure bugs).

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- A unit assertion: in vercel-sandbox mode, `tools.find(t => t.name === "grep")` is `vercelGrepTool`, not pi's.

## Depends on

- 1.1 (boundFs)
- 1.2 (vercel*Ops)
- 1.5 (vercelGrepTool)

## References

- Plan § "buildFilesystemAgentTools".
- PLUGIN_MODEL.md lines 558-575 (filesystemPlugin pattern).
- Pi RFC: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564.

title: [Phase 1.5] vercelGrepTool — custom AgentTool with pi-grep-compatible schema
description:
# Phase 1.5 — vercelGrepTool (pi-grep-schema-compatible custom AgentTool)

## Principle alignment

This tool is the canonical example of **Principle 3 case 2** ("pi's tool architecture cannot accommodate our backend"). Justification, in three lines, that the PR description must reproduce verbatim:

1. Pi's `GrepOperations` interface only exposes `isDirectory` + `readFile` (for context lines). The rg spawn is hardcoded local in `grep.js:144`. There is no Operations seam to redirect the search.
2. Issue pi-mono#3782 made dedicated grep a default tool because bare `bash grep` over `node_modules` burned ~98k tokens per session. Dropping grep is not an option.
3. Therefore vercel-sandbox needs a custom AgentTool that spawns rg inside the VM via `sandbox.exec`. **No other custom tool is justified by this case.**

Even though we're writing a custom tool, **Principle 1 still applies to the schema**: `vercelGrepTool.parameters` MUST be byte-for-byte identical to pi's `createGrepTool(cwd).parameters`. The model's grep behavior is identical across modes; only the underlying execution differs.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/vercelGrepTool.ts`:

```ts
export function vercelGrepTool(sandbox: Sandbox): AgentTool
```

### Schema (mirrors pi's `dist/core/tools/grep.d.ts` exactly)

```ts
{
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    pattern: { type: "string", description: "Regex (or literal if literal=true)." },
    path: { type: "string", description: "Sub-tree to search; defaults to workspace root." },
    glob: { type: "string", description: "File glob filter, e.g. \"*.ts\"." },
    ignoreCase: { type: "boolean" },
    literal: { type: "boolean", description: "Treat pattern as literal string, not regex." },
    context: { type: "integer", minimum: 0 },
    limit: { type: "integer", minimum: 1, maximum: 5000 },
  },
  required: ["pattern"],
  additionalProperties: false,
}
```

### Execute logic

Mirror pi's rg invocation in `dist/core/tools/grep.js:130-180`:
- Build args: `--no-heading -n -m <limit>`. Add `-i` for `ignoreCase`. Add `-F` for `literal`. Add `-C <n>` for `context`. Add `-g <glob>` for glob filter. Add `--smart-case` if neither flag set.
- Run `rg ${args} ${pattern} ${path ?? cwd}` via `sandbox.exec(...)`.
- Parse stdout into matches: `<file>:<lineNo>:<text>` (or `<file>-<lineNo>-<text>` for context lines). Truncate at `limit`.
- Return `ToolResult` with `details: { matches, truncated, ... }` matching pi's `GrepToolDetails` shape so a renderer keyed on tool name `grep` handles both pi-built and our-built versions identically.

## Tests

`__tests__/vercelGrepTool.test.ts`:
- **Schema parity test**: snapshot assertion that `JSON.stringify(vercelGrepTool(mockSandbox).parameters) === JSON.stringify(createGrepTool(cwd).parameters)`. **This is the Principle 1 enforcement gate** — if pi changes its schema, our test fails and we update.
- Forward each schema field correctly to rg args.
- Parse output (mock `sandbox.exec` returning recorded rg stdout).
- Honor `limit` truncation.
- Surface ripgrep exit-code-1 (no matches found) as success with empty matches, not as an error.
- Honor `ctx.abortSignal`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- Tests pass.
- **Schema snapshot equals pi's grep schema exactly** (Principle 1).
- `details` shape matches pi's `GrepToolDetails` (renderer parity).
- Manual visual check: a renderer keyed on `grep` works identically for pi-built (direct/local) and ours (vercel-sandbox).

## References

- Plan § "Design principles" Principles 1 + 3.
- Plan § "vercelGrepTool design" + R3.
- Pi grep impl: `node_modules/.pnpm/@mariozechner+pi-coding-agent@0.67.68_*/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/tools/grep.js`.
- Pi RFC for default grep: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/3782.
close_reason: Done — see commit d35660c; Claude review verdict: ship

title: M4: Name-collision precedence (document + runtime enforcement)
description:
**Goal:** When a plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, which wins? File: `src/server/catalog/mergeTools.ts`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins. Plugin tools registered AFTER defaults → plugin overrides. Document this.

**Future consideration:** namespace ours (`fs.bash`) — deferred.

**Log a warning:** on collision: `[catalog] Tool "bash" overridden by plugin <name>`.

**Tests:** register a conflicting tool; assert which wins; assert warning logged.

## Acceptance Criteria

Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.
acceptance_criteria: Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.
close_reason: Implemented runtime collision enforcement via catalog/mergeTools: last-registered wins across standard -> extraTools -> plugin tools, with plugin override warning format '[catalog] Tool "<name>" overridden by plugin <name>'. Wired createAgentApp to use mergeTools and plugin-name labeling. Added mergeTools tests for merge order + collision override/warn behavior and documented precedence in docs/PLUGINS.md. Validation: vitest (mergeTools + createAgentApp), package typecheck, invariants all pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 9a9118d.

title: core-M0: Dockerfile.reference (multi-stage, pnpm workspace, node:20-slim)
description:
## Goal
Commit `packages/core/Dockerfile.reference` — the multi-stage Dockerfile that child apps extend for their PaaS deploys.

## Why it matters
M5_FULL_APP and M5_FLY_CONFIG both reference "extending core's reference Dockerfile" but no bead actually creates the file. Without it, every child app reinvents the same multi-stage build.

## Acceptance criteria
- File `packages/core/Dockerfile.reference` committed.
- Multi-stage build:
  - Stage 1 `base`: `node:20-slim` + corepack + pnpm.
  - Stage 2 `deps`: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (cached layer).
  - Stage 3 `build`: `pnpm -r build` against monorepo workspace.
  - Stage 4 `runtime`: `node:20-slim`, copy `dist/`, expose 3000, CMD invokes the child app's main.js.
- `EXPOSE 3000` + `HEALTHCHECK` directive hitting `/health` every 30s.
- Pinned base-image SHA (security best practice).
- Documented headers explaining each stage.
- `packages/core/README.md` (or CORE.md §Deployment) references the file path so child apps know where to find it.

## Considerations
- Don't include the child app's name in the reference — the CMD line is the bit child apps override (e.g. `CMD ["node", "apps/full-app/dist/server/main.js"]`).
- Use distroless or alpine? `node:20-slim` is the safe default — alpine has musl quirks with native deps; distroless lacks shell for debugging.
- Image size target: <200MB after build.

## Tests
- `docker build -f packages/core/Dockerfile.reference .` succeeds.
- Image scans clean against `trivy` (CI step).
- `docker run --rm <image> --version` returns Node version.

## Refs
CORE.md §Deployment §Reference Dockerfile.
close_reason: M0 Dockerfile.reference shipped. 4-stage multi-stage build with pinned node:20-slim SHA, HEALTHCHECK, EXPOSE 3000. Codex SHIP.

title: Refactor WorkspaceAuthProvider to React Query
description:
## Context

`packages/core/src/front/WorkspaceAuthProvider.tsx` is hand-rolled with `useState`/`useEffect` + raw `apiFetchJson`. Everything else in core uses React Query. This means: no auto-refetch on workspace mutations from `WorkspaceSwitcher`, no shared cache with the workspaces list, no devtools visibility, manual cancel-on-unmount via `cancelled` flag.

## Scope

1. Refactor `WorkspaceAuthProvider` to use React Query:
   ```ts
   const workspacesQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['workspaces'], queryFn: fetchWorkspaces })
   const detailQuery = useQuery({
     queryKey: ['workspace', resolvedId],
     queryFn: () => fetchWorkspace(resolvedId),
     enabled: !!resolvedId,
   })
   ```
   No `refetchInterval` — v7 spec confirms states resolve synchronously, no transient state to poll.
2. `useCurrentWorkspace` and `useWorkspaceRole` hooks pull from these queries.
3. `WorkspaceSwitcher`'s mutations (create, update name, delete) invalidate `['workspaces']` AND `['workspace', id]`.
4. Update tests at `packages/core/src/front/__tests__/WorkspaceAuthProvider.test.tsx`:
   - Hook returns workspace from cache when present.
   - Mutation invalidates and refetches.
   - Loading/error states surface correctly.
5. Update consumers (any code using `useCurrentWorkspace`) to handle React Query's loading state if needed.

## Why this exists

Consistency: the rest of the codebase uses RQ, so the user-facing behavior of the workspace context should match (no stale data after a switcher mutation). Also gives us devtools visibility.

## Acceptance criteria

- `WorkspaceAuthProvider` uses `useQuery` and `useQueryClient`.
- After `WorkspaceSwitcher` create/update/delete, the active workspace data refreshes without a manual reload.
- All existing `WorkspaceAuthProvider` tests pass; new test confirms invalidate-on-mutation.

## Considerations

- The queries need to live INSIDE the `<QueryClientProvider>` boundary set up by `BoringApp`.
- The "no current workspace" state (user has zero workspaces) must still work cleanly.
- The route param `:id` resolution from `useParams()` interacts with `enabled` — handle the empty string case.

## Out of scope

- Adding `refetchInterval` (no transient runtime states in v7 — they resolve sync).
- Changing the public API of `useCurrentWorkspace` / `useWorkspaceRole`.

## Spec reference

Decision #7 in v7 spec. Phase 1.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met; verified via bkqe.1 closure. Work landed at commit 1e6d5f9 (React Query refactor).

title: Phase 4: Storybook Visual Regression
description:
## Goal
Storybook stories for all standalone components and dockview chrome. Screenshot comparison via Chromatic or Percy.

## What to implement
### Stories for standalone components
- FileTree (various sizes: 10, 100, 1000 files)
- CodeEditor (JS, Python, JSON, large file, read-only)
- MarkdownEditor (rich content, toolbar states)
- DataCatalog (empty, multiple sources)

### Stories for dockview chrome
- PanelChrome (with/without icon, dirty state, close button)
- TabBar (multiple tabs, active tab, overflow)
- GroupChrome (locked, collapsible)

### Stories for panes in isolation (mocked data)
- Each pane with TestWorkspaceProvider + mock data

### Visual regression
- Screenshot comparison via Chromatic (preferred) or Percy
- Catches: CSS regressions, theme inconsistencies, responsive breakpoints
- Run on every PR (~3 min)

## Success criteria
- All components have stories
- Light + dark mode variants
- Visual regression catches theme/CSS changes
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: M3b: E2E — chat feels like a real chat in both modes
description:
**Goal:** Polish gate — chat experience is smooth in both `local` and `vercel-sandbox` modes.

**Scenarios (Playwright):**
1. Send "list files" → bash tool card renders via `<Tool>` with terminal output.
2. Send "edit foo.md to fix a typo" → DiffView renders unified diff.
3. Trigger `/help` → command list shows.
4. Trigger `/reset` → new session created, old session appears in toolbar.
5. Type `/model haiku` → switch confirmed.
6. Run a 6s bash → heartbeat ticker updates in the `<Tool>` card.

**Logging:** `[m3b-polish]` tag; capture DOM snapshots on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 6 scenarios pass in both modes (tool renders, DiffView, /help, /reset, /model, heartbeats).
acceptance_criteria: All 6 scenarios pass in both modes (tool renders, DiffView, /help, /reset, /model, heartbeats).

title: Infra: E2E logging harness (browser console + server logs → artifacts)
description:
**Goal:** Playwright fixture + reporter that captures ALL logs (browser console, network, server stdout) per test into structured artifact files. File: `e2e/fixtures/loggingHarness.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Why needed:** the user explicitly asked for "great, detailed logging so we can be sure that everything is working perfectly after implementation." E2E failures in CI with no log context = impossible to debug.

**Captures per test:**
- **Browser console:** all `console.log/warn/error` + page errors.
- **Network:** all requests/responses (method, URL, status, duration, bytes). Redact Authorization headers.
- **Server stdout/stderr:** piped from spawned backend process.
- **Test timeline:** structured events (`test.start`, `page.goto`, `assertion.fail`, etc.).
- **DOM snapshot on failure:** HTML dump for layout debugging.
- **Screenshot on failure:** native Playwright feature + our metadata overlay.

**Artifact layout (on failure):**
```
e2e-artifacts/
  <test-name>/
    browser-console.log           # timestamped lines
    network.har                    # HAR format for replay
    server.log                     # backend stdout/stderr
    dom.html
    screenshot.png
    timeline.json                  # structured events
```

**Fixture usage:**
```ts
test('mode flip', async ({ page, backend, logging }) => {
  logging.mark('starting mode flip test')
  await page.goto(backend.url)
  // ... test body
  logging.mark('verifying response')
  // ...
})
// If test fails → entire artifact dir written + linked in reporter output.
// If test passes → artifact dir deleted (unless BORING_E2E_KEEP=1 set).
```

**Reporter hooks:**
- On `test.afterEach` failure → flush artifacts + print artifact path to stderr.
- On test pass with `BORING_E2E_KEEP=1` → keep artifacts anyway.
- On `test.afterAll` → zip all artifacts into `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` for CI upload.

**CI integration:**
- GitHub Actions: upload `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` as workflow artifact (retained 7 days).
- Debug workflow: download artifact + local replay.

**Tests:**
- Passing test: artifacts not written (unless keep env var set).
- Failing test: all 6 artifact files present; tarball created.
- Server log capture: intentional backend error appears in `server.log`.
- HAR file: openable in Chrome DevTools.

**Logging (of the harness itself):** `[e2e-logging]` prefix. Log artifact dir path + flush events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every e2e test that fails produces a full artifact bundle in `e2e-artifacts/<test-name>/`. CI uploads the bundle on failure. A developer can download + explore the bundle to understand exactly what happened. Log redaction works for Authorization headers. Artifacts auto-clean on pass (keep-env override available).

title: fix(agent): resolve @babel/core bundling failure in pnpm build
description:
**Problem:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` fails at the tsup stage.

```
ERROR: Could not resolve "@babel/preset-typescript/package.json"
  ../../node_modules/.pnpm/@babel+core@7.29.0/node_modules/@babel/core/lib/config/files/module-types.js:165:36:
    165 │ ...t packageJson = require("@babel/preset-typescript/package.json");
```

**Cause:** tsup bundles `@vitejs/plugin-react` → `@babel/plugin-transform-react-jsx-self` → `@babel/core`, which tries to `require('@babel/preset-typescript/package.json')` at build time. preset-typescript isn't in the dep tree.

**Options:**
1. Add `@babel/preset-typescript` as devDependency.
2. Mark `@babel/core` and `@vitejs/plugin-react` as external in `tsup.config.ts` (they shouldn't be bundled into the published CLI anyway).
3. Strip Vite plugin imports from server/CLI bundle entrypoint.

**Recommended:** #2 — server/CLI bundle has no business pulling in vite plugins. Mark them external + any other dev-only deps.

**Impact:** blocks `npx @boring/agent` publish. Tests + typecheck still green; only prod build breaks.

**Verification:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` exits 0 and emits `dist/bin/boring-agent.js`.

## Acceptance Criteria

pnpm build succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/ contains: dist/shared.js, dist/server.js, dist/front.js, dist/bin/boring-agent.js. npx-like invocation works.
close_reason: Fixed tsup bundling failure by marking dev-only @vitejs/plugin-react and @babel/core as externals in packages/agent/tsup.config.ts (plus shared external constants). Verification: pnpm --dir packages/agent run build now succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/bin/boring-agent.js emitted and build-artifacts check passes. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: fab7bfc.

Added targeted tests for normalization, concurrency dedupe, direct-status recreate, and store.put failure cache safety. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). title: Export getWorkspaceCommands(workspaceId, navigate) builder for command-palette integration
description:
## Context

`@boring/workspace` ships a command palette (Cmd+K-style). Apps want quick actions for the current workspace: "Open settings", "Manage members", "Create invite", etc. v7 specifies that core EXPORTS a builder; apps register the commands with their palette. No core → workspace dep.

## Scope

1. Create `packages/core/src/front/workspace/commands.ts`:
   ```ts
   import type { NavigateFunction } from 'react-router-dom'

   export interface WorkspaceCommand {
     id: string
     label: string
     keywords?: string[]
     run: () => void
   }

   export function getWorkspaceCommands(workspaceId: string, navigate: NavigateFunction): WorkspaceCommand[] {
     return [
       {
         id: 'workspace.settings',
         label: 'Workspace settings',
         keywords: ['workspace', 'settings', 'edit', 'rename', 'delete'],
         run: () => navigate(`/w/${workspaceId}/settings`),
       },
       {
         id: 'workspace.members',
         label: 'Manage members',
         keywords: ['members', 'team', 'people', 'roles'],
         run: () => navigate(`/w/${workspaceId}/members`),
       },
       {
         id: 'workspace.invites',
         label: 'Invite to workspace',
         keywords: ['invite', 'add', 'new member'],
         run: () => navigate(`/w/${workspaceId}/invites`),
       },
     ]
   }
   ```

2. Export from core's frontend barrel (`src/front/index.ts`).

3. Tests at `workspace/__tests__/commands.test.ts`:
   - **Returns 3 commands**.
   - **Each command has unique id**.
   - **Each command's run() calls navigate with the correct path**.
   - **Each command has at least one keyword**.

4. Documentation snippet for app authors (in `core/docs/CORE.md` or as a code comment): how to register the commands with `@boring/workspace`'s palette.

## Why this exists

Discoverability: power users want keyboard access. Centralizing the command builder in core means every app gets the same command set without copy-pasting; updating commands (renaming, adding new ones) propagates to all apps via a core upgrade.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four tests pass.
- Builder exported from core's frontend.
- A reference snippet shows app authors how to wire it into `@boring/workspace`'s palette.

## Considerations

- The builder is pure (no React hooks); apps call it inside their command-registration code.
- Avoid coupling: do NOT import anything from `@boring/workspace` (would create a dep cycle).
- Future commands (e.g. "Switch workspace", "Sign out") can be added; keep the array stable so app-side sorting is deterministic.

## Tests

Listed above.

## Out of scope

- Hotkey assignments (palette host's job).
- Command icons (palette host's job, optional).

## Spec reference

Decision #1 ("export command-palette builders") in v7 spec. Phase 3. Depends on P3D, P3E, P3F (so the routes the commands navigate to actually exist).
close_reason: Committed at cd7a0e6. 4/4 tests pass. Builder exported from core barrel. Pure function, no React hooks, no dep cycle.

title: Infra-Test: Path-traversal attack corpus (fixture)
description:
**Goal:** Central test fixture of known path-traversal attack vectors. Every Workspace adapter runs it.

**Vectors (minimum 20):**
```
../etc/passwd
../../etc/passwd
..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd                          # URL-encoded
..\etc\passwd                             # Windows-style
/etc/passwd                                 # absolute
//etc//passwd                               # double-slash
./../etc/passwd
foo/../../etc/passwd
foo/../bar/../../etc/passwd
foo\x00/etc/passwd                         # null byte
foo\n/etc/passwd                           # newline injection
foo/../../../../../../../etc/passwd        # many levels
.../...//                                   # ninja
C:\Windows\System32                        # Windows absolute
/proc/self/environ                          # sensitive proc file
/dev/null
~/.ssh/id_rsa                               # tilde expansion
$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa                           # var expansion
<symlink-outside-workspace>                 # symlink escape (set up in test)
<path-ending-with-spaces>
```

**File:** `src/__tests__/fixtures/pathTraversalCorpus.ts` + harness `runPathTraversalCorpus(workspace)`.

**Used by:** every Workspace conformance test.

**Logging:** for each rejected vector, log `[path-attack] rejected: <vector> -> <reason>`. For any vector that succeeds → test fails with stack trace.

**Acceptance:** 100% rejection rate across NodeWorkspace + VercelSandboxWorkspace + any future adapter.

## Acceptance Criteria

100% rejection rate across all Workspace adapters. Fixture imported by conformance suite.
close_reason: shipped — cross-reviewed by claude: ship

title: M1: Session routes (basic in-memory)
description:

**In M1 scope:**
- `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions` → list
- `POST /api/v1/agent/sessions` → create (returns SessionSummary)

- `DELETE /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id` → delete (204)

**Tests:** CRUD roundtrip; delete twice → second returns 404.

**Not in M1:** session title generation (M3b), changes endpoint (M3b), stream resume (M3a).

## Acceptance Criteria

CRUD roundtrip works against in-memory store. Delete twice → second returns 404.

title: M3a: Stream resumption — ring buffer + cursor route
description:
**Goal:** Survivable streaming across client disconnects. Files: `src/server/http/routes/chat.ts` (extended), `src/server/http/streamBuffer.ts`.

**Architecture:**
- **Transport-owned.** Harness has no `reconnect?()`. Any future harness inherits resume automatically.
- Per `(sessionId, turnId)`, keep an **in-memory ring buffer** of emitted chunks + monotonic `chunkIdx`.

**Ring buffer details:**
- Max chunks per buffer: 2000 (tuneable). Older chunks evicted; resume requests for evicted cursors get a 'replay from persistent store' fallback.
- TTL: 60s idle after turn completion. Dropped thereafter.
- Memory bound: ~2000 chunks × ~1KB avg = ~2MB per active turn. Acceptable for v1.

**Flow:**
1. On `POST /api/v1/agent/chat`: allocate buffer, stream chunks to client AND append to buffer.
2. On turn complete: mark buffer complete, hold for TTL, then GC.
3. On `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/:turnId?cursor=<n>`:
   - If buffer exists + turn in-flight: replay chunks `> cursor`, continue live stream.
   - If buffer exists + turn complete: replay from buffer, close.
   - If buffer evicted: fall back to `PiSessionStore.load(sessionId)` + serialize from `UIMessage[]` (lossy — no exact chunk-level replay, but semantically correct).
   - If turn not found: 404.

**Client-side (useChat):**
- Wired with `experimental_resume: true`.
- On page reload mid-stream: useChat calls the resume endpoint with last known cursor.

**Tests:**
- Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect → receives chunks `> cursor` only (no duplicates).
- Completed turn replay returns full transcript.
- Buffer eviction: stale buffers dropped after 60s (assert memory release).
- Evicted buffer resume: serves from SessionStore fallback.
- Cursor > buffer length → 416 Range Not Satisfiable.

**Logging:** `[resume]` prefix; log cursor + buffer size + eviction events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.
acceptance_criteria: Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.
close_reason: shipped

title: core-M3: UserSettingsPage (/me) — display info + change password + delete account
description:
## Goal
The `/me` page shipped by `<BoringApp>`. Displays user info, allows displayName + settings JSON edit, gates account deletion behind email-confirm typing.

## Why it matters
`<BoringApp>` mounts `/me` as a default route. Without an actual page component, the route 404s or renders nothing.

## Acceptance criteria
- `<UserSettingsPage>` rendered at `/me` by default.
- Sections (cards):
  1. **Profile**: editable `displayName` + read-only `email` (email change deferred to v1.x). Save button calls `PUT /api/v1/me/settings`.
  2. **Theme**: reuse `<ThemeToggle>` component.
  3. **Sign out**: button calling `signOut()`.
  4. **Danger zone**: "Delete account" button. Click opens a confirm dialog that requires the user to type their email (read from useUser); enabled only when typed === actual. Submits to `DELETE /api/v1/me` with `{ confirm: email }`.
- 409 `last_owner` from delete → inline error explaining workspace transfer is needed.
- 400 from PUT settings → inline form errors.
- All form interactions use proper aria-labels for accessibility.

## Considerations
- Form state via `react-hook-form` + Zod resolver (consistency with auth pages).
- Confirm dialog uses workspace's `<AlertDialog>` shadcn primitive.

## Tests
- Render `/me` as authenticated user → all sections visible.
- Edit displayName + save → PUT made, useUser refetches.
- Type wrong email in delete confirm → button disabled.
- Type correct email + click → DELETE /me invoked → user redirected to `/auth/signin`.
- 409 last_owner → inline error with link to workspace member management.

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Sign-in / sign-up / reset / verify pages (overall pattern); §M2_ROUTE_DELETE_ME bead.
close_reason: UserSettingsPage at /me: profile section (email, name, createdAt), change password form (react-hook-form + Zod, calls changePassword with revokeOtherSessions), delete account with AlertDialog (type DELETE to confirm, sends email as confirm to DELETE /api/v1/me, handles sole-owner 409). Added useChangePassword hook. BoringApp wired to real page. 12 tests. Commit c5addb2.

title: M3a: Abort propagation (useChat.stop → pi → sandbox.exec)
description:
**Goal:** `useChat.stop()` cleanly halts the full pipeline. Files: chat route + harness + sandbox adapters.

**Chain:**
1. Client `stop()` → fetch abort.
2. Route's AbortController signals.
3. Passes to `harness.sendMessage(..., {abortSignal})`.
4. Harness passes to pi's `runTurn({abortSignal})`.
5. Pi propagates to any in-flight `tool.execute({abortSignal})`.
6. Our tools pass to `sandbox.exec({abortSignal})`.
7. Sandbox adapter kills the process (bwrap) or aborts the Vercel runCommand.

**Accepted risk:** pi's abort propagation is unproven for all cases. Test each mode in M1 and M2.

**Tests:** mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end; assert no orphaned bwrap child processes; assert sandbox.exec promise rejects with AbortError.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.
acceptance_criteria: Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.
close_reason: shipped

title: Phase 3a: Workspace Bridge — State Store + Commands
description:
## Goal
WorkspaceBridge implementation: command dispatcher, typed event system, selector-based subscriptions, usePanelActions hook, and bridge command validation. Bridge reads/writes the single Zustand store.

## Full Type Definitions

### WorkspaceBridge interface
\`\`\`typescript
interface WorkspaceBridge {
  // Read (agent sees what user sees)
  getOpenPanels(): PanelState[]
  getActiveFile(): string | null
  getDirtyFiles(): string[]
  getVisibleFiles(): string[]

  // Write (agent acts like a user) — all return Promise<CommandResult>
  openFile(path: string, opts?: { mode?: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }): Promise<CommandResult>
  openPanel(config: DynamicPaneConfig): Promise<CommandResult>
  closePanel(id: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  expandToFile(path: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  showNotification(msg: string, level?: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'): Promise<CommandResult>
  navigateToLine(file: string, line: number): Promise<CommandResult>
  markDirty(path: string): void
  markClean(path: string): void

  // Subscribe — typed events with discriminated union
  subscribe<K extends keyof BridgeEventMap>(
    event: K,
    handler: (data: BridgeEventMap[K]) => void
  ): Unsubscribe

  // Selector-based subscription (fires only when selected slice changes)
  select<T>(selector: (state: WorkspaceState) => T, handler: (value: T) => void): Unsubscribe

  // Connectivity
  onDisconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
  onReconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
}
\`\`\`

### CommandResult
\`\`\`typescript
interface CommandResult {
  seq: number
  status: 'ok' | 'error'
  error?: { code: string, message: string }
}
\`\`\`

### BridgeEventMap (exhaustive, no stringly typing)
\`\`\`typescript
interface BridgeEventMap {
  'panel:opened': { panelId: string; params: Record<string, unknown> }
  'panel:closed': { panelId: string }
  'panel:activated': { panelId: string; previousPanelId: string | null }
  'file:opened': { path: string; mode: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }
  'file:saved': { path: string }
  'file:dirty': { path: string; dirty: boolean }
  'sidebar:toggled': { collapsed: boolean }
  'notification:shown': { message: string; level: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error' }
  'pane:error': { panelId: string; error: string; stack?: string }
}
// Apps extend via module augmentation:
// declare module '@boring/workspace' {
//   interface BridgeEventMap { 'myapp:custom': { payload: string } }
// }
\`\`\`

### UIStatePut (PUT body format)
\`\`\`typescript
interface UIStatePut {
  v: 1                                    // protocol version
  causedBy: 'user' | 'agent' | 'restore' // prevents agent echo loops
  openPanels: PanelState[]
  activePanel: string | null
  activeFile: string | null
  visibleFiles: string[]                  // file paths shown in tree
  dirtyFiles: string[]                    // files with unsaved changes
}
\`\`\`

## What to implement

### bridge/commands.ts — command definitions + dispatcher
- Command definitions with per-kind dispatch:
  - openFile(path, opts?) → file-type routing via PanelRegistry.resolve(), then addPanel via DockviewShellApi
  - openPanel(config) → addPanel with explicit component. Idempotent: duplicate ID activates existing (prefer_existing: true)
  - closePanel(id) → removePanel. Reject if essential flag is set.
  - showNotification(msg, level) → add to notifications slice in store
  - navigateToLine(file, line) → openFile + set cursor position via panel params
  - expandToFile(path) → expand tree to path, scroll to file
  - markDirty(path) / markClean(path) → update dirtyFiles map in store
- All write commands return Promise<CommandResult>
- Fire-and-forget works (just don't await)
- Max 50 open panels per workspace (hard cap, not rate)

### bridge/useWorkspaceBridge.ts — hook for panes
- Returns WorkspaceBridge interface
- Backed by the single Zustand store
- Local commands (from user) → immediate Zustand update + PUT state
- Agent commands (from SSE) → same Zustand update code path
- causedBy field distinguishes source on PUT

### hooks/usePanelActions.ts — exported public hook
- open(path): resolve file type → open in correct editor, activate panel
- close(panelId): close panel, activate next tab
- activate(panelId): bring panel to front
- openToSide(path): open file in a new panel beside current
- Thin wrapper around DockviewShellApi + bridge methods
- Extracted from v1's usePanelActions.js (16 KB). v2 is much smaller — only core open/close/activate. File-specific logic stays in panes.

### Bridge State Scope
- Bridge sends: openPanels, activePanel, activeFile, visibleFiles (file tree paths), dirtyFiles
- Bridge does NOT send: full file tree state (agent queries files via its own tools)
- This eliminates bandwidth problem of broadcasting 10K+ entries on every panel change

### dirtyFiles slice
- Map<string, { panelId: string, savedAt: number | null }>
- Updated by editors via bridge.markDirty/markClean
- Exposed to agents via bridge.getDirtyFiles()
- Enables auto-save-before-commit workflows

## SECURITY-CRITICAL: Bridge Command Validation

Every bridge command passes through a per-kind Zod validator before execution. Unknown kinds are rejected. This runs client-side on SSE commands AND is enforced server-side by @boring/agent.

| Kind | Params | Constraints |
|------|--------|-------------|
| openFile | path: string, mode?: 'view'\|'edit'\|'diff' | path max 1024 chars, no path traversal (../) |
| openPanel | id: string, component: string, params?: Record<string, JsonSerializable> | id alphanum+dash max 64, component must exist in registry, params max 16KB |
| closePanel | id: string | must be open, must NOT be essential |
| showNotification | msg: string, level?: 'info'\|'warn'\|'error' | msg max 500 chars, plain text only (NO HTML) |
| navigateToLine | file: string, line: number | file path validated same as openFile, line positive integer |
| expandToFile | path: string | path validated same as openFile |

### Path parameter safety
openFile, navigateToLine, expandToFile paths MUST NOT shortcircuit to direct file reads. All path params go through same validation as agent-side fileRoutes.

### Text parameter safety
showNotification msg, panel titles: rendered as React text nodes ({msg}), NEVER via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Truncated to max length.

### Rate limiting
Deferred to post-launch. Bridge commands are infrequent (~1 per agent turn). Max 50 open panels hard cap enforced.

## Risk 14: Bridge command lifecycle (async but returns Promise)
bridge.openFile('/file.ts') is async internally (needs file-type resolution + DockviewShellApi.addPanel). Agent calls openFile then immediately reads getOpenPanels() — file isn't there yet.

Mitigation: All commands return Promise<CommandResult>. Agent can await:
\`\`\`typescript
const { seq, status } = await bridge.openFile('/file.ts')
const panels = bridge.getOpenPanels()  // now includes the file
\`\`\`

## v1 References
- frontendState.js (2.6 KB) — current UI state bridge: panel snapshots, command queuing
- uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) — state shape: { openPanels, activeFile, commands }
- usePanelActions.js (16 KB) — open/close/activate, file-to-panel routing

## Success Criteria
- All bridge commands work and return CommandResult
- Events fire for all state changes (each event type tested)
- Selector subscriptions only fire when selected slice changes
- dirtyFiles accurately tracks unsaved changes
- Module augmentation works for custom events
- usePanelActions hook: open, close, activate work correctly
- Validation rejects invalid commands (bad paths, essential panels, oversized params)
- Text params rendered safely (no XSS)
- Max 50 panels enforced
- causedBy correctly set on PUT body for user vs agent vs restore

## Unit Tests
- Each bridge command: happy path + error path (bad params, essential, max panels)
- BridgeEventMap: each event type fires correctly
- select() subscription: fires on slice change, NOT on unrelated changes
- dirtyFiles: markDirty adds entry, markClean removes, getDirtyFiles returns correct list
- usePanelActions: open resolves file type correctly, close activates next tab
- Zod validation: each command kind validated with correct constraints
- Path validation: ../ rejected, max length enforced
- Text validation: >500 chars truncated, HTML not interpreted
- causedBy: user actions get 'user', SSE commands get 'agent', restore gets 'restore'
- Idempotency: openPanel with duplicate ID activates existing (no second panel)
close_reason: done

title: M4: Fallback renderer for plugin-registered tools

## Acceptance Criteria

Added tests for unknown tool JSON rendering, __fallback override behavior, and precedence over known defaults. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings).
