title: core-M5: apps/full-app fly.toml + Dockerfile + migrate.ts standalone script
description:
## Goal
Commit the production deployment artifacts for `apps/full-app/`: a real `fly.toml`, child-app Dockerfile extending `core/Dockerfile.reference`, and the `migrate.ts` standalone migration script that Fly's `release_command` invokes.

## Why it matters
The SKILL `boring-app-setup` walks operators through Fly deployment, but the actual config files don't exist in the repo. Without them, every operator copy-pastes from the skill's snippets — error-prone. This bead makes deployment "checkout, set secrets, fly deploy" instead of "follow this 8-step doc."

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/full-app/fly.toml`:
  - `app = "boring-ui-v2"` (or operator-configurable; pick v2 since we're deploying fresh)
  - `primary_region = "cdg"` (Paris — matches existing Neon eu-central-1 latency)
  - `[build] dockerfile = "Dockerfile"`
  - `[deploy] release_command = "node dist/server/migrate.js"`
  - `[http_service] internal_port = 3000, force_https = true, auto_stop_machines = true, min_machines_running = 1`
  - `[[vm]] memory = "512mb", cpu_kind = "shared", cpus = 1`
  - `[checks] type = "http", path = "/health", interval = "10s", timeout = "3s", grace_period = "30s"`
- `apps/full-app/Dockerfile` extends `packages/core/Dockerfile.reference` and sets `CMD ["node", "apps/full-app/dist/server/main.js"]`.
- `apps/full-app/src/server/migrate.ts`: standalone script that:
  - Loads config via `loadConfig()`.
  - Calls `runMigrations(config)` from `@boring/core/server`.
  - First migration step: `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;` (verifies pgcrypto available before running schema migrations).
  - Logs progress + exit codes.
  - exits(0) on success, exit(1) on failure with stack trace.
- `apps/full-app/.fly/secrets.example` — comment-block listing every secret to set via `fly secrets set`. References the vault paths so operator can `fly secrets set DATABASE_URL=$(vault kv get -field=database_url secret/agent/app/boring-ui-v2/prod)`.

## Vault integration

The skill's deploy quickstart instructs operators to source secrets from vault:

```bash
fly secrets set \
  DATABASE_URL=$(vault kv get -field=database_url secret/agent/app/boring-ui/prod) \
  BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=$(vault kv get -field=session_secret secret/agent/app/boring-ui/prod) \
  WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(vault kv get -field=settings_key secret/agent/app/boring-ui/prod) \
  BETTER_AUTH_URL=https://boring-ui-v2.fly.dev \
  MAIL_FROM=onboarding@resend.dev \
  MAIL_TRANSPORT_URL=resend://$(vault kv get -field=api_key secret/agent/services/resend) \
  CORS_ORIGINS=https://boring-ui-v2.fly.dev \
  --app boring-ui-v2
```

Reuses existing session_secret + settings_key from `secret/agent/app/boring-ui/prod` (per locked decision); resend api_key from `secret/agent/services/resend`; sandbox `onboarding@resend.dev` for MAIL_FROM (no DNS setup needed for first deploy).

## Tests
- `fly deploy --remote-only` against a staging Fly app succeeds end-to-end.
- `fly logs` shows: migrate.ts ran successfully, server listening on :3000, /health 200.
- `fly status` shows machines running, last release succeeded.

## Refs
CORE.md §Deployment; .claude/skills/boring-app-setup/SKILL.md §Part 2; M0_DOCKERFILE_REFERENCE bead.
close_reason: fly.toml (cdg, release_command=migrate, /health check, no scale-to-zero), migrate.ts (loadConfig + runMigrations with advisory lock). Dockerfile unchanged — existing multi-stage build already copies dist/ + drizzle/. trustProxy confirmed in createCoreApp. Typecheck clean.

title: Fix jest-dom matcher registration for jsdom-environment front tests
description:
## Context

14 front-end component tests fail with 'Invalid Chai property: toBeDisabled/toBeInTheDocument/toHaveTextContent'. The jest-dom matchers from `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` are not being registered in the jsdom test environment.

## Root cause

`packages/core/src/front/__tests__/_setup.ts` calls `await import('@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest')` inside a `beforeEach` callback. The matchers extend vitest's `expect` in the node environment context, but front tests annotated with `// @vitest-environment jsdom` get a different `expect` instance.

## Fix

Add a jsdom-specific setup file (`setupFiles` in vitest config with environment filtering), or move the jest-dom import to a file-level side-effect import that runs in EACH environment.

## Affected tests

ConfigProvider.test.tsx (5), UserSettingsPage.test.tsx (1), VerifyEmailPage.test.tsx (2), userNavComponents.test.tsx (6)

title: M1: Pi event → UIMessage stream adapter
description:
**Goal:** Convert pi `SessionEntry` events to AI SDK `UIMessageChunk` stream. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/streamAdapter.ts`.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/canonicalStream.ts` — study event-emission pattern but REWRITE for `createUIMessageStream` (not raw SSE).

**Full event mapping table (pi → AI SDK):**

| Pi SessionEntry kind | AI SDK UIMessageChunk | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `text-start` | `{type:'text-start', id}` | |
| `text-delta` | `{type:'text-delta', id, delta}` | streamed content |
| `text-end` | `{type:'text-end', id}` | |
| `reasoning-start` | `{type:'reasoning-start', id}` | thinking blocks |
| `reasoning-delta` | `{type:'reasoning-delta', id, delta}` | |
| `reasoning-end` | `{type:'reasoning-end', id}` | |
| `tool-input-start` | `{type:'tool-input-start', toolCallId, toolName}` | |
| `tool-input-delta` | `{type:'tool-input-delta', toolCallId, delta}` | |
| `tool-input-available` | `{type:'tool-input-available', toolCallId, input}` | complete input |
| `tool-output-available` | `{type:'tool-output-available', toolCallId, output}` | |
| `tool-output-error` | `{type:'tool-output-error', toolCallId, errorText}` | |
| `finish` | `{type:'finish'}` | |
| `abort` | `{type:'error', errorText:'Aborted'}` + `{type:'finish'}` | convert abort to clean finish |
| `usage` | `{type:'data-usage', data:{input,output,cost}}` | for /cost slash command |
| `*` (unknown) | `{type:'data-status', data:{level:'warn', msg:'unknown pi event', original}}` | future-compat |

**Heartbeat (M3b):** no-op here; injected upstream by the route (see `m3b.heartbeat-events`).

**Tests:**
- Conformance fixture: given a scripted pi event stream, assert exact UIMessageChunk sequence.
- Per-event-type round-trip: every mapping in the table has a unit test.
- Unknown/future pi event types are passed through as `data-status` so they render as warnings rather than crash.
- Abort events produce clean UI (no 'Error: ...' stack in chat; just a clean 'aborted' status).

**Logging:** `[stream-adapter]` prefix; log each unknown event type with sample data for forensics.

**Dependencies:** blocked by `m1.pi-harness`.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every pi event type in the mapping table has a unit test. Unknown event types become `data-status` warnings, not crashes. Abort events map to clean finish.
acceptance_criteria: Every pi event type in the mapping table has a unit test. Unknown event types become `data-status` warnings, not crashes. Abort events map to clean finish.
close_reason: shipped — full pi event→UIMessageChunk mapping table + createEventListener, 21 tests, codex ship

title: Phase 1: Panel Registry + Command Registry
description:
## Goal
Panel registration system (register/get/list/resolve panels by ID or file pattern) and separate CommandRegistry for command palette commands.

## What to implement

### PanelRegistry (src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts)
- register(id, config: PanelConfig): register a panel
- get(id): get panel config by ID
- list(): all registered panels
- has(id): check existence
- resolve(filename): file-type routing — match filename against filePatterns
- getComponents(): returns component map for DockviewReact (lazy-loaded via React.lazy)

### File-type routing precedence (Risk 13)
Explicit precedence rules:
1. App-registered panels checked before built-ins
2. Longest suffix match wins (*.test.ts > *.ts > *)
3. Tied: first registered wins (deterministic — registration order)
4. bridge.openFile(path, { panel: 'code-editor' }) with explicit panel bypasses routing

Implementation: resolve(filename) iterates panels in registration order (app first, then built-in). For each panel with filePatterns, test each glob against filename. Track best match by suffix length. Return panel with longest suffix match.

### Built-in panels (all lazy-loaded except empty)
- filetree: () => import('./panes/FileTreePane') (~15KB gz with react-arborist)
- code-editor: () => import('./panes/CodeEditorPane') (~250KB gz)
- markdown-editor: () => import('./panes/MarkdownEditorPane') (~300KB gz)
- data-catalog: () => import('./panes/DataCatalogPane') (~2KB gz)
- empty: eagerly loaded (<1KB)

### Default file-type routes
- *.md, *.mdx → markdown-editor
- * (fallback) → code-editor

### Capability gating
- PanelConfig.requiresCapabilities?: string[]
- Registry filters available panels based on WorkspaceProvider.capabilities prop
- e.g., agent panel requires 'agent.chat' capability
- list() and getComponents() only return panels whose capabilities are satisfied

### CommandRegistry (src/registry/CommandRegistry.ts) — tenth pass, separate from panels
- registerCommand(config: CommandConfig): register a command
- getCommands(): all registered commands
- getCommand(id): get by ID
- CommandConfig { id: string, title: string, run: () => void, shortcut?: string, when?: () => boolean }
- Built-in commands: Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Agent Panel, Save File, Close Tab
- Used by CommandPalette (Phase 4) with > prefix
- Panels and extensions register custom commands at runtime

### RegistryProvider (src/registry/RegistryProvider.tsx)
- React context providing both PanelRegistry and CommandRegistry
- useRegistry() hook for panels
- useCommandRegistry() hook for commands

### Types (src/registry/types.ts)

```typescript
interface PanelConfig {
  id: string
  component: React.ComponentType<PaneProps> | (() => Promise<{ default: React.ComponentType<PaneProps> }>)
  title?: string
  icon?: React.ComponentType<{ size: number }>  // lucide-react compatible
  placement?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right' | 'bottom'
  essential?: boolean  // cannot be closed by user
  hideHeader?: boolean
  requiresCapabilities?: string[]
  filePatterns?: string[]  // file globs this pane handles (e.g., ['*.csv', '*.parquet'])

  // Lifecycle hooks — called by DockviewShell, run outside React render cycle
  onActivate?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void
  onDeactivate?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void
  onClose?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void | Promise<void>

  // State serialization for persistence
  serializeState?: (panelId: string) => Record<string, JsonSerializable> | null
}

interface PanelLifecycleApi {
  panelId: string
  title: string
  setTitle(title: string): void
  close(): void
  focus(): void
  isActive: boolean
}

interface PaneProps {
  panelId: string
  params: Record<string, unknown>
  api: PanelLifecycleApi
  bridge: WorkspaceBridge
}

interface CommandConfig {
  id: string
  title: string
  run: () => void
  shortcut?: string
  when?: () => boolean
}
```

### getFileIcon utility (src/utils/fileIcons.ts)
- getFileIcon(filename: string): React.ComponentType<{ size: number }>
- Maps file extensions to lucide-react icons
- Used by FileTree and TabBar for consistent file icons
- Exported from public API: `export { getFileIcon } from './utils/fileIcons'`

### Panel authoring — typed object literals (no factory function)
```typescript
import type { PanelConfig } from '@boring/workspace'

export const csvViewer: PanelConfig = {
  id: 'csv-viewer',
  component: () => import('./CsvViewer'),
  title: 'CSV Viewer',
  icon: TableIcon,
  filePatterns: ['*.csv', '*.tsv'],
}

// Then in app shell:
<WorkspaceProvider panels={[csvViewer, imageViewer, agentPane]}>
```

Apps can override by registering a panel with more specific patterns:
```tsx
<WorkspaceProvider
  panels={[
    { id: 'csv-viewer', component: CsvViewer, filePatterns: ['*.csv', '*.tsv'] },
    { id: 'image-viewer', component: ImageViewer, filePatterns: ['*.png', '*.jpg', '*.svg'] },
  ]}
/>
// Now opening a .csv file routes to CsvViewer instead of code-editor
```

## v1 references
- panes.jsx (484 lines) — registration, lazy loading, capability gating
- DROP: requiresRouters, requiresFeatures (legacy)
- KEEP: requiresCapabilities for optional runtime gating

## Risk mitigations embedded
- **Risk 11** (Panel ID not found in registry): DockviewShell validates all panel IDs against the registry in onReady before calling api.addPanel(). Unknown ID → console.error with available IDs, skip the panel. In dev mode, throw to catch typos early.
- **Risk 13** (filePatterns routing precedence): Explicit precedence rules above. App panels always checked before built-ins. Longest suffix match wins. First registered breaks ties. Explicit panel param bypasses routing entirely.

## Considerations
- Panel registry populated by WorkspaceProvider from panels prop + built-in defaults
- Apps can override built-in panels by registering with same ID (app wins)
- App-registered panels with matching filePatterns override defaults
- Initial bundle = dockview + shadcn + zustand + registry only (~120KB gz). Every pane is a separate chunk.
- No definePanel() factory — typed object literal gives same autocomplete (ninth pass decision)

## Unit tests (registry/registry.test.ts)

### PanelRegistry
- Test: register(id, config) stores panel, get(id) returns it
- Test: register duplicate ID overwrites previous
- Test: list() returns all registered panels
- Test: has(id) returns true for registered, false for unregistered
- Test: getComponents() returns map of {id: React.lazy(component)} for all panels
- Test: getComponents() wraps sync components and async importers correctly

### File-type routing (resolve)
- Test: resolve('readme.md') → 'markdown-editor'
- Test: resolve('main.ts') → 'code-editor' (fallback)
- Test: resolve('data.csv') with csv-viewer registered → 'csv-viewer'
- Test: app panel *.ts overrides built-in *.ts (app checked first)
- Test: *.test.ts beats *.ts (longest suffix wins)
- Test: tied suffix length → first registered wins
- Test: resolve() with no matching panels → returns undefined/null

### Capability gating
- Test: panel with requiresCapabilities=['agent.chat'] + capabilities={'agent.chat': true} → available
- Test: panel with requiresCapabilities=['agent.chat'] + capabilities={} → filtered out
- Test: panel with no requiresCapabilities → always available
- Test: list() respects capability filter
- Test: getComponents() respects capability filter

### CommandRegistry
- Test: registerCommand(config) stores command, getCommand(id) returns it
- Test: getCommands() returns all registered commands
- Test: command with when: () => false filtered from active commands
- Test: built-in commands (Toggle Sidebar, Save File) auto-registered

### RegistryProvider
- Test: useRegistry() returns PanelRegistry inside RegistryProvider
- Test: useCommandRegistry() returns CommandRegistry inside RegistryProvider
- Test: hooks throw outside RegistryProvider context

### getFileIcon utility
- Test: getFileIcon('file.ts') returns TypeScript icon
- Test: getFileIcon('file.py') returns Python icon
- Test: getFileIcon('unknown.xyz') returns default file icon

## Success criteria
- register/get/list/has/resolve all work correctly
- Lazy loading works (React.lazy + Suspense)
- File-type routing respects precedence rules
- Capability gating filters unavailable panels
- CommandRegistry stores and retrieves commands
- Both registries accessible via hooks within WorkspaceProvider
- Built-in panels registered automatically
- App panels override built-ins when same ID provided
- getFileIcon returns correct icons for known extensions
close_reason: PanelRegistry + CommandRegistry implemented with discriminated-union type safety, file-type routing, capability gating, source-aware tie-breaking, try/catch when predicates. 35 tests, 4 rounds of cross-review. Commit edb7967.

title: core-M6: rate-limit per-endpoint config + integration test
description:
## Goal
M2 implemented rate-limit on routes; this bead is the hardening pass: configurable limits, integration tests per endpoint, tuning, structured 429 responses.

## Endpoint paths

Same as M2_RATE_LIMIT bead: rate limits apply to better-auth's BACKEND handler paths, not frontend route slugs. See M2_RATE_LIMIT for the canonical mapping table.

The 5 rate-limited paths:
- \`/auth/sign-in/email\`
- \`/auth/sign-up/email\`
- \`/auth/forget-password\` (better-auth's spelling — NOT \"forgot\")
- \`/auth/send-verification-email\`
- \`POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites\`

\`/auth/sign-out\` is explicitly excluded.

## Acceptance criteria
- \`CoreConfig.rateLimit\` (optional) with shape \`{ [endpoint: string]: { max: number; window: string } }\` for overriding defaults.
- Default values match M2_RATE_LIMIT bead exactly.
- 429 envelope tested per CORE.md §API reference §Error handling.

## Tests
- **Per-endpoint hammer test**: for each of the 5 rate-limited better-auth API paths:
  1. Make N+1 requests where N = the documented limit, all from same IP within the window.
  2. Assert: first N succeed (or fail for non-rate-limit reasons), N+1th returns 429.
  3. Assert: 429 has \`Retry-After\` header AND body matches \`{error, code: 'rate_limited', message, requestId}\`.
- **Workspace-scoped key**: 21 invite-create requests on same workspace from DIFFERENT IPs → 21st gets 429.
- **Different-workspace isolation**: 20 invites on A then 1 on B → both succeed.
- **Window expiry**: hit limit, wait window-duration + 1s, assert next request succeeds.
- **Signout pass-through**: 6 rapid \`/auth/sign-out\` within 1 minute → all 200.
- All tests log structured pino events.

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Security additions §Rate limiting; §Milestones §M6; M2_RATE_LIMIT bead.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: Risk: Abandoned Vercel sandbox leaks — document + monitor, no release hook in v1
description:
**Goal:** Document exposure + provide a later-mitigation path for abandoned Vercel sandboxes. Spec §Risks: "Abandoned Vercel sandbox leaks — no release-on-session-close hook. Vercel's own TTL eventually deletes."

**v1 posture:** ACCEPT the exposure. No session-close release hook. Each abandoned session costs ~45 min × active-CPU-ms.

**Why accept now:**
- Exposure is bounded by Hobby's 45-min auto-TTL or Pro's 5-hour TTL.
- Per spec pricing: ~$0.10 per abandoned 30-min session.
- Mitigation cost (lifecycle-aware release hook) is moderate (~40 LOC) but requires tricky lifecycle logic (when IS a session "closed"? browser tab close doesn't reliably fire).

**What to document (`docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md` section):**
- Per-user estimated monthly cost for typical usage patterns.
- Billing monitoring recommendation: set Vercel budget alert at $X/month for the team account.
- Manual cleanup: `vercel sandbox list --team <id>` + `vercel sandbox stop <id>` CLI workflow.

**Trigger for building mitigation:**
- First user reports surprise bill OR
- Monitoring shows > 10 abandoned sandboxes at peak.

**Pre-designed mitigation (for reference when triggered):**
1. Browser `beforeunload` → POST `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/release` → call `sandbox.stop()`.
2. Backend idle timer: if no requests for 10 min → stop sandbox (recreate on next request via snapshot).
3. Manual `--idle-stop` CLI flag.

**Monitoring (lightweight, v1):**
- Backend logs `[sandbox] created workspaceId=X sandboxId=Y` on each create.
- Simple grep over logs: count creates vs stops per day.
- Later: expose `/api/v1/metrics` Prometheus-format endpoint (deferred).

**Tests:**
- Documented smoke: boot agent, terminate ungracefully (kill -9), assert sandbox shows as "alive" in Vercel. (Manual, not CI.)
- Budget alert test: verify alert fires (manual, on Vercel dashboard).

**Logging:** `[sandbox]` prefix on all create/stop events with workspaceId + sandboxId + cost-estimate annotation.

## Acceptance Criteria

Known-limitations doc section exists with cost estimate. Logging in place for post-hoc analysis. Mitigation design documented here so future implementation is fast. No v1 code for release-on-close.
close_reason: Shipped orphan-sandbox risk docs + monitoring/guard instrumentation. Added docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md with failure modes, cost exposure, monitoring, manual cleanup, and mitigation triggers; added [sandbox] create/stop logs with cost annotation and a 24h idle orphan guard in vercel mode; added resolveSandboxHandle tests for lifecycle logging + stale sandbox recycle. Cross-review via claude -p: SHIP (minor follow-up nits only). Note: commit also contains pre-staged workspace cache files from shared worktree state.

title: core-M2: routes — workspace members (list enriched, add, remove)
description:
## Goal
Members endpoints. listMembers returns enriched `WorkspaceMember & { user }` from store.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members`: requireWorkspaceMember(); returns enriched array (with user pick: id/email/name/image).
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); body `{ userId, role }`; returns added member. 409 if already member.
- `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members/:userId`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); calls `removeMember`; on `{removed: false, code}` returns 409 with code.

## Tests
- listMembers returns user details (frontend can render names without separate user-fetch).
- Owner removing the last owner → 409 last_owner.
- Removing non-member → 409 not_member.
- Editor trying to add member → 403 forbidden.

## Refs
CORE.md §API reference §HTTP surface; §DB §WorkspaceStore.listMembers.
close_reason: Member routes shipped: GET enriched list, POST add, DELETE remove with 409 guards. 10 tests.

title: core-M1: schema — user_settings + composite PK + CASCADE FK
description:
## Goal
Define `user_settings` with `(userId, appId)` composite PK and `userId → users.id ON DELETE CASCADE` (user settings are purely user-owned).

## Why it matters
v1 bug: PostgresUserStore queried by `user_id` only, not `(user_id, app_id)` — silent cross-app data leak. v2 enforces correctness at the DB layer with a composite PK.

## Acceptance criteria
- Table `user_settings`:
  - `(userId, appId)` composite primary key
  - `settings` jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
  - `email` text NOT NULL DEFAULT '' (display copy of user's email; not authoritative)
  - `displayName` text NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
  - `updatedAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
- FK `userId → users.id ON DELETE CASCADE` — purely user-owned data, safe to cascade.
- B-tree index on `(userId)` for "all settings for user across apps" queries (rare, but not pathological).

## Considerations
- `email` and `displayName` here are display copies, NOT identity. Authoritative email lives on `users.email` (better-auth). The settings copy is updated on user edit.
- CASCADE here is safe (vs RESTRICT on members/invites) because user_settings has no business value outside the user.

## Tests
- Integration: insert (userA, appX) and (userA, appY); both succeed (composite key works).
- Integration: DELETE user; assert user_settings rows disappear (cascade fires).
- Integration: try INSERT (userA, appX) twice; assert composite PK violation.

## Refs
CORE.md §DB Schema "userSettings"; §Traps from v1 → PostgresUserStore app_id ignored.
close_reason: M1 user_settings schema shipped. Composite PK (user_id, app_id), uuid FK to users.id ON DELETE CASCADE, B-tree index, defaults. Migration + 4 integration tests. Codex SHIP.

title: core-M0: CI workflow (typecheck + test on PR)
description:
## Goal
GitHub Actions workflow that runs `pnpm --dir packages/core typecheck && test && lint` on every PR touching `packages/core/**`.

## Why it matters
Without CI gating, the M0 scaffold can rot silently. Beads landing in M1-M7 need green checks to merge. This bead is the green check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `.github/workflows/core.yml` (or extension of an existing workflow) runs typecheck + test + lint on PR + main pushes.
- Path filter limits triggers to `packages/core/**` and `packages/workspace/**` (since core depends on workspace).
- Cache `pnpm` store for speed.
- Workflow is green on the M0-scaffold PR before merge.

## Considerations
- The repo may already have a CI workflow — extend it rather than creating a parallel one.
- `lint` script in core's package.json: `tsc --noEmit` is acceptable as the lint gate for v1 (no eslint config required yet).

## Tests
- The workflow itself is the test. Run a sentinel PR with a deliberate type error; assert workflow fails.

## Refs
None — this is plumbing.
close_reason: Already satisfied by existing .github/workflows/ci.yml — runs typecheck, unit tests, lint, bundle budget, invariants, and e2e on every PR + main push with pnpm cache. No changes needed.

title: Test Bead Two
description: Type: feature

title: Infra: Structured logging standard + breadcrumb pattern
description:
**Goal:** Every subsystem emits structured logs with a consistent prefix so CI failures are debuggable in one scroll. File: `src/server/logging.ts`.

**Pattern:**
```ts
export function createLogger(prefix: string) {
  return {
    info: (msg, fields?) => console.log(JSON.stringify({level:'info', prefix, msg, ...fields, t: new Date().toISOString()})),
    warn: ...,
    error: ...,
  }
}
```

**Required prefixes:**
- `[chat]` — chat route + stream
- `[sandbox:direct/bwrap/vercel]` — exec ops
- `[workspace]` — fs ops
- `[session]` — session CRUD
- `[bridge]` — UI bridge commands + state
- `[oidc]` — Vercel auth
- `[bake]` — snapshot bake
- `[ready-status]` — cold-start UX
- `[path-attack]` — rejected traversal attempts
- `[abort]` — abort signal flow
- `[plugin]` — pi extension loading
- `[test:<suite>]` — test suites (e2e, conformance, etc.)

**Log levels:** debug (verbose, gated by `BORING_AGENT_VERBOSE=1`), info, warn, error.

**Sensitive fields:** API keys, OIDC tokens → redacted (`***`) in all logs.

**Acceptance:** every subsystem file imports `createLogger(prefix)`; grep confirms no raw `console.log` in `src/server/**`.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every server file uses `createLogger(prefix)`. Grep confirms no raw console.log. Sensitive fields redacted.

title: M0: Interface — AgentTool + ToolCatalog + CatalogDeps
description:
**Goal:** Lock the catalog contract. File: `src/shared/tool.ts` + `src/shared/catalog.ts`.

**Interface (exact shapes from spec §The four abstractions > Catalog & Tools):**
```ts
// tool.ts
export interface AgentTool {
  name: string
  description: string
  parameters: JSONSchema                // JSON Schema (AI SDK-native)
  execute(
    params: Record<string, unknown>,
    ctx: ToolExecContext,
  ): Promise<ToolResult>
}

export interface ToolExecContext {
  abortSignal: AbortSignal
  toolCallId: string
  onUpdate?: (partial: string) => void   // optional progress callback for long-running tools
}

export interface ToolResult {
  content: Array<{ type: 'text'; text: string }>
  isError?: boolean
  details?: unknown                       // opaque debug info (e.g., exit codes, truncation flags)
}

// catalog.ts
export interface CatalogDeps {
  workspace: Workspace
  sandbox: Sandbox
  uiBridge?: UiBridge                     // optional; when present, catalog adds get_ui_state + exec_ui
  fileSearch?: FileSearch                 // optional; used by routes not exposed as an agent tool
}
export type ToolCatalog = (deps: CatalogDeps) => AgentTool[]
```

**Notes:**
- Parameters use **JSON Schema** (AI SDK-native). Pi uses TypeBox internally; we adapt at the harness boundary.
- `ToolExecContext.onUpdate(partial)` is for long-streaming tool output (agent can stream partial progress to UI before final result).
- `CatalogDeps.uiBridge` and `fileSearch` are **optional** — tools that need them guard internally. Document which built-in tools need which deps in the dep matrix.
- Name collisions: last-registered wins in v1 (documented in M4).

**Tests:** Type-level stub. Dep-matrix lint: ensure `standardCatalog` builds with only `{workspace, sandbox}` (uiBridge/fileSearch omitted → no crash, just degraded feature).

**Reference code:** old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/tools-impl.mjs` — tool execute shapes. Adapt.

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Phase 4: Command Palette + Keyboard Shortcuts
description:
## Goal
Cmd+P command palette with file quick-open + command mode (> prefix). Unified keyboard shortcut layer resolving editor vs workspace conflicts (Risk 8).

## What to implement

### Command palette (src/components/CommandPalette.tsx)
- Built on shadcn Command/CommandDialog
- Cmd+P opens palette
- No prefix = file quick-open (fuzzy search over DataProvider file list)
- \> prefix = filter registered commands from CommandRegistry
- Commands contributed via registry.registerCommand({ id, title, run, shortcut? })
- Built-in commands: Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Agent Panel, Save File, Close Tab
- Recently used commands/files shown at top (persisted in preferences)
- Max 50 results shown, sorted by frecency

### Keyboard shortcuts
- Cmd+P → open command palette
- Cmd+B → toggle sidebar
- Cmd+\\ → toggle agent panel
- Cmd+S → save active file (calls flushSave from useEditorLifecycle)
- Cmd+W → close active tab
- Workspace-level shortcuts on workspace container (always win via event.stopPropagation)
- Editor shortcuts (Cmd+Z undo, etc.) handled by focused editor only

### Keybinding conflict resolution (Risk 8)
- Workspace shortcuts registered on workspace root container element
- Editor shortcuts only active when editor has focus
- tiptap: setEditable(false) on blur, true on focus — prevents shortcut capture when unfocused
- CM6: readOnly compartment reconfigured on blur/focus — prevents shortcut capture when unfocused
- Priority: workspace shortcuts > active editor shortcuts > browser defaults

### Integration
- CommandPalette rendered by WorkspaceProvider (always available, portal to body)
- Panels and extensions register commands at runtime via CommandRegistry
- Shortcuts displayed in command palette entries (right-aligned, dimmed)

## v1 references
- useKeyboardShortcuts.js — shortcut registration pattern, priority handling

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Palette open/close
1. Cmd+P opens command palette dialog
2. Escape closes palette and returns focus to previous element
3. Clicking outside palette closes it
4. Palette not rendered in DOM when closed (conditional render, not display:none)

### File quick-open (no prefix)
5. Typing filename filters file list with fuzzy matching
6. Fuzzy match: "apx" matches "App.tsx" and "api/index.ts"
7. Enter on selected file calls bridge.openFile(path)
8. Arrow keys navigate through results
9. Empty file list shows "No files found" state
10. Recently opened files shown first (before typing)

### Command mode (> prefix)
11. Typing ">" switches to command mode
12. Commands from CommandRegistry appear in list
13. Selecting command calls its run() function
14. Commands show shortcut hint (e.g., "Cmd+B" next to "Toggle Sidebar")
15. Unregistered command query shows "No matching commands"

### Built-in commands
16. "Toggle Sidebar" command toggles sidebar visibility
17. "Toggle Agent Panel" command toggles agent panel
18. "Save File" command calls flushSave on active editor
19. "Close Tab" command closes active tab

### Keyboard shortcuts (workspace level)
20. Cmd+B toggles sidebar (works even when editor focused)
21. Cmd+\\ toggles agent panel
22. Cmd+S saves active file
23. Cmd+W closes active tab
24. Cmd+P opens palette (even when palette already has focus — no-op or refocus)

### Keybinding conflict (Risk 8)
25. Cmd+Z in focused CM6 editor triggers undo (editor wins)
26. Cmd+Z when no editor focused does NOT trigger anything (no workspace undo)
27. Cmd+S in focused editor saves file (workspace shortcut, not editor's save)
28. Unfocused tiptap editor does NOT capture keyboard events (setEditable(false))
29. Unfocused CM6 editor does NOT capture keyboard events (readOnly compartment)

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
30. Cmd+P → palette opens → type filename → Enter → file tab opens
31. Cmd+P → type ">toggle" → select "Toggle Sidebar" → sidebar toggles
32. Cmd+B toggles sidebar (verify visibility change)
33. Focus editor → Cmd+Z → verify undo works in editor
34. Click outside editor → Cmd+Z → verify no side effects

## Success criteria
- Cmd+P opens command palette
- File quick-open works with fuzzy matching
- \> prefix shows registered commands
- All keyboard shortcuts work
- No keybinding conflicts between editors and workspace shortcuts
- All 34 tests pass

title: core-M2: react-email templates (5: verify, reset, magic-link, invite, welcome)
description:
## Goal
Five react-email components under `packages/core/src/server/mail/templates/*.tsx`, rendered to HTML+plaintext at send time via `@react-email/render`.

## Why it matters
These are user-facing communications. Branded (uses `appName` from config), accessible (plaintext fallback), spam-resistant (proper HTML structure).

## Acceptance criteria
- Five templates implemented:
  - `VerifyEmail` ({verifyUrl, appName, expiresInHours})
  - `ResetPassword` ({resetUrl, appName, expiresInHours})
  - `MagicLink` ({loginUrl, appName, expiresInMinutes})
  - `WorkspaceInvite` ({acceptUrl, inviterName, workspaceName, role, expiresInDays})
  - `Welcome` ({appName, getStartedUrl}) — feature-flagged via `features.sendWelcomeEmail`
- Each renders to HTML + plaintext via `renderAsync(<Component {...props} />)`.
- Each uses `@react-email/components` primitives (Button, Container, Section, Text, Hr) for email-client compatibility.
- Subject lines hardcoded per template.
- HTML inlines CSS automatically via react-email.
- Storybook-style preview file (`__previews__/*.tsx`) for each template (run via `email dev` if installed; otherwise via vitest snapshot of rendered HTML).

## Considerations
- Don't import shadcn primitives — email clients (Outlook!) don't support modern CSS. Use react-email's hand-tested primitives.
- Localization: English-only in v1 per locked decision. Strings hardcoded in JSX.

## Tests
- Snapshot test: render each template with fixture props; assert HTML matches snapshot.
- Plaintext extraction works (no missing fallback).
- All URLs in HTML pass through `safeRedirect` validation if they would be redirected to (sanity).

## Refs
CORE.md §Auth §Email sending §Templates.
close_reason: All 5 react-email templates implemented with tests. Layout + VerifyEmail + ResetPassword + MagicLink + WorkspaceInvite + Welcome. 9 tests passing. Render helpers exported from server barrel.

title: Frontend: /invites/:token page in BoringApp (resolve preview + accept flow)
description:
## Context

The `/invites/:token` page is the email-link landing page. Users click an invite link in their inbox, land here, see "You've been invited to {workspaceName} as {role}", and accept or decline.

## Scope

1. Add new component `packages/core/src/front/auth/InviteAcceptPage.tsx`:
   - Read `:token` from `useParams`.
   - Use React Query to call `POST /api/v1/invites/resolve` for preview.
   - States to handle:
     - Loading → spinner.
     - Signed out → redirect to `/auth/signin?next=/invites/:token`.
     - Resolve error 404 → "This invite is no longer valid. It may have been revoked or never existed."
     - Resolve error 410 → "This invite has expired."
     - Resolve error 423 → "This invite is temporarily locked due to too many failed attempts. Try again later."
     - Resolve success → render preview card with workspace name, role, expiresAt; Accept and Decline buttons.
   - On Accept: call `POST /api/v1/invites/accept`. Handle:
     - 200 → invalidate `['workspaces']` query, navigate to `/w/:id`.
     - 403 WRONG_USER_EMAIL → "This invite is for a different email. Sign in with that account to accept."
     - 410 → "This invite has expired."
     - 429 → "Too many attempts. Please wait a minute."
   - On Decline: simply navigate away (don't call any API; let the invite expire naturally).

2. Add the route to `BoringApp.tsx`:
   ```tsx
   <Route path="/invites/:token" element={<InviteAcceptPage />} />
   ```

3. Update `routes` table with `invitesAccept: '/invites/:token'`.

4. Tests at `auth/__tests__/InviteAcceptPage.test.tsx`:
   - **Signed out**: redirects with `next` param.
   - **Resolve success**: renders preview with workspace name + role.
   - **Resolve 404**: renders "no longer valid" message.
   - **Resolve 410**: renders "expired" message.
   - **Resolve 423**: renders "locked" message.
   - **Accept happy path**: calls accept, invalidates workspaces query, navigates to `/w/:id`.
   - **Accept wrong email**: renders "for a different email" message.
   - **Decline**: navigates without API call.

## Why this exists

Without a frontend page, invite emails would have to link directly to the API endpoint — which means signed-out users see a bare JSON error or get redirected awkwardly. The page handles the auth boundary, the preview, and the error cases that the API can't communicate visually.

## Acceptance criteria

- All eight tests pass.
- Page renders correctly in `apps/full-app` after manual verification.
- The `next` param survives the signin redirect (existing AuthGate behavior).

## Considerations

- Invite emails (in `WorkspaceInvite` template) need to link to `${appUrl}/invites/${token}` instead of an API URL. Update the email template's `acceptUrl` accordingly. Coordinate with P3B (the routes ship the new endpoints).
- Decline silently: if we ever want to track declines (analytics), we'd add a `/decline` endpoint. Not in v7 scope.
- The page must NOT use `useCurrentWorkspace` — at this point the user might not have any workspaces yet (post-sign-up flow). Use `useUser` only.

## Tests

Listed above.

## Out of scope

- Decline-tracking endpoint.
- Resend invite UI (admin would do this via the InvitesPage — separate bead P3G).

## Spec reference

"Invite-accept page" in v7 spec. Phase 3. Depends on P3B (the resolve/accept endpoints). Coordinate with the WorkspaceInvite mail template's URL.

title: M3b: Slash commands infrastructure + 5 built-ins
description:
**Goal:** `/command` parser + registry + 5 built-ins. Files: `src/front/slashCommands/{parser,registry,builtins}.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Parser:** detects `^/(\w+)(\s+(.*))?$` in composer input; matches registered commands; falls through to normal message if no match.

**Registry API:**
```ts
export function useAgentChat() {
  ...
  return { ..., registerCommand(name, handler) }
}
// handler: (args: string) => void | Promise<void>
```

**5 built-ins:**
- `/clear` — hide (don't delete) messages from display.
- `/reset` — delete current session, create new (confirm).
- `/model <name>` — swap active model.
- `/help` — show command list.
- `/cost` — show per-session token/cost estimates (stubbed in v1: "coming soon" if no usage data yet).

All 5 built-ins fire correct actions. Unknown slash falls through as message. registerCommand extension works.

title: M2: Snapshot-bake flow (one-time per python_packages hash)
description:
**Goal:** On backend startup, if `[sandbox].python_packages` is set and `snapshot_id` is not, create a seed sandbox, install packages, snapshot, cache the id. File: `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/bake.ts` (~150 LOC).

**Flow:**
1. Compute hash of sorted `python_packages + system_packages`.
2. Check local snapshot cache DB for matching hash → if found, use that snapshotId.
3. Otherwise: create seed sandbox with `runtime: python3.13`.
4. Install `system_packages` (dnf install …).
5. Install `python_packages` (`pip install`).
6. Call `sandbox.snapshot()`.
7. Cache `{hash, snapshotId}` locally (JSON file or via SandboxHandleStore extension).
8. Dispose seed sandbox.

**Triggers:** runs once at backend startup (async, non-blocking initial responses). If bake fails, log + fall back to per-call installs (slower but functional).

**Tests:** bake flow executes end-to-end against a mock Vercel client; hash stable across runs; existing cache skips bake.

## Acceptance Criteria

Bake executes end-to-end against mock. Hash stable across runs. Existing cache skips bake. Failure falls back to per-call installs with log.
acceptance_criteria: Bake executes end-to-end against mock. Hash stable across runs. Existing cache skips bake. Failure falls back to per-call installs with log.

title: Migrate workspace shells from imperative ChatCenteredShell to declarative ChatLayout
description:
# Migrate workspace shells from imperative ChatCenteredShell to declarative ChatLayout

**Epic | P1 | Path:** `boring-ui-v2/packages/workspace/`

## Context

Today every consuming app uses `ChatCenteredShell` (imperative full-page composition that hardcodes the chat panel, top bar, drawer, and surface positions). The package also ships `ChatLayout` / `IdeLayout` — declarative dockview layouts that compose panels-by-id — but **zero apps consume them**. They were aspirational architecture; nobody migrated.

The plugin model (epic boring-ui-v2-j9p7, closed) finally gives the workspace package a clean panels + commands + catalogs + agentTools registration story. With that in place, the declarative layout pattern is the right destination:

- App declares LAYOUT SHAPE (`<ChatLayout nav="..." center="..." surface="..." sidebar="..." />`).
- Workspace registers CORE PANELS (chat, session-list, workbench-left, artifact-surface) automatically.
- App contributes ITS OWN PLUGINS (e.g. boring-macro's `macroPlugin` with chart panels + `execute_sql` tool).
- Plugins extend the registries; layouts reference panels by id; dockview resolves.

Result: declarative composition. Apps swap panels by changing ids, plug in new capabilities by adding plugins, none of which requires changes to the layout config or the workspace package.

## Three-tier public API

The package exposes three consumption tiers, all public:

**Tier 1 — declarative pre-shaped layouts** (the easy path; ~80% of apps)
```tsx
<WorkspaceProvider plugins={[macroPlugin]}>
  <TopBar appTitle="..." right={<UserMenu />} />
  <ChatLayout nav="session-list" center="chat" surface="artifact-surface" sidebar="charts" />
</WorkspaceProvider>
```

**Tier 2 — custom LayoutConfig with same chrome** (escape hatch for non-stock shapes)
```tsx
import { ResponsiveDockviewShell, type LayoutConfig } from "@boring/workspace"

const myLayout: LayoutConfig = { version: "2.0", groups: [/* custom */] }

<WorkspaceProvider plugins={[...]}>
  <TopBar ... />
  <ResponsiveDockviewShell layout={myLayout} />
</WorkspaceProvider>
```

**Tier 3 — full custom** (rare; raw primitives)
```tsx
import { DockviewShell, useViewportBreakpoint, useResponsiveSidebarCollapse } from "@boring/workspace"
// app composes any chrome + dockview shape it needs
```

All three tiers share the SAME core panel registry (chat, session-list, workbench-left, artifact-surface). The difference is just how the app shapes the layout around them.

## Substrate vs plugin (architectural commitment)

- **Substrate** = constitutive workspace panels (chat, session-list, workbench-left, artifact-surface). Registered automatically by `WorkspaceProvider`. NOT a plugin. Apps cannot opt out — these ARE what `@boring/workspace` is.
- **Default plugins** = optional capabilities apps can disable via `excludeDefaults`. Today: just `filesystemPlugin` (file tree + editors).
- **App plugins** = host-specific contributions. Apps register via `<WorkspaceProvider plugins={[macroPlugin, ...]}>`.

There is no `chatExperiencePlugin`. Chat is core, not a plugin contribution.

## Goals

- Decompose `src/components/chat/*` into per-pane folders under `src/panes/`, mirroring the editor reorg pattern.
- Auto-register core panels in `WorkspaceProvider`.
- Expose Tier 2 (`ResponsiveDockviewShell`) as public API.
- Migrate `workspace-playground` and `boring-macro-v2` to Tier 1. boring-macro extracts its existing macro-specific UI into a `macroPlugin`.
- Migrate `full-app` to Tier 1.
- Delete `ChatCenteredShell` and friends after all consumers migrated.

## Non-goals

- Tier 2 / Tier 3 example apps. Tier 2 stays exposed; we don't ship an app that uses it today. The escape hatch exists for future needs.
- Touching `@boring/agent`'s ChatPanel API. Workspace's chat panel is a thin wrapper, not a fork.
- Changing the plugin model itself (closed under j9p7).

## Phase breakdown — 7 child beads

A. **Decompose chat shells into per-pane folders** — mechanical moves, no behavior change
B. **Wire core panel registrations in WorkspaceProvider** — substrate auto-mounting
C. **Lift TopBar chrome + expose ResponsiveDockviewShell as public API** — Tier 2 public surface
D. **Migrate workspace-playground to ChatLayout (Tier 1)** — canary
E. **Migrate boring-macro-v2: ChatLayout + extract macroPlugin** — second consumer; proves plugin extension path
F. **Migrate full-app to ChatLayout / IdeLayout** — third consumer
G. **Delete ChatCenteredShell + ChatShellContext + uiCommandStream/Dispatcher cleanup** — close out

A and B can run in parallel. C depends on A (for ChatTopBar lift). D, E, F can run in parallel after A+B+C land. G is the last bead, depends on D+E+F closing.

## Spec / reference

- `packages/workspace/docs/plans/PLUGIN_MODEL.md` (v7.0, plugin model contract)
- `packages/workspace/docs/plans/UNIFIED_EVENT_BUS.md` (events bus that uiCommandStream consumes)
- Closed precondition: epic `boring-ui-v2-j9p7` (plugin model machinery)
notes: Closed 2026-04-29: epic merged into boring-ui-v2-j9p7 per v7.4 merger. The DECLARATIVE_LAYOUT_MIGRATION.md content is now PLUGIN_MODEL.md §'Phase 1.5: Consumer migration'. Phase A-G beads live under j9p7 (j9p7.24-29 + j9p7.18-20).

title: core-M7: SIZE.md bundle gate + 10% growth-without-approval block
description:
## Goal
A `packages/core/SIZE.md` file with current bundle sizes + a CI step that fails PRs which grow size by >10% without an `approved-size-bump` label. THIS BEAD IS THE SOLE OWNER of bundle-size enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- `pnpm --dir packages/core build` outputs gzipped sizes for entry points.
- `SIZE.md` committed with current numbers in a JSON code block + last-updated commit SHA.
- `scripts/check-bundle-size.ts`:
  1. Build core in CI.
  2. Compare gzipped sizes of `dist/server/index.js`, `dist/server/db/index.js`, `dist/front/index.js`, `dist/shared/index.js` to baseline in `SIZE.md`.
  3. Fail if any entry grew >10% AND PR doesn't carry `approved-size-bump` label.
- Excludes peer-dep workspace primitives from the budget.
- M3_STORYBOOK bead does NOT have a bundle-size gate (just Storybook build); this bead is canonical owner.

## Tests
- Self-test: artificially bloat a file → script fails with clear message naming the entry that grew.
- Add label → CI passes.
- All events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-zwf1'`.

## Refs
CORE.md §Milestones §M7 §Bundle size check.
close_reason: scripts/check-bundle-size.ts tracks front (16.85 KB) + server (23.86 KB) gzip in SIZE.md, fails >10% drift, escape hatches: baseline bump or // approved-growth annotation. CI job core-bundle-budget in ci.yml. Self-tested inflation→failure→revert. Committed cf4ede4.

title: Phase 1: WorkspaceProvider
description:
## Goal
Top-level provider component that composes all workspace contexts: registry, bridge, theme, data, store.

## What to implement

### WorkspaceProvider (src/WorkspaceProvider.tsx)
Composes 5 contexts into a single provider:
1. WorkspaceStoreContext — Zustand store (useWorkspaceStore)
2. RegistryContext — PanelRegistry + CommandRegistry (useRegistry, useCommandRegistry)
3. WorkspaceBridgeContext — bridge commands (useWorkspaceBridge)
4. ThemeContext — theme state (useTheme)
5. DataProviderContext — React Query hooks (useFileData, useFileContent, useFileList, useFileWrite)

### Full Props specification (WorkspaceProviderProps)

```typescript
interface WorkspaceProviderProps {
  children: React.ReactNode

  // --- Registry ---
  panels?: PanelConfig[]              // Additional panels beyond built-ins (e.g., ChatPanel)
                                       // Built-ins (filetree, editor, empty) auto-registered

  // --- Capabilities (for panel registry filtering) ---
  capabilities?: Record<string, boolean>  // e.g. { 'agent.chat': true, 'workspace.files': true }

  // --- Data layer ---
  apiBaseUrl?: string                 // Base URL for HTTP data provider. Default: '' (same origin)
  authHeaders?: Record<string, string> // Injected into every HTTP request (e.g., { Authorization: 'Bearer ...' })

  // --- Theme ---
  defaultTheme?: 'light' | 'dark'    // Initial theme. Default: 'light'
  onThemeChange?: (theme: 'light' | 'dark') => void

  // --- Persistence ---
  workspaceId?: string                // Scopes persistence key: 'boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId}'
                                       // Omit for single-workspace apps (falls back to 'boring-ui-v2:layout')
  storageKey?: string                 // Full override for persistence key (takes precedence over workspaceId)
  persistenceEnabled?: boolean        // Default: true. Set false for tests / ephemeral mode

  // --- Layout version migration (tenth pass) ---
  onLayoutVersionMismatch?: (persisted: string, current: string, layout: unknown) => SerializedLayout | null
                                       // Called when persisted layout version !== LayoutConfig.version
                                       // Return migrated layout or null (= reset to defaults)
                                       // Default: () => null (reset)

  // --- Bridge (Phase 3) ---
  bridgeEndpoint?: string             // SSE endpoint for agent→workspace commands. Default: '/api/v1/ui/commands/next'
                                       // Set null to disable bridge (standalone mode)

  // --- Error callbacks ---
  onLayoutError?: (error: Error) => void  // Invalid/corrupted layout detected
  onAuthError?: (statusCode: number) => void  // 401/403 from data provider
}
```

### Contexts provided (each accessible via hook)

| Context | Hook | What it provides |
|---------|------|-----------------|
| WorkspaceStoreContext | useWorkspaceStore() (internal) | Zustand store — layout, active panel, sidebar |
| WorkspaceBridgeContext | useWorkspaceBridge() | Imperative commands — openFile(), openPanel(), etc. |
| RegistryContext | useRegistry() | Panel registry — get(), list(), has(), getComponents() |
| ThemeContext | useTheme() | Theme state — { theme, setTheme } |
| DataProviderContext | useFileData() | React Query hooks — useFileContent(), useFileList(), useFileWrite() |

### Behavior
- Registers built-in panels + app panels into PanelRegistry
- Filters panels based on capabilities
- Sets up Zustand store with persistence configuration (workspaceId/storageKey scoping)
- Initializes bridge SSE connection (if bridgeEndpoint provided)
- Sets up theme provider with defaultTheme + persistence
- Sets up DataProvider with apiBaseUrl + authHeaders
- Sets up React Query QueryClient internally

### Risk 2 mitigation: Circular dependency (workspace ↔ agent)
Both types in core + component via props. Zero direct cross-imports.

- WorkspaceBridge interface + command types live in @boring/core. Workspace implements it, agent consumes the type from core.
- ChatPanel is NOT imported by workspace. Instead, workspace accepts a panels config prop. The app shell passes the agent component in:

```tsx
// In the app shell (NOT in workspace or agent package):
import { IdeLayout } from '@boring/workspace'
import { ChatPanel } from '@boring/agent'

<IdeLayout
  panels={[
    { id: 'agent', component: ChatPanel, placement: 'right' }
  ]}
/>
```

Workspace never imports from agent. Agent never imports from workspace. Both import types from core.

## Considerations
- Tier 3 (standalone) apps do NOT use WorkspaceProvider — components accept all data via props
- Tier 1/2 apps MUST wrap in WorkspaceProvider
- WorkspaceProvider is the ONLY component that knows about all the contexts
- Each context accessible via its own hook (no prop drilling)

## v1 references
- No direct single-provider equivalent in v1 (contexts scattered across App.jsx and layout components)
- v1 pattern: IdeLayout.jsx inlines many concerns that v2 extracts to WorkspaceProvider

## Unit tests (WorkspaceProvider.test.tsx)

### Context composition
- Test: all 5 contexts accessible via hooks inside WorkspaceProvider
- Test: useRegistry() returns PanelRegistry with built-in panels registered
- Test: useCommandRegistry() returns CommandRegistry with built-in commands
- Test: useWorkspaceBridge() returns WorkspaceBridge interface
- Test: useTheme() returns current theme
- Test: useFileData() hooks are available

### Panel registration
- Test: panels prop registers custom panels in registry alongside built-ins
- Test: custom panel with same ID as built-in overrides the built-in
- Test: capabilities prop filters unavailable panels from registry

### Persistence configuration
- Test: workspaceId sets correct localStorage key (boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId})
- Test: storageKey overrides workspaceId when both provided
- Test: persistenceEnabled=false → no localStorage reads/writes

### Bridge initialization
- Test: bridgeEndpoint provided → SSE connection initiated
- Test: bridgeEndpoint=null → no SSE connection (standalone mode)
- Test: bridgeEndpoint not provided → uses default '/api/v1/ui/commands/next'

### Error callbacks
- Test: onAuthError called on 401 from data provider
- Test: onLayoutError called on corrupted layout detection

### Theme
- Test: defaultTheme='dark' → initial theme is dark
- Test: onThemeChange callback fires when theme changes

## Success criteria
- All 5 contexts accessible via hooks inside WorkspaceProvider
- panels prop registers custom panels in registry
- capabilities prop filters unavailable panels
- Persistence configured with correct workspaceId-scoped key
- Bridge connection established when bridgeEndpoint provided
- Works with IdeLayout and ChatLayout children
- Type-safe props with full TypeScript autocomplete
